name: 2WikiMultihopQA
intro: 2WikiMultiHopQA是一个多跳问答数据集，旨在通过要求模型阅读多个段落以回答问题来测试推理和推断能力。该数据集的独特之处在于它为多跳问题提供了包含推理路径的证据信息，这既能为模型的预测提供全面的解释，也有助于评估模型的推理能力。数据集的构建利用了结构化（Wikidata）和非结构化数据，并通过精心设计的流程和模板来保证问题的质量和多跳推理的必要性。
eval_feature: Multi-hop, 长文本, 过程导向
intro_en: 2WikiMultiHopQA is a multi-hop question answering dataset designed to test reasoning and inference abilities by requiring models to read multiple paragraphs to answer questions. Its uniqueness lies in providing evidence containing reasoning paths for multi-hop questions, which not only offers comprehensive explanations for model predictions but also helps evaluate the model's reasoning capabilities. The dataset is constructed using both structured (Wikidata) and unstructured data, with carefully designed processes and templates to ensure question quality and the necessity of multi-hop reasoning.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Wikipedia Articles) --> B[Information Extraction]
    B --> C[Multi-hop Path Identification]
    C --> D[Question Generation]
    D --> E[Answer & Evidence Generation]
    E --> F{{Automated Generation Tool}}
    F --> G[Automated Quality Checks]
    G --> H{Quality Threshold Met?}
    H -- Yes --> I[Human Review & Refinement]
    H -- No --> J[Discard / Re-generate]
    I --> K(2WikiMultihopQA Dataset)
    K --> L[Model Evaluation]
    L --> M[Performance Metrics]
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Data Sourcing
        A([Start]) --> B[/Wikipedia Articles/]
        A --> C[/Wikidata Knowledge Base/]
    end

    subgraph Template Creation
        B --> D{Extract Summaries}
        C --> E{Extract Triples (s,r,o)}
        D & E --> F(Categorize Entities by 'instance of')
        F --> G(Spacy NER on HotpotQA Train Data)
        G --> H(Generate Initial Templates L)
        H --> I{Filter Single-hop/Context-Dependent Templates}
        I --> J(Manually Refine Templates for Top-50 Entities)
        J --> K(Manually Create Inference/Compositional Templates)
        K --> L{Utilize Logical Rules from KB (AMIE Model)}
        L --> M(Verify 28 Logical Rules)
    end

    subgraph Data Generation
        M --> N(Select Two Random Entities e1, e2)
        N --> O(Obtain Wikidata Statements for e1, e2)
        O --> P(Identify Mutual Relations M)
        P --> Q(Acquire Wikipedia Info for e1, e2)
        Q --> R{Check Relation Usability (Answer in Wiki Article)}
        R --> S(Extract Values o1, o2 from Triples)
        S --> T{Verify o1 in Wiki(e1) and o2 in Wiki(e2)}
        T --> U(Generate Question Q)
        U --> V(Concatenate Wiki Articles for Context C)
        V --> W(Identify Sentence-level SFs)
        W --> X(Form Evidence E from Triples)
        X --> Y(Determine Answer A)
        Y --> Z[/Raw QA Pairs/]

        M --> AA(Select Random Entity e)
        AA --> BB(Obtain Wikidata Statements for e)
        BB --> CC(Filter 1-hop Relations H1)
        CC --> DD(Derive 2-hop Relations H2)
        DD --> EE(Acquire Wikipedia Articles for Entities in H2)
        EE --> FF{Check Multi-hop Requirements}
        FF --> GG(Generate Bridge Question Q)
        GG --> HH(Concatenate Wiki Articles for Context C)
        HH --> II(Identify Sentence-level SFs)
        II --> JJ(Form Evidence E from Triples)
        JJ --> KK(Determine Answer A)
        KK --> Z
    end

    subgraph Post-processing and Finalization
        Z --> LL(Balance Yes/No Questions)
        LL --> MM{Discard Ambiguous Cases}
        MM --> NN(Collect Distractor Paragraphs using Bigram TF-IDF)
        NN --> OO(Select Top-8 Distractors based on Entity Type)
        OO --> PP(Shuffle 10 Paragraphs for Final Context)
        PP --> QQ(Split Data into Train, Dev, Test Sets)
        QQ --> RR(Single-hop Model Evaluation for Difficulty)
        RR --> SS(Split into Train-Medium, Train-Hard, Dev, Test)
        SS --> TT([End])
    end

    style A fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style TT fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

flowchart_zh:
flowchart TD
    subgraph 数据来源
        A([开始]) --> B[/维基百科文章/]
        A --> C[/维基数据知识库/]
    end

    subgraph 模板创建
        B --> D{提取摘要}
        C --> E{提取三元组 (s,r,o)}
        D & E --> F(按“实例”分类实体)
        F --> G(对 HotpotQA 训练数据进行 Spacy NER)
        G --> H(生成初始模板 L)
        H --> I{过滤单跳/上下文相关模板}
        I --> J(手动精炼前 50 个实体的模板)
        J --> K(手动创建推理/组合模板)
        K --> L{利用知识库中的逻辑规则 (AMIE 模型)}
        L --> M(验证 28 条逻辑规则)
    end

    subgraph 数据生成
        M --> N(选择两个随机实体 e1, e2)
        N --> O(获取 e1, e2 的维基数据语句)
        O --> P(识别共同关系 M)
        P --> Q(获取 e1, e2 的维基百科信息)
        Q --> R{检查关系可用性 (答案在维基文章中)}
        R --> S(从三元组中提取值 o1, o2)
        S --> T{验证 o1 在 Wiki(e1) 中且 o2 在 Wiki(e2) 中}
        T --> U(生成问题 Q)
        U --> V(连接维基文章作为上下文 C)
        V --> W(识别句子级 SFs)
        W --> X(从三元组中形成证据 E)
        X --> Y(确定答案 A)
        Y --> Z[/原始问答对/]

        M --> AA(选择随机实体 e)
        AA --> BB(获取 e 的维基数据语句)
        BB --> CC(过滤 1 跳关系 H1)
        CC --> DD(推导 2 跳关系 H2)
        DD --> EE(获取 H2 中实体的维基百科文章)
        EE --> FF{检查多跳要求}
        FF --> GG(生成桥接问题 Q)
        GG --> HH(连接维基文章作为上下文 C)
        HH --> II(识别句子级 SFs)
        II --> JJ(从三元组中形成证据 E)
        JJ --> KK(确定答案 A)
        KK --> Z
    end

    subgraph 后处理和最终确定
        Z --> LL(平衡是/否问题)
        LL --> MM{丢弃模糊案例}
        MM --> NN(使用 Bigram TF-IDF 收集干扰段落)
        NN --> OO(根据实体类型选择前 8 个干扰项)
        OO --> PP(打乱 10 个段落作为最终上下文)
        PP --> QQ(将数据拆分为训练集、开发集和测试集)
        QQ --> RR(单跳模型评估难度)
        RR --> SS(拆分为训练-中、训练-难、开发、测试)
        SS --> TT([结束])
    end

    style A fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style TT fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    style B fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

name: AA-LCR
intro: AA-LCR包含100个基于文本的难题，需要跨多个真实世界文档进行推理，每个文档集平均约100k输入tokens。问题设计旨在使答案不能直接从文档中检索，而必须从多个信息源进行推理。
build_method: 专家构建, 人工标注, 混合
task_type: 开放式问答, 知识问答, 数学推理
eval_feature: 长文本推理, 多文档推理
task_type_en: Open-ended QA, Knowledge QA, Math Reasoning
intro_en: AA-LCR consists of 100 text-based challenges requiring reasoning across multiple real-world documents, with each document set averaging about 100k input tokens. The questions are designed so that answers cannot be directly retrieved from the documents but must be inferred from multiple information sources.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Real-world Documents) --> B[Expert Challenge Design]
    B --> C[Document Selection & Integration]
    C --> D[Complex Question Generation]
    D --> E[Manual Annotation]
    E --> F{{Hybrid Construction Methods}}
    F --> G{Quality Assurance Check}
    G -- Fail --> H[Re-annotation & Revision]
    H --> E
    G -- Pass --> I[Final AA-LCR Dataset]
    I --> J[Model Evaluation]
    J --> K(Performance Metrics)
flowchart_en:

    subgraph Start["Start"]
        A([Benchmark Development Start])

        style H fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff

        style I fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff

        style J fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff

        style K fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
        L{Models Fail to Get Right?}

    end

    subgraph HumanValidation["Human Validation"]

        style N fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff

name: AA-Omniscience
intro: AA-Omniscience是一个旨在衡量大型语言模型跨领域知识可靠性的基准。它包含6000个问题，涵盖42个经济相关主题，评估模型的事实召回能力和知识校准。该基准通过全知指数（Omniscience Index）来衡量模型表现，该指数会惩罚幻觉并奖励不确定时的弃权。评估结果揭示了前沿模型在事实性和校准方面的持续弱点。
build_method: 专家构建, 人工标注
intro_en: AA-Omniscience is a benchmark designed to measure the cross-domain knowledge reliability of large language models. It contains 6,000 questions covering 42 economics-related topics, evaluating models' factual recall and knowledge calibration. The benchmark uses the Omniscience Index to assess performance, which penalizes hallucinations and rewards abstentions when uncertain. Evaluation results reveal persistent weaknesses in state-of-the-art models regarding factual accuracy and calibration.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A[(42 Economics Topics)] --> B[Expert Knowledge Acquisition]
    B --> C[Question Generation]
    C --> D[Manual Annotation]
    D --> E{Quality Control & Validation}
    E -- Fail --> F[Refine & Re-annotate]
    F --> E
    E -- Pass --> G(AA-Omniscience Dataset
6,000 Questions)
    G --> H[LLM Evaluation]
    H --> I{{Omniscience Index}}
    I --> J(Factual Recall & Calibration Results)
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD

        A([Start]) --> B(Define 6 Domains & 42 Topics)
        style A fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

        C -->|Yes| D[/Authoritative Publications/]
        C -->|No| B

        E --> F[/Relevant for Current Tasks/]

        style G fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    subgraph Question Generation["Question Generation"]
        G --> H(Automated Question Generation Agent)

name: ACPBench Hard
intro: ACPBench Hard是ACPBench的困难版本，用于评估大型语言模型在复杂推理和规划任务中的能力。该基准包含更具挑战性的任务，要求模型进行多步推理和复杂的动作规划。
intro_en: ACPBench Hard is the challenging version of ACPBench, designed to evaluate large language models' abilities in complex reasoning and planning tasks. This benchmark includes more difficult tasks that require multi-step reasoning and intricate action planning.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Initial Task Pool) --> B{{Automated Task Generator}}
    B --> C[Generate Complex Tasks]
    C --> D[Apply Difficulty Enhancements]
    D --> E{Task Validity Check}
    E -- Invalid --> F[Discard Task]
    E -- Valid --> G[Verify Solvability]
    G -- Unsolvable --> F
    G -- Solvable --> H{{Expert Review/Automated QC}}
    H --> I{Filter for 'Hard' Criteria}
    I -- Fail --> F
    I -- Pass --> J[Assemble Final Dataset]
    J --> K(ACPBench Hard Dataset\nComplex Reasoning Tasks)
    K --> L[Define Evaluation Protocol]
    L --> M[Model Performance Assessment]

name: ADR-Bench
intro: ADR-Bench 是一个旨在弥补中文领域评估空白的基准测试，用于评估真实世界的深度研究场景。它通过多样化的查询集和双重人工与专家评估，严格评估长周期、开放式深度研究任务。在 ADR-Bench 上，Step-DeepResearch 显著优于同类模型，并与顶级的闭源模型相媲美，证明了精细训练能够使中等规模模型以行业领先的成本效益达到专家级能力。
intro_en: ADR-Bench is a benchmark designed to fill the evaluation gap in the Chinese domain, assessing real-world deep research scenarios. It rigorously evaluates long-term, open-ended deep research tasks through a diverse query set and dual human and expert assessments. On ADR-Bench, Step-DeepResearch significantly outperforms similar models and rivals top closed-source models, demonstrating that fine-tuning enables medium-sized models to achieve expert-level capabilities with industry-leading cost efficiency.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Chinese Research Data Sources) --> B[Automated Data Collection]
    B --> C[Query Generation & Task Formulation]
    C --> D{Initial Data Filtering}
    D -- Pass --> E[Human Annotation & Curation]
    D -- Fail --> F[Discard Incomplete/Irrelevant Data]
    E --> G{{Expert Review & Validation}}
    G --> H(ADR-Bench Dataset\nDiverse Query Set)
    H --> I[Agent Model Execution]
    I --> J[Dual Human & Expert Assessment]
    J --> K[Performance Metrics Calculation]
    K --> L[Benchmark Results]

name: AdvancedIF
intro: AdvancedIF是一个全面的基准测试，包含1600多个提示和专家策划的评估标准，旨在评估大型语言模型遵循复杂、多轮和系统级指令的能力。它引入了RIFL（基于评估标准的指令遵循学习）训练流程，通过评估标准的生成和验证来提升模型的指令遵循能力，为更强大、更可靠的人工智能系统铺平了道路。
build_method: 专家构建, 人工标注
task_type: 生成, 对话, 指令遵循
intro_en: AdvancedIF is a comprehensive benchmark containing over 1,600 prompts and expert-curated evaluation criteria, designed to assess large language models' ability to follow complex, multi-turn, and system-level instructions. It introduces the RIFL (Evaluation Criteria-based Instruction Following Learning) training process, which enhances models' instruction-following capabilities through the generation and validation of evaluation criteria, paving the way for more powerful and reliable AI systems.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Human Experts) --> B[Instruction & Criteria Design]
    B --> C[Drafting Complex Prompts]
    B --> D[Defining Evaluation Criteria]
    C --> E[Multi-turn Instruction Formulation]
    D --> F[Expert Review & Refinement]
    E --> G[Manual Annotation]
    F --> G
    G --> H{Quality Check by Experts}
    H -->|Pass| I(Curated Prompts & Criteria)
    H -->|Fail| J[Revise & Re-annotate]
    J --> G
    I --> K(AdvancedIF Dataset\n1600+ Prompts)
    K --> L[LLM Instruction Following Assessment]
    L --> M{{RIFL Evaluation Framework}}
    M --> N(Model Performance Metrics)
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    subgraph "Benchmark Construction: AdvancedIF"
        start_bm([Start Benchmark Construction]) --> data_source[/Complex Instruction Data/]
        data_source --> prompt_collection(Collect >1,600 Prompts)
        prompt_collection --> expert_review{Expert Review of Prompts?}
        expert_review -- Yes --> rubric_creation(Expert-Curated Rubric Generation)
        expert_review -- No --> prompt_refinement(Refine Prompts)
        prompt_refinement --> expert_review
        rubric_creation --> rubric_validation{Validate Rubrics?}
        rubric_validation -- Yes --> advancedif_benchmark[/AdvancedIF Benchmark (1,600+ Prompts, Rubrics)/]
        rubric_validation -- No --> rubric_refinement(Refine Rubrics)
        rubric_refinement --> rubric_validation
    end

    subgraph "RIFL Pipeline: Rubric-based Instruction-Following Learning"
        advancedif_benchmark --> rifl_start([Start RIFL Pipeline])
        rifl_start --> rubric_gen(Automated Rubric Generation)
        rubric_gen --> rubric_verifier_train(Train Finetuned Rubric Verifier)
        rubric_verifier_train --> reward_shaping(Reward Shaping for RL)
        reward_shaping --> llm_training(Reinforcement Learning for LLMs)
        llm_training --> evaluation_advancedif(Evaluate on AdvancedIF Benchmark)
        evaluation_advancedif --> evaluation_public(Evaluate on Public Benchmarks)
        evaluation_advancedif -- 6.7% Gain --> llm_improvement(Improved LLM Instruction Following)
        evaluation_public -- Strong Results --> llm_improvement
        llm_improvement --> end_rifl([End RIFL Pipeline])
    end

    style start_bm fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style end_rifl fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style data_source fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style prompt_collection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style prompt_refinement fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style expert_review fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style rubric_creation fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style rubric_validation fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style rubric_refinement fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff

    style rifl_start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style rubric_gen fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style rubric_verifier_train fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style reward_shaping fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style llm_training fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style evaluation_advancedif fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style evaluation_public fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style llm_improvement fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
flowchart_zh:
flowchart TD
    subgraph "基准构建: AdvancedIF"
        start_bm([开始基准构建]) --> data_source[/复杂指令数据/]
        data_source --> prompt_collection(收集 >1,600个提示)
        prompt_collection --> expert_review{专家评审提示?}
        expert_review -- 是 --> rubric_creation(专家策划的评估标准生成)
        expert_review -- 否 --> prompt_refinement(优化提示)
        prompt_refinement --> expert_review
        rubric_creation --> rubric_validation{验证评估标准?}
        rubric_validation -- 是 --> advancedif_benchmark[/AdvancedIF基准 (1,600+提示, 评估标准)/]
        rubric_validation -- 否 --> rubric_refinement(优化评估标准)
        rubric_refinement --> rubric_validation
    end

    subgraph "RIFL流程: 基于评估标准的指令遵循学习"
        advancedif_benchmark --> rifl_start([开始RIFL流程])
        rifl_start --> rubric_gen(自动化评估标准生成)
        rubric_gen --> rubric_verifier_train(训练微调的评估标准验证器)
        rubric_verifier_train --> reward_shaping(强化学习的奖励塑形)
        reward_shaping --> llm_training(LLM强化学习)
        llm_training --> evaluation_advancedif(在AdvancedIF基准上评估)
        evaluation_advancedif --> evaluation_public(在公共基准上评估)
        evaluation_advancedif -- 6.7% 提升 --> llm_improvement(改进LLM指令遵循能力)
        evaluation_public -- 强劲结果 --> llm_improvement
        llm_improvement --> end_rifl([结束RIFL流程])
    end

    style rubric_refinement fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style advancedif_benchmark fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

name: AetherCode
intro: AetherCode是一个新的基准测试，旨在通过来自IOI和ICPC等顶级编程竞赛的问题来评估大型语言模型的推理和编码能力。该基准测试通过结合具有挑战性的问题设计和稳健的评估，为LLM的能力提供了更忠实的衡量标准，并为未来代码推理领域的研究设立了新标准。
intro_en: AetherCode is a new benchmark designed to evaluate large language models' reasoning and coding abilities using problems from top programming contests like IOI and ICPC. By combining challenging problem design with robust evaluation, this benchmark offers a more faithful measure of LLM capabilities and sets a new standard for future research in code reasoning.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Top Programming Contests: IOI, ICPC) --> B[Problem Extraction & Selection]
    B --> C[Problem Adaptation & Rephrasing]
    C --> D[Ground Truth Solution Generation]
    D --> E{{Automated Test Case Generation}}
    E --> F{Problem Suitability Check}
    F -->|Pass| G[Human Expert Review]
    F -->|Fail| H[Discard Unsuitable Problems]
    G --> I(AetherCode Benchmark Dataset)
    I --> J[LLM Reasoning & Coding Evaluation]
    J --> K(Evaluation Results)
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    style Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style ProcessingFiltering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style AnnotationLabeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style EvaluationMetric fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([Start]) --> Phase1

    subgraph Phase1["Data Sourcing & Initial Selection"]
        A[Identify Premier Programming Contests]:::DataCollection --> B[/IOI & ICPC Problem Archives/]
        B --> C(Collect Raw Problem Statements):::DataCollection
        C --> D{Initial Problem Filtering?}
        D -- Yes --> E(Filter for Broader Coverage & Difficulty):::ProcessingFiltering
        D -- No --> C
    end

    Phase1 --> Phase2

    subgraph Phase2["Problem Processing & Standardization"]
        E --> F(Standardize Problem Format):::ProcessingFiltering
        F --> G(Extract Problem Constraints & Examples):::ProcessingFiltering
        G --> H{Problem Uniqueness Check?}
        H -- Duplicate --> E
        H -- Unique --> I(Categorize Problems by Topic):::AnnotationLabeling
    end

    Phase2 --> Phase3

    subgraph Phase3["Test Suite Generation"]
        I --> J(Automated Test Case Generation):::ProcessingFiltering
        J --> K{Initial Test Cases Generated?}
        K -- Yes --> L(Human Curation of Test Cases):::AnnotationLabeling
        K -- No --> J
        L --> M(Generate Edge Cases & Strong Test Cases):::AnnotationLabeling
        M --> N[/Comprehensive Test Suite/]
    end

    Phase3 --> Phase4

    subgraph Phase4["Quality Control & Validation"]
        N --> O{Test Case Correctness Check?}
        O -- Incorrect --> L
        O -- Correct --> P(Expert Review of Problems & Test Suites):::QualityControl
        P --> Q{Expert Validation Passed?}
        Q -- No --> E
        Q -- Yes --> R(Generate Reference Solutions):::ProcessingFiltering
        R --> S{Reference Solutions Verified?}
        S -- No --> R
        S -- Yes --> T[/Validated Benchmark Components/]
    end

    Phase4 --> Phase5

    subgraph Phase5["Benchmark Assembly & Evaluation"]
        T --> U(Define Evaluation Metrics):::EvaluationMetric
        U --> V(Assemble Final Benchmark):::OutputResult
        V --> W(Evaluate LLMs on AetherCode):::EvaluationMetric
        W --> X[/LLM Performance Results/]
    end

    Phase5 --> End([End])

    style Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    Start([开始]) --> Phase1

    subgraph Phase1["数据来源与初步筛选"]
        A[识别顶级编程竞赛]:::DataCollection --> B[/IOI & ICPC 问题档案/]
        B --> C(收集原始问题陈述):::DataCollection
        C --> D{初步问题筛选？}
        D -- 是 --> E(筛选以获得更广泛的覆盖范围和难度):::ProcessingFiltering
        D -- 否 --> C
    end

    Phase1 --> Phase2

    subgraph Phase2["问题处理与标准化"]
        E --> F(标准化问题格式):::ProcessingFiltering
        F --> G(提取问题约束和示例):::ProcessingFiltering
        G --> H{问题唯一性检查？}
        H -- 重复 --> E
        H -- 唯一 --> I(按主题分类问题):::AnnotationLabeling
    end

    Phase2 --> Phase3

    subgraph Phase3["测试套件生成"]
        I --> J(自动化测试用例生成):::ProcessingFiltering
        J --> K{初始测试用例已生成？}
        K -- 是 --> L(人工策划测试用例):::AnnotationLabeling
        K -- 否 --> J
        L --> M(生成边缘案例和强测试用例):::AnnotationLabeling
        M --> N[/综合测试套件/]
    end

    Phase3 --> Phase4

    subgraph Phase4["质量控制与验证"]
        N --> O{测试用例正确性检查？}
        O -- 不正确 --> L
        O -- 正确 --> P(专家评审问题和测试套件):::QualityControl
        P --> Q{专家验证通过？}
        Q -- 否 --> E
        Q -- 是 --> R(生成参考解决方案):::ProcessingFiltering
        R --> S{参考解决方案已验证？}
        S -- 否 --> R
        S -- 是 --> T[/已验证的基准组件/]
    end

    Phase4 --> Phase5

    subgraph Phase5["基准组装与评估"]
        T --> U(定义评估指标):::EvaluationMetric
        U --> V(组装最终基准):::OutputResult
        V --> W(在 AetherCode 上评估 LLM):::EvaluationMetric
        W --> X[/LLM 性能结果/]
    end

    Phase5 --> End([结束])

name: Agent-SafetyBench
intro: Agent-SafetyBench是一个综合性安全评测基准，旨在评估大型语言模型代理的安全性。该基准包含349个交互环境和2000个测试用例，覆盖8类安全风险和10种常见的失败模式。研究发现，现有主流代理的安全性堪忧，凸显了该领域巨大的提升空间。
build_method: 人工标注, 专家构建
eval_feature: 对抗性, 过程导向, 领域特定
intro_en: Agent-SafetyBench is a comprehensive safety evaluation benchmark designed to assess the safety of large language model agents. It includes 349 interactive environments and 2000 test cases, covering 8 categories of safety risks and 10 common failure modes. Research shows that the safety of current mainstream agents is concerning, highlighting significant room for improvement in this field.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Initial Safety Concepts) --> B[Scenario Ideation]
    B --> C{{Manual Annotation}}
    C --> D[Draft Test Cases]
    D --> E{{Expert Construction}}
    E --> F{Review & Refine}
    F -->|Approved| G(349 Interactive Environments)
    F -->|Approved| H(2000 Test Cases)
    F -->|Rejected| B
    G & H --> I[Categorization & Linking]
    I --> J(Agent-SafetyBench Dataset
8 Risk Categories, 10 Failure Modes)
    J --> K[LLM Agent Evaluation]
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    style StartEnd fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style ProcessingFiltering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style AnnotationLabeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style EvaluationMetric fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([Start]) --> Phase1

    subgraph Phase1["Phase 1: Foundation and Scoping"]
        A[Define Risk Categories]:::DataCollection
        B[Established 8 Safety Risk Categories]:::DataCollection
        C[Define 10 Failure Modes]:::DataCollection
        A --> B
        B --> C
    end

    Phase1 --> Phase2

    subgraph Phase2["Phase 2: Environment and Test Case Generation"]
        D[Develop 349 Interactive Environments]:::ProcessingFiltering
        D1[68 Environments from Existing Benchmarks]:::DataCollection
        D2[42 New Environments with Public APIs]:::DataCollection
        D3[220 New Environments without Public APIs (Real-world)]:::DataCollection
        D4[19 New Environments without Public APIs (Future)]:::DataCollection
        D --> D1
        D --> D2
        D --> D3
        D --> D4
        E[Create 2,000 Test Cases]:::ProcessingFiltering
        E1[250 Test Cases per Risk Category]:::DataCollection
        E2[Generate Test Cases using LLMs (e.g., GPT-4)]:::ProcessingFiltering
        E3[Provide Risk Type and Example to LLM]:::DataCollection
        D --> E
        E --> E1
        E1 --> E2
        E2 --> E3
    end

    Phase2 --> Phase3

    subgraph Phase3["Phase 3: Quality Assurance"]
        F[Manual Review of Samples]:::QualityControl
        F1{At least 2 Rounds of Human Review?}:::QualityControl
        F --> F1
        F1 -- Yes --> G[Automated Validation Checks]:::QualityControl
        G --> H[Cross-Validation of Test Cases]:::QualityControl
        H1{98% Test Case Reasonability?}:::QualityControl
        H --> H1
        H1 -- Yes --> I[Cross-Validation of Safety Labels]:::QualityControl
        I1{97.5% Safety Label Agreement?}:::QualityControl
        I --> I1
        I1 -- Yes --> J[High Quality Data Ensured]:::QualityControl
        F1 -- No --> F
        H1 -- No --> E2
        I1 -- No --> I
    end

    Phase3 --> Phase4

    subgraph Phase4["Phase 4: Evaluation and Scorer Development"]
        K[Evaluate 16 Popular LLM Agents]:::EvaluationMetric
        L[Record Interaction History (Tool Calls, Responses)]:::DataCollection
        K --> L
        M[Fine-tune Scorer Model (Qwen2.5-7B-Chat)]:::ProcessingFiltering
        M1[Train with 4,000 Annotated Interaction Records]:::DataCollection
        M2[Labels: 2,186 Unsafe, 1,814 Safe]:::DataCollection
        M3[Training for 4 Epochs on 4 A100 GPUs]:::ProcessingFiltering
        L --> M
        M --> M1
        M1 --> M2
        M2 --> M3
        N[Automated Safety Assessment]:::EvaluationMetric
        N1[Assign 'Safe' or 'Unsafe' Label to Each Record]:::EvaluationMetric
        M3 --> N
    end

    Phase4 --> Phase5

    subgraph Phase5["Phase 5: Final Benchmark and Analysis"]
        O[Assemble Final Agent-SafetyBench]:::OutputResult
        P[Calculate Total Safety Score for Each Agent]:::EvaluationMetric
        N --> O
        O --> P
        Q[Analyze Failure Modes]:::EvaluationMetric
        R[Analyze Helpfulness (Fulfillable vs. Unfulfillable)]:::EvaluationMetric
        P --> Q
        P --> R
        S[Release Benchmark Publicly on GitHub]:::OutputResult
        Q --> S
        R --> S
    end

    S --> End([End])

    classDef StartEnd fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    classDef DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    classDef ProcessingFiltering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    classDef AnnotationLabeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    classDef QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    classDef EvaluationMetric fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    classDef OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([开始]) --> Phase1

    subgraph Phase1["阶段 1: 基础与范围界定"]
        A[定义风险类别]:::DataCollection

        A --> B
        B --> C
    end

    Phase1 --> Phase2

    subgraph Phase2["阶段 2: 环境与测试用例生成"]
        D[开发 349 个交互环境]:::ProcessingFiltering
        D1[来自现有基准的 68 个环境]:::DataCollection
        D2[42 个带公共 API 的新环境]:::DataCollection
        D3[220 个不带公共 API 的新环境 (真实世界)]:::DataCollection
        D4[19 个不带公共 API 的新环境 (未来)]:::DataCollection
        D --> D1
        D --> D2
        D --> D3
        D --> D4
        E[创建 2,000 个测试用例]:::ProcessingFiltering
        E1[每个风险类别 250 个测试用例]:::DataCollection
        E2[使用 LLM (例如 GPT-4) 生成测试用例]:::ProcessingFiltering
        E3[向 LLM 提供风险类型和示例]:::DataCollection
        D --> E
        E --> E1
        E1 --> E2
        E2 --> E3
    end

    Phase2 --> Phase3

    subgraph Phase3["阶段 3: 质量保证"]
        F[样本人工审查]:::QualityControl
        F1{至少 2 轮人工审查?}:::QualityControl
        F --> F1
        F1 -- 是 --> G[自动化验证检查]:::QualityControl
        G --> H[测试用例交叉验证]:::QualityControl
        H1{98% 测试用例合理性?}:::QualityControl
        H --> H1
        H1 -- 是 --> I[安全标签交叉验证]:::QualityControl
        I1{97.5% 安全标签一致性?}:::QualityControl
        I --> I1
        I1 -- 是 --> J[确保高质量数据]:::QualityControl
        F1 -- 否 --> F
        H1 -- 否 --> E2
        I1 -- 否 --> I
    end

    Phase3 --> Phase4

    subgraph Phase4["阶段 4: 评估与评分器开发"]
        K[评估 16 种流行 LLM 代理]:::EvaluationMetric
        L[记录交互历史 (工具调用, 响应)]:::DataCollection
        K --> L
        M[微调评分器模型 (Qwen2.5-7B-Chat)]:::ProcessingFiltering
        M1[使用 4,000 个带注释的交互记录进行训练]:::DataCollection
        M2[标签: 2,186 个不安全, 1,814 个安全]:::DataCollection
        M3[在 4 个 A100 GPU 上训练 4 个 epoch]:::ProcessingFiltering
        L --> M
        M --> M1
        M1 --> M2
        M2 --> M3
        N[自动化安全评估]:::EvaluationMetric
        N1[为每个记录分配“安全”或“不安全”标签]:::EvaluationMetric
        M3 --> N
    end

    Phase4 --> Phase5

    subgraph Phase5["阶段 5: 最终基准与分析"]
        O[组装最终 Agent-SafetyBench]:::OutputResult
        P[计算每个代理的总安全分数]:::EvaluationMetric
        N --> O
        O --> P
        Q[分析失败模式]:::EvaluationMetric
        R[分析有用性 (可完成 vs. 不可完成)]:::EvaluationMetric
        P --> Q
        P --> R
        S[在 GitHub 上公开发布基准]:::OutputResult
        Q --> S
        R --> S
    end

    S --> End([结束])

    classDef StartEnd fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    classDef DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    classDef ProcessingFiltering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    classDef AnnotationLabeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    classDef QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    classDef EvaluationMetric fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    classDef OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

name: AgentDAM
intro: AgentDAM评估AI网页导航代理是否遵守数据最小化隐私原则。通过模拟真实网页场景测试代理是否仅在必要时使用敏感信息，结果显示基于GPT-4、Llama-3和Claude的代理易过度使用敏感数据。
metric: Privacy Leakage Rate, Task Success Rate
modality: 文本, 图像, 多模态
intro_en: AgentDAM evaluates whether AI web navigation agents adhere to the data minimization privacy principle. By simulating real web scenarios, it tests if agents use sensitive information only when necessary. Results show that agents based on GPT-4, Llama-3, and Claude tend to overuse sensitive data.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Real Web Scenarios) --> B[Simulate Web Navigation]
    B --> C{{LLM Generation: Scenarios/Prompts}}
    C --> D[Agent Interaction & Data Capture]
    D --> E(Raw Interaction Data)
    E --> F[Identify Sensitive Data Usage]
    F --> G[Manual Annotation: Privacy Adherence]
    G --> H{Annotation Quality Check}
    H -- Pass --> I(Annotated AgentDAM Dataset)
    H -- Fail --> G
    I --> J[Evaluate Agent Data Minimization]
    J --> K[Generate Evaluation Report]
    K --> L(Evaluation Results)
metric_en: Privacy Leakage Rate, Task Success Rate
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    start([Start Benchmark Construction])
    style start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    subgraph Phase_1["Task Design"]
        TD1(Select Web Applications: Reddit, E-commerce, GitLab)
        TD2(Define 8 Task Types for Each Application)
    end

    subgraph Phase_2["Data Collection & Generation"]
        DC1[/Human Annotators: Create user_instruction/]
        DC2[/Human Annotators: Create Data Seed (PLOT, SENSITIVE_DATA)/]
        DC3{Is SENSITIVE_DATA irrelevant to task?}
        DC4(LLM (Llama-3.3-70B): Generate user_data from Data Seed)
        DC5(Sample Multiple Outputs per Data Seed (2x repetitions))
        DC6[/Final Dataset: 246 entries/]

        DC1 --> DC2
        DC2 --> DC3
        DC3 -- Yes --> DC4
        DC3 -- No --> DC2
        DC4 --> DC5
        DC5 --> DC6
    end

    subgraph Phase_3["Agentic Setup"]
        AS1(Define Benign Threat Model)
        AS2(Select Agent Environments: WEBARENA, VISUALWEBARENA, Claude Sonnet)
        AS3(Define Interaction Model: POMDP)
        AS4[/Observation (o_t): user_instruction, user_data, webpage representation/]
        AS5{Choose Webpage Representation?}
        AS6(axtree: Text Format for all LLMs)
        AS7(SOM: Image Format with Bounding Boxes & IDs for VLM/LLM)
        AS8(Claude Sonnet: Plain Screenshots)
        AS9(BLIP-2: Add Caption to Image as Signal)
        AS10(Agent Generates Action (a_t))
        AS11(Environment Executes Action & Updates State (s_t+1, o_t+1))

        AS1 --> AS2
        AS2 --> AS3
        AS3 --> AS4
        AS4 --> AS5
        AS5 -- Text Only --> AS6
        AS5 -- Multimodal --> AS7
        AS5 -- Claude --> AS8
        AS7 --> AS9
        AS6 --> AS10
        AS7 --> AS10
        AS8 --> AS10
        AS9 --> AS10
        AS10 --> AS11
    end

    subgraph Phase_4["Evaluation"]
        E1(Utility Evaluation: Reward Function R: S x A -> {0,1})
        E2(Performance Evaluator: Check if Desired State Achieved)
        E3(Privacy Evaluation: Examine Action Text for SENSITIVE_DATA)
        E4{Is SENSITIVE_DATA present?}
        E5(LLM-as-a-Judge (gpt-4o): CoT Reasoning for Leakage Detection)
        E6(Binary Score: 1=Leakage, 0=No Leakage)
        E7(Human Evaluation: Validate LLM Judge (98% Agreement))
        E8[/Privacy Leakage Rate & Privacy Performance Metric/]

        E1 --> E2
        E3 --> E4
        E4 -- Yes --> E5
        E4 -- No --> E6
        E5 --> E6
        E6 --> E7
        E7 --> E8
    end

    subgraph Phase_5["Mitigation Strategies"]
        M1(Pre-filtering: LLM for Data Minimization (Ineffective))
        M2(Post-filtering: Model Checks Final Output (Ineffective))
        M3(Privacy-aware System Prompt with CoT Demonstrations (Default Mitigation))
    end

    start --> Phase_1
    Phase_1 --> Phase_2
    Phase_2 --> Phase_3
    Phase_3 --> Phase_4
    Phase_4 --> Phase_5
    Phase_5 --> end([End Benchmark Construction])

name: AgentHarm
intro: AgentHarm是用于衡量LLM代理有害性的基准，包含110个跨11类危害（如诈骗、网络犯罪）的恶意任务。评测代理在面对越狱攻击时是否服从有害请求及执行多步骤恶意行为的鲁棒性。
org: EPFL, Gray Swan AI, UK AI Safety Institute, Carnegie Mellon University, University of Oxford, Center
eval_feature: 对抗性, 多轮, 结果导向
intro_en: AgentHarm is a benchmark for measuring the harmfulness of LLM agents, containing 110 malicious tasks across 11 harm categories (e.g., scams, cybercrime). It evaluates agents' compliance with harmful requests and robustness in executing multi-step malicious behaviors under jailbreak attacks.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A[Define Harm Categories] --> B(Harmful Scenario Concepts)
    B --> C{{Expert Brainstorming Sessions}}
    C --> D[Draft Malicious Tasks]
    D --> E[Specify Multi-step Behaviors]
    E --> F{Expert Review & Validation}
    F -- "Pass" --> G[Refine Task Details]
    F -- "Fail" --> D
    G --> H(AgentHarm Dataset: 110 Tasks)
    H --> I[Evaluate Compliance with Harmful Requests]
    H --> J[Evaluate Robustness to Jailbreak Attacks]
    I --> K(Compliance Scores)
    J --> L(Robustness Scores)
    K & L --> M[Benchmark Results Analysis]

name: AGIEval
intro: AGIEval是一个以人为中心的基准测试，旨在评估基础模型在人类水平任务上的通用能力。它通过使用标准化考试（如高考、法学院入学考试和数学竞赛）来评估模型，以反映模型在现实世界场景中的表现。该基准测试揭示了模型的优势和局限性，为提升其通用能力提供了有价值的见解。
org: Microsoft Research, Sun Yat-sen University, ByteDance Inc.
intro_en: AGIEval is a human-centered benchmark designed to evaluate the general capabilities of foundation models on human-level tasks. It assesses models using standardized exams such as the Gaokao, law school entrance exams, and math competitions to reflect their performance in real-world scenarios. This benchmark reveals the strengths and limitations of models, providing valuable insights for improving their general abilities.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Standardized Exams) --> B[Exam Acquisition]
    B --> C[Question Extraction & Digitization]
    C --> D[Question Adaptation for AI]
    D --> E[Formatting & Standardization]
    E --> F{Human Expert Review}
    F -->|Pass| G(AGIEval Benchmark Dataset)
    F -->|Fail| H[Refine/Discard]
    G --> I[Foundation Model Assessment]
    I --> J(Performance Metrics)

name: Aider Polyglot
intro: Aider Polyglot 基准测试评估大型语言模型在多语言代码编辑方面的能力。它包含225个来自Exercism平台的编程练习，涵盖C++、Go、Java、JavaScript、Python和Rust。该基准测试专注于最具挑战性的问题，旨在区分顶级代码生成模型的性能。
intro_en: The Aider Polyglot benchmark evaluates large language models' abilities in multilingual code editing. It includes 225 programming exercises from the Exercism platform, covering C++, Go, Java, JavaScript, Python, and Rust. This benchmark focuses on the most challenging problems to differentiate the performance of top code generation models.

flowchart LR
    A(Exercism Platform) --> B[Select Programming Exercises]
    B --> C{Filter by Difficulty}
    C -- "Most Challenging" --> D(Raw Programming Exercises)

    G --> H(Aider Polyglot Dataset\n225 Exercises: C++, Go, Java, JS, Python, Rust)
    H --> I[Evaluate LLM Code Editing Ability]
    I --> J[Benchmark Results]

name: AIME
intro: MathArena是一个创新的数学基准测试，它利用如AIME等定期举办的数学竞赛中的高质量、高难度问题，对大型语言模型进行实时评估。该基准通过在题目发布后立即进行评估，有效避免了数据污染问题。MathArena不仅评估模型的解题准确性，还是首个评估模型证明撰写能力的基准。
task_type: 数学问题, 开放式问答
intro_en: MathArena is an innovative math benchmark that uses high-quality, challenging problems from regularly held math competitions like AIME to evaluate large language models in real time. By assessing models immediately after problem release, it effectively prevents data contamination. MathArena not only measures solution accuracy but is also the first benchmark to evaluate models' proof-writing abilities.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Math Competitions: AIME) --> B[Problem Acquisition]
    B --> C{{Expert Selection Method}}
    C --> D[Problem Curation]
    D --> E{Problem Quality Check}
    E -->|Pass| F[Expert Verification]
    E -->|Fail| G[Problem Rejection]
    F --> H(High-Quality Problems)
    H --> I(MathArena Dataset)
    I --> J[Real-time LLM Evaluation]
    J --> K(LLM Performance Metrics)
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Data Sourcing["Data Sourcing"]
        A([Start]) --> B[/Recurring Math Competitions/]
        B --> C{Select Competition Type?}
        C -- AIME/CMIMC --> D[/High-Quality Problems/]
        C -- IMO --> E[/Proof-Writing Problems/]
    end

    subgraph Problem Processing["Problem Processing"]
        D --> F(Collect Challenging Problems)
        E --> G(Collect Proof-Writing Problems)
        F --> H{Problem Release?}
        G --> H
        H -- Yes --> I(Immediate Problem Integration)
        H -- No --> F
    end

    subgraph Model Evaluation["Model Evaluation"]
        I --> J(Evaluate LLMs in Real-Time)
        J --> K{Contamination Risk?}
        K -- Eliminated --> L(Assess Reasoning Capabilities)
        K -- Present (e.g., AIME 2024) --> M(Identify Data Contamination)
        L --> N(Evaluate Harder Competitions e.g., CMIMC 2025)
        L --> O(Evaluate Proof-Writing on IMO 2025)
        N --> P[/Evaluation Results (Reasoning)/]
        O --> Q[/Evaluation Results (Proof-Writing)/]
    end

    subgraph Benchmark Maintenance["Benchmark Maintenance"]
        P --> R(Aggregate Evaluation Data)
        Q --> R
        R --> S(Analyze Model Performance)
        S --> T{Progress Noted?}
        T -- Yes --> U(Track LLM Progress)
        T -- No --> V(Identify Areas for Improvement)
        U --> W(Integrate Newly Released Competitions)
        V --> W
        W --> X([End])
    end

name: AInsteinBench
intro: AInsteinBench是一个用于评估大型语言模型在科学计算开发中能力的基准测试。它基于真实的科研软件生态系统，任务来源于六个科学领域的代码拉取请求，涵盖量子化学和分子动力学等。该基准旨在考察模型完成复杂的、由测试驱动的端到端编码任务的能力，而不仅仅是简单的代码生成。
build_method: 混合, 网络爬取, 专家构建
eval_feature: 领域特定, 结果导向
intro_en: AInsteinBench is a benchmark for evaluating large language models' capabilities in scientific computing development. It is based on real scientific software ecosystems, with tasks sourced from code pull requests across six scientific fields, including quantum chemistry and molecular dynamics. The benchmark aims to assess models' ability to complete complex, test-driven end-to-end coding tasks, rather than simple code generation.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Real Scientific Software Ecosystems) --> B[Web Crawling]
    B --> C(Raw Code Pull Requests)
    C --> D[Extract Scientific Computing Tasks]
    D --> E{Categorize by 6 Scientific Fields}
    E --> F[Standardize Task Format]
    F --> G{{Expert Review & Curation}}
    G --> H{Task Validity Check}
    H -- Fail --> I[Discard/Refine]
    H -- Pass --> J(AInsteinBench Final Dataset)
    J --> K[Evaluate LLMs]
    K --> L(Scientific Computing Capabilities)
    L --> M[Performance Analysis]
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Data Sourcing
        A([Start]) --> B[/Scientific Repositories/]
        B --> C(Select Repositories)
        C --> D{Criteria Met?}
        D -- Yes --> E[/Selected Repositories/]
        D -- No --> C
        E --> F(Crawl Pull Requests via GitHub API)
        F --> G[/Pull Request Metadata/]
    end

    subgraph Data Collection & Filtering
        G --> H(Extract Metadata: Issue, Commits, Tests)
        H --> I{Is it a Scientific Bug?}
        I -- No --> J(Discard PR)
        I -- Yes --> K{Includes Test Updates?}
        K -- No --> J
        K -- Yes --> L{Merged to Main Branch?}
        L -- No --> J
        L -- Yes --> M[/Filtered Pull Requests/]
    end

    subgraph Environment Setup
        M --> N(Map PR to Historical Dependencies)
        N --> O(Restore Commit)
        O --> P(Configure Test File)
        P --> Q(Configure Fix File)
        Q --> R(Build Isolated Docker Image)
        R --> S[/Reproducible Environment/]
    end

    subgraph Task Generation
        S --> T{Question Type?}
        T -- Repo-Crawled --> U(FP Filtering: Verify Fail-to-Pass)
        T -- Repo-Crawled --> V(New Components Extraction)
        V --> V1(Analyze Git Diff for New APIs)
        V1 --> V2(Extract Raw API Signatures)
        V2 --> V3{Filter APIs by Test Utilization?}
        V3 -- Yes --> V4(Format into Minimal API Block)
        V4 --> V5(Append to Issue Description)
        T -- Synthesized --> W(Identify Computational Modules)
        W --> X(Ablate Feature: Mask Source Code)
        X --> Y(Construct Task Prompt)
        Y --> Z(Test Verification: Pair with Simulations)
        U --> AA[/Curated Tasks/]
        V5 --> AA
        Z --> AA
    end

    subgraph Human Verification
        AA --> BB(Recruit Domain Experts)
        BB --> CC(Expert Annotation Stage 1: Issue Verification)
        CC --> DD{Scientific Meaningful & Clear?}
        DD -- No --> EE(Discard Task)
        DD -- Yes --> FF(Determine Problem Difficulty)
        FF --> GG(Expert Annotation Stage 2: Test Verification)
        GG --> HH{Tests Adequate & Robust?}
        HH -- No --> II(Revise Tests)
        HH -- Yes --> JJ[/Verified Benchmark Tasks/]
        II --> GG
    end

    subgraph Evaluation
        JJ --> KK(Deploy CodeAct Agent)
        KK --> LL(Evaluate Models: GPT-4o, Gemini, etc.)
        LL --> MM(Measure Metrics: Solve Rate, Domain, Difficulty, Efficiency)
        MM --> NN[/Evaluation Results/]
        NN --> OO([End])

    end

    style A fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

flowchart TD
    subgraph 数据来源["数据来源"]
        A([开始]) --> B[/科学存储库/]

        C --> D{符合标准?}
        D -- 是 --> E[/选定的存储库/]
        D -- 否 --> C
        E --> F(通过 GitHub API 抓取拉取请求)

    subgraph 数据收集与过滤["数据收集与过滤"]
        G --> H(提取元数据: 问题, 提交, 测试)

        I -- 否 --> J(丢弃拉取请求)
        I -- 是 --> K{包含测试更新?}
        K -- 否 --> J
        K -- 是 --> L{合并到主分支?}
        L -- 否 --> J
        L -- 是 --> M[/过滤后的拉取请求/]
    end

    subgraph 环境设置["环境设置"]
        M --> N(将拉取请求映射到历史依赖项)
        N --> O(恢复提交)
        O --> P(配置测试文件)
        P --> Q(配置修复文件)
        Q --> R(构建隔离的 Docker 镜像)
        R --> S[/可复现环境/]
    end

    subgraph 任务生成["任务生成"]
        S --> T{问题类型?}
        T -- 爬取型 --> U(FP 过滤: 验证失败到通过)
        T -- 爬取型 --> V(新组件提取)
        V --> V1(分析 Git Diff 以获取新 API)
        V1 --> V2(提取原始 API 签名)
        V2 --> V3{按测试利用率过滤 API?}
        V3 -- 是 --> V4(格式化为最小 API 块)
        V4 --> V5(附加到问题描述)
        T -- 合成型 --> W(识别计算模块)
        W --> X(削弱功能: 屏蔽源代码)
        X --> Y(构建任务提示)
        Y --> Z(测试验证: 与模拟配对)
        U --> AA[/精选任务/]
        V5 --> AA
        Z --> AA
    end

    subgraph 人工验证["人工验证"]
        AA --> BB(招募领域专家)
        BB --> CC(专家标注阶段 1: 问题验证)
        CC --> DD{科学意义明确?}
        DD -- 否 --> EE(丢弃任务)
        DD -- 是 --> FF(确定问题难度)
        FF --> GG(专家标注阶段 2: 测试验证)
        GG --> HH{测试充分且健壮?}
        HH -- 否 --> II(修订测试)
        HH -- 是 --> JJ[/已验证基准任务/]
        II --> GG
    end

    subgraph 评估["评估"]
        JJ --> KK(部署 CodeAct 代理)
        KK --> LL(评估模型: GPT-4o, Gemini 等)
        LL --> MM(衡量指标: 解决率, 领域, 难度, 效率)
        MM --> NN[/评估结果/]
        NN --> OO([结束])

    end

name: AIRS-Bench
intro: AIRS-Bench 是一个为前沿人工智能科研 Agent 设计的基准测试套件，包含20个源自顶尖机器学习论文的任务。它旨在评估 Agent 在整个研究生命周期中的能力，涵盖语言建模、数学和生物信息学等多个领域，并促进自主科学研究的发展。该基准的设计灵活，方便集成新任务和进行跨框架的严格比较。
eval_feature: 过程导向, 结果导向, 领域特定
intro_en: AIRS-Bench is a benchmark suite designed for cutting-edge AI research Agents, comprising 20 tasks sourced from top machine learning papers. It aims to evaluate Agents' capabilities across the entire research lifecycle, covering areas such as language modeling, mathematics, and bioinformatics, and promotes autonomous scientific research. The benchmark is flexibly designed for easy integration of new tasks and rigorous cross-framework comparisons.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Top Machine Learning Papers) --> B[Task Identification]
    B --> C{Relevance & Scope Filter}
    C -- Pass --> D[Expert Task Design]
    C -- Fail --> E[Discard Irrelevant Tasks]
    D --> F[Task Specification & Formatting]
    F --> G(Initial Task Definitions)
    G --> H[Expert Review & Validation]
    H --> I{Quality & Completeness Check}
    I -- Approved --> J(AIRS-Bench Task Suite)
    I -- Revise --> D
    J --> K[Agent Capability Evaluation]
    K --> L[Performance Analysis]
    L --> M(Evaluation Results)
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    style Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style ProcessingFiltering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style AnnotationLabeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style EvaluationMetric fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([Start]) --> TaskConfig(Develop Task Configuration Standard) :::ProcessingFiltering

    subgraph DataSourcing["Data Sourcing & Initial Collection"]
        TaskConfig --> DataSources[/State-of-the-art ML Papers/] :::DataCollection
        DataSources --> InitialSourcing(Semi-manual Sourcing of Tasks) :::DataCollection
        InitialSourcing --> InitialTasks(Initial ~100 Tasks from ~85 Papers) :::DataCollection
    end

    subgraph Filtering["Filtering & Refinement"]
        InitialTasks --> CriteriaCheck{Check Selection Criteria?} :::ProcessingFiltering
        CriteriaCheck -- Yes --> SOTACriteria(SOTA Results 2020-2025) :::ProcessingFiltering
        SOTACriteria --> DatasetAvailability(Dataset Available (HuggingFace)) :::ProcessingFiltering
        DatasetAvailability --> TrainTestSplit(Train/Test Splits Exist) :::ProcessingFiltering
        TrainTestSplit --> ManualCreation(Manually Create Each Task) :::AnnotationLabeling
        CriteriaCheck -- No --> Discard(Discard Task) :::ProcessingFiltering
    end

    subgraph QualityControl["Quality Control & Verification"]
        ManualCreation --> ReviewSOTA(Review & Verify SOTA Results) :::QualityControl
        ReviewSOTA --> ConfirmNumbers(Confirm Reported Numbers) :::QualityControl
        ConfirmNumbers --> CheckMetrics(Check Eval Metrics & Splits) :::QualityControl
        CheckMetrics --> UpdateSOTA(Update SOTA if Better Score Found) :::QualityControl
        UpdateSOTA --> HumanChecks(Leverage Human Checks) :::QualityControl
        HumanChecks --> FinalSelection(Select 20 Final Tasks) :::ProcessingFiltering
    end

    subgraph Evaluation["Benchmark Evaluation & Output"]
        FinalSelection --> EvalProtocol(Introduce Evaluation Protocol) :::EvaluationMetric
        EvalProtocol --> AggregateResults(Aggregate Results Across Seeds & Tasks) :::EvaluationMetric
        AggregateResults --> Metrics(Define Metrics: Valid Submission Rate, Normalized Performance, Elo Ratings) :::EvaluationMetric
        Metrics --> Normalization(Normalize Scores: 0.0 Weakest, 1.0 Human SOTA) :::EvaluationMetric
        Normalization --> AgentEval(Agent Generates Code for ML Model) :::EvaluationMetric
        AgentEval --> ExecuteCode(Execute Agent-Generated Code) :::EvaluationMetric
        ExecuteCode --> MeasurePerformance(Measure Model Performance) :::EvaluationMetric
        MeasurePerformance --> End([End]) :::OutputResult
    end
flowchart_zh:
flowchart TD
    style Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style ProcessingFiltering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style AnnotationLabeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style EvaluationMetric fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([开始]) --> TaskConfig(开发任务配置标准) :::ProcessingFiltering

    subgraph DataSourcing["数据来源与初步收集"]
        TaskConfig --> DataSources[/最先进的机器学习论文/] :::DataCollection
        DataSources --> InitialSourcing(半手动任务来源) :::DataCollection
        InitialSourcing --> InitialTasks(初步的~100个任务来自~85篇论文) :::DataCollection
    end

    subgraph Filtering["筛选与细化"]
        InitialTasks --> CriteriaCheck{检查选择标准?} :::ProcessingFiltering
        CriteriaCheck -- 是 --> SOTACriteria(SOTA结果 2020-2025) :::ProcessingFiltering
        SOTACriteria --> DatasetAvailability(数据集可用(HuggingFace)) :::ProcessingFiltering
        DatasetAvailability --> TrainTestSplit(存在训练/测试集划分) :::ProcessingFiltering
        TrainTestSplit --> ManualCreation(手动创建每个任务) :::AnnotationLabeling
        CriteriaCheck -- 否 --> Discard(丢弃任务) :::ProcessingFiltering
    end

    subgraph QualityControl["质量控制与验证"]
        ManualCreation --> ReviewSOTA(审查与验证SOTA结果) :::QualityControl
        ReviewSOTA --> ConfirmNumbers(确认报告数字) :::QualityControl
        ConfirmNumbers --> CheckMetrics(检查评估指标与划分) :::QualityControl
        CheckMetrics --> UpdateSOTA(如果发现更好的分数则更新SOTA) :::QualityControl
        UpdateSOTA --> HumanChecks(利用人工检查) :::QualityControl
        HumanChecks --> FinalSelection(选择20个最终任务) :::ProcessingFiltering
    end

    subgraph Evaluation["基准评估与输出"]
        FinalSelection --> EvalProtocol(引入评估协议) :::EvaluationMetric
        EvalProtocol --> AggregateResults(聚合跨种子和任务的结果) :::EvaluationMetric
        AggregateResults --> Metrics(定义指标: 有效提交率, 归一化性能, Elo评分) :::EvaluationMetric
        Metrics --> Normalization(归一化分数: 0.0最弱, 1.0人类SOTA) :::EvaluationMetric
        Normalization --> AgentEval(代理生成ML模型的代码) :::EvaluationMetric
        AgentEval --> ExecuteCode(执行代理生成的代码) :::EvaluationMetric
        ExecuteCode --> MeasurePerformance(测量模型性能) :::EvaluationMetric
        MeasurePerformance --> End([结束]) :::OutputResult
    end

name: ALERT
intro: ALERT是一个与模型无关的推荐解释评估基准。它包含一个涵盖15个亚马逊电商类别、包含2761个用户交互的的多样化数据集。该基准还引入了两种新颖的基于大型语言模型（LLM）的自动评估器，能够实现可扩展且与人类偏好对齐的解释评估，旨在推动更有效的推荐解释系统的发展。
org: Worcester Polytechnic Institute, Amazon.com, Northwestern University, University of Notre Dame, Univ
intro_en: ALERT is a model-agnostic recommendation explanation evaluation benchmark. It includes a diverse dataset of 2,761 user interactions across 15 Amazon e-commerce categories. The benchmark also introduces two novel LLM-based automatic evaluators that enable scalable, human-aligned explanation assessment, aiming to advance more effective recommendation explanation systems.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Amazon E-commerce Data) --> B[Extract User Interactions]
    B --> C(Raw User Interactions)
    C --> D[Categorize & Structure Data]
    D --> E{{LLM Generation}}
    E --> F(LLM Generated Explanations)
    F --> G[Manual Review]
    G --> H{Quality Check}
    H -->|Pass| I(Curated Data & Explanations)
    H -->|Fail| J[Discard/Revise]
    I --> K(ALERT Benchmark Dataset\n2,761 Interactions, 15 Categories)
    K --> L{{LLM-based Automatic Evaluators}}
    L --> M[Model Evaluation]
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    start([Start])
    style start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    subgraph Phase1["Phase 1: Data Sourcing and Collection"]
        data_input[/Source Amazon User Purchase Data/]
        select_categories(Select 15 Diverse Amazon Categories)
        data_input -->|Raw Data| select_categories
    end
    style data_input fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style select_categories fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    subgraph Phase2["Phase 2: Data Filtering and Quality Control"]
        filter_title(Filter by Item Title: >= 6 Alphabetic Chars)
        filter_user_history(Filter by User History: 5-20 Transactions)
        filter_rating(Filter by Rating: 5-Star Purchases Only)
        check_category_size{Category Purchase Behaviors > 200?}
        sample_data(Randomly Sample Max 200/Category)
        filtered_dataset[/Filtered Dataset: 2,761 User-Item Interactions/]

        select_categories -->|Category Data| filter_title
        filter_title -->|Filtered Items| filter_user_history
        filter_user_history -->|Filtered Users| filter_rating
        filter_rating -->|5-Star Purchases| check_category_size
        check_category_size -->|Yes| sample_data
        check_category_size -->|No| filtered_dataset
        sample_data -->|Sampled Data| filtered_dataset
    end
    style filter_title fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style filter_user_history fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style filter_rating fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style check_category_size fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style sample_data fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style filtered_dataset fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    subgraph Phase3["Phase 3: Profile Construction"]
        construct_user_profile(Construct User Profiles from Purchase History)
        define_recommended_item(Define Recommended Item: Most Recent Purchase)
        alert_dataset[/Curated ALERT Benchmark Dataset/]

        filtered_dataset -->|User-Item Data| construct_user_profile
        construct_user_profile -->|User Profiles| define_recommended_item
        define_recommended_item -->|Item Context| alert_dataset
    end
    style construct_user_profile fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style define_recommended_item fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style alert_dataset fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    subgraph Phase4["Phase 4: Evaluator Criteria and Architecture Development"]
        develop_criteria(Develop Evaluation Criteria with E-commerce Team & User Studies)
        define_criteria(Define Criteria: Reasoning, Clear, Engaging, Neutral Tone)
        design_evaluators(Design LLM-as-a-Judge: ALERT-Gen & ALERT-Disc)
        implement_evaluators(Implement ALERT-Gen (Claude-3-Sonnet) & ALERT-Disc)

        alert_dataset -->|Benchmark Data| develop_criteria
        develop_criteria -->|Initial Criteria| define_criteria
        define_criteria -->|Final Criteria| design_evaluators
        design_evaluators -->|Evaluator Design| implement_evaluators
    end
    style develop_criteria fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style define_criteria fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style design_evaluators fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style implement_evaluators fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff

    subgraph Phase5["Phase 5: Evaluation Process Design"]
        design_eval_process(Design "Divide and Aggregate" Evaluation Process)
        divide_step(Divide: LLM Judge Prompts for Each Criterion)
        aggregate_step(Aggregate: Majority Voting for Final Judgment)
        define_metric(Define Metric: Win Rate)

        implement_evaluators -->|Evaluators| design_eval_process
        design_eval_process -->|Process Design| divide_step
        divide_step -->|Individual Judgments| aggregate_step
        aggregate_step -->|Aggregated Preferences| define_metric
    end
    style design_eval_process fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style divide_step fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style aggregate_step fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style define_metric fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff

    subgraph Phase6["Phase 6: Validation and Reliability Testing"]
        create_meta_eval_set(Create Meta-Evaluation Dataset with Negative Examples)
        conduct_human_eval(Conduct Human Evaluation: 3 Annotators, 88 Pairs)
        calculate_iaa(Calculate Inter-Annotator Agreement (IAA = 0.24))
        correlate_human_judgment(Correlate Automated Evaluators with Expert Human Judgments)

        define_metric -->|Evaluation Framework| create_meta_eval_set
        create_meta_eval_set -->|Meta-Eval Data| conduct_human_eval
        conduct_human_eval -->|Human Judgments| calculate_iaa
        calculate_iaa -->|IAA Results| correlate_human_judgment
    end
    style create_meta_eval_set fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style conduct_human_eval fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style calculate_iaa fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style correlate_human_judgment fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff

    subgraph Phase7["Phase 7: Final Benchmark Output"]
        final_output[/Final ALERT Benchmark: Dataset + LLM Evaluators/]
        end_node([End])

        correlate_human_judgment -->|Validated Framework| final_output
        final_output --> end_node
    end
    style final_output fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    style end_node fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    start --> data_input

name: AlignBench
intro: AlignBench是一个多维度中文大语言模型对齐评测基准。它采用人机协同的数据构建流程，包含683个源于真实场景的查询，并为知识密集型问题提供了可靠的网络证据。该基准利用结合思维链的规则校准多维LLM评审机制进行自动化评估，以确保结果的高可靠性和可解释性。
build_method: 人机协同, 真实用户查询
task_type: 开放式问答, 生成, 对话
intro_en: AlignBench is a multidimensional Chinese large language model alignment benchmark. It uses a human-AI collaborative data construction process, containing 683 queries derived from real-world scenarios, and provides reliable web evidence for knowledge-intensive questions. The benchmark employs an automated evaluation mechanism combining chain-of-thought reasoning with rule-based calibration for multidimensional LLM review, ensuring high reliability and interpretability of results.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Real-world Scenarios) --> B[Human-AI Collaborative Data Construction]
    B --> C[Derive 683 Queries]
    C --> D(Initial Query Set)
    D --> E[Integrate Web Evidence]
    E --> F{Knowledge-intensive Question Validation}
    F -->|Pass| G[Refine & Augment Queries]
    F -->|Fail| H[Discard/Re-evaluate]
    G --> I(Final AlignBench Dataset\n683 Queries)
    I --> J[LLM Alignment Evaluation]
    J --> K(Evaluation Results)
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    style StartEnd fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style ProcessingFiltering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style AnnotationLabeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style EvaluationMetric fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([Start]) --> DataSourcing[/Real-world Scenarios & Researcher Problems/]

    subgraph Data Curation Phase
        DataSourcing --> QueryCuration(Query Curation)
        QueryCuration --> QueryRules{Apply Query Rules?}
        QueryRules -- Yes --> QueryRefinement(Refine Queries)
        QueryRules -- No --> RejectQuery(Reject Query)
        QueryRefinement --> GPT4Generate(GPT-4 Generates Initial Answers)
        GPT4Generate --> HumanReview(Human Review & Revision)
        HumanReview --> IsKnowledgeIntensive{Knowledge-Intensive Query?}
        IsKnowledgeIntensive -- Yes --> WebSearch(Web Search for Evidence)
        WebSearch --> EvidenceCollection[/URLs & Quotations/]
        EvidenceCollection --> SynthesizeReference(Synthesize Verified Reference)
        IsKnowledgeIntensive -- No --> VerifiedReference(Human Verified Reference)
        SynthesizeReference --> VerifiedReference
        VerifiedReference --> FinalQueries[/683 Curated Queries/]
    end

    subgraph Taxonomy & Dataset Composition
        FinalQueries --> TaxonomyCategorization(Categorize into 8 Taxonomies)
        TaxonomyCategorization --> DatasetComposition[/AlignBench Dataset (8 Categories)/]
    end

    subgraph LLM Evaluation Phase
        DatasetComposition --> LLMResponse(LLM Generates Responses)
        LLMResponse --> GPT4Judge(GPT-4 as Judge)
        GPT4Judge --> CoTGenerate(Generate Explanations with CoT)
        CoTGenerate --> RuleCalibration(Apply Rule-Calibrated Judgment)
        RuleCalibration --> MultiDimJudge(Multi-dimensional Judgment)
        MultiDimJudge --> FinalRatings(Generate Final Ratings)
        FinalRatings --> EvaluationResults[/Evaluation Results/]
    end

    EvaluationResults --> End([End])

    Start([开始]) --> DataSourcing[/真实场景与研究者问题/]

    subgraph Data Curation Phase["数据整理阶段"]
        DataSourcing --> QueryCuration(查询整理)
        QueryCuration --> QueryRules{应用查询规则?}
        QueryRules -- 是 --> QueryRefinement(精炼查询)
        QueryRules -- 否 --> RejectQuery(拒绝查询)

        FinalRatings --> EvaluationResults[/评估结果/]
    end

    class EvaluationResults OutputResult

name: All-Angles
intro: All-Angles Bench是一个旨在评估多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）多视角理解能力的基准测试。它包含2100多个经过人工精心标注的多视角问答对，涵盖90个不同的真实世界场景。该基准测试通过六项任务（计数、属性识别、相对距离、相对方向、物体操作和相机姿态估计）来评估模型在多视角几何一致性和跨视角对应方面的能力。实验结果表明，当前MLLMs在处理部分遮挡视图的跨视角对应和建立粗略相机姿态方面表现不佳，与人类水平存在显著差距。
org: University of California, Berkeley, Meta, New York University, Kennesaw State University, National T
task_type: 选择题, 开放式问答, 图像问答
eval_feature: 多视角, 几何一致性, 跨视角对应
task_type_en: Multiple Choice, Open-ended QA, Visual QA
intro_en: All-Angles Bench is a benchmark designed to evaluate the multi-view understanding capabilities of multimodal large language models (MLLMs). It contains over 2,100 carefully annotated multi-view question-answer pairs across 90 different real-world scenarios. The benchmark assesses models through six tasks—counting, attribute recognition, relative distance, relative direction, object manipulation, and camera pose estimation—to evaluate multi-view geometric consistency and cross-view correspondence. Experimental results show that current MLLMs perform poorly in cross-view correspondence with partially occluded views and in estimating coarse camera poses, with a significant gap compared to human-level performance.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Real-world Scenarios) --> B[Identify 90 Scenarios]
    B --> C(Selected Scenarios Data)
    C --> D[Gather Multi-view Data]
    D --> E(Raw Multi-view Data)
    E --> F{{Manual Annotation}}
    F --> G[Annotate Q&A Pairs]
    G --> H(Annotated Draft Data)
    H --> I[Perform Quality Control]
    I --> J{Annotation Quality Check}
    J -->|Pass| K(Final Dataset: 2100+ Q&A Pairs)
    J -->|Fail| L[Re-annotation/Refinement]
    L --> G
    K --> M[Evaluate MLLMs]
    M --> N(Six Assessment Types)
    N --> O(Benchmark Results)

    Start([Start]) --> S1[Identify Research Gap: MLLM Multi-View Understanding]:::Start

    subgraph Phase1["Data Sourcing & Collection"]
        S1 --> P1_1[/90 Diverse Real-World Scenarios/]
        P1_1 --> P1_2(Collect Multi-View Visual Data):::DataCollection
    end

    subgraph Phase2["Data Processing & Annotation"]
        P1_2 --> P2_1(Generate >2,100 Question-Answer Pairs):::ProcessingFiltering
        P2_1 --> P2_2(Human Annotation of Q&A Pairs):::AnnotationLabeling
        P2_2 --> P2_3{Categorize Q&A into 6 Tasks?}
        P2_3 -->|Yes| P2_4(Counting, Attribute ID, Relative Distance, Relative Direction, Object Manipulation, Camera Pose Estimation):::AnnotationLabeling
        P2_4 --> P2_5(Focus on Geometric Correspondence & Cross-View Alignment):::ProcessingFiltering
    end

    subgraph Phase3["Quality Control & Validation"]
        P2_5 --> P3_1(Review Human Annotations):::QualityControl
        P3_1 --> P3_2(Check Consistency Across Views):::QualityControl
        P3_2 --> P3_3(Identify Partially Occluded Views):::QualityControl
        P3_3 --> P3_4(Verify Coarse Camera Poses):::QualityControl
        P3_4 --> P3_5{Annotation Quality Sufficient?}
        P3_5 -->|No| P2_2
        P3_5 -->|Yes| P3_6[/Validated Annotated Q&A Pairs/]
    end

    subgraph Phase4["Benchmark Construction & Evaluation"]
        P3_6 --> P4_1(Compile All-Angles Bench):::ProcessingFiltering
        P4_1 --> P4_2[/All-Angles Bench Dataset/]
        P4_2 --> P4_3(Select 27 Representative MLLMs):::DataCollection
        P4_3 --> P4_4(Evaluate MLLMs Against Human Evaluators):::EvaluationMetric
        P4_4 --> P4_5(Analyze Performance Gap):::EvaluationMetric
        P4_5 --> P4_6(Identify Underperforming Aspects):::EvaluationMetric
        P4_6 --> P4_7(Cross-View Correspondence for Occluded Views):::EvaluationMetric
        P4_6 --> P4_8(Establishing Coarse Camera Poses):::EvaluationMetric
    end

    subgraph Phase5["Insights & Recommendations"]
        P4_7 & P4_8 --> P5_1(Propose Domain-Specific Refinements/Modules):::OutputResult
        P5_1 --> P5_2([All-Angles Bench: Valuable Insights]):::OutputResult
    end

    P5_2 --> End([End]):::End

    end

    subgraph Phase2["数据处理与标注"]

        P2_1 --> P2_2(人工标注问答对):::AnnotationLabeling
        P2_2 --> P2_3{将问答对分类为6个任务？}

        P2_4 --> P2_5(关注几何对应与跨视角对齐):::ProcessingFiltering

name: AlpacaEval 2.0
intro: AlpacaEval 2.0是一个自动化评估框架，利用基于GPT-4的成对比较生成模型胜率。引入长度控制胜率，减少模型偏好长回答的偏差，提高与人类偏好的相关性和评测鲁棒性。
task_type: 排序, 对话, 开放式问答
intro_en: AlpacaEval 2.0 is an automated evaluation framework that uses GPT-4-based pairwise comparisons to generate model win rates. It introduces length-controlled win rates to reduce bias toward longer responses, enhancing correlation with human preferences and evaluation robustness.
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Data Sourcing["Data Sourcing"]
        A([Start]) --> B[/805 Instructions/]
        B --> C[/Baseline Model (b) & Evaluated Model (m) Responses/]
    end

    subgraph Data Collection & Initial Evaluation["Data Collection & Initial Evaluation"]
        C --> D(Generate Responses for each Instruction)
        D --> E{GPT-4 Turbo Evaluator Pairwise Comparison}
        E --> F(Output Probability of Preferring Evaluated Model)
        F --> G(Compute Original AlpacaEval Win Rate)
    end

    subgraph Bias Identification & Problem Formulation["Bias Identification & Problem Formulation"]
        G --> H{Is there a Length Bias?}
        H -- Yes --> I(AlpacaEval Known to Favor Longer Outputs)
        I --> J(Formulate Counterfactual Question: "What if lengths were equal?")
    end

    subgraph Debiasing Methodology["Debiasing Methodology"]
        J --> K[/AlpacaEval Leaderboard Ratings {x, zm, zb, m, b, y}/]
        K --> L(Fit Generalized Linear Model (GLM))
        L --> M{GLM Predicts Biased Preferences}
        M -- Based on --> N[/Mediators (Length Difference) & Other Features/]
        N --> O(GLM Components: Model Identity, Output Length, Instruction Difficulty)
        O --> P(Logistic Regression Model: q(y=1|zm,zb,m,b,x))
        P --> Q(Ensure Identity & Symmetry Properties)
    end

    subgraph Length-Controlled Prediction["Length-Controlled Prediction"]
        Q --> R(Condition GLM with Zero Length Difference)
        R --> S(Remove Length Term from Regression)
        S --> T(Predict Length-Controlled Preferences)
        T --> U(Compute Length-Controlled AlpacaEval Win Rate (winrateLC))
    end

    subgraph Validation & Output["Validation & Output"]
        U --> V(Improve Robustness to Verbosity Manipulation)
        V --> W(Increase Spearman Correlation with LMSYS Chatbot Arena)
        W --> X([End])
    end

    style A fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style X fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style B fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style C fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style D fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff

    style N fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style O fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff

    style Q fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff

    style S fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff

    style U fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style V fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style W fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
flowchart_zh:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Data Sourcing["数据来源"]
        A([开始]) --> B[/805 条指令/]
        B --> C[/基线模型 (b) 和评估模型 (m) 响应/]
    end

    subgraph Data Collection & Initial Evaluation["数据收集与初步评估"]
        C --> D(为每条指令生成响应)
        D --> E{GPT-4 Turbo 评估器成对比较}
        E --> F(输出偏好评估模型的概率)
        F --> G(计算原始 AlpacaEval 胜率)
    end

    subgraph Bias Identification & Problem Formulation["偏差识别与问题提出"]
        G --> H{是否存在长度偏差？}
        H -- 是 --> I(AlpacaEval 已知偏爱更长的输出)
        I --> J(提出反事实问题：“如果长度相等会怎样？”)
    end

    subgraph Debiasing Methodology["去偏差方法"]
        J --> K[/AlpacaEval 排行榜评分 {x, zm, zb, m, b, y}/]
        K --> L(拟合广义线性模型 (GLM))
        L --> M{GLM 预测有偏差的偏好}
        M -- 基于 --> N[/中介变量 (长度差异) 和其他特征/]
        N --> O(GLM 组件：模型身份、输出长度、指令难度)
        O --> P(逻辑回归模型：q(y=1|zm,zb,m,b,x))
        P --> Q(确保同一性和对称性)
    end

    subgraph Length-Controlled Prediction["长度控制预测"]
        Q --> R(将 GLM 条件设置为零长度差异)
        R --> S(从回归中移除长度项)
        S --> T(预测长度控制的偏好)
        T --> U(计算长度控制的 AlpacaEval 胜率 (winrateLC))
    end

    subgraph Validation & Output["验证与输出"]
        U --> V(提高对冗长操作的鲁棒性)
        V --> W(增加与 LMSYS Chatbot Arena 的 Spearman 相关性)
        W --> X([结束])
    end

name: AMEGA-LLM
intro: AMEGA-LLM是评估大型语言模型对医疗指南依从性的基准，通过模拟临床交互和开放式问答，涵盖24个诊断场景和13个医学专业，提供比传统基准更全面真实的评估。
org: DATEXIS, Beuth University of Applied Sciences Berlin
build_method: 人工标注，医疗指南专家构建
eval_feature: 评估LLM对医疗指南的依从性，模拟临床交互，开放式问答格式
scale: 24个诊断场景，163个问题，1497个评估标准
build_method_en: Manual Annotation, Medical Guideline Experts Construction
eval_feature_en: Evaluate LLM compliance with medical guidelines, Simulate clinical interaction, Open-ended QA format
intro_en: AMEGA-LLM is a benchmark for evaluating large language models' adherence to medical guidelines. It offers a more comprehensive and realistic assessment than traditional benchmarks by simulating clinical interactions and open-ended Q&A across 24 diagnostic scenarios and 13 medical specialties.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Raw Medical Text Sources) --> B[Data Collection]
    B --> C[Medical Text Preprocessing]
    C --> D[Medical Entity Recognition]
    D --> E[Entity Normalization & Linking]
    E --> F{{Automatic Entity Generation Module}}
    F --> G{Quality Check & Filtering}
    G -- Pass --> H[Expert Annotation & Review]
    G -- Fail --> I[Discarded Data]
    H --> J(Final AMEGA-LLM Dataset)
    J --> K[Model Evaluation: Entity Extraction]
    J --> L[Model Evaluation: Entity Generation]
    K --> M(Evaluation Metrics)
    L --> M
scale_en: 24 items diagnostic scenarios, 163 questions, 1497 items evaluation criteria
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    style Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style A_InitialCase fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style B_Questions fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style C_Sections fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style D_Criteria fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style E_CandidateModel fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style F_CandidateOutput fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    style G_EvaluatorModel fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style H_EvaluateCriteria fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style I_CriteriaMetDecision fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style J_ApplyCriterionScores fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style K_MultiplyScores fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style L_SumScores fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style M_ReaskProcess fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style N_RefineAnswer fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style O_AggregatedScores fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    style P_FinalBenchmark fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([Start]) --> A_InitialCase(Initial Case)
    A_InitialCase --> B_Questions(Questions)
    B_Questions --> C_Sections(Sections)
    C_Sections --> D_Criteria(Criteria)

    subgraph Benchmark Content Creation
        A_InitialCase
        B_Questions
        C_Sections
        D_Criteria
    end

    D_Criteria --> E_CandidateModel((Candidate Model))
    E_CandidateModel --> F_CandidateOutput[/Candidate Model Output/]

    subgraph Candidate Model Interaction
        E_CandidateModel
        F_CandidateOutput
    end

    F_CandidateOutput --> G_EvaluatorModel((Evaluator Model))
    G_EvaluatorModel --> H_EvaluateCriteria(Evaluate against Predefined Criteria)
    H_EvaluateCriteria --> I_CriteriaMetDecision{Criteria Met?}

    I_CriteriaMetDecision -->|No, Reask Permitted| M_ReaskProcess{Reask Process?}
    M_ReaskProcess -->|Yes| N_RefineAnswer(Refine Answer)
    N_RefineAnswer --> E_CandidateModel

    I_CriteriaMetDecision -->|Yes or No, Reask Not Permitted| J_ApplyCriterionScores(Apply Criterion Scores)
    J_ApplyCriterionScores --> K_MultiplyScores(Multiply Criterion Scores)
    K_MultiplyScores --> L_SumScores(Sum Scores at Different Levels)
    L_SumScores --> O_AggregatedScores[/Aggregated Scores/]

    subgraph Evaluation Process
        G_EvaluatorModel
        H_EvaluateCriteria
        I_CriteriaMetDecision
        J_ApplyCriterionScores
        K_MultiplyScores
        L_SumScores
        M_ReaskProcess
        N_RefineAnswer
        O_AggregatedScores
    end

    O_AggregatedScores --> P_FinalBenchmark([Final Benchmark])
    P_FinalBenchmark --> End([End])

flowchart TD
    style Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    style L_SumScores fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style M_ReaskProcess fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style N_RefineAnswer fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style O_AggregatedScores fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    style P_FinalBenchmark fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([开始]) --> A_InitialCase(初始案例)
    A_InitialCase --> B_Questions(问题)
    B_Questions --> C_Sections(部分)
    C_Sections --> D_Criteria(标准)

    subgraph 基准内容创建
        A_InitialCase
        B_Questions
        C_Sections
        D_Criteria
    end

    D_Criteria --> E_CandidateModel((候选模型))
    E_CandidateModel --> F_CandidateOutput[/候选模型输出/]

    subgraph 候选模型交互
        E_CandidateModel
        F_CandidateOutput
    end

    F_CandidateOutput --> G_EvaluatorModel((评估模型))
    G_EvaluatorModel --> H_EvaluateCriteria(根据预定义标准进行评估)
    H_EvaluateCriteria --> I_CriteriaMetDecision{符合标准?}

    I_CriteriaMetDecision -->|否，允许重新提问| M_ReaskProcess{重新提问流程?}
    M_ReaskProcess -->|是| N_RefineAnswer(优化答案)
    N_RefineAnswer --> E_CandidateModel

    I_CriteriaMetDecision -->|是或否，不允许重新提问| J_ApplyCriterionScores(应用标准分数)
    J_ApplyCriterionScores --> K_MultiplyScores(乘以标准分数)
    K_MultiplyScores --> L_SumScores(汇总不同级别的分数)
    L_SumScores --> O_AggregatedScores[/汇总分数/]

    subgraph 评估流程
        G_EvaluatorModel
        H_EvaluateCriteria
        I_CriteriaMetDecision
        J_ApplyCriterionScores
        K_MultiplyScores
        L_SumScores
        M_ReaskProcess
        N_RefineAnswer
        O_AggregatedScores
    end

    O_AggregatedScores --> P_FinalBenchmark([最终基准])
    P_FinalBenchmark --> End([结束])

name: AndroidWorld
intro: AndroidWorld是一个功能齐全的Android环境，为20个真实Android应用中的116个程序化任务提供奖励信号。与现有交互式环境不同，AndroidWorld动态构建参数化任务，并以自然语言表达，从而实现更大、更真实的任务测试。它通过专用初始化、成功检查和拆卸逻辑确保可复现性，并支持对设备系统状态的修改和检查。
build_method: 自动生成, 专家构建
eval_feature: 动态更新, 防污染, 过程评估
intro_en: AndroidWorld is a fully functional Android environment that provides reward signals for 116 programmatic tasks across 20 real Android apps. Unlike existing interactive environments, AndroidWorld dynamically constructs parameterized tasks expressed in natural language, enabling larger and more realistic task evaluations. It ensures reproducibility through dedicated initialization, success checks, and teardown logic, and supports modification and inspection of device system states.

name: ARC (AI2 Reasoning Challenge)
intro: ARC（AI2推理挑战赛）是一个由艾伦人工智能研究所（AI2）发布的科学问答数据集。它包含7787个从小学到高中的非图表类科学问题，旨在评估AI系统在需要深度推理的复杂问题上的表现。该基准分为“简单”和“挑战”两个部分，其中“挑战”部分的问题是现有算法难以正确解答的。
intro_en: ARC (AI2 Reasoning Challenge) is a science question-answering dataset released by the Allen Institute for AI (AI2). It contains 7,787 non-diagram science questions ranging from elementary to high school level, designed to evaluate AI systems' performance on complex problems requiring deep reasoning. The benchmark is divided into "Easy" and "Challenge" sections, with the "Challenge" section containing questions that existing algorithms struggle to answer correctly.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Existing Educational Materials) --> B[Expert Selection & Curation]
    B --> C{Filter: Non-Diagram Questions}
    C -->|Pass| D[Question Formatting & Structuring]
    C -->|Fail| J[Discard Irrelevant Questions]
    D --> E{{Expert Review for Reasoning Complexity}}
    E --> F(ARC Dataset: 7,787 Science Questions)
    F --> G[AI Model Inference]
    G --> H[Performance Evaluation Metrics]
    H --> I(AI System Performance Results)

    subgraph Phase["Phase 3: Dataset Partitioning"]
        K[/Challenge Set: 2,590 questions/]

name: ARC-AGI
intro: ARC-AGI基准测试系列旨在衡量在新型任务上的少样本泛化能力，这是智能的核心方面。ARC Prize 2025全球竞赛使用了ARC-AGI-2数据集，其任务复杂性更高。该基准测试已成为AI推理的行业标准，但目前的AI推理性能仍受限于知识覆盖范围。ARC-AGI-3引入了需要探索、规划、记忆、目标获取和对齐能力的交互式推理挑战。
build_method: 专家构建, 自动生成
task_type: 抽象推理, 交互式任务
intro_en: The ARC-AGI benchmark series aims to measure few-shot generalization ability on novel tasks, a core aspect of intelligence. The ARC Prize 2025 global competition uses the ARC-AGI-2 dataset, which features higher task complexity. This benchmark has become the industry standard for AI reasoning, but current AI reasoning performance remains limited by knowledge coverage. ARC-AGI-3 introduces interactive reasoning challenges requiring exploration, planning, memory, goal acquisition, and alignment capabilities.

    J --> K[AI Model Reasoning Test]
    K --> L(Benchmark Results)
