    V --> W
    W --> End

name: Arena-Hard-Auto
intro: Arena-Hard-Auto 是一个自动化构建的基准测试，它利用 BenchBuilder 流水线从大规模众包数据（如 Chatbot Arena）中筛选出500个具有挑战性的开放式提示。该基准旨在通过LLM作为裁判进行自动化评估，以衡量和区分前沿语言模型的性能，并确保与人类偏好高度相关。
build_method: 混合, 众包, LLM生成
intro_en: Arena-Hard-Auto is an automatically constructed benchmark that uses the BenchBuilder pipeline to select 500 challenging open-ended prompts from large-scale crowdsourced data such as Chatbot Arena. This benchmark aims to measure and differentiate the performance of state-of-the-art language models through automated evaluation with LLMs as judges, ensuring high correlation with human preferences.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Large-scale Crowdsourced Data) --> B{{BenchBuilder Pipeline}}
    B --> C[Select Challenging Prompts]
    C --> D{Prompt Quality Filter}
    D -->|Pass| E[LLM Generation/Refinement]
    D -->|Fail| F[Discard Low-Quality Prompts]
    E --> G(500 Open-ended Prompts)
    G --> H(Arena-Hard-Auto Benchmark)
    H --> I[Measure LLM Performance]
    I --> J[Differentiate SOTA Models]
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Data Sourcing & Initial Filtering
        start_node([Start])
        start_node --> A[/Initial Prompt Pool (200,000 prompts)/]
        A --> B(Source from Chatbot Arena & WildChat-1M)
        B --> C(Filter: Remove Duplicates, Multi-turn, Non-English)
        C --> D[/Filtered Prompt Pool/]
        style start_node fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
        style B fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
        style C fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    end

    subgraph Quality Assessment & Clustering
        D --> E(Encode Prompts using OpenAI text-embedding-3-small)
        E --> F(Reduce Dimensions with UMAP)
        F --> G(Apply HDBSCAN for Hierarchical Clustering)
        G --> H(Generate Topic Clusters)
        H --> I(Summarize & Name Topics using LLM)
        I --> J(Assign Quality Score to each Prompt using GPT-4-Turbo Judge)
        J --> K{Quality Score >= 6 AND Mean Topic Score >= 5?}
        K -- Yes --> L[/High-Quality Prompts & Clusters/]
        K -- No --> M(Discard Low-Quality Prompts/Clusters)
        style E fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
        style F fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
        style G fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
        style H fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
        style I fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
        style J fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
        style K fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
        style M fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    end

    subgraph Benchmark Construction
        L --> N(Sample 2 Prompts from each of 250 High-Quality Clusters)
        N --> O(Ensure No PII or Offensive Content)
        O --> P[/Arena-Hard-Auto Benchmark (500 prompts)/]
        style N fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
        style O fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    end

    subgraph Benchmark Validation & Evaluation
        P --> Q(Validate GPT-4-Turbo Quality Scores)
        Q --> R(Construct Ground Truth Labels for 200 Sampled Queries)
        R --> S(Collect Majority Votes from GPT-4o, Claude-3-Opus, Gemini-1.5-Pro)
        S --> T{GPT-4-Turbo Agreement >= 85.6%?}
        T -- Yes --> U(Proceed to LLM-as-a-Judge Evaluation)
        T -- No --> V(Review & Refine Quality Scoring)
        U --> W(Evaluate Models using LLM-as-a-Judge Framework)
        W --> X(Pairwise Comparison against GPT-4-0314 Baseline)
        X --> Y(Score Outputs on 5-point Likert Scale by GPT-4-Turbo/Gemini-1.5-Pro)
        Y --> Z(Apply Chain-of-Thought Prompting)
        Z --> AA(Use Two-Game Setup for Position Bias Mitigation)
        AA --> BB(Aggregate Judgments using Bradley & Terry Model)
        BB --> CC(Bootstrap Confidence Intervals for Win-Rates)
        CC --> DD(Calculate Separability with Confidence, Agreement with Confidence, Pair Rank Brier Score)
        DD --> end_node([End])
        style Q fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
        style R fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
        style S fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
        style T fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
        style V fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
        style U fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
        style W fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
        style X fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
        style Y fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
        style Z fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
        style AA fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
        style BB fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
        style CC fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
        style DD fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
        style end_node fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    end
flowchart_zh:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Data Sourcing & Initial Filtering["数据来源与初步筛选"]
        start_node([开始])
        start_node --> A[/初始提示池 (200,000 条提示)/]
        A --> B(从 Chatbot Arena 和 WildChat-1M 获取数据)
        B --> C(筛选: 移除重复、多轮对话、非英文内容)

        style C fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    end

    subgraph Quality Assessment & Clustering["质量评估与聚类"]
        D --> E(使用 OpenAI text-embedding-3-small 对提示进行编码)
        E --> F(使用 UMAP 降低维度)
        F --> G(应用 HDBSCAN 进行分层聚类)
        G --> H(生成主题簇)
        H --> I(使用 LLM 总结和命名主题)
        I --> J(使用 GPT-4-Turbo 评判器为每条提示分配质量分数)
        J --> K{质量分数 >= 6 且平均主题分数 >= 5?}
        K -- 是 --> L[/高质量提示与簇/]
        K -- 否 --> M(丢弃低质量提示/簇)
        style E fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
        style F fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
        style G fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
        style H fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
        style I fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
        style J fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
        style K fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
        style M fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    end

    subgraph Benchmark Construction["基准构建"]
        L --> N(从 250 个高质量簇中每个采样 2 条提示)
        N --> O(确保不含个人身份信息或冒犯性内容)
        O --> P[/Arena-Hard-Auto 基准 (500 条提示)/]
        style N fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
        style O fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    end

    subgraph Benchmark Validation & Evaluation["基准验证与评估"]
        P --> Q(验证 GPT-4-Turbo 质量分数)
        Q --> R(为 200 条采样查询构建真实标签)
        R --> S(收集 GPT-4o, Claude-3-Opus, Gemini-1.5-Pro 的多数投票)
        S --> T{GPT-4-Turbo 一致性 >= 85.6%?}
        T -- 是 --> U(进行 LLM-as-a-Judge 评估)
        T -- 否 --> V(审查和完善质量评分)
        U --> W(使用 LLM-as-a-Judge 框架评估模型)
        W --> X(与 GPT-4-0314 基线进行成对比较)

        Y --> Z(应用思维链提示)
        Z --> AA(采用双局设置以减轻位置偏差)
        AA --> BB(使用 Bradley & Terry 模型聚合判断)
        BB --> CC(对胜率进行自举置信区间计算)
        CC --> DD(计算置信度分离性、置信度一致性和成对排名布里尔分数)
        DD --> end_node([结束])
        style Q fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
        style R fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
        style S fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff

name: ART
intro: ART (Automated Red Teaming) 是一个用于自动化红队测试的基准，旨在评估AI系统在面对对抗性攻击时的鲁棒性和安全性。
intro_en: ART (Automated Red Teaming) is a benchmark for automated red teaming tests, designed to evaluate the robustness and security of AI systems against adversarial attacks.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Real-world Attack Scenarios) --> B[Automated Attack Simulation]
    C(Existing Adversarial Datasets) --> D[Data Aggregation]
    B --> D
    D --> E[Data Structuring & Formatting]
    E --> F{Adversarial Content Filter}
    F -- "Pass" --> G[Expert Review & Validation]
    F -- "Fail" --> H[Discard Invalid Data]
    G --> I{{Red Teaming Scenario Generation}}
    I --> J(ART Red Teaming Dataset)
    J --> K[AI System Robustness Evaluation]
    K --> L[Security Metric Reporting]
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    style Start_End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    style Annotation_Labeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style Quality_Control fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style Evaluation_Metric fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style Output_Result fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    subgraph Phase_1["Data Sourcing & Collection"]

        B[/Initial Data Sources/](Data_Collection)

        D(Collect Raw Data):::Data_Collection

        F(Acquire Data):::Data_Collection
        G(Log Data Provenance):::Data_Collection
        A --> B

        C --> D
        D --> E
        E -- Yes --> F
        E -- No --> C
        F --> G
    end

    subgraph Phase_2["Data Preprocessing & Filtering"]
        H(Load Raw Data):::Processing_Filtering
        I(Clean Data):::Processing_Filtering
        J(Normalize Data):::Processing_Filtering
        K{Filtering Criteria Met?}:::Quality_Control
        L(Filter Irrelevant Data):::Processing_Filtering
        M(Split Data into Train/Test/Validation Sets):::Processing_Filtering
        G --> H
        H --> I
        I --> J
        J --> K
        K -- Yes --> M
        K -- No --> L

    end

    subgraph Phase_3["Annotation & Labeling"]

        R --> S
        S --> T
        T --> U
        U -- Yes --> W
        U -- No --> V
        V --> S
    end

    subgraph Phase_5["Evaluation & Output"]

        Y(Run Baseline Models):::Evaluation_Metric
        Z(Compute Performance Scores):::Evaluation_Metric
        AA(Generate Benchmark Report):::Output_Result
        BB([End]):::Start_End
        W --> X
        X --> Y
        Y --> Z

name: ART Benchmark
intro: 该基准测试（Agent Red Teaming, ART）旨在评估大型语言模型驱动的AI代理在面对攻击时的安全性。通过一项大规模的红队竞赛，研究人员收集了180万次提示注入攻击，其中超过6万次成功导致了策略违规。ART基准测试包含了这些高影响力的攻击，并对19个先进模型进行了评估，揭示了当前AI代理在安全方面的关键漏洞。
org: Carnegie Mellon University, University of Oxford, Center for AI Safety, Gray Swan AI, UK AI Security
build_method: 人工标注, 自动生成, 混合
intro_en: The ART Benchmark (Agent Red Teaming) aims to evaluate the security of large language model-driven AI agents against attacks. Through a large-scale red teaming competition, researchers collected 1.8 million prompt injection attacks, over 60,000 of which successfully caused policy violations. The ART Benchmark includes these high-impact attacks and evaluates 19 advanced models, revealing critical security vulnerabilities in current AI agents.

name: ArtifactsBench
intro: ArtifactsBench 针对大型语言模型生成的动态交互式视觉作品提出评测基准，弥补传统基准只关注算法正确性而忽视视觉质量和交互性的不足。该框架自动渲染生成作品并通过时间序列截图捕捉动态行为，实现多模态自动评测。
build_method: 自动化多模态评估, MLLM-as-Judge, 人工专家验证
modality: 文本, 图像, 代码, 多模态
eval_feature: 动态交互, 多模态, 自动化
build_method_en: Automated Multimodal Evaluation, MLLM-as-Judge, Manual Expert Verification
intro_en: ArtifactsBench proposes an evaluation benchmark for dynamic interactive visual works generated by large language models, addressing the limitation of traditional benchmarks that focus solely on algorithmic correctness while neglecting visual quality and interactivity. The framework automatically renders generated works and captures dynamic behaviors through time-series screenshots, enabling multimodal automatic evaluation.
flowchart_en:

    style Data_Collection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style Processing_Filtering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style Annotation_Labeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff

    style Evaluation_Metric fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style Output_Result fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    subgraph Phase_1["Data Sourcing & Collection"]
        A([Start]):::Start_End
        B[/Initial Data Sources/](Data_Collection)
        C(Identify Relevant Datasets):::Data_Collection
        D(Collect Raw Data):::Data_Collection
        E{Data Availability Check}:::Quality_Control
        F(Acquire Data):::Data_Collection
        G(Log Data Provenance):::Data_Collection
        A --> B
        B --> C
        C --> D
        D --> E
        E -- Yes --> F
        E -- No --> C
        F --> G
    end

    subgraph Phase_2["Data Preprocessing & Filtering"]
        H(Load Raw Data):::Processing_Filtering
        I(Clean Data):::Processing_Filtering
        J(Normalize Data):::Processing_Filtering
        K{Filtering Criteria Met?}:::Quality_Control
        L(Filter Irrelevant Data):::Processing_Filtering
        M(Split Data into Train/Test/Validation Sets):::Processing_Filtering
        G --> H
        H --> I
        I --> J
        J --> K
        K -- Yes --> M
        K -- No --> L
        L --> I
    end

    subgraph Phase_3["Annotation & Labeling"]
        N(Define Annotation Guidelines):::Annotation_Labeling
        O(Human Annotation Task):::Annotation_Labeling
        P{Annotation Consensus Check}:::Quality_Control
        Q(Review Discrepancies):::Annotation_Labeling
        R(Automated Labeling):::Annotation_Labeling
        M --> N
        N --> O
        O --> P
        P -- High Consensus --> R
        P -- Low Consensus --> Q
        Q --> O
    end

    subgraph Phase_4["Quality Control & Validation"]
        S(Perform Data Validation):::Quality_Control
        T(Expert Review of Samples):::Quality_Control
        U{Quality Threshold Met?}:::Quality_Control
        V(Iterative Refinement):::Quality_Control
        W(Benchmark Dataset Finalization):::Quality_Control

name: ASPERA
intro: ASPERA是一个评估大型语言模型执行复杂操作能力的框架，包含模拟环境和数据生成引擎，用于创建具有挑战性的任务。其附带的Asper-Bench数据集包含250个任务，用于测试基于自定义助手库的程序生成能力。
build_method: LLM生成, 人工标注, 混合
intro_en: ASPERA is a framework for evaluating large language models' ability to perform complex operations, featuring a simulation environment and a data generation engine to create challenging tasks. Its accompanying Asper-Bench dataset includes 250 tasks designed to test program generation capabilities based on custom assistant libraries.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A[Simulation Environment] --> C{{LLM Generation}}
    B[Data Generation Engine] --> C
    C --> D(Generated Tasks)
    D --> E{Manual Annotation/Review}
    E -->|Refine| F[Task Refinement]
    E -->|Approve| G(Asper-Bench Dataset\n250 Tasks)
    F --> G
    G --> H[Model Evaluation]
    H --> I(Performance Metrics)
    I --> J[Agent Capability Assessment]
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    style Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style Processing fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style Annotation fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style Evaluation fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style Output fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([Start]) --> Setup

    subgraph Setup["Initial Setup & Framework Components"]
        Setup --> A[Assistant Library Simulation]:::DataCollection
        Setup --> B[Human-assisted LLM Data Generation Engine]:::DataCollection
    end

    subgraph TaskGeneration["ASPERA Task Generation"]
        B --> C(Initialize Interactive Chat Session):::Processing
        C --> D(LLM Prompted for AEP Generation):::Processing
        D --> E{AEP Quality Check?}
        E -- Yes --> F(Developer Edits AEP):::Annotation
        E -- No --> D
        F --> G(Append History of Generated Queries):::Processing
        G --> H(Apply Focus Instructions for Diversity):::Processing
        H --> I(Interactive Filtering of Queries):::QualityControl
        I --> J(LLM Prompted for SIP Generation):::Processing
        J --> K(Custom Environment State Definition):::Annotation
        K --> L(LLM Prompted for EP Generation):::Processing
    end

    subgraph ValidationAndRefinement["Validation & Refinement"]
        L --> M(Execute AEP in Initialized Environment):::Evaluation
        M --> N{User Goal Met & No Side Effects?}
        N -- Yes --> O(Record Task as Valid):::Processing
        N -- No --> F
        O --> P(Collect Evaluation Metrics):::Evaluation
        P --> Q(Asper-Bench Dataset Integration):::Output
    end

    subgraph AsperBench["Asper-Bench Dataset"]
        Q --> R[/250 Challenging Tasks/]
        R --> S(Analyze Compositional Use of Primitives):::Evaluation
        R --> T(Evaluate AST Depth Distribution):::Evaluation
        R --> U(Assess Complex Time Expressions):::Evaluation
        R --> V(Test Logical Reasoning & Instructions):::Evaluation
    end

    V --> End([End])
    U --> End
    T --> End
    S --> End
flowchart_zh:
flowchart TD
    style Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    style Evaluation fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style Output fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([开始]) --> Setup

    subgraph Setup["初始设置与框架组件"]
        Setup --> A[助手库模拟]:::DataCollection
        Setup --> B[人工辅助LLM数据生成引擎]:::DataCollection
    end

    subgraph TaskGeneration["ASPERA任务生成"]
        B --> C(初始化交互式聊天会话):::Processing
        C --> D(提示LLM生成AEP):::Processing
        D --> E{AEP质量检查?}
        E -- 是 --> F(开发者编辑AEP):::Annotation
        E -- 否 --> D
        F --> G(追加已生成查询的历史记录):::Processing
        G --> H(应用焦点指令以增加多样性):::Processing
        H --> I(交互式过滤查询):::QualityControl
        I --> J(提示LLM生成SIP):::Processing
        J --> K(自定义环境状态定义):::Annotation
        K --> L(提示LLM生成EP):::Processing
    end

    subgraph ValidationAndRefinement["验证与完善"]
        L --> M(在初始化环境中执行AEP):::Evaluation
        M --> N{用户目标达成且无副作用?}
        N -- 是 --> O(记录任务为有效):::Processing
        N -- 否 --> F
        O --> P(收集评估指标):::Evaluation
        P --> Q(Asper-Bench数据集集成):::Output
    end

    subgraph AsperBench["Asper-Bench数据集"]
        Q --> R[/250个挑战性任务/]
        R --> S(分析原语的组合使用):::Evaluation
        R --> T(评估AST深度分布):::Evaluation
        R --> U(评估复杂时间表达式):::Evaluation
        R --> V(测试逻辑推理与指令):::Evaluation
    end

    V --> End([结束])
    U --> End
    T --> End
    S --> End

name: AstaBench
intro: AstaBench是一个旨在严格评估AI代理科学研究能力的评测套件。它包含超过2400个问题，覆盖科学发现的全过程和多个学科领域，并提供一个配备了生产级搜索工具的可控、可复现的评测环境。该基准还提供九类科学优化的Asta代理和众多基线模型，以促进对AI代理核心能力的公平比较。
task_type: Agent任务, 代码生成, Data Analysis
eval_feature: 领域特定, 过程导向
task_type_en: Agent Task, Code Generation, Data Analysis
intro_en: AstaBench is a benchmark suite designed to rigorously evaluate AI agents' scientific research capabilities. It includes over 2,400 questions covering the entire scientific discovery process across multiple disciplines and offers a controllable, reproducible evaluation environment equipped with production-level search tools. The benchmark also provides nine types of science-optimized Asta agents and numerous baseline models to facilitate fair comparisons of core AI agent abilities.

    C --> D

    E --> F[Structure & Refine Questions]
    F --> G[Expert Review & Validation]
    G --> H{Quality Check}
    H -->|Pass| I(AstaBench Question Set\n2,400 questions)
    H -->|Fail| F
    I --> J[AI Agent Evaluation]
    J --> K(Evaluation Results)

name: AudioMC
intro: Audio MultiChallenge是一个开源基准，旨在评估端到端语音对话系统在自然多轮交互中的表现。该基准基于文本MultiChallenge框架，并新增了“语音编辑”维度，以测试模型对语音修正和回溯的鲁棒性。它包含452段由47名说话人参与的对话，揭示了当前先进模型在处理编辑追踪、音频线索和长程上下文方面的挑战。
build_method: 混合, 人工标注, Agentic生成
metric: Average Pass Rate (APR), Average Rubric Score (ARS)
eval_feature: 多轮, 长文本, 语音编辑
intro_en: Audio MultiChallenge is an open-source benchmark designed to evaluate end-to-end speech dialogue systems in natural multi-turn interactions. Built on the text-based MultiChallenge framework, it introduces a "speech editing" dimension to test models' robustness in speech correction and backtracking. It includes 452 dialogues involving 47 speakers, highlighting current state-of-the-art models' challenges in handling edit tracking, audio cues, and long-range context.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Text-based MultiChallenge Framework) --> B{{Agentic Dialogue Generation}}
    B --> C[Speech Synthesis]
    C --> D[Introduce Speech Editing Scenarios]
    D --> E(Raw Audio Data)
    E --> F{{Human Annotation}}
    F --> G{Quality Check}
    G -->|Pass| H(Curated AudioMC Dataset)
    G -->|Fail| I[Review & Refine]
    I --> F
    H --> J[Evaluate Speech Dialogue Systems]
    J --> K[Test Robustness (Correction/Backtracking)]
metric_en: Average Pass Rate (APR), Average Rubric Score (ARS)

name: AutoCodeBench
intro: AutoCodeBench是一个大规模代码生成基准，包含3920个问题，均匀分布在20种编程语言中。它旨在评估大型语言模型在具有挑战性、多样化和实用性的多语言任务上的表现。该基准利用名为AutoCodeGen的自动化方法生成，通过LLM与多语言沙箱的交互来创建高质量的测试用例，无需人工标注。
intro_en: AutoCodeBench is a large-scale code generation benchmark containing 3,920 problems evenly distributed across 20 programming languages. It aims to evaluate large language models on challenging, diverse, and practical multilingual tasks. The benchmark is generated using an automated method called AutoCodeGen, which creates high-quality test cases through interactions between LLMs and multilingual sandboxes, requiring no manual annotation.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A[Initial Problem Requirements] --> B{{Automated Generation Method}}
    B --> C(Generated Code Problems)
    C --> D{{LLM Generation}}
    D --> E[Problem Filtering & Distribution]
    E --> F{Quality Validation}
    F -- Fail --> G[Problem Refinement]
    G --> E
    F -- Pass --> H(AutoCodeBench Dataset\n3,920 Problems, 20 Languages)
    H --> I[Model Evaluation]
    I --> J[Performance Analysis]
    J --> K[Benchmark Release]
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Benchmark Construction Process
        start_node([Start])
        style start_node fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

        subgraph AutoCodeGen["Automated Dataset Generation (AutoCodeGen)"]
            direction LR
            A1[/Initial Problem Ideas/] --> A2(Generate Test Inputs with LLMs)
            style A2 fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
            A2 --> A3(Execute Code in Multilingual Sandbox)
            style A3 fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
            A3 --> A4(Obtain Test Outputs)
            style A4 fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
            A4 --> A5(Reverse-Order Problem Generation)
            style A5 fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
            A5 --> A6{High Data Quality Achieved?}
            style A6 fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff

            A6 -- Yes --> A7(Apply Multiple Filtering Steps)
            style A7 fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
            A7 --> A8[/High-Quality Multilingual Code Generation Datasets/]
            style A8 fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
        end

        start_node --> AutoCodeGen

        subgraph AutoCodeBench_Construction["AutoCodeBench Benchmark Construction"]
            direction LR
            B1[/High-Quality Multilingual Code Generation Datasets/] --> B2(Compile 3,920 Problems)
            style B1 fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
            style B2 fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
            B2 --> B3(Distribute Evenly Across 20 Programming Languages)
            style B3 fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
            B3 --> B4(Design for Challenging, Diverse, Practical Tasks)
            style B4 fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
            B4 --> B5[/AutoCodeBench: Large-Scale Code Generation Benchmark/]
            style B5 fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
        end

        A8 --> AutoCodeBench_Construction

        subgraph Benchmark_Evaluation["Benchmark Evaluation"]
            direction LR
            C1[/AutoCodeBench/] --> C2(Evaluate 30+ LLMs)
            style C1 fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
            style C2 fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
            C2 --> C3(Evaluate on AutoCodeBench-Lite)
            style C3 fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
            C3 --> C4(Assess Few-Shot Capabilities with AutoCodeBench-Complete)
            style C4 fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
            C4 --> C5[/Evaluation Results: LLMs Struggle with Complexity/]
            style C5 fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
        end

        B5 --> Benchmark_Evaluation

        Benchmark_Evaluation --> end_node([End])
        style end_node fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    end

name: AutoCodeBench-V2
intro: AutoCodeBench是一个大规模代码生成基准，包含3920个问题，均匀分布在20种编程语言中。它旨在评估大型语言模型在具有挑战性、多样化和实用性的多语言任务上的表现。该基准利用名为AutoCodeGen的自动化方法生成，通过LLM与多语言沙箱的交互来创建高质量的测试用例，无需人工标注。
intro_en: AutoCodeBench is a large-scale code generation benchmark consisting of 3,920 problems evenly distributed across 20 programming languages. It aims to evaluate large language models on challenging, diverse, and practical multilingual tasks. The benchmark is generated using an automated method called AutoCodeGen, which creates high-quality test cases through interactions between LLMs and a multilingual sandbox, requiring no manual annotation.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Diverse Programming Task Pool) --> B{{Automated Generation Engine}}
    A --> C{{LLM-based Generation Module}}
    B --> D[Generate Initial Problem Candidates]
    C --> D
    D --> E(Raw Problem Candidates)
    E --> F{Filter for Quality & Diversity}
    F -- Pass --> G[Refine & Format Problems]
    F -- Fail --> E
    G --> H{Validate Correctness & Difficulty}
    H -- Valid --> I(Final AutoCodeBench-V2 Dataset\n3920 Problems, 20 Languages)
    H -- Invalid --> G
    I --> J[Evaluate LLMs on Code Generation]
    J --> K[Analyze Model Performance]
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    start([Start])
    style start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    subgraph Phase1["Problem Identification"]
        A[Identify limitations of existing benchmarks] --> B{Manual annotation issue?}
        B -- Yes --> C[Time-consuming & difficult to scale]
        B -- No --> D{Python-centric?}
        D -- Yes --> E[Limited difficulty & uneven language distribution]
        D -- No --> F[Multilingual benchmarks lack difficulty]
        C --> G[/Existing Benchmarks/]
        E --> G
        F --> G
        G --> H[Need for automated, high-difficulty, multilingual benchmark generation]
    end
    style A fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style H fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    H --> subgraph Phase2["AutoCodeGen Method Development"]
        I[Propose AutoCodeGen: Automated generation method] --> J[No manual annotations]
        J --> K[High-difficulty multilingual datasets]
    end
    style I fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    K --> subgraph Phase3["Data Generation"]
        L[Generate test inputs with LLMs] --> M[Obtain test outputs via multilingual sandbox]
        M --> N[/Raw Code Generation Data/]
    end
    style L fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style M fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style N fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    N --> subgraph Phase4["Data Quality Control"]
        O[Reverse-order problem generation] --> P[Multiple filtering steps]
        P --> Q{Data quality check?}
        Q -- Pass --> R[/High-Quality Code Generation Data/]
        Q -- Fail --> S[Discard/Refine data]
        S --> O
    end
    style O fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style P fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style Q fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style R fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    style S fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff

    R --> subgraph Phase5["Benchmark Creation"]
        T[Introduce AutoCodeBench] --> U[3,920 problems]
        U --> V[20 programming languages]
        V --> W[Challenging, diverse, practical multilingual tasks]
        W --> X[AutoCodeBench-Lite: Simplified version]
        W --> Y[AutoCodeBench-Complete: Few-shot for base models]
    end
    style T fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style U fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    style V fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    style W fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    style X fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    style Y fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    W --> subgraph Phase6["Evaluation"]
        Z[Evaluate 30+ LLMs on AutoCodeBench/Lite] --> AA{LLMs struggle?}
        AA -- Yes --> BB[Confirm complexity & diversity]
        AA -- No --> CC[Re-evaluate/Refine benchmark]
        BB --> DD[Inspire focus on challenging multilingual scenarios]
    end
    style Z fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style AA fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style BB fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    style CC fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style DD fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    DD --> end([End])
    style end fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
flowchart_zh:
flowchart TD
    start([开始])
    style start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    subgraph Phase1["问题识别"]
        A[识别现有基准的局限性] --> B{手动标注问题?}

        B -- 否 --> D{以Python为中心?}

        C --> G[/现有基准/]

        G --> H[需要自动化、高难度、多语言基准生成]
    end

    style H fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    H --> subgraph Phase2["AutoCodeGen方法开发"]
        I[提出AutoCodeGen：自动化生成方法] --> J[无需手动标注]
        J --> K[高难度多语言数据集]
    end
    style I fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    K --> subgraph Phase3["数据生成"]
        L[使用LLM生成测试输入] --> M[通过多语言沙盒获取测试输出]
        M --> N[/原始代码生成数据/]
    end
    style L fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style M fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style N fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    N --> subgraph Phase4["数据质量控制"]
        O[逆序问题生成] --> P[多重过滤步骤]
        P --> Q{数据质量检查?}
        Q -- 通过 --> R[/高质量代码生成数据/]
        Q -- 失败 --> S[丢弃/精炼数据]
        S --> O
    end
    style O fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style P fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style Q fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style R fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    style S fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff

    R --> subgraph Phase5["基准创建"]
        T[引入AutoCodeBench] --> U[3,920个问题]
        U --> V[20种编程语言]
        V --> W[具有挑战性、多样化、实用的多语言任务]
        W --> X[AutoCodeBench-Lite：简化版]
        W --> Y[AutoCodeBench-Complete：针对基础模型的少样本]
    end
    style T fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style U fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    style V fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    style W fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    W --> subgraph Phase6["评估"]

        AA -- 否 --> CC[重新评估/精炼基准]
        BB --> DD[激发对挑战性多语言场景的关注]
    end
    style Z fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style AA fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style BB fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    style CC fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff

    DD --> end([结束])

name: BABE
intro: BABE（Biology Arena BEnchmark）是一个综合性基准，旨在评估生物AI系统的实验推理能力。该基准独特地构建于同行评审的研究论文和真实世界的生物学研究，确保任务反映了实际科学探究的复杂性和跨学科性质。BABE挑战模型进行因果推理和跨尺度推理，提供了一个评估AI系统能否像科学家一样进行推理的框架，从而更真实地衡量其对生物学研究的贡献潜力。
org: Junting Zhou, Jin Chen, Linfeng Hao, Denghui Cao, Zheyu Wang, Qiguang Chen, Chaoyou Fu, Jiaze Chen,
eval_feature: 因果推理, 跨尺度推理
intro_en: BABE (Biology Arena BEnchmark) is a comprehensive benchmark designed to evaluate the experimental reasoning abilities of biological AI systems. Uniquely built on peer-reviewed research papers and real-world biological studies, it ensures tasks reflect the complexity and interdisciplinary nature of actual scientific inquiry. BABE challenges models with causal and cross-scale reasoning, providing a framework to assess whether AI systems can reason like scientists, thereby more accurately measuring their potential contribution to biological research.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Peer-reviewed Research Papers) --> C[Data Collection & Extraction]
    B(Real-world Biological Studies) --> C
    C --> D[Information Structuring]
    D --> E{{Hybrid Methodology}}
    E --> F[Task Formulation]
    F --> G{Quality Assurance & Expert Validation}
    G -->|Validated| H(BABE Dataset\nExperimental Reasoning Tasks)
    H --> I[AI System Evaluation]
    I --> J[Experimental Reasoning Assessment]
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    start([Start]) --> A(Define Benchmark Scope and Goals)
    A --> B[/Initial Data Sources/]
    B --> C{Identify Relevant Biological Data?}
    C -- Yes --> D(Collect Raw Biological Data)
    C -- No --> E(Refine Data Sourcing Strategy)
    E --> B
    D --> F(Pre-process and Clean Data)
    F --> G{Filter Irrelevant/Low-Quality Data?}
    G -- Yes --> H(Apply Filtering Criteria)
    G -- No --> I(Review Filtered Data)
    H --> I
    I --> J(Annotate Data for Experimental Reasoning)
    J --> K{Perform Human Expert Review?}
    K -- Yes --> L(Validate Annotations with Experts)
    K -- No --> M(Automated Annotation Quality Check)
    L --> M
    M --> N{Meet Quality Thresholds?}
    N -- Yes --> O(Construct Benchmark Tasks)
    N -- No --> P(Re-annotate/Recollect Data)
    P --> J
    O --> Q(Define Evaluation Metrics)
    Q --> R(Pilot Testing and Validation)
    R --> S{Benchmark Robust and Fair?}
    S -- Yes --> T(Finalize Benchmark Dataset)
    S -- No --> U(Iterate on Design/Data)
    U --> O
    T --> V([End])

    style start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style V fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style D fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style F fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style H fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style J fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style L fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style M fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style Q fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style R fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style T fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
flowchart_zh:
flowchart TD
    start([开始]) --> A(定义基准范围和目标)
    A --> B[/初始数据源/]
    B --> C{识别相关生物数据？}
    C -- 是 --> D(收集原始生物数据)
    C -- 否 --> E(优化数据源策略)
    E --> B
    D --> F(预处理和清洗数据)
    F --> G{过滤不相关/低质量数据？}
    G -- 是 --> H(应用过滤标准)
    G -- 否 --> I(审查过滤后的数据)
    H --> I

name: BABILong
intro: BABILong基准用于测试语言模型在极长文档中跨事实推理的能力。包含20种多样的推理任务，如事实链、简单归纳、演绎、计数及列表/集合处理。这些任务本身具有挑战性，且当所需事实分散在长文本中时难度更大。
org: AIRI, DeepPavlov.ai, and London Institute for Mathematical Sciences
eval_feature: 长文本, 防污染, 思维链
intro_en: The BABILong benchmark tests language models' ability to perform cross-fact reasoning in extremely long documents. It includes 20 diverse reasoning tasks such as fact chaining, simple induction, deduction, counting, and list/set processing. These tasks are inherently challenging and become even more difficult when the required facts are scattered throughout long texts.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Task Schemas & Rules) --> B[Define Task Parameters]
    B --> C{{Automated Data Generator}}
    C --> D[Generate Raw Task Instances]
    D --> E[Construct Long Documents]
    E --> F{Validate Task Logic & Length}
    F -->|Pass| H(BABILong Dataset)
    F -->|Fail| G[Refine & Regenerate]
    G --> D
    H --> I[Language Model Evaluation]
    I --> J[Performance Metrics]
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    style Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style ProcessingFiltering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style AnnotationLabeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style EvaluationMetrics fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([Start]) --> S1(Identify Gap in LLM Evaluation) 

    subgraph Data Sourcing
        S1 --> S2[/PG19 Corpora (Books)/] 
        S2 --> S3(Extract Long Natural Documents) 
    end

    subgraph Task Generation
        S3 --> S4[/bAbI Benchmark (Weston et al., 2016)/] 
        S4 --> S5(Define 20 Reasoning Tasks: QA1-QA20) 
        S5 --> S6(Simulate Interactions: Characters, Objects, Locations) 
        S6 --> S7(Generate Facts: e.g., "Mary traveled to the office.") 
        S7 --> S8(Formulate Questions based on Facts: e.g., "Where is Mary?") 
    end

    subgraph Dataset Augmentation (Long Context Creation)
        S8 --> S9(Extend Task Length for Long Context Evaluation) 
        S9 --> S10(Select Irrelevant Text from PG19 Dataset) 
        S10 --> S11(Interleave Task Sentences with Irrelevant Text) 
        S11 --> S12{Desired Length Reached?} 
        S12 -- No --> S11
        S12 -- Yes --> S13(Create Augmented Samples up to Millions of Tokens) 
        S13 --> S14(Provide Predefined Splits up to 10M Tokens) 
    end

    subgraph Evaluation
        S14 --> S15(Select >30 Long-Input LLMs) 
        S15 --> S16(Evaluate Models on QA Tasks) 
        S16 --> S17{Accuracy > 85%?} 
        S17 -- Yes --> S18(Satisfactory Performance) 
        S17 -- No --> S19(Performance Degradation) 
        S19 --> S20(Analyze Context Utilization: 10-20% effective) 
        S20 --> S21(Assess Impact of Reasoning Complexity) 
        S21 --> S22(Compare RAG Methods: ~60% accuracy on single-fact QA) 
        S22 --> S23(Evaluate RMT/ARMT: up to 50M tokens) 
    end

    S18 --> End([End])
    S23 --> End([End])

    end

    S23 --> End([结束])

name: BeyondAIME
intro: BeyondAIME是一个精心策划的测试集，旨在评估高级数学推理能力。其问题来源于高中和大学数学竞赛，难度与AIME竞赛问题相当。该基准测试的创建遵循高难度、抗污染、注重推理而非知识、鲁棒问题设计和自动化评估等核心原则。
intro_en: BeyondAIME is a carefully curated test set designed to evaluate advanced mathematical reasoning abilities. Its problems are sourced from high school and college math competitions, with difficulty comparable to AIME problems. The benchmark is created following core principles of high difficulty, contamination resistance, emphasis on reasoning over knowledge, robust problem design, and automated evaluation.

    subgraph Problem Processing["Problem Processing"]
        D --> F(Collect Challenging Problems)
        E --> G(Collect Proof-Writing Problems)
        F --> H{Problem Release?}

        H -- No --> F
    end

    subgraph Model Evaluation["Model Evaluation"]
        I --> J(Evaluate LLMs in Real-Time)
        J --> K{Contamination Risk?}

        K -- Present (e.g., AIME 2024) --> M(Identify Data Contamination)
        L --> N(Evaluate Harder Competitions e.g., CMIMC 2025)
        L --> O(Evaluate Proof-Writing on IMO 2025)
        N --> P[/Evaluation Results (Reasoning)/]
        O --> Q[/Evaluation Results (Proof-Writing)/]
    end

    subgraph Benchmark Maintenance["Benchmark Maintenance"]
        P --> R(Aggregate Evaluation Data)
        Q --> R
        R --> S(Analyze Model Performance)

        T -- No --> V(Identify Areas for Improvement)
        U --> W(Integrate Newly Released Competitions)
        V --> W
        W --> X([End])
    end

name: BFCL-v4
intro: BFCL-v4是一个用于评估大型语言模型函数调用能力的综合基准。它涵盖了多种编程语言中的串行和并行函数调用，并采用创新的抽象语法树（AST）评估方法。该基准通过专家策划和用户贡献的函数，全面测试模型在多样化、真实的工具使用场景中的表现。
build_method: 专家构建, 众包, 混合
metric: Accuracy, AST Match, Relevance, Irrelevance, Max Delta, SD, Mean
eval_feature: 多轮, 动态, 实时, 防污染
intro_en: BFCL-v4 is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating large language models' function calling capabilities. It covers serial and parallel function calls across multiple programming languages and employs an innovative abstract syntax tree (AST) evaluation method. The benchmark thoroughly tests models in diverse, real-world tool usage scenarios using expert-curated and user-contributed functions.

    E --> F[Refined Function Call Scenarios]
    F --> G[AST Generation & Validation]
    G --> H{AST Consistency Check}
    H -->|Pass| I[Final BFCL-v4 Dataset]
    H -->|Fail| J[Scenario Revision]
    J --> F
    I --> K[LLM Function Calling Evaluation]
    K --> L{{AST-based Evaluation Method}}
    L --> M(Evaluation Results & Metrics)
metric_en: Accuracy, AST Match, Relevance, Irrelevance, Max Delta, SD, Mean
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Data Sourcing & Collection
        A([Start]) --> B[/Expert-curated Functions & Prompts/]
        B --> C[/User-contributed Functions & Prompts/]
        C --> D{Combine Data Sources?}
        D -- Yes --> E[/Raw Function Calling Evaluation Data/]
        D -- No --> E
    end

    subgraph Data Curation & Generation
        E --> F(Generate Data with GPT-4o-0806)
        F --> G{Hybrid Validation Strategy?}
        G -- Yes --> H(Manual Validation)
        G -- Yes --> I(Programmatic Validation)
        G -- Yes --> J(LLM-based Validation)
        H & I & J --> K[/Curated Function Calling Evaluation Data/]
        G -- No --> K
    end

    subgraph Model Response Generation
        K --> L(LLM Inference: Gorilla, OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, Others)
        L --> M[/Model Output/]
    end

    subgraph Evaluation
        M --> N(Decode AST from Model Output)
        N --> O[/AST Output/]

        M --> Q(Decode Executable from Model Output)

        P & S --> T(Aggregate Evaluation Results)
    end

    subgraph Leaderboard & Analysis

        U --> V([End])
    end

name: BIG-Bench Hard
intro: BIG-Bench Extra Hard (BBEH) 是一个为推动大型语言模型（LLM）推理能力评估极限而设计的新基准。它通过引入比BIG-Bench Hard (BBH) 更具挑战性的新任务，解决了现有基准测试饱和的问题。该基准的评估结果表明，即便是最先进的模型在通用推理能力上仍有巨大的提升空间。
build_method: 混合（专家构建, 改编现有数据集）
metric: Accuracy, Exact Match, Harmonic Average Accuracy
task_type: 选择题, 开放式问答
eval_feature: 思维链, 零样本, 少样本
build_method_en: Hybrid (Expert Construction, Adaptation of Existing Datasets)
intro_en: BIG-Bench Extra Hard (BBEH) is a new benchmark designed to push the limits of large language models' (LLM) reasoning capabilities. By introducing tasks more challenging than those in BIG-Bench Hard (BBH), it addresses the saturation issue of existing benchmarks. Evaluation results show that even state-of-the-art models have significant room for improvement in general reasoning ability.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Existing Datasets
(e.g., BIG-Bench Hard)) --> B[Task Adaptation]
    C[Expert Knowledge] --> D[New Task Creation]
    B --> E{Increase Difficulty?}
    D --> E
    E -- Yes --> F[Task Refinement]
    E -- No --> G[Discard/Revise]
    F --> H[Expert Validation]
    H --> I(BIG-Bench Extra Hard
(BBEH) Dataset)
    I --> J[LLM Reasoning Evaluation]
    J --> K[Performance Analysis]
metric_en: Accuracy, Exact Match, Harmonic Average Accuracy
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    start_node([Start BBEH Construction]) style fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    subgraph Data Sourcing & Initial Task Definition["Data Sourcing & Initial Task Definition"]
        A[/BIG-Bench Hard (BBH) Tasks/] style fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
        B(Identify 23 BBH Tasks) style fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
        C(Define Target Reasoning Capabilities) style fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    end

    subgraph Task Generation & Refinement["Task Generation & Refinement"]
        D(Generate Novel Task Counterparts for each BBH Task) style fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff

name: BigCodeBench
intro: BigCodeBench 是一个评测大型语言模型（LLMs）处理复杂实用编程任务能力的基准。它要求模型调用多种库函数，超越简单算法题。该基准包含自然语言指令变体，显示当前模型在复杂指令和精确函数使用上仍有不足，指明未来研究方向。
build_method: LLM生成, 人工标注, 专家构建
build_method_en: LLM Generation, Human Annotation, Expert Curation
intro_en: BigCodeBench is a benchmark for evaluating large language models' (LLMs) ability to handle complex, practical programming tasks. It requires models to invoke various library functions, going beyond simple algorithm problems. The benchmark includes natural language instruction variants, highlighting current models' limitations in handling complex instructions and precise function usage, and indicating directions for future research.

    B --> C[Initial Task Generation]
    C --> D[Manual Annotation]
    D --> E[Expert Construction]
    E --> F{Quality Review}
    F -->|Pass| G(Curated BigCodeBench Dataset)
    F -->|Fail| H[Refinement Loop]
    H --> D
    G --> I[LLM Evaluation]
    I --> J(Evaluation Results)

name: BigOBench
intro: BigOBench 是一个新颖的编程基准，用于评估生成式语言模型理解和生成满足时间与空间复杂度约束代码的能力。它弥补了现有评测忽视计算复杂度理解的空白。该基准提供工具，通过性能分析推断任意Python函数的算法复杂度，包括人类或模型生成的代码。
eval_feature: 领域特定, 结果导向
intro_en: BigOBench is a novel programming benchmark designed to evaluate generative language models' ability to understand and generate code that meets time and space complexity constraints. It addresses the gap in existing evaluations that overlook computational complexity understanding. The benchmark provides tools to infer the algorithmic complexity of any Python function through performance analysis, applicable to both human- and model-generated code.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A[Existing Codebases/Problems] --> B(Problem Collection)
    B --> C[Code Parsing & Problem Extraction]
    C --> D{{Complexity Analysis Tool/Expert}}
    D --> E{Complexity Constraint Generation}
    E --> F[Hybrid Data Integration]
    F --> G{Quality Control & Verification}
    G -->|Pass| H(BigOBench Dataset
Code, Constraints)
    G -->|Fail| I[Refinement/Discard]
    H --> J[Model Evaluation Platform]
    J --> K[Performance Metrics
Time/Space Complexity]
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    classDef StartEnd fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    classDef DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    classDef ProcessingFiltering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    classDef AnnotationLabeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    classDef QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    classDef EvaluationMetrics fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    classDef OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    subgraph Benchmark Construction Process
        A([Start]):::StartEnd

        subgraph Data Sourcing & Collection
            B[/Coding Problems from Code Contests/]
            C[/Human- or LLM-generated Solutions/]
            B --> C
            class B,C DataCollection
        end

        subgraph Dynamic Complexity Inference Framework
            D(Input: Python Function Inputs Dataclass)
            C --> D
            E(Process & Modify Code Inputs)
            D --> E
            F{Expand Input Size?}
            E --> F
            G(Generate Synthetic Inputs)
            F -- Yes --> G
            H(Queue Synthetic Inputs)
            G --> H
            I(Execute in Independent Sandboxes)
            H --> I
            J(Measure Runtime Cprofiler & Memory Tracemalloc)
            I --> J
            K(Apply Non-Negative Least Squares Curve Fitting)
            J --> K
            L(Compute Coefficients & Residuals for Complexity Classes)
            K --> L
            M{Simplicity Bias Applied?}
            L --> M
            N(Select Gold Complexity Class Minimizer of Residuals)
            M -- Yes --> N
            O(Apply Curve Fitting per Measure Set)
            N --> O
            P(Aggregate Global Complexity Ensemble Methods)
            O --> P
            class D,E,F,G,H,I,J,K,L,M,N,O,P ProcessingFiltering
        end

        subgraph Annotation & Labeling
            Q(Annotate with Inferred Time/Space Complexity Labels)
            P --> Q
            class Q AnnotationLabeling
        end

        subgraph Quality Control & Validation
            R(Evaluate Models on Benchmark)
            Q --> R
            S(Highlight Strengths & Weaknesses)
            R --> S
            class R,S QualityControl
        end

        subgraph Evaluation & Metrics
            T(Compute All@1 Metric & Pass@1 Scores)
            S --> T
            class T EvaluationMetrics
        end

        U([End]):::StartEnd
        T --> U
    end

            S --> T
            class T EvaluationMetrics
        end

        U([结束]):::StartEnd
        T --> U
    end

name: BioASQ
intro: BioASQ是国际生物医学语义索引和问答挑战赛系列，涵盖多语言临床总结、命名实体链接、心脏病临床编码及肠脑相互作用信息提取等多项任务，是生物医学NLP领域最重要的评测赛事之一。
org: CLEF, National Center for Scientific Research (Greece)
task_type: 问答, 文本摘要, 命名实体识别, 信息抽取
eval_feature: 大规模生物医学语义索引和问答，年度挑战赛形式
task_type_en: Question Answering, Text Summarization, Named Entity Recognition, Information Extraction
eval_feature_en: Large-scale Biomedical Semantic Indexing and QA, Annual Challenge Format
intro_en: BioASQ is an international series of biomedical semantic indexing and question answering challenges, covering multiple tasks such as multilingual clinical summarization, named entity linking, cardiac clinical coding, and gut-brain interaction information extraction. It is one of the most important benchmarks in the biomedical NLP field.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Biomedical Literature) --> B[Expert Identification of Relevant Docs]
    B --> C[Information Extraction & Summarization]
    C --> D[Question Generation by Experts]
    D --> E[Answer Formulation by Experts]
    E --> F{Quality Check: Relevance & Accuracy}
    F -- Pass --> G[Refinement & Annotation]
    F -- Fail --> B
    G --> H{{Inter-Annotator Agreement}}
    H --> I(Final BioASQ Dataset)
    I --> J[Model Evaluation Phase]
    J --> K{{QA Metrics Calculation}}
    K --> L(Challenge Leaderboard)
scale_en: Tens of thousands of biomedical QA pairs

name: BioMysteryBench
intro: BioMysteryBench旨在评估AI科学家在组学数据驱动的生物发现方面的能力。该基准测试要求模型处理未经处理的原始数据，并回答诸如“哪个基因被敲除了”或“样本感染了什么病毒”等难题，从而评估其解决复杂分析挑战的能力。
intro_en: BioMysteryBench aims to evaluate AI scientists' ability in omics data-driven biological discovery. This benchmark requires models to process raw, unprocessed data and answer challenging questions such as "Which gene was knocked out?" or "What virus infected the sample?" to assess their capability in tackling complex analytical challenges.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Raw Omics Data) --> B[Omics Data Acquisition]
    B --> C[Data Cleaning & Normalization]
    C --> D{{Feature Extraction}}
    D --> E{Data Quality Control}
    E -->|Pass| F[Challenging Question Generation]
    E -->|Fail| G[Data Rejection]
    F --> H[Expert Answer Annotation]
    H --> I(BioMysteryBench Dataset)
    I --> J[Model Inference]
    J --> K[Answer Comparison]
    K --> L[Performance Metrics]
    L --> M[Benchmark Results]
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    A([Start])
    style A fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    A -->|Paper not accessible| B([End])
    style B fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
flowchart_zh:
flowchart TD
    A([开始])
    style A fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    A -->|论文无法访问| B([结束])
    style B fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

name: BIRD-SQL
intro: BIRD-SQL是一个大规模、跨领域的文本到SQL基准测试，包含超过12,751个文本-SQL对和95个数据库，覆盖37个专业领域。该基准测试的重点是处理真实世界中的挑战，例如不干净的数据库内容、需要外部知识才能回答的自然语言问题，以及在大规模数据库中SQL查询的效率。
org: The University of Hong Kong, DAMO Academy, Alibaba Group, Tsinghua University, Shanghai AI Laborator
build_method: 混合, 人工标注, 网络爬取
metric: Execution Accuracy (EX), Valid Accuracy (VA), Reward-based Valid Efficiency Score (R-VES)
modality: 文本, 代码, 表格, SQL
intro_en: BIRD-SQL is a large-scale, cross-domain text-to-SQL benchmark containing over 12,751 text-SQL pairs and 95 databases across 37 professional fields. It focuses on real-world challenges such as noisy database content, natural language questions requiring external knowledge, and the efficiency of SQL queries on large-scale databases.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Diverse Databases) --> C
    B(Web Content) --> C{{Web Crawling}}
    C --> D[Data Extraction & Cleaning]
    D --> E[Natural Language Question Generation]
    D --> F[SQL Query Generation]
    E --> G[Pairing NLQ with SQL]
    F --> G
    G --> H[Manual Annotation & Verification]
    H --> I{Quality Review}
    I -->|Pass| J(BIRD-SQL Dataset\n12,751 Text-SQL Pairs)
    I -->|Fail| D
    J --> K[Text-to-SQL Model Evaluation]
    K --> L[Performance Analysis]
metric_en: Execution Accuracy (EX), Valid Accuracy (VA), Reward-based Valid Efficiency Score (R-VES)
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    style Start_End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    style Evaluation_Metric fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style Output_Result fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([Start]) --> Data_Sourcing(Identify & Source Databases)
    Data_Sourcing --> Data_Collection_Phase[/95 Databases, 33.4 GB, 37 Domains/]
    Data_Collection_Phase --> Data_Cleaning(Handle Dirty Database Contents)
    Data_Cleaning --> NL_Question_Gen(Generate Natural Language Questions)
    NL_Question_Gen --> SQL_Query_Gen(Generate Corresponding SQL Queries)
    SQL_Query_Gen --> Pair_Creation(Create Text-SQL Pairs: 12,751 total)
    Pair_Creation --> External_Knowledge(Integrate External Knowledge)
    External_Knowledge --> SQL_Efficiency(Focus on SQL Efficiency)
    SQL_Efficiency --> Database_Value_Comp(Ensure Database Value Comprehension)
    Database_Value_Comp --> Quality_Check{Meet Quality Standards?}
    Quality_Check -- Yes --> Benchmark_Assembly(Assemble BIRD Benchmark)
    Quality_Check -- No --> Refinement(Refine Data & Pairs)
    Refinement --> Pair_Creation
    Benchmark_Assembly --> Model_Evaluation(Evaluate Text-to-SQL Models)
    Model_Evaluation --> Performance_Analysis(Analyze Execution Accuracy)
    Performance_Analysis --> Decision_Point{Accuracy > Threshold?}
    Decision_Point -- Yes --> Efficiency_Analysis(Conduct SQL Efficiency Analysis)
    Decision_Point -- No --> Further_Research(Identify Areas for Improvement)
    Efficiency_Analysis --> Insights(Generate Insights for Efficient SQLs)
    Insights --> Final_Benchmark([BIRD Benchmark Released])

    subgraph Data Acquisition & Preparation
        Data_Sourcing

name: BLINK
intro: BLINK是一个针对多模态大语言模型（LLMs）的新基准，专注于其他评估中未发现的核心视觉感知能力。它将14个经典的计算机视觉任务重新格式化为3,807个多项选择题，并配有单张或多张图像和视觉提示。BLINK旨在刺激社区帮助多模态LLMs赶上人类水平的视觉感知。
org: Princeton University, Meta, University of Washington
intro_en: BLINK is a new benchmark for multimodal large language models (LLMs) that focuses on core visual perception abilities not captured by other evaluations. It reformats 14 classic computer vision tasks into 3,807 multiple-choice questions, accompanied by single or multiple images and visual prompts. BLINK aims to encourage the community to help multimodal LLMs reach human-level visual perception.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Classic CV Tasks) --> B[Task Reformatting]
    B --> C{{Question Generation}}
    C --> D(Initial MC Questions)
    D --> E[Add Images & Prompts]
    E --> F(Raw BLINK Questions)
    F --> G{Content Validation}
    G -- Pass --> H(Final BLINK Dataset)
    G -- Fail --> I[Refinement]
    I --> E
    H --> J[Evaluate Multimodal LLMs]
    J --> K[Visual Perception Abilities]

name: BoolQ
intro: BoolQ是一个自然语言是非问答数据集。其问题是在无提示、无约束的自然场景下产生的，因此具有相当的挑战性。这些问题通常涉及复杂的非事实信息，需要模型具备强大的推理能力才能正确解答。
build_method: 人工标注, 网络爬取
intro_en: BoolQ is a natural language yes/no question answering dataset. Its questions are generated in unconstrained, natural scenarios without prompts, making it quite challenging. These questions often involve complex non-factual information, requiring models to have strong reasoning abilities to answer correctly.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Web Content) --> B{{Web Scraping}}
    B --> C[Text Extraction & Structuring]
    C --> D[Manual Question Generation]
    D --> E[Manual Answer Annotation]
    E --> F{Quality Review & Validation}
    F -- Pass --> G(BoolQ Dataset)
    F -- Fail --> H[Discard/Revise]
    G --> I[Model Evaluation]
    I --> J[Yes/No QA Task]
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Data Sourcing["Data Sourcing"]
        A([Start]) style fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
        A --> B[/Google Search Queries/]
        B --> C{Heuristic Filter: Yes/No Indicator Words & Length?}
        style B fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
        C -- Yes --> D{Wikipedia Page in Top 5 Results?}
        C -- No --> E(Discard Query)
        style E fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    end

    subgraph Human Annotation["Human Annotation (3-Step Process)"]
        D -- Yes --> F[/Question & Wikipedia Page/]
        style F fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
        F --> G{Is Question Good? (Comprehensible, Unambiguous, Factual)}
        G -- No --> H(Discard Question)
        style H fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
        G -- Yes --> I(Find Passage in Wikipedia Article)
        style I fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
        I --> J{Is Question Answerable by Passage?}
        J -- No --> K(Mark as Not Answerable)
        style K fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
        J -- Yes --> L(Annotate Answer: Yes/No)
        style L fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    end

    subgraph Quality Control & Finalization["Quality Control & Finalization"]
        L --> M{Is Answer Yes/No?}
        M -- No --> N(Discard Non-Yes/No Answer)
        style N fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
        M -- Yes --> O(Pair Question with Selected Passage)
        style O fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
        O --> P[/16,000 BoolQ Examples/]
        style P fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    end

    subgraph Evaluation["Evaluation"]
        P --> Q(Human Annotator Accuracy: 90%)
        style Q fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
        P --> R(Majority Baseline Accuracy: 62%)
        style R fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
        P --> S(BERT + MultiNLI + BoolQ Retrain Accuracy: 80.4%)
        style S fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    end

    E --> Z([End]) style fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    H --> Z
    K --> Z
    N --> Z
    Q --> Z
    R --> Z
    S --> Z

    subgraph Data Sourcing["数据来源"]
        A([开始]) style fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
        A --> B[/Google搜索查询/]

        style B fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

        C -- 否 --> E(丢弃查询)

    end

        D -- 是 --> F[/问题 & 维基百科页面/]
        style F fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

        G -- 否 --> H(丢弃问题)
        style H fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
        G -- 是 --> I(在维基百科文章中查找段落)
        style I fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
        I --> J{问题是否可通过段落回答?}
        J -- 否 --> K(标记为无法回答)
        style K fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
        J -- 是 --> L(标注答案: 是/否)

    end

    subgraph Quality Control & Finalization["质量控制与最终确定"]
        L --> M{答案是是/否?}

    E --> Z([结束]) style fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    H --> Z
    K --> Z
    N --> Z
    Q --> Z
    R --> Z
    S --> Z

name: BrowseComp
intro: BrowseComp是一个用于衡量AI代理浏览网页能力的基准。它包含1266个具有挑战性的问题，需要代理在互联网上持续导航以寻找难以发现的信息。尽管问题困难，但答案简短且易于验证，使其成为一个简单易用的评测工具。
intro_en: BrowseComp is a benchmark for evaluating AI agents' web browsing capabilities. It includes 1,266 challenging questions that require agents to continuously navigate the internet to find hard-to-discover information. Despite the difficulty, the answers are brief and easy to verify, making it a straightforward and user-friendly evaluation tool.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR

    F --> G{Answer Verification}
    G -- Pass --> H(BrowseComp Dataset\n1,266 Questions)
    G -- Fail --> E
    H --> I[AI Agent Evaluation]
    I --> J(Agent Performance Reports)

name: BrowseComp-Plus
intro: BrowseComp-Plus是BrowseComp的衍生基准，专为深度研究代理设计，采用固定且精心筛选的语料库。每个查询都包含人工验证的支持文档和挖掘出的挑战性负样本，以实现可控的实验。该基准能够对深度研究代理和检索方法进行全面的评估和解耦分析，从而促进对检索有效性、引用准确性和上下文工程的深入理解。
build_method: 人工标注, 专家构建
task_type: Agent任务, 开放式问答, 事实验证
task_type_en: Agent Task, Open-ended QA, Fact Verification
intro_en: BrowseComp-Plus is a derivative benchmark of BrowseComp, designed specifically for in-depth agent research, using a fixed and carefully curated corpus. Each query includes manually verified supporting documents and mined challenging negative samples for controlled experiments. This benchmark enables comprehensive evaluation and decoupled analysis of advanced agents and retrieval methods, promoting a deeper understanding of retrieval effectiveness, citation accuracy, and context engineering.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Original BrowseComp Data) --> B[Corpus Curation]
    B --> C(Curated Corpus)
    C --> D[Query Collection]
    C --> E[Supporting Document Collection]
    C --> F[Negative Sample Mining]
    D --> G{{Manual Annotation}}
    E --> G
    F --> G
    G --> H{{Expert Construction}}
    H --> I{Manual Verification}
    I -->|Pass| J(Verified Queries & Docs)
    I -->|Fail| K[Discard Invalid]
    J --> L(BrowseComp-Plus Dataset)
    K --> L
    L --> M[Agent Research & Evaluation]
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    style Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style ProcessingFiltering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style AnnotationLabeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style EvaluationMetrics fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([Start])

    subgraph DataSourcing["Data Sourcing"]
        A[Original BrowseComp Dataset (1,266 Questions)] --> B(Generate Search Queries with GPT-5-Turbo)
        B --> C[/Google Search API/]
        C --> D(Retrieve Top 100 Documents per Query)
        D --> E[/Candidate Document Pool (~3.8M Documents)/]
    end
    style DataSourcing fill:#e0f2f7,stroke:#b2ebf2

    subgraph DataProcessing["Data Processing"]
        E --> F(Deduplicate Documents by URL) --> G[/Unique Document Pool (~1.5M Documents)/]
        G --> H(Human Annotation: Verify Document Relevance)
        H --> I{Document Contains Direct Answer?}
        I -- Yes --> J[/Human-Verified Gold Documents (25,834)/]
        I -- No --> K(Flag for Negative Mining)
    end
    style DataProcessing fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#c8e6c9

    subgraph NegativeMining["Negative Mining"]
        G --> L(BM25 Retriever: Identify Top 20 Documents per Query) --> M(Cross-Encoder Model: Re-rank Documents)
        M --> N{High Relevance Score & Not Gold?}
        N -- Yes --> O[/Challenging Negative Documents (50,000)/]
        N -- No --> P(Discard Document)
    end
    style NegativeMining fill:#fbe9e7,stroke:#ffccbc

    subgraph BenchmarkConstruction["Benchmark Construction"]
        J & O --> Q(Combine Gold and Challenging Negative Documents)
        Q --> R[/Final Curated Corpus/]
        A --> S(Integrate Original 1,266 Questions)
        R & S --> T([BrowseComp-Plus Benchmark])
    end
    style BenchmarkConstruction fill:#fffde7,stroke:#fff59d

    subgraph ExperimentSetup["Experiment Setup"]
        T --> U(LLMs with Retriever Tool) --> V(Apply BrowseComp Prompt)
        V --> W(Prompt Revisions: Custom Tool Use & Source Citation)
        W --> X(Retriever Configuration: Top k=5 Results, 512 Token Truncation)
        X --> Y{Ground-Truth Answer Present in Truncated Gold Documents?}
        Y -- Yes (86.5%) --> Z(Proceed to Evaluation)
        Y -- No --> AA(Analyze Impact of Truncation)
    end
    style ExperimentSetup fill:#e3f2fd,stroke:#bbdefb

    subgraph Evaluation["Evaluation"]
        Z --> AB(Deep Research Effectiveness Metrics)
        AB --> AC(Accuracy: LLM-as-Judge GPT-4.1) --> AD[/Accuracy Score/]
        AB --> AE(Recall: Human-Verified Evidence Documents) --> AF[/Recall Score/]
        AB --> AG(Search Calls: Avg. Search API Invocations) --> AH[/Search Calls Count/]
        AB --> AI(Calibration Error: Confidence vs. Accuracy) --> AJ[/Calibration Error Score/]
        Z --> AK(Retrieval Effectiveness Metrics)
        AK --> AL(TREC Practice: Create Query-Document Relevance Label File)
        AL --> AM(Compute Recall@k and nDCG@k) --> AN[/Recall@k & nDCG@k Scores/]
    end
    style Evaluation fill:#f3e5f5,stroke:#e1bee7

    T --> End([End])

    Start --> DataSourcing
    DataSourcing --> DataProcessing
    DataProcessing --> NegativeMining
    NegativeMining --> BenchmarkConstruction
    BenchmarkConstruction --> ExperimentSetup
    ExperimentSetup --> Evaluation
    Evaluation --> End

    subgraph Legend["Legend"]
        LegendStart([Start/End])
        LegendProcess(Process/Action)
        LegendDecision{Decision/Branching}
        LegendData[/Data Input/Output/]
        LegendStart -- Start/End Nodes --> LegendStartStyle
        LegendProcess -- Data Collection --> LegendDataCollectionStyle
        LegendProcess -- Processing/Filtering --> LegendProcessingFilteringStyle
        LegendProcess -- Annotation/Labeling --> LegendAnnotationLabelingStyle
        LegendProcess -- Quality Control --> LegendQualityControlStyle
        LegendProcess -- Evaluation/Metric --> LegendEvaluationMetricsStyle
        LegendData -- Output/Result --> LegendOutputResultStyle
    end
    style LegendStartStyle fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style LegendDataCollectionStyle fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style LegendProcessingFilteringStyle fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style LegendAnnotationLabelingStyle fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style LegendQualityControlStyle fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style LegendEvaluationMetricsStyle fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style LegendOutputResultStyle fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
flowchart_zh:
flowchart TD
    style Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    style OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([开始])

    subgraph DataSourcing["数据来源"]
        A[原始BrowseComp数据集 (1,266个问题)] --> B(使用GPT-5-Turbo生成搜索查询)
        B --> C[/Google搜索API/]
        C --> D(为每个查询检索前100个文档)

    end

    subgraph DataProcessing["数据处理"]
        E --> F(按URL去重文档) --> G[/唯一文档池 (~1.5M文档)/]

    end
    style DataProcessing fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#c8e6c9

    subgraph NegativeMining["负例挖掘"]

        M --> N{高相关性分数且非黄金文档?}
        N -- 是 --> O[/挑战性负例文档 (50,000)/]
        N -- 否 --> P(丢弃文档)
    end
    style NegativeMining fill:#fbe9e7,stroke:#ffccbc

    subgraph BenchmarkConstruction["基准构建"]
        J & O --> Q(结合黄金文档和挑战性负例文档)
        Q --> R[/最终精选语料库/]
        A --> S(整合原始1,266个问题)

    end
    style BenchmarkConstruction fill:#fffde7,stroke:#fff59d

    subgraph ExperimentSetup["实验设置"]
        T --> U(带有检索工具的LLM) --> V(应用BrowseComp提示)
        V --> W(提示修订: 自定义工具使用和来源引用)
        W --> X(检索器配置: 前k=5个结果, 512个Token截断)
        X --> Y{截断的黄金文档中是否存在真实答案?}
        Y -- 是 (86.5%) --> Z(继续评估)
        Y -- 否 --> AA(分析截断的影响)
    end
    style ExperimentSetup fill:#e3f2fd,stroke:#bbdefb

    subgraph Evaluation["评估"]
        Z --> AB(深度研究有效性指标)
        AB --> AC(准确率: LLM作为评判者GPT-4.1) --> AD[/准确率分数/]
        AB --> AE(召回率: 人工验证的证据文档) --> AF[/召回率分数/]
        AB --> AG(搜索调用: 平均搜索API调用次数) --> AH[/搜索调用次数/]

    end

name: C-Eval
intro: C-Eval是一个全面的中文评测套件，用于评估基础模型在中文环境下的高级知识和推理能力。它包含13948道覆盖52个学科和四个难度等级的多项选择题。C-Eval Hard是其子集，专注于需要高级推理能力的挑战性学科。
org: Shanghai Jiao Tong University, Tsinghua University, The Hong Kong University of Science and Technolo
eval_feature: 零样本, 少样本, 思维链
intro_en: C-Eval is a comprehensive Chinese evaluation suite designed to assess foundational models' advanced knowledge and reasoning abilities in Chinese contexts. It includes 13,948 multiple-choice questions across 52 subjects and four difficulty levels. C-Eval Hard is a subset focusing on challenging subjects that require advanced reasoning skills.

name: C^3-Benchmark
intro: C3-Bench是一个开源基准，用于评估基于大型语言模型的智能体在多任务场景中的鲁棒性。它涵盖复杂工具关系导航、关键信息隐藏处理和动态决策路径管理，旨在揭示模型漏洞并推动智能体性能可解释性研究。
eval_feature: 对抗性, 多轮, 领域特定
intro_en: C3-Bench is an open-source benchmark designed to evaluate the robustness of large language model-based agents in multi-task scenarios. It covers complex tool relationship navigation, key information hiding, and dynamic decision path management, aiming to expose model vulnerabilities and advance research on agent performance interpretability.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Real-world Agent Scenarios) --> B[Expert Task Design]
    B --> C{{LLM-based Scenario Generation}}
    C --> D{Scenario Complexity Check}
    D -->|Pass| E[Integrate Tool Relationships]
    D -->|Fail| F[Refine Task Parameters]
    F --> B
    E --> G[Hide Key Information]
    G --> H[Dynamic Decision Path Management]
    H --> I[Expert Review & Validation]
    I --> J(C^3-Benchmark Dataset)
    J --> K[Agent Robustness Evaluation]
    K --> L[Performance Analysis]
    L --> M(Evaluation Results)

name: CC-OCR
intro: CC-OCR基准旨在全面评估大型多模态模型在光学字符识别（OCR）方面的能力。它涵盖了多场景文本阅读、多语言文本阅读、文档解析和关键信息提取四个核心OCR任务。该基准包含39个子集，共7,058张完全标注的图像，其中41%来源于真实应用场景并首次发布。CC-OCR通过多样化的场景、任务和挑战，旨在推动LMM在OCR中心任务上的持续进步。
org: Alibaba Group, South China University of Technology
task_type: 图像问答, 文本识别, 信息抽取
task_type_en: Visual Question Answering, Text Recognition, Information Extraction
intro_en: The CC-OCR benchmark aims to comprehensively evaluate large multimodal models' capabilities in optical character recognition (OCR). It covers four core OCR tasks: multi-scene text reading, multilingual text reading, document parsing, and key information extraction. The benchmark includes 39 subsets with a total of 7,058 fully annotated images, 41% of which come from real-world applications and are newly released. By offering diverse scenarios, tasks, and challenges, CC-OCR seeks to drive continuous progress of LMMs in core OCR tasks.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Diverse Raw Data
(Public Datasets, Web Sources)) --> B[Data Collection & Aggregation]
    B --> C[Initial Data Preprocessing
(Filtering, Cleaning)]
    C --> D{{OCR Annotation Tools}}
    D --> E[OCR-specific Annotation
(Text Detection, Recognition, KIE)]
    E --> F{{Data Augmentation Techniques}}
    F --> G{Quality Control & Validation}
    G -->|Pass| H[Dataset Splitting
(39 Subsets)]
    G -->|Fail| I[Data Refinement]
    I --> E
    H --> J(Final CC-OCR Benchmark Dataset)
    J --> K[Model Evaluation
(Performance Metrics)]
    K --> L[Benchmark Results & Leaderboard]

name: CCBench
intro: CCBench是一个用于评估AI编程代理在真实世界任务中表现的基准测试，这些任务基于小型代码库（<10k LoC），且不属于大型语言模型训练数据的一部分。所有任务均来源于CodeCrafters用户的私有提交，CodeCrafters是一个工程师通过从零开始实现流行软件来参与挑战的平台。
build_method: 专家构建, 人工标注
intro_en: CCBench is a benchmark for evaluating AI programming agents on real-world tasks based on small codebases (<10k LoC) that are not part of large language model training data. All tasks come from private submissions by CodeCrafters users, a platform where engineers participate in challenges by implementing popular software from scratch.

flowchart LR
    A(CodeCrafters Users) --> B[Private Task Submissions]
    B --> C[Expert Construction]
    C --> D[Manual Annotation]
    D --> E{Annotation Review}
    E -- Pass --> F[Refined Tasks]
    E -- Fail --> G[Task Revision]
    G --> D
    F --> H(CCBench Dataset)
    H --> I(Small Codebases <10k LoC)
    H --> J[AI Agent Evaluation]
    J --> K(Real-world Tasks)
    J --> L(Performance Metrics)

name: CF-Div2-Stepfun
intro: CF-Div2-Stepfun 是一个评测大型语言模型竞赛编程能力的基准，选取2024年9月至2025年2月的CodeForces Division 2比赛题目以防止数据泄露。基准采用严格的离线沙箱评分，包含大量边界和压力测试，全面检验模型表现。
eval_feature: 防污染, 对抗性, 领域特定
intro_en: CF-Div2-Stepfun is a benchmark for evaluating large language models' coding abilities, using CodeForces Division 2 problems from September 2024 to February 2025 to prevent data leakage. It employs strict offline sandbox scoring and includes extensive boundary and stress tests to comprehensively assess model performance.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(CodeForces Div2 Problems
(Sep 2024 - Feb 2025)) --> B[Problem Selection & Extraction]
    B --> C[Transform to Exam Questions]
    C --> D[Design Boundary & Stress Tests]
    D --> E{{Expert Construction & Review}}
    E --> F{Quality Assurance Check}
    F -->|Pass| G(CF-Div2-Stepfun Benchmark
(Coding Problems & Tests))
    F -->|Fail| C
    G --> H[Offline Sandbox Scoring]
    H --> I[LLM Coding Ability Evaluation]

name: CFBench
intro: CFBench是一个针对大型语言模型（LLMs）的综合性约束遵循基准测试。它旨在评估LLMs理解和遵循自然语言指令中复杂约束的能力，弥补了现有评估在约束全面性和真实性方面的不足。该基准包含1,000个精心策划的样本，涵盖200多个真实场景和50多个NLP任务，并提出了一个创新的约束类型系统框架和多维度评估方法。
build_method: 专家构建, 人工标注
intro_en: CFBench is a comprehensive constraint-following benchmark for large language models (LLMs). It aims to evaluate LLMs' ability to understand and adhere to complex constraints in natural language instructions, addressing the limitations of existing evaluations in constraint comprehensiveness and realism. The benchmark includes 1,000 carefully curated samples covering over 200 real-world scenarios and more than 50 NLP tasks, and introduces an innovative constraint type system framework along with a multidimensional evaluation approach.

flowchart LR
    A(Existing Natural Language Scenarios) --> B[Expert Design & Construction]
    B --> C{Constraint Definition & Refinement}
    C --> D[Instruction Generation]
    D --> E[Manual Annotation of Expected Outputs]
    E --> F{{Expert Review & Validation}}
    F --> G(CFBench Dataset
Complex NL Instructions & Constraints)
    G --> H[LLM Evaluation]
    H --> I[Constraint Following Score]

name: CG-Bench
intro: CG-Bench是一个针对长视频理解中线索溯源问答的新型基准测试。它旨在解决现有基准主要关注短视频或依赖多项选择题的局限性，这些局限性可能导致模型在没有真正理解视频内容的情况下给出答案。CG-Bench强调模型检索相关线索的能力，包含1,219个手动策划的视频和12,129个问答对，涵盖感知、推理和幻觉三种主要问题类型。该基准还引入了线索溯源的白盒和黑盒评估方法，以全面评估模型对长视频的理解能力。
org: Nanjing University, Shanghai Artificial Intelligence Laboratory, Fudan University, Zhejiang Universi
intro_en: CG-Bench is a novel benchmark for clue tracing question answering in long video understanding. It addresses the limitations of existing benchmarks that mainly focus on short videos or rely on multiple-choice questions, which may lead models to answer without truly understanding the video content. CG-Bench emphasizes models' ability to retrieve relevant clues and includes 1,219 manually curated videos and 12,129 QA pairs, covering three main question types: perception, reasoning, and hallucination. The benchmark also introduces white-box and black-box clue tracing evaluation methods to comprehensively assess models' understanding of long videos.

name: ChartMimic
intro: ChartMimic是一个旨在评估大型多模态模型（LMM）视觉接地代码生成能力的基准。它利用信息丰富的可视化图表和文本指令作为输入，要求LMM生成相应的图表渲染代码。ChartMimic包含4,800个人工整理的三元组（图表、指令、代码），代表了科学论文中真实的图表使用案例，涵盖18种常规类型和4种高级类型，细分为201个子类别。该基准强调评估LMM协调视觉理解、代码生成和跨模态推理等多种认知能力的能力。
org: Tsinghua University, Beijing Institute of General Artificial Intelligence (BIGAI), Beijing Academy o
intro_en: ChartMimic is a benchmark designed to evaluate the visual grounding code generation capabilities of large multimodal models (LMMs). It uses information-rich visual charts and textual instructions as input, requiring LMMs to generate corresponding chart rendering code. ChartMimic contains 4,800 manually curated triplets (chart, instruction, code) representing real chart use cases from scientific papers, covering 18 common types and 4 advanced types, subdivided into 201 subcategories. This benchmark emphasizes assessing LMMs' abilities to coordinate visual understanding, code generation, and cross-modal reasoning.

(4,800 Samples))
    I --> J[LMM Evaluation]
    J --> K(Evaluation Results)

name: ChartQA
intro: ChartQA是一个大规模基准测试，旨在评估模型对图表进行视觉和逻辑推理的能力。它包含9.6K个人工编写的问题以及23.1K个从人工编写的图表摘要中生成的问题。该基准测试通过结合视觉特征和图表数据表来回答问题，以解决视觉和逻辑推理的独特挑战。
org: York University, Nanyang Technological University, Salesforce Research
build_method: 人工标注, 自动生成
task_type: 开放式问答, 图像问答
eval_feature: 视觉推理, 逻辑推理
intro_en: ChartQA is a large-scale benchmark designed to evaluate models' abilities in visual and logical reasoning over charts. It includes 9.6K human-written questions and 23.1K questions generated from human-written chart summaries. This benchmark addresses unique challenges in visual and logical reasoning by combining visual features with chart data tables to answer questions.

        AA(生成基准报告):::Output_Result
        BB([结束]):::Start_End
        W --> X
        X --> Y
        Y --> Z
        Z --> AA
        AA --> BB
    end

name: CharXiv
intro: 图表理解在多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）应用于分析科学论文或财务报告等实际任务中扮演着关键角色。现有数据集常侧重于过度简化和同质化的图表，导致对模型进展的评估过于乐观。CharXiv是一个全面的评估套件，包含2,323个来自arXiv论文的自然、具有挑战性和多样化的图表。它包括描述性问题和推理问题，所有图表和问题均由人类专家精心挑选、整理和验证。
org: Princeton Language and Intelligence (PLI), Princeton University, University of Wisconsin, Madison, T
intro_en: Chart understanding plays a crucial role in applying multimodal large language models (MLLMs) to real-world tasks such as analyzing scientific papers or financial reports. Existing datasets often focus on overly simplified and homogeneous charts, leading to overly optimistic assessments of model progress. CharXiv is a comprehensive evaluation suite containing 2,323 natural, challenging, and diverse charts sourced from arXiv papers. It includes both descriptive and reasoning questions, with all charts and questions carefully selected, curated, and validated by human experts.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Diverse Chart Sources) --> B[Expert Identification & Selection]
    B --> C{Chart Complexity Filter}
    C -- Pass --> D[Expert Annotation & Question Generation]
    C -- Fail --> E[Discard Simplified Charts]
    D --> F{{Annotation Guidelines & Tools}}
    F --> G[Expert Review & Validation]
    G --> H(CharXiv Dataset\nComplex Charts for MLLMs)
    H --> I[MLLM Evaluation on Chart Understanding]

flowchart TD

    subgraph DataSourcing["Data Sourcing"]

        D(Select Charts for CharXiv)
        A --> B
        B --> C
        C -- Yes --> D
        C -- No --> B
    end
    style DataSourcing fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    subgraph QuestionDesign["Question Design"]
        E(Design Two Types of Questions)
        F{Question Type?}
        G(Descriptive Questions: Basic Chart Elements)
        H(Reasoning Questions: Complex Visual Elements)
        E --> F
        F -- Basic Info --> G
        F -- Complex Analysis --> H
    end
    style QuestionDesign fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff

    subgraph AnnotationAndCuration["Annotation & Curation"]
        I(Human Experts Curate Questions & Answers)
        J(Human Experts Verify Ground-Truth Answers)
        K{Are Answers Validated?}
        L(Finalize Curated QA Pairs)
        I --> J
        J --> K
        K -- Yes --> L
        K -- No --> I
    end
    style AnnotationAndCuration fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff

    subgraph DatasetSplit["Dataset Split"]
        M[/2,323 Curated Charts & Questions/]
        N(Randomly Sample 1,000 Charts)
        O[/Validation Set (1,000 Charts)/]
        P[/Test Set (Remaining 1,323 Charts)/]
        M --> N
        N --> O
        N --> P
    end
    style DatasetSplit fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    subgraph Evaluation["Evaluation"]
        Q(Evaluate MLLMs on CharXiv)
        R(LLM-based Automatic Grading)
        S(Measure Human Performance: 80.5% Reasoning, 92.1% Descriptive)
        T[/Evaluation Results (Accuracy: GPT-4o 47.1%, InternVL 29.2%)/]
        Q --> R
        Q --> S
        R --> T
        S --> T
    end
    style Evaluation fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff

    end_node([End])
    style end_node fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    QuestionDesign --> AnnotationAndCuration
    AnnotationAndCuration --> DatasetSplit
