name: CheXBench
intro: CheXBench是用于评估胸部X光图像理解的综合基准，涵盖图像感知、图像-文本推理和文本生成等八种不同任务类型，旨在推动胸部X光AI模型的开发和临床应用。
org: Stanford Center for Artificial Intelligence in Medicine and Imaging
build_method: 人工标注, 自动生成
task_type: 多选题, 问答, 文本生成
eval_feature: 胸部X光图像理解, 图像-文本推理, 文本生成，八种任务类型
eval_feature_en: chest X-ray image understanding, image-text reasoning, text generation, eight task types
intro_en: CheXBench is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating chest X-ray image understanding, covering eight different task types including image perception, image-text reasoning, and text generation. It aims to advance the development and clinical application of chest X-ray AI models.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Medical Image Databases) --> B[Image Acquisition]
    B --> C{Data Access Approval}
    C -- Approved --> D[Image Preprocessing]
    C -- Rejected --> M[Discard Data]
    D --> E{{Anonymization & Normalization Tools}}
    E --> F[Expert Review & Selection]
    F --> G[Manual Annotation]
    G --> H{Annotation Consistency Check}
    H -- Pass --> I(Curated CheXBench Dataset)
    H -- Fail --> G
    I --> J[Vision-Language Model Evaluation]
    J --> K(Evaluation Results)
    K --> L[Benchmark Reporting]

name: CheXGenBench
intro: CheXGenBench是一个统一的评估框架，旨在全面评估合成胸部X光图像的保真度、隐私性和下游实用性，通过20多种定量指标标准化评估文本到图像生成模型。
org: The University of Edinburgh, Samsung AI Center
build_method: 基于现有数据集和人工标注
eval_feature: 评估合成胸部X光图像的保真度, 隐私性和下游实用性
build_method_en: based on existing datasets and manual annotation
eval_feature_en: evaluate fidelity, privacy, and downstream utility of synthetic chest X-ray images
intro_en: CheXGenBench is a unified evaluation framework designed to comprehensively assess the fidelity, privacy, and downstream utility of synthetic chest X-ray images, standardizing the evaluation of text-to-image generation models through over 20 quantitative metrics.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Existing Chest X-ray Datasets) --> B[Data Preprocessing]
    B --> C{{Image Generation Models}}
    C --> D(Generated Chest X-ray Images)
    D --> E[Expert Evaluation]
    E --> F{Quality & Clinical Relevance Check}
    F -- Pass --> G(High-Quality Generated Images)
    F -- Fail --> H[Discard/Iterate]
    G --> I(CheXGenBench Final Dataset)
    I --> J[Model Evaluation Framework]
    J --> K[Assess Image Quality]
    J --> L[Assess Clinical Relevance]
    K --> M(Evaluation Metrics)
    L --> M
    M --> N[Benchmark Results]

        E{Model Size?}

        G(LoRA with rank 32 for models > 1B parameters)

    end

        J[/SynthCheX-75K Dataset (75K images)/]

            Q(Radiology Report Generation)
        end
    end

    subgraph Phase["Benchmarking and Output"]

        S([End])
    end

name: Chinese SimpleQA
intro: Chinese SimpleQA是首个全面的中文基准，旨在评估大型语言模型回答简短问题的能力。该基准涵盖6个主要主题和99个子主题，并通过严格的质量控制流程确保问题和答案的高质量。其特点包括中文、多样性、高质量、静态和易于评估，可用于指导开发者理解模型在中文事实性方面的表现。
org: LivingFutureLab, Tsinghua University, Beijing Institute of Technology, Shanghai Jiao Tong University
eval_feature: 动态更新, 防污染, 对抗性, 思维链, 零样本
eval_feature_en: Dynamic Update, Contamination-free, Adversarial, Chain-of-Thought, Zero-shot
intro_en: Chinese SimpleQA is the first comprehensive Chinese benchmark designed to evaluate large language models' ability to answer short questions. It covers 6 main topics and 99 subtopics, with strict quality control ensuring high-quality questions and answers. Features include Chinese language, diversity, high quality, static nature, and ease of evaluation, making it useful for developers to understand model performance on Chinese factuality.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A((Knowledge Sources)) --> B[Topic & Subtopic Structuring]
    B --> C[Manual Question Creation]
    C --> D[Manual Answer Creation]
    D --> E{Initial QA Pair Validation}
    E -- Valid --> F[Quality Control & Refinement]
    E -- Invalid --> G[Discard/Revise]
    F --> H{{Hybrid Annotation Method}}
    H --> I(Chinese SimpleQA Dataset)
    I --> J[LLM Evaluation]
    J --> K[Performance Analysis]

name: CiteBench
intro: CiteBench是一个用于科学引文文本生成的基准测试，旨在系统地研究从多个来源聚合和综合文本知识。该基准统一了多个不同的数据集，支持在不同的任务设计和领域之间对引文生成模型进行标准化评估。CiteBench的发布为引文生成领域的未来研究提供了新的见解和评估方法。
org: Ubiquitous Knowledge Processing (UKP) Lab, Technical University of Darmstadt
build_method: 混合（整合多个现有数据集）
intro_en: CiteBench is a benchmark for scientific citation text generation, designed to systematically study the aggregation and synthesis of textual knowledge from multiple sources. It unifies various datasets to enable standardized evaluation of citation generation models across different task designs and domains. The release of CiteBench offers new insights and evaluation methods for future research in citation generation.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Multiple Existing Scientific Datasets) --> B[Aggregate Datasets]
    B --> C[Unify & Standardize Data]
    C --> D[Extract Citation Text]
    D --> E[Clean & Filter Data]
    E --> F{Data Quality Check}
    F -- Pass --> G[Expert Review]
    F -- Fail --> E
    G --> H(Unified CiteBench Dataset)
    H --> I[Train Citation Generation Models]
    I --> J[Standardized Model Evaluation]
    J --> K[Performance Analysis & Reporting]

name: CiteEval
intro: CiteEval是一个基于原则的引用评估框架，旨在对生成文本中的引用质量进行细粒度评估。该框架不仅考虑被引用的来源，还涵盖了检索上下文、用户查询和生成的文本。基于此框架，研究人员构建了CiteBench，一个带有人工高质量标注的多领域基准。为了实现高效评估，还开发了与人类判断高度相关的模型指标套件CiteEval-Auto。
build_method: 人工标注, 专家构建
metric: Context Attribution, Citation Editing, Citation Rating, Correlation with human judgment
intro_en: CiteEval is a principle-based citation evaluation framework designed for fine-grained assessment of citation quality in generated text. The framework considers not only the cited sources but also the retrieval context, user queries, and the generated text. Based on this framework, researchers developed CiteBench, a multi-domain benchmark with high-quality human annotations. To enable efficient evaluation, they also created CiteEval-Auto, a suite of model metrics highly correlated with human judgments.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A((Generated Text)) --> E[Data Collection]
    B((Cited Sources)) --> E
    C((Retrieval Context)) --> E
    D((User Queries)) --> E
    E --> F{{CiteEval Framework Principles}}
    F --> G[Manual Annotation]
    G --> H[Expert Construction]
    H --> I{Annotation Quality Check}
    I -- Pass --> J(Fine-grained Citation Assessments)
    I -- Fail --> G
    J --> K[CiteEval Dataset]
    K --> L[Model Evaluation]
    L --> M[Citation Quality Assessment Results]
metric_en: Context Attribution, Citation Editing, Citation Rating, Correlation with human judgment
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Framework Design
        A([Start])
        A -->|Initiate Project| B(Define CiteEval Principles)
        B -->|Guide by Principles| C(Identify Key Citation Quality Factors)
        C -->|Consider| D[/Input: Cited Sources, Retrieval Context, User Query, Generated Text/]
    end

    subgraph Benchmark Construction (CiteBench)
        D -->|Inform Data Collection| E(Collect Multi-Domain Data)
        E -->|Process Data| F(Perform Human Annotation on Citation Quality)
        F -->|Review| G{Human Annotation Quality Check?}
        G -->|No| H(Revise Annotations)
        H -->|Iterate| F
        G -->|Yes| I(Finalize High-Quality Human Annotations)
        I -->|Result| J([CiteBench Benchmark])
    end

    subgraph Automated Metric Development (CiteEval-Auto)
        J -->|Utilize Benchmark| K(Develop Model-Based Metrics)
        K -->|Train Models| L(Train CiteEval-Auto Models)
        L -->|Evaluate Against| M(Correlate with Human Judgments)
        M -->|Assess| N{Strong Correlation Achieved?}
        N -->|No| O(Refine Model-Based Metrics)
        O -->|Iterate| L
        N -->|Yes| P([CiteEval-Auto Metrics Suite])
    end

    subgraph Evaluation and Validation
        P -->|Apply Metrics| Q(Conduct Experiments Across Diverse Systems)
        Q -->|Analyze Results| R(Compare CiteEval-Auto with Existing Metrics)
        R -->|Deep Dive| S(Analyze Multifaceted Nature of Citations)
        S -->|Finalize| T([Final CiteEval Framework])
    end

    T -->|Project Complete| U([End])

flowchart TD
    subgraph 框架设计
        A([开始])
        A -->|启动项目| B(定义CiteEval原则)
        B -->|原则指导| C(识别关键引用质量因素)
        C -->|考虑| D[/输入：引用的来源、检索上下文、用户查询、生成文本/]
    end

    subgraph 基准构建 (CiteBench)
        D -->|指导数据收集| E(收集多领域数据)
        E -->|处理数据| F(对引用质量进行人工标注)
        F -->|审查| G{人工标注质量检查？}
        G -->|否| H(修订标注)
        H -->|迭代| F
        G -->|是| I(最终确定高质量人工标注)
        I -->|结果| J([CiteBench基准])
    end

    subgraph 自动化指标开发 (CiteEval-Auto)
        J -->|利用基准| K(开发基于模型的指标)
        K -->|训练模型| L(训练CiteEval-Auto模型)
        L -->|对照评估| M(与人工判断相关联)
        M -->|评估| N{达到强相关性？}
        N -->|否| O(完善基于模型的指标)
        O -->|迭代| L
        N -->|是| P([CiteEval-Auto指标套件])
    end

    subgraph 评估与验证
        P -->|应用指标| Q(在不同系统上进行实验)
        Q -->|分析结果| R(将CiteEval-Auto与现有指标进行比较)
        R -->|深入探究| S(分析引用的多面性)
        S -->|最终确定| T([最终CiteEval框架])
    end

    T -->|项目完成| U([结束])

name: CL-bench
intro: CL-bench是一个用于评估大型语言模型上下文学习能力的基准。该基准包含500个复杂上下文和1899个任务，要求模型从特定上下文中学习新知识并进行推理，而非简单地依赖预训练知识。CL-bench旨在推动模型发展超越简单检索或阅读理解的复杂推理能力，以应对现实世界中复杂的、依赖上下文的任务。
eval_feature: 长文本, 防污染, 领域特定
intro_en: CL-bench is a benchmark for evaluating large language models' in-context learning abilities. It includes 500 complex contexts and 1899 tasks, requiring models to learn new knowledge and perform reasoning from specific contexts rather than relying solely on pre-trained knowledge. CL-bench aims to advance models beyond simple retrieval or reading comprehension toward complex reasoning needed for real-world, context-dependent tasks.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A[Knowledge Base/Real-world Scenarios] --> B(Expert Selection of Relevant Information)
    B --> C[Context Design: Crafting 500 Complex Contexts]
    C --> D[Task Generation: Creating 1899 In-Context Learning Tasks]
    D --> E[Instruction Formulation: Defining Model Prompts]
    E --> F{Expert Review & Validation}
    F -->|Pass| G(Pilot Testing with Sample Models)
    F -->|Fail| C
    G --> H{Refinement Based on Pilot Results}
    H -->|Needs Refinement| C
    H -->|Approved| I(Final CL-bench Dataset
500 Contexts, 1899 Tasks)
    I --> J[Model Evaluation: In-Context Learning Abilities]
    J --> K[Performance Analysis & Reporting]

name: ClemBench
intro: ClemBench是一个基于大型语言模型自我对弈对话游戏的基准，用于评估模型的指令遵循、策略目标和语言理解能力。该框架能够适应新的发展，避免数据污染，且测试尚未饱和，为研究提示语言对性能的影响等问题提供了平台。
org: University of Potsdam, Heinrich Heine University Düsseldorf, MBZUAI
intro_en: ClemBench is a benchmark based on self-play dialogue games using large language models, designed to evaluate models' instruction following, strategic goals, and language understanding abilities. The framework adapts to new developments, prevents data contamination, and remains unsaturated, providing a platform for studying the impact of prompt language on performance.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Initial Dialogue Game Concepts) --> B[Expert Design of Game Rules & Scenarios]
    B --> C{{Large Language Models (LLMs)}}
    C --> D[LLM Self-Play Dialogue Generation]
    D --> E(Raw Dialogue Data)
    E --> F[Expert Review & Refinement]
    F --> G{Data Contamination Check}
    G -->|Pass| H(ClemBench Dialogue Dataset)
    G -->|Fail| I[Discard & Regenerate]
    H --> J[Model Evaluation: Instruction Following, Strategy, Language Understanding]
    J --> K(Evaluation Results & Metrics)

name: ClinicalBench
intro: ClinicalBench是基于MIMIC-III和MIMIC-IV真实临床数据的预测建模评测基准，全面比较通用和医疗大型语言模型与传统机器学习模型在临床预测任务上的表现，涵盖死亡率预测、再入院预测等关键任务。
org: Illinois Institute of Technology, Harvard Medical School, Weill Cornell Medicine
eval_feature: LLM与传统ML模型头对头比较，基于真实临床数据
build_method_en: Based on real clinical data (MIMIC-III and MIMIC-IV)
eval_feature_en: Head-to-head comparison between LLM and traditional ML models, based on real clinical data
intro_en: ClinicalBench is a predictive modeling benchmark based on real clinical data from MIMIC-III and MIMIC-IV. It comprehensively compares the performance of general and medical large language models with traditional machine learning models on clinical prediction tasks, including key tasks such as mortality prediction and readmission prediction.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(MIMIC-III & MIMIC-IV Databases) --> B[Data Extraction]
    B --> C[Data Cleaning & Preprocessing]
    C --> D[Anonymization]
    D --> E[Feature Engineering]
    E --> F{Clinical Expert Review}
    F --> G[Dataset Splitting]
    G --> H(ClinicalBench Dataset)
    H --> I[LLM Evaluation]
    H --> J[Traditional ML Model Evaluation]
    I --> K[Performance Comparison]
    J --> K
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    style StartEnd fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    style QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style EvaluationMetrics fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    subgraph A["Benchmark Initialization"]
        S([Start]):::StartEnd
        S --> A1(Define Clinical Prediction Tasks):::ProcessingFiltering
        A1 --> A2[/Length-of-Stay, Mortality, Readmission/]
        A2 --> A3(Identify Prediction Features):::ProcessingFiltering
        A3 --> A4[/Demographics, Diagnosis, Procedures, Medications/]
    end

    subgraph B["Data Sourcing & Collection"]
        B1(Access Clinical Databases):::DataCollection
        B1 --> B2[/MIMIC-III v1.4 & MIMIC-IV v2.2/]
        B2 --> B3(Extract Raw Patient Data):::DataCollection
    end

    subgraph C["Data Preprocessing & Filtering"]
        C1(Filter First-Time Visits):::ProcessingFiltering
        B3 --> C1
        C1 --> C2{Is patient first-time visit?}
        C2 -- Yes --> C3(Filter Age < 18):::ProcessingFiltering
        C2 -- No --> C_Discard(Discard Data)
        C3 --> C4{Is patient age >= 18?}
        C4 -- Yes --> C5(Filter Missing Values):::ProcessingFiltering
        C4 -- No --> C_Discard
        C5 --> C6{Are there missing values?}
        C6 -- No --> C7[/Cleaned Patient Data/]
        C6 -- Yes --> C_Discard
        C_Discard --> E([End]):::StartEnd
    end

    subgraph D["Data Conversion & Division"]
        C7 --> D1(Convert Clinical Codes to Text):::AnnotationLabeling
        D1 --> D2[/ICD-9/10, ATC Codes to Text/]
        D2 --> D3(Design LLM-Specific Prompts):::AnnotationLabeling
        D3 --> D4{Traditional ML or LLM Method?}
        D4 -- Traditional ML --> D5(Stratified Split):::ProcessingFiltering
        D5 --> D6(Apply Under-sampling for Training Set):::ProcessingFiltering
        D6 --> D7[/Train, Validation, Test Sets (Traditional ML)/]
        D4 -- LLM --> D8(Directly Assess on Test Set):::ProcessingFiltering
        D8 --> D9[/Test Set (LLM Prompting)/]
        D4 -- LLM Fine-tuning --> D10(Stratified Split & Under-sampling):::ProcessingFiltering
        D10 --> D11[/Train, Validation, Test Sets (LLM Fine-tuning)/]
    end

    subgraph E["Model Benchmarking"]
        D7 --> E1(Train Traditional ML Models):::ProcessingFiltering
        E1 --> E2[/XGBoost, LR, DT, RF, AdaBoost, SVM, NB, MLP, Transformer, RNN/]
        D9 --> E3(Prompt General-purpose LLMs):::ProcessingFiltering
        E3 --> E4[/14 General-purpose LLMs/]
        D9 --> E5(Prompt Medical LLMs):::ProcessingFiltering
        E5 --> E6[/8 Medical LLMs/]
        D11 --> E7(Fine-tune LLMs):::ProcessingFiltering
        E7 --> E8[/LLMs with 2 Fine-tuning Strategies/]
    end

    subgraph F["Evaluation & Results"]
        E2 --> F1(Evaluate Traditional ML Performance):::EvaluationMetrics
        E4 --> F2(Evaluate General-purpose LLM Performance):::EvaluationMetrics
        E6 --> F3(Evaluate Medical LLM Performance):::EvaluationMetrics
        E8 --> F4(Evaluate Fine-tuned LLM Performance):::EvaluationMetrics

        F5 --> F6[/Macro F1%, AUROC% Results/]
        F6 --> F7(Analyze Findings & Conclusions):::EvaluationMetrics
    end

    F7 --> End([End]):::StartEnd

name: ClinicBench
intro: ClinicBench是牛津大学和哈佛大学联合发布的临床场景大模型评测基准，涵盖临床语言生成、理解和推理等11个任务，引入了六个新数据集和复杂临床任务，全面评估LLM的临床能力。
org: University of Oxford, Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health
build_method: 结合现有数据集并构建六个新数据集，GPT-4生成并专家评审
task_type: 多选题, 问答, 摘要, 实体识别, 关系抽取, 分类
eval_feature: 评估LLM在复杂临床问题解决, 临床语言理解, 生成和推理方面的能力
task_type_en: Multiple choice, Q&A, Summarization, Named Entity Recognition, Relation Extraction, Classification
build_method_en: Combining existing datasets and constructing six new datasets, GPT-4 generated and expert reviewed
eval_feature_en: Evaluating LLM capabilities in complex clinical problem solving, clinical language understanding, generation, and reasoning
intro_en: ClinicBench, jointly released by Oxford University and Harvard University, is a clinical scenario large model evaluation benchmark covering 11 tasks including clinical language generation, understanding, and reasoning. It introduces six new datasets and complex clinical tasks to comprehensively assess the clinical capabilities of LLMs.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Medical Literature & Clinical Cases) --> B[Expert Identification & Curation]
    B --> C[Scenario Structuring]
    C --> D[Task Definition]
    D --> E[Ground Truth Generation]
    E --> F{Expert Review & Validation}
    F -->|Approved| G(ClinicBench Dataset\nClinical Scenarios)
    F -->|Rejected| B
    G --> H[LLM Evaluation]
    H --> I[Performance Analysis]
    I --> J(Benchmark Leaderboard)

name: CMATH
intro: CMATH是一个包含1.7k小学数学应用题的数据集，题目来源于真实的中文练习册和考试。该数据集旨在评估大型语言模型（LLMs）的小学数学能力，并发现只有GPT-4在所有年级中表现良好。此外，CMATH还评估了LLMs对干扰信息的鲁棒性，发现GPT-4能保持鲁棒性。
intro_en: CMATH is a dataset containing 1.7k elementary school math word problems sourced from real Chinese workbooks and exams. It aims to evaluate the elementary math abilities of large language models (LLMs), with only GPT-4 performing well across all grade levels. Additionally, CMATH assesses LLMs' robustness to distracting information, finding that GPT-4 maintains robustness.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Real Chinese Workbooks & Exams) --> B[Problem Identification]
    B --> C[Data Extraction & Digitization]
    C --> D{{Expert Construction Method}}
    D --> E[Problem Selection & Filtering]
    E --> F[Content Standardization]
    F --> G{Expert Quality Review}
    G -- Pass --> H[Problem Refinement]
    G -- Fail --> I[Discard/Revisit]
    H --> J(CMATH Dataset\n1.7k Math Problems)
    J --> K[LLM Evaluation Setup]
    K --> L[Model Performance Testing]
    L --> M[Result Analysis & Reporting]

name: CMB
intro: CMB (A Comprehensive Medical Benchmark in Chinese) 是一个完全基于中文语言文化背景设计的综合性、多层次的医学基准。它旨在解决直接翻译英文医学评测所带来的文化和语境不符问题，全面评估大型语言模型在中文医疗环境下的知识水平和临床咨询能力。该基准不仅包含中医，还覆盖了所有临床医学专业和不同专业级别。
build_method: 考试题, 专家构建, 人工标注
task_type: 选择题, 开放式问答
eval_feature: 领域特定, 零样本, 少样本, 思维链
scale: 280,839道选择题和74个复杂病例咨询问题
build_method_en: Exam questions, Expert Curation, Human Annotation
intro_en: CMB (A Comprehensive Medical Benchmark in Chinese) is a comprehensive, multi-level medical benchmark designed entirely based on Chinese language and cultural context. It aims to address the cultural and contextual mismatches caused by directly translating English medical evaluations, providing a thorough assessment of large language models' knowledge and clinical consultation abilities in the Chinese medical environment. This benchmark includes traditional Chinese medicine and covers all clinical medical specialties and various professional levels.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Chinese Medical Knowledge Base) --> B[Collect Medical Exam Questions]
    B --> C{Initial Data Filtering}
    C -- Pass --> D[Expert-led Content Construction]
    C -- Fail --> J[Discard Irrelevant Data]
    D --> E[Manual Annotation & Labeling]
    E --> F[Multi-level Structure Design]
    F --> G[Expert Review & Validation]
    G --> H(CMB Comprehensive Medical Benchmark)
    H --> I[Model Evaluation]
    I --> K(Medical Model Performance)
scale_en: 280,839 multiple choice questions and 74 items complex case consultation questions

name: CMExam
intro: CMExam是一个来源于中国国家执业医师资格考试的综合性数据集，旨在评估大型语言模型在医学领域的表现。它包含6万多个选择题，用于标准化和客观评估，并提供解决方案解释以进行开放式推理评估。该数据集还包含由医学专业人士标注的疾病组、临床科室、医学学科、能力领域和问题难度级别等五种额外的问答注释。CMExam是第一个提供全面医学注释的中文医学考试数据集。
org: Shanghai Jiao Tong University, Nankai University, Harvard Business School, Massachusetts Institute o
task_type: 选择题, 开放式问答
intro_en: CMExam is a comprehensive dataset derived from the National Medical Licensing Examination of China, designed to evaluate the performance of large language models in the medical field. It contains over 60,000 multiple-choice questions for standardized and objective assessment, along with solution explanations for open-ended reasoning evaluation. The dataset also includes five additional QA annotations labeled by medical professionals: disease groups, clinical departments, medical disciplines, competency areas, and question difficulty levels. CMExam is the first Chinese medical exam dataset to provide comprehensive medical annotations.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A((National Medical Licensing Examination of China)) --> B[Extract Raw Exam Content]
    B --> C[Parse & Structure Questions]
    C --> D[Standardize Question Format]
    D --> E[Add Solution Explanations]
    E --> F{{Hybrid Construction Method}}
    F --> G{Review & Validate Content}
    G -->|Pass| H[Refine & Finalize Dataset]
    G -->|Fail| B
    H --> I((CMExam Dataset\n60K+ MCQs))
    I --> J[LLM Evaluation Setup]
    J --> K[Model Answering Questions]
    K --> L[Compare Answers with Solutions]
    L --> M[Assess LLM Medical Performance]
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Data Sourcing
        A([Start]) --> B[/Chinese National Medical Licensing Examination/]
        B --> C(Identify relevant exam questions)
    end

    subgraph Data Collection
        C --> D[/Raw Exam Questions (60K+ MCQs)/]
        D --> E(Extract multiple-choice questions)
        E --> F(Extract solution explanations)
    end

    subgraph Data Processing & Filtering
        F --> G{Is question valid and complete?}
        G -- Yes --> H(Standardize question format)
        G -- No --> I(Discard invalid/incomplete questions)
        H --> J{Is explanation clear and concise?}
        J -- Yes --> K(Integrate questions and explanations)
        J -- No --> L(Refine explanation for clarity)
        L --> K
    end

    subgraph Annotation
        K --> M[/Cleaned Questions & Explanations/]
        M --> N(Invite medical professionals)
        N --> O(Label disease groups)
        O --> P(Label clinical departments)
        P --> Q(Label medical disciplines)
        Q --> R(Label areas of competency)
        R --> S(Label question difficulty levels)
        S --> T[/Annotated Dataset/]
    end

    subgraph Quality Control
        T --> U{Are annotations consistent and accurate?}
        U -- Yes --> V(Perform inter-annotator agreement checks)
        U -- No --> W(Review and correct inconsistent annotations)
        W --> U
        V --> X(Finalize annotated dataset)
    end

    subgraph Evaluation & Validation
        X --> Y[/Final CMExam Dataset/]
        Y --> Z(Select representative LLMs and QA algorithms)
        Z --> AA(Conduct experiments on CMExam)
        AA --> BB(Measure accuracy and weighted F1 score)
        BB --> CC{Is LLM performance satisfactory?}
        CC -- No --> DD(Analyze performance gaps)
        CC -- Yes --> EE(Compare with human accuracy: 71.6%)
    end

    subgraph Output
        EE --> FF[/Evaluation Results & Insights/]
        FF --> GG([End])
    end

name: CMMLU
intro: CMMLU是一个综合性的中文评估基准，专门用于评估语言模型在中文语境下的知识和推理能力。它涵盖了从基础学科到高级专业水平的67个主题，包含自然科学、社会科学、工程、人文以及中国特定的文化内容。该基准旨在全面衡量模型在中文环境下的高级能力。
org: MBZUAI, LibrAI, Shanghai Jiao Tong University, Microsoft Research Asia, The University of Melbourne
build_method: 从考试题等来源收集，经人工整理
eval_feature: 零样本, 少样本, 思维链
build_method_en: Collected from exam questions and other sources, manually curated
intro_en: CMMLU is a comprehensive Chinese evaluation benchmark designed to assess language models' knowledge and reasoning abilities in Chinese contexts. It covers 67 subjects ranging from basic disciplines to advanced professional levels, including natural sciences, social sciences, engineering, humanities, and China-specific cultural content. The benchmark aims to thoroughly measure models' advanced capabilities in Chinese environments.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A[Diverse Chinese Sources] --> B[Initial Data Collection]
    B --> C[Manual Organization & Filtering]
    C --> D[Data Cleaning & Formatting]
    D --> E[Subject Categorization (67 Subjects)]
    E --> F{Expert Review & Validation}
    F -->|Pass| G(CMMLU Final Dataset)
    G --> H[Language Model Evaluation]
    H --> I[Knowledge & Reasoning Assessment]
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    style Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    style EvaluationMetrics fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([Start]) --> DataSourcing(Identify Diverse Knowledge Domains)

    subgraph Data Acquisition["Data Acquisition"]
        DataSourcing --> DataSources[/Freely Available Sources (e.g., USMLE, OUP Books)/]
        DataSources --> ManualCollection(Manually Collect Questions)
        ManualCollection --> TotalQuestions[/15908 Questions Collected/]
        TotalQuestions --> TaskCategorization(Categorize into 57 Tasks)
        TaskCategorization --> DifficultyAssignment(Assign Difficulty Levels: Elementary to Professional)
        DifficultyAssignment --> QuestionFormatting(Format Questions as Multiple Choice)
        QuestionFormatting --> MathExpressionHandling(Encode Math Expressions with LaTeX/Symbols)
    end

    subgraph Data Preparation["Data Preparation"]
        QuestionFormatting --> DataSplit(Split Dataset)
        DataSplit --> FewShotDev[/5 Questions per Subject/]
        DataSplit --> ValidationSet[/1540 Questions for Hyperparameter Tuning/]
        DataSplit --> TestSet[/14079 Questions, Min 100 per Subject/]
    end

    subgraph Benchmark Evaluation["Benchmark Evaluation"]
        TestSet --> ModelEvaluation(Evaluate Models: Zero-shot & Few-shot Settings)
        ModelEvaluation --> AccuracyCalculation(Compute Classification Accuracy)
        AccuracyCalculation --> PerformanceAnalysis(Analyze Model Performance Across Tasks)
        PerformanceAnalysis --> IdentifyShortcomings(Identify Model Blind Spots & Lopsided Performance)
        IdentifyShortcomings --> CalibrationCheck(Check Model Calibration: Confidence vs. Accuracy)
        CalibrationCheck --> HumanPerformanceComparison(Compare to Human Performance: Unspecialized 34.5%, Expert ~89.8%)
    end

    HumanPerformanceComparison --> End([End])

    style Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    Start([开始]) --> DataSourcing(确定多样化知识领域)

        DataSourcing --> DataSources[/免费可用资源 (例如: USMLE, OUP书籍)/]
        DataSources --> ManualCollection(手动收集问题)
        ManualCollection --> TotalQuestions[/收集15908个问题/]
        TotalQuestions --> TaskCategorization(分类为57个任务)
        TaskCategorization --> DifficultyAssignment(分配难度级别: 初级到专业级)
        DifficultyAssignment --> QuestionFormatting(将问题格式化为多项选择题)
        QuestionFormatting --> MathExpressionHandling(使用LaTeX/符号编码数学表达式)
    end

    end

name: CMMMU
intro: CMMMU是面向中文环境的大规模多学科多模态理解基准，评估大型多模态模型在需要大学水平学科知识和推理的任务表现。包含来自六大核心学科、30个专业和39种图像类型的12000个手工收集多模态问题，旨在推动下一代专家级多模态AI发展。
org: 01.AI, The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology
build_method: 人工标注, 专家构建
modality: 文本, 图像, 多模态
task_type: 选择题, 开放式问答
intro_en: CMMMU is a large-scale multidisciplinary multimodal understanding benchmark for the Chinese context, evaluating the performance of large multimodal models on tasks requiring university-level disciplinary knowledge and reasoning. It includes 12,000 manually collected multimodal questions from six core disciplines, 30 specialties, and 39 image types, aiming to advance the development of next-generation expert-level multimodal AI.

    F --> G{Quality Check}
    G -- Pass --> H(Curated Multimodal Questions)
    G -- Fail --> D[Question Structuring & Formatting]
    H --> I(Final CMMMU Dataset\n12,000 Questions)
    I --> J[Model Evaluation Tasks]
    J --> K[Performance Assessment]
    K --> L(Evaluation Results)

name: CodeElo
intro: CodeElo 是基于CodeForces平台的标准化竞赛级代码生成基准，旨在解决现有基准在私有测试用例、特殊裁判支持和执行环境方面的不足。该基准汇集了CodeForces上最近六个月的竞赛题目，并包含详细的比赛分区、题目难度评级和算法标签。它采用独特的Elo评分系统，通过直接向平台提交代码进行评判，从而实现与人类选手表现相当且方差更低的可靠评估。
build_method: 网络爬取, 专家构建
eval_feature: 结果导向, 领域特定
intro_en: CodeElo is a standardized competition-level code generation benchmark based on the CodeForces platform, designed to address limitations of existing benchmarks in private test cases, special judge support, and execution environments. It aggregates contest problems from CodeForces over the past six months, including detailed contest divisions, problem difficulty ratings, and algorithm tags. Using a unique Elo rating system, it evaluates submissions by directly submitting code to the platform, achieving reliable assessments comparable to human performance with lower variance.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A[CodeForces Platform] --> B{{Web Scraping}}
    B --> C(Raw Contest Problems)
    C --> D[Problem Standardization]
    D --> E[Private Test Case Creation]
    E --> F[Special Judge Support Integration]
    F --> G{{Expert Construction}}
    G --> H{Quality Assurance Check}
    H -- Pass --> I(CodeElo Benchmark Dataset)
    H -- Fail --> D
    I --> J[Code Generation Model Evaluation]
    J --> K[Execution Environment Testing]
    K --> L[Performance Metrics Calculation]
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    style StartEnd fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style ProcessingFiltering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style AnnotationLabeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style EvaluationMetric fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([CodeElo Benchmark Construction]):::StartEnd

    subgraph Data Sourcing
        DS1[/CodeForces Platform/]:::DataCollection
        DS2(Gather problems from rated contests) --> DS3(Select recent six months of contests)
        DS3 --> DS4(Scrape problems in raw HTML format)
    end

    subgraph Data Parsing & Preprocessing
        DP1(Parse HTML content) --> DP2(Extract problem description, input/output format, examples, notes)
        DP2 --> DP3(Restructure prompts for testing)
        DP3 --> DP4{Is HTML format challenging for humans?}
        DP4 -- Yes --> DP5(Preserve critical info & structure)
        DP4 -- No --> DP6(Easily processed by LLMs)
    end

    subgraph Classification & Categorization
        CC1(Source classifications from CodeForces) --> CC2(Categorize by Contest Difficulty Division: Div. 1, 2, 3, 4, 1+2)
        CC2 --> CC3(Categorize by Problem Difficulty Rating: x rating = 50% pass probability)
        CC3 --> CC4(Categorize by Problem Algorithm Tags: 35 tags, top 16 cover 90%)
        CC4 --> CC5{Are problem tags visible to models?}
        CC5 -- No --> CC6(Ensure tags are not hints for solutions)
    end

    subgraph Evaluation Method (Solution Submission & Judgment)
        EM1(Use automatic submission bot) --> EM2(Submit model solutions to CodeForces platform)
        EM2 --> EM3(Receive test results from CodeForces)
        EM3 --> EM4{Is status \'Accept\' ?}
        EM4 -- Yes --> EM5(Solution passes all test cases)
        EM4 -- No --> EM6(Solution fails test cases)
        EM5 --> EM7(Support Special Judges for problems with non-unique correct answers)
        EM7 --> EM8(Ensure aligned execution environment)
        EM8 --> EM9(Allow up to eight attempts per problem)
        EM9 --> EM10(Apply penalty points for failed attempts)
    end

    subgraph Elo Rating Calculation System
        ERC1(Initialize Elo rating system) --> ERC2(Treat each contest as independent)
        ERC2 --> ERC3(Calculate model\'s expected rating \'r\' for each contest)
        ERC3 --> ERC4(Consider \'n\' human participants with ratings r(i))
        ERC4 --> ERC5(Position model\'s performance within human ranking \'m\')
        ERC5 --> ERC6[Apply Elo rating formula: m = sum(1 / (1 + 10^((r - r(i))/400)))]
        ERC6 --> ERC7(Use binary search to determine \'r\')
        ERC7 --> ERC8(Result: Standardized Elo Rating)
        ERC8 --> ERC9(Lower variance compared to official platform method)
    end

    subgraph Quality Control & Validation
        QC1(Achieve Zero False Positives)
        QC1 --> QC2(Ensure robust test cases)
    end

    Start --> DS1
    DS4 --> DP1
    DP5 --> CC1
    DP6 --> CC1
    CC6 --> EM1
    EM6 --> EM1
    EM10 --> ERC1
    ERC9 --> QC1
    QC2 --> End([CodeElo Final Benchmark]):::OutputResult
flowchart_zh:
flowchart TD
    style StartEnd fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style ProcessingFiltering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style AnnotationLabeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style EvaluationMetric fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([CodeElo 基准构建]):::StartEnd

    subgraph 数据来源
        DS1[/CodeForces 平台/]:::DataCollection
        DS2(从评分比赛中收集问题) --> DS3(选择最近六个月的比赛) 
        DS3 --> DS4(抓取原始HTML格式的问题)
    end

    subgraph 数据解析与预处理
        DP1(解析HTML内容) --> DP2(提取问题描述、输入/输出格式、示例、注释)
        DP2 --> DP3(为测试重构提示)
        DP3 --> DP4{HTML格式对人类是否具有挑战性?}
        DP4 -- 是 --> DP5(保留关键信息和结构)
        DP4 -- 否 --> DP6(LLM易于处理)
    end

    subgraph 分类与归类
        CC1(从CodeForces获取分类) --> CC2(按比赛难度划分：Div. 1, 2, 3, 4, 1+2)
        CC2 --> CC3(按问题难度评分：x评分 = 50%通过概率)
        CC3 --> CC4(按问题算法标签分类：35个标签，前16个覆盖90%)
        CC4 --> CC5{问题标签对模型可见吗?}
        CC5 -- 否 --> CC6(确保标签不作为解决方案的提示)
    end

    subgraph 评估方法 (解决方案提交与判断)
        EM1(使用自动提交机器人) --> EM2(向CodeForces平台提交模型解决方案)
        EM2 --> EM3(接收CodeForces的测试结果)
        EM3 --> EM4{状态是“接受”吗?}
        EM4 -- 是 --> EM5(解决方案通过所有测试用例)
        EM4 -- 否 --> EM6(解决方案未能通过测试用例)
        EM5 --> EM7(支持针对非唯一正确答案问题的特殊评判)
        EM7 --> EM8(确保对齐的执行环境)
        EM8 --> EM9(每个问题最多允许八次尝试)
        EM9 --> EM10(对失败的尝试施加惩罚)
    end

    subgraph Elo 评分计算系统
        ERC1(初始化Elo评分系统) --> ERC2(将每个比赛视为独立)
        ERC2 --> ERC3(计算每个比赛的模型预期评分“r”)
        ERC3 --> ERC4(考虑“n”个具有评分r(i)的人类参与者)
        ERC4 --> ERC5(将模型的表现置于人类排名“m”中)
        ERC5 --> ERC6[应用Elo评分公式: m = sum(1 / (1 + 10^((r - r(i))/400)))]
        ERC6 --> ERC7(使用二分查找确定“r”)
        ERC7 --> ERC8(结果: 标准化Elo评分)
        ERC8 --> ERC9(与官方平台方法相比方差更低)
    end

    subgraph 质量控制与验证
        QC1(实现零误报) 
        QC1 --> QC2(确保鲁棒的测试用例)
    end

    Start --> DS1
    DS4 --> DP1
    DP5 --> CC1
    DP6 --> CC1
    CC6 --> EM1
    EM6 --> EM1
    EM10 --> ERC1
    ERC9 --> QC1
    QC2 --> End([CodeElo 最终基准]):::OutputResult

name: CodeSimpleQA
intro: CodeSimpleQA是一个全面的双语基准测试，旨在评估代码大型语言模型在回答代码相关问题时的事实准确性。它包含精心策划的英文和中文问答对，涵盖多种编程语言和主要计算机科学领域。该基准测试旨在解决现有代码相关基准侧重于代码执行正确性，而忽略编程知识事实准确性的问题。研究还创建了CodeSimpleQA-Instruct，一个包含66M样本的大规模指令语料库，并开发了一个结合监督微调和强化学习的后训练框架。
org: Jian Yang, Wei Zhang, Yizhi Li, Shawn Guo, Haowen Wang, Aishan Liu, Ge Zhang, Zili Wang, Zhoujun Li,
intro_en: CodeSimpleQA is a comprehensive bilingual benchmark designed to evaluate the factual accuracy of large code language models in answering code-related questions. It contains carefully curated English and Chinese QA pairs covering multiple programming languages and key computer science domains. This benchmark addresses the issue that existing code-related benchmarks focus on code execution correctness while neglecting the factual accuracy of programming knowledge. The study also created CodeSimpleQA-Instruct, a large-scale instruction corpus with 66M samples, and developed a post-training framework combining supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning.

    G --> B
    F --> H(CodeSimpleQA Dataset\nBilingual QA Pairs)
    H --> I[Model Evaluation\nFactual Accuracy]
    I --> J(Evaluation Results)

name: ComplexFuncBench
intro: ComplexFuncBench针对复杂函数调用设计，涵盖五个真实场景，涉及多步调用和参数约束。要求模型进行长参数填写、参数值推理和处理128k长上下文。基准还提出了自动评估框架ComplexEval，用于量化复杂函数调用任务表现。
org: Beijing University of Posts and Telecommunications, Zhipu AI, Tsinghua University, Federal Reserve B
eval_feature: 多轮, 长文本, 领域特定
intro_en: ComplexFuncBench is designed for complex function calls, covering five real-world scenarios involving multi-step calls and parameter constraints. It requires models to handle long parameter filling, parameter value reasoning, and processing 128k-length contexts. The benchmark also introduces an automatic evaluation framework, ComplexEval, to quantify performance on complex function call tasks.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Real-world Scenarios) --> B[Expert Definition]
    B --> C(Initial Complex Function Calls)
    C --> D{{LLM Generation}}
    D --> E[Parameter Filling & Reasoning]
    E --> F(Generated Complex Scenarios)
    F --> G{Expert Review & Validation}
    G --"Pass"--> H(Validated ComplexFuncBench Dataset)
    G --"Fail"--> I[Refinement]
    I --> D
    H --> J[Model Evaluation]
    J --> K(Evaluation Results)

        G[/初步数据集 (1,000个样本)/]:::OutputResult
        H{是否存在严重错误(参数值、顺序)?}:::Decision
        E --> F
        F --> G
        G --> H
    end

    subgraph Stage 2: Fine-grained Annotation["阶段2：细粒度注释"]

        L(高级标注员纠正参数错误):::AnnotationLabeling
        M(移除重叠功能):::ProcessingFiltering
        N(删除模糊的API响应):::ProcessingFiltering
        O{是否存在剩余错误?}:::Decision
        P[/模板数据集 (100个样本)/]:::OutputResult
        I --> J
        J --> K
        K --> L
        L --> M
        M --> N
        N --> O
        O -- 是 --> J
        O -- 否 --> P
    end

    subgraph Stage 3: Generalization["阶段3：泛化"]
        Q(将100个模板样本泛化到1000个):::DataCollection
        R(使用GPT-4o修改位置/约束):::AnnotationLabeling
        S(为每个原始查询生成9个新查询):::AnnotationLabeling
        T(培训初级标注员进行参数填充):::AnnotationLabeling
        U(删除用于消歧的API响应):::ProcessingFiltering
        V[/最终ComplexFuncBench数据集 (1,000个样本)/]:::OutputResult
        Q --> R
        R --> S
        S --> T
        T --> U
        U --> V
    end

    subgraph Data Composition["数据组成"]
        W[600个单领域样本, 400个跨领域样本]:::OutputResult
        X[平均3.26步, 每个样本5.07次调用]:::OutputResult
        V --> W
        V --> X
    end

    Start --> A
    D --> E
    H -- 是 --> I
    H -- 否 --> I
    P --> Q
    V --> End([结束]):::EndNode

name: ComputeEval
intro: ComputeEval是一个用于评估大型语言模型（LLM）生成CUDA代码能力的基准测试。它包含一系列手工制作的CUDA编程挑战，旨在测试模型在并行编程、内存管理和线程同步等方面的能力。该框架通过在沙箱环境中对生成的代码进行功能正确性测试来评估模型性能。
build_method: 专家构建, 人工标注
eval_feature: 领域特定, 结果导向
intro_en: ComputeEval is a benchmark for evaluating large language models' (LLM) ability to generate CUDA code. It consists of a series of handcrafted CUDA programming challenges designed to test the model's skills in parallel programming, memory management, and thread synchronization. The framework assesses model performance by testing the functional correctness of the generated code in a sandbox environment.

flowchart LR

    E --> F{Challenge Quality Check}
    F -- Fail --> G[Refinement & Iteration]
    G --> C
    F -- Pass --> H(Validated CUDA Challenges)
    H --> I(ComputeEval Benchmark Dataset)
    I --> J[LLM CUDA Code Generation Evaluation]
    J --> K(Model Performance Results)

flowchart TD

    subgraph Initial Program Generation (OptiML-G)

    end

    subgraph Kernel Optimization (OptiML-X)

        H --> I(Search-Based Optimizer (OptiML-X))
        I --> J(Monte Carlo Tree Search over LLM-driven Edits)
        J --> K(Candidate Transformation)
    end

    subgraph Verification & Profiling
        K --> L(Compile Candidate Kernel)
        L --> M(Verify Candidate Kernel)
        M --> N(Profile with Nsight Compute)
        N --> O[/Profiler Feedback/]
    end

    subgraph Evaluation & Refinement
        O --> P(Hardware-Aware Reward Calculation)

        U -- No --> J
    end

    end

name: CountBench
intro: CountBench是一个用于评估视觉语言模型（VLM）对象计数能力的新型图像-文本计数基准。它通过自动创建反事实示例来改进VLM的定量理解，并鼓励模型区分正确和不正确的计数描述。该基准旨在评估模型对图像中对象数量的理解。
build_method: 自动生成, 人工标注
intro_en: CountBench is a novel image-text counting benchmark designed to evaluate the object counting ability of vision-language models (VLMs). It improves VLMs' quantitative understanding by automatically generating counterfactual examples and encourages models to distinguish between correct and incorrect counting descriptions. This benchmark aims to assess models' comprehension of object quantities in images.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Existing Image-Text Data) --> B[Identify Objects & Counts]
    B --> C{{Automatic Generation Algorithm}}
    C --> D[Generate Counterfactual Examples]
    D --> E{Counterfactual Quality Check}
    E -- Fail --> F[Discard Invalid Examples]
    E -- Pass --> G[Manual Annotation for Validation]
    G --> H(Validated Counterfactual Examples)
    H --> I[Combine Original & Counterfactuals]
    I --> J(CountBench Dataset)
    J --> K[VLM Inference on CountBench]
    K --> L[Compare VLM Predictions to Ground Truth]
    L --> M[Evaluate Object Counting Ability]

name: CritPt
intro: CritPt是一个用于评估大型语言模型在科学领域中识别和分析临界点能力的基准测试。该基准涵盖物理、化学和生物学等多个科学领域的临界现象问题。
intro_en: CritPt is a benchmark designed to evaluate large language models' ability to identify and analyze critical points in scientific domains. It covers critical phenomena problems across multiple fields, including physics, chemistry, and biology.

    H --> I[LLM Evaluation on Critical Points]
    I --> J[Performance Metrics Calculation]
    J --> K(Evaluation Results)

name: CruxEval
intro: CruxEval是一个用于评估代码语言模型推理、理解和执行能力的基准。它包含800个Python函数，每个函数都配有输入输出对，并设计了输入预测和输出预测两个任务。该基准旨在衡量模型在简单代码片段上的执行预测和代码推理能力。
task_type: 代码推理, 输入, 输出预测
eval_feature: 代码推理, 执行跟踪
intro_en: CruxEval is a benchmark for evaluating code language models' reasoning, understanding, and execution abilities. It includes 800 Python functions, each paired with input-output examples, and features two tasks: input prediction and output prediction. The benchmark aims to assess models' execution prediction and code reasoning skills on simple code snippets.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR

    B{{LLM Generation}} --> C
    C --> D[Pair with Input-Output Examples]
    D --> E{Manual Screening}
    E -- Pass --> F[Refine & Validate]
    E -- Fail --> D
    F --> G((CruxEval Dataset\n800 Python Functions\nI/O Pairs))
    G --> H[Model Evaluation Tasks\n(Input/Output Prediction)]
    H --> I((Evaluation Metrics\nReasoning, Understanding, Execution))

    subgraph Candidate Filtering
        E --> F{Apply Filtering Criteria} :::ProcessingFiltering
        F --> G{Compile Time Checks?} :::ProcessingFiltering
        G -- Yes --> H(Arguments Used, Code Length 75-300 chars, No Syntax Errors, Proper Assertions) :::ProcessingFiltering
        G -- No --> I(Discard Candidate) :::QualityControl

        J -- Yes --> K(No Float Ops, Int Args <=3, String/List Args <=3, Run Time < 2s, No Uncaught Exceptions) :::ProcessingFiltering
        J -- No --> I
        K --> L{Undesirable Code Checks?} :::ProcessingFiltering
        L -- Yes --> M(No Imports, Deterministic, No Side Effects) :::ProcessingFiltering
        L -- No --> I
        M --> N[/Filtered Candidates/] :::OutputResult
    end

    subgraph Quality Control & Validation
        N --> O(Bootstrap Sampling of ~1700 Samples) :::QualityControl
        O --> P(Measure Performance Difference: Code Llama 34B vs 13B) :::QualityControl
        P --> Q{Is Noise Sufficiently Small?} :::QualityControl
        Q -- Yes --> R(Confirm 800 Examples Sufficient for Statistical Significance) :::QualityControl
        Q -- No --> S(Adjust Filtering/Sampling) :::QualityControl
    end

    subgraph Final Benchmark Creation
        R --> T(Randomly Select 800 Samples) :::ProcessingFiltering
        T --> U[/CRUXEval Benchmark (800 Python Functions, Input-Output Pairs)/] :::OutputResult
        U --> V(Define Two Tasks: Input Prediction & Output Prediction) :::OutputResult
        V --> End([End])
    end

        T --> U[/CRUXEval 基准 (800 个 Python 函数，输入-输出对)/] :::OutputResult

        V --> End([结束])
    end

name: CSR-Bench
intro: CSR-Bench是一个针对计算机科学研究项目的基准，旨在评估大型语言模型（LLM）在处理复杂代码开发任务时的能力。该基准从准确性、效率和部署脚本质量等多个方面对LLM进行评估，探索其在自主进行计算机科学研究方面的潜力。
org: Amazon, University of California, Los Angeles
build_method: 专家构建, 网络爬取
eval_feature: 过程导向, 结果导向
intro_en: CSR-Bench is a benchmark for computer science research projects designed to evaluate large language models (LLMs) in handling complex code development tasks. It assesses LLMs across accuracy, efficiency, and deployment script quality, exploring their potential for autonomous computer science research.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Web Data) --> C{{Web Crawling}}
    B(CS Project Repositories) --> D[Expert Task Identification]
    C --> E[Raw Data Filtering]
    D --> F[Code Task Formulation]
    E --> G[Test Case Generation]
    F --> G
    G --> H{Expert Validation}
    H --> I(CSR-Bench Dataset\nComplex Code Tasks)
    I --> J[LLM Execution]
    J --> K[Accuracy Metric]
    J --> L[Efficiency Metric]
    J --> M[Deployment Script Quality]

name: CURIE
intro: CURIE是一个科学长上下文理解、推理和信息提取基准，用于评估大型语言模型在辅助真实科学工作流程中的能力。包含10个挑战性任务，共580个问题与解答，涵盖六个科学领域。评估模型的领域专业知识、长上下文理解及多步推理能力。
modality: 文本, 图像, 多模态
task_type: 开放式问答, 抽取, 摘要
eval_feature: 长文本, 领域特定, 多轮
intro_en: CURIE is a scientific long-context understanding, reasoning, and information extraction benchmark designed to evaluate large language models' capabilities in supporting real scientific workflows. It includes 10 challenging tasks with a total of 580 questions and answers across six scientific domains. The benchmark assesses models' domain expertise, long-context comprehension, and multi-step reasoning abilities.

    F -->|Fail| E
    G --> H(CURIE Dataset\n10 Tasks, 580 Q&A)
    H --> I[LLM Evaluation]
    I --> J(Model Performance Metrics)

name: CValues
intro: CValues是首个中文人类价值观评测基准，评估LLM在安全性和责任感方面的对齐能力。包含专家收集的10个场景下的对抗安全提示及8个领域的责任提示，支持人工和自动评估，全面衡量中文LLM的价值观对齐。
build_method: 人工标注, 专家构建
task_type: 开放式问答, 安全测试, 排序
intro_en: CValues is the first Chinese human values evaluation benchmark that assesses LLMs' alignment in safety and responsibility. It includes adversarial safety prompts across 10 expert-curated scenarios and responsibility prompts in 8 domains, supporting both manual and automated evaluation to comprehensively measure value alignment in Chinese LLMs.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Expert Knowledge & Human Values) --> B[Expert-Curated Scenarios]
    B --> C[Prompt Development]
    C --> D{{Manual Annotation}}
    D --> E[Categorization & Refinement]
    E --> F{Quality Check}
    F -- Pass --> G[Adversarial Prompt Generation]
    F -- Fail --> D
    G --> H(CValues Benchmark Dataset)
    H --> I[Manual Evaluation]
    H --> J[Automated Evaluation]
    I --> K[LLM Alignment Assessment]
    J --> K
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    style Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style ProcessingFiltering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style AnnotationLabeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style EvaluationMetrics fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([Start]) --> A(Identify LLM Risks & Need for Value Alignment)
    A --> B(Analyze Gap: Lack of Chinese Context & Value Focus)
    B --> C(Define CValues Benchmark Objective: Safety & Responsibility)

    subgraph Data Collection Phase
        C --> D_1(Manually Collect Adversarial Safety Prompts)
        D_1 --> D_2[/10 Scenarios/]
        C --> E_1(Induce Responsibility Prompts by Experts)
        E_1 --> E_2[/8 Domains/]
        D_2 & E_2 --> F{Prompt Quality Check?}
        F -- No --> G(Filter Low-Quality Prompts)
        G --> F
        F -- Yes --> H[/Final Prompt Set/]
    end

    subgraph Evaluation Methodology Development
        H --> I(Design Human Evaluation Protocol)
        H --> J(Develop Multi-choice Prompts for Automatic Evaluation)
        I & J --> K(Define Evaluation Metrics: Safety & Responsibility Scores)
    end

    subgraph Benchmark Execution & Analysis
        K --> L(Apply CValues to Chinese LLMs)
        L --> M(Perform Human Evaluation)
        L --> N(Perform Automatic Evaluation)
        M & N --> O[/Collected Evaluation Results/]
        O --> P(Analyze Safety Performance)
        O --> Q(Analyze Responsibility Performance)
39	        P & Q --> R{Benchmark Adequacy?}
40	        R -- No --> S(Refine Benchmark)
41	        S --> C
42	        R -- Yes --> T(Document Findings)
43	    end
44	
45	    T --> U(Release Benchmark & Code on ModelScope & Github)
46	    U --> End([End])
flowchart_zh:
flowchart TD
    style Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style ProcessingFiltering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style AnnotationLabeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style EvaluationMetrics fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([开始]) --> A(识别LLM风险与价值观对齐需求)
    A --> B(分析差距：缺乏中文语境与价值观关注)
    B --> C(定义CValues基准目标：安全与责任)

    subgraph 数据收集阶段
        C --> D_1(手动收集对抗性安全提示)
        D_1 --> D_2[/10个场景/]
        C --> E_1(专家引导生成责任提示)
        E_1 --> E_2[/8个领域/]
        D_2 & E_2 --> F{提示质量检查?}
        F -- 否 --> G(过滤低质量提示)
        G --> F
        F -- 是 --> H[/最终提示集/]
    end

    subgraph 评估方法论开发
        H --> I(设计人工评估协议)
        H --> J(开发多项选择题用于自动评估)
        I & J --> K(定义评估指标：安全与责任分数)
    end

    subgraph 基准执行与分析
        K --> L(将CValues应用于中文LLM)
        L --> M(进行人工评估)
        L --> N(进行自动评估)
        M & N --> O[/收集到的评估结果/]
        O --> P(分析安全性能)
        O --> Q(分析责任性能)
        P & Q --> R{基准充分性?}

        S --> C
        R -- 是 --> T(记录发现)
    end

    T --> U(在ModelScope和Github发布基准与代码)
    U --> End([结束])

name: CVBench
intro: CVBench是首个旨在严格评估跨视频关系推理能力的诊断基准。它包含1000个问答对，涵盖跨视频对象关联、跨视频事件关联和跨视频复杂推理三个层次。该基准挑战模型分析和整合来自动态视觉流的时空模式，并揭示了当前多模态大语言模型在跨视频理解方面的瓶颈。
org: Sun Yat-sen University, The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology, Tencent AI Lab, Universi
intro_en: CVBench is the first diagnostic benchmark designed to rigorously evaluate cross-video relational reasoning abilities. It contains 1,000 QA pairs covering three levels: cross-video object association, cross-video event association, and cross-video complex reasoning. The benchmark challenges models to analyze and integrate spatiotemporal patterns from dynamic visual streams, revealing current multimodal large language models' bottlenecks in cross-video understanding.
mermaid_flowchart:

    D --> G[Complex Reasoning Annotation]
    E & F & G --> H[QA Pair Generation]
    H --> I{Annotation & QA Verification}
    I -->|Pass| J[Dataset Structuring]
    I -->|Fail| K[Re-annotation]
    K --> D
    J --> L(CVBench Dataset\n1,000 QA Pairs)
    L --> M[Model Evaluation\n(Cross-Video Reasoning)]

name: CVE-Bench
intro: CVE-Bench是一个评估AI智能体利用真实世界网络应用漏洞能力的基准。它通过一个沙盒框架，模拟真实世界的网络攻击场景，旨在解决现有基准在覆盖范围和真实性方面的不足。该基准基于严重级别的常见漏洞和暴露（CVE），并提供有效的漏洞利用评估。初步评估显示，最先进的智能体框架可以解决高达13%的漏洞。
build_method: 专家构建, 自动生成
eval_feature: 动态更新, 防污染, 对抗性, 过程评估
eval_feature_en: Dynamic Update, Contamination-free, Adversarial, Process Evaluation
intro_en: CVE-Bench is a benchmark for evaluating AI agents' ability to exploit real-world network application vulnerabilities. It uses a sandbox framework to simulate real-world network attack scenarios, addressing the limitations of existing benchmarks in coverage and realism. The benchmark is based on high-severity Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) and provides effective vulnerability exploitation assessment. Preliminary evaluations show that state-of-the-art agent frameworks can exploit up to 13% of the vulnerabilities.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Real-world CVEs) --> B[Vulnerability Identification]
    B --> C[Expert Analysis of Exploits]
    C --> D[Design Attack Scenarios]
    D --> E{{Sandbox Framework Setup}}
    E --> F[Integrate Vulnerabilities]
    F --> G[Automated Scenario Generation]
    G --> H{Scenario Realism Check}
    H -->|Pass| I[Expert Validation]
    H -->|Fail| D
    I --> J(Simulated Attack Scenarios)
    J --> K[AI Agent Evaluation]
    K --> L[Performance Metrics]

name: Cybench
intro: Cybench是一个用于评估语言模型在网络安全任务中能力和风险的框架。它包含40个专业级别的夺旗（CTF）任务，这些任务来源于4个不同的CTF竞赛，涵盖了广泛的难度。每个任务都包含描述、起始文件，并在一个代理可以执行命令和观察输出的环境中初始化。为了更详细的评估，每个任务还引入了子任务。
intro_en: Cybench is a framework for evaluating language models' capabilities and risks in cybersecurity tasks. It includes 40 professional-level CTF tasks sourced from 4 different CTF competitions, covering a wide range of difficulties. Each task contains a description, starter files, and is initialized in an environment where an agent can execute commands and observe outputs. For more detailed evaluation, each task also includes subtasks.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(CTF Competitions) --> B[Task Identification]
    B --> C[Task Extraction]
    C --> D{Initial Task Filtering}
    D -- Pass --> E[Task Refinement & Initialization]
    D -- Fail --> K[Discard Task]
    E --> F{{Hybrid Construction Method}}
    F --> G{Professional Level Vetting}
    G -- Approved --> H(Cybench Dataset: 40 CTF Tasks)
    G -- Rejected --> K
    H --> I[Language Model Evaluation]
    I --> J(Evaluation Results: Capabilities & Risks)

    S --> finish

name: CyberGym
intro: CyberGym是一个大规模基准测试，旨在评估AI智能体在真实世界网络安全方面的能力。它包含1,507个真实漏洞，涵盖188个软件项目。该基准主要任务是让智能体根据文本描述和代码库生成漏洞复现的概念验证测试。CyberGym不仅能有效区分AI智能体的网络安全能力，还促成了35个零日漏洞的发现。
eval_feature: 动态更新, 防污染, 过程评估
intro_en: CyberGym is a large-scale benchmark designed to evaluate AI agents' capabilities in real-world cybersecurity. It includes 1,507 real vulnerabilities across 188 software projects. The main task is for agents to generate proof-of-concept tests reproducing vulnerabilities based on textual descriptions and codebases. CyberGym effectively differentiates AI agents' cybersecurity skills and has contributed to the discovery of 35 zero-day vulnerabilities.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Real-world Software Projects) --> B[Identify Vulnerabilities]
    C(Vulnerability Databases) --> B
    B --> D[Extract Relevant Data]
    D --> E[Generate Textual Descriptions]
    E --> F{{PoC Generation Engine}}
    F --> G[Generate Proof-of-Concept Tests]
    G --> H{Review & Validate PoC}
    H -->|Pass| I[Integrate into Dataset]
    H -->|Fail| J[Refine & Re-generate]
    I --> K(CyberGym Dataset
1507 Vulns, 188 Projects)
    K --> L[AI Agent Evaluation]
    L --> M(Evaluation Results)

name: DAComp
intro: DAComp包含210个任务，模拟复杂企业数据智能工作流，涵盖工业级架构的数据工程和开放式业务问题的数据分析。该基准用于评估自主数据智能体在企业环境中的能力。
org: Institute of Automation, CAS, University of Chinese Academy of Sciences, ByteDance Seed, TikTok, UC
modality: 文本, 代码, 表格, SQL
task_type: Agent任务, 代码生成, SQL Generation, 开放式问答
eval_feature: 过程导向, 结果导向, 多轮, 领域特定
task_type_en: Agent Task, Code Generation, SQL Generation, Open-ended QA
intro_en: DAComp includes 210 tasks simulating complex enterprise data intelligence workflows, covering industrial-scale data engineering architectures and open-ended business data analysis. This benchmark is used to evaluate the capabilities of autonomous data agents in enterprise environments.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Simulated Enterprise Workflows) --> B[Expert Task Design]
    B --> C{{LLM-based Task Generation}}
    C --> D{Task Quality Check}
    D -- Fail --> E[Refinement by Experts]
    D -- Pass --> F[Final Task Selection]
    E --> C
    F --> G(DAComp Dataset\n210 Tasks)
    G --> H[Autonomous Data Agent Evaluation]
    H --> I(Evaluation Results)
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Data Sourcing and Task Definition
        A([Start])
        A -->|Define Scope| B(Define Enterprise Data Intelligence Workflows)
        B -->|Identify Needs| C(Define 210 Complex Tasks)
    end

    subgraph Data Preparation
        C -->|Gather Data| D[/Collect/Generate Data for Each Task/]
        D -->|Initial Review| E{Is Data Relevant and High Quality?}
        E -- No --> F(Refine Data Collection/Generation)
        F --> D
        E -- Yes --> G(Filter Irrelevant/Low-Quality Data)
        G -->|Labeling| H(Annotate Data for Specific Intelligence Tasks)
    end

    subgraph Workflow Simulation and Quality Control
        H -->|Execute Workflows| I(Simulate Enterprise Data Intelligence Workflows)
        I -->|Generate Results| J[/Generate Outputs for Simulated Workflows/]
        J -->|Check Data Consistency| K(Perform Data Quality Control)
        K --> L{Data Quality Meets Thresholds?}
        L -- No --> M(Re-annotate/Re-process Data)
        M --> H
        L -- Yes --> N(Perform Workflow Simulation Accuracy Check)
        N --> O{Workflow Simulation Accurate?}
        O -- No --> P(Adjust Workflow Parameters/Logic)
        P --> I
    end

    subgraph Validation and Finalization
        O -- Yes --> Q(Validate Benchmark Tasks and Data)
        Q -->|Define Metrics| R(Define Evaluation Metrics)
        R -->|Assemble| S(Assemble Final DAComp Benchmark)

name: DeepPlanning
intro: DeepPlanning 是一个旨在解决现有LLM规划评估局限性的基准测试。它引入了复杂的、真实世界的任务，需要主动信息获取、局部约束推理和全局约束优化。该基准测试的特点是多日旅行规划和多产品购物任务，即使是前沿的Agentic LLM也难以应对。
eval_feature: 可验证约束, 主动信息获取, 局部约束推理, 全局约束优化, 显式推理模式, 并行工具使用
eval_feature_en: Verifiable Constraints, Active Information Acquisition, Local Constraint Reasoning, Global Constraint Optimization, Explicit Reasoning Mode, Parallel Tool Usage
intro_en: DeepPlanning is a benchmark designed to address the limitations of existing LLM planning evaluations. It introduces complex, real-world tasks requiring active information gathering, local constraint reasoning, and global constraint optimization. The benchmark features multi-day travel planning and multi-product shopping tasks, which are challenging even for state-of-the-art Agentic LLMs.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A[Real-world Scenarios] --> B{{Web Data Sources}}
    B --> C[Active Information Gathering]
    C --> D[Task & Constraint Definition]
    D --> E[Multi-day Travel Plan Generation]
    E --> F{{Hybrid Method}}
    F --> G{Constraint Validation}
    G -- Pass --> H[Refined Planning Tasks]
    G -- Fail --> D
    H --> I(DeepPlanning Benchmark Dataset)
    I --> J[LLM Planning Evaluation]
    J --> K(Evaluation Results)
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Start["Benchmark Construction Process"]
        A([Start]) --> B(Define Benchmark Scope and Goals)
    end

    subgraph Data Sourcing["Data Sourcing and Initial Collection"]
        B --> C[/Identify Potential Data Sources/]
        C --> D(Collect Raw Data from Various Sources)
        D --> E{Is Data Volume Sufficient?}
        E -- No --> C
        E -- Yes --> F[/Initial Raw Dataset/]
    end

    subgraph Data Preprocessing["Data Preprocessing and Filtering"]
        F --> G(Clean and Preprocess Raw Data)
        G --> H{Apply Filtering Criteria?}
        H -- Yes --> I(Filter Irrelevant/Low-Quality Data)
        H -- No --> J[/Preprocessed Dataset/]
        I --> J
        J --> K{Check for Data Bias?}
        K -- Yes --> L(Mitigate Bias and Rebalance Data)
        K -- No --> M[/Filtered and Balanced Dataset/]
        L --> M
    end

    subgraph Data Annotation["Data Annotation and Labeling"]
        M --> N(Define Annotation Guidelines)
        N --> O(Human Annotation/Expert Review)
        O --> P{Annotation Quality Acceptable?}
        P -- No --> N
        P -- Yes --> Q[/Annotated Dataset/]
    end

    subgraph Quality Control["Quality Control and Validation"]
        Q --> R(Perform Inter-Annotator Agreement Check)
        R --> S{Agreement Score > Threshold (e.g., 0.8)?}
        S -- No --> O
        S -- Yes --> T(Validate Data Consistency and Integrity)
        T --> U[/Validated Dataset/]
    end

    subgraph Benchmark Construction["Benchmark Construction and Evaluation"]
        U --> V(Split Data into Train/Validation/Test Sets)
        V --> W(Define Evaluation Metrics and Procedures)
        W --> X(Run Baseline Models on Benchmark)
        X --> Y{Baseline Performance as Expected?}
        Y -- No --> V
        Y -- Yes --> Z(Finalize Benchmark Structure and Documentation)
        Z --> AA[/DeepPlanning Benchmark v1.0/]
    end

    subgraph End["Completion"]
        AA --> BB([End])
    end

        T --> U[/验证数据集/]
    end

    subgraph Benchmark Construction["基准构建和评估"]
        U --> V(将数据分成训练/验证/测试集)
        V --> W(定义评估指标和程序)
        W --> X(在基准上运行基线模型)

        Y -- 否 --> V
        Y -- 是 --> Z(最终确定基准结构和文档)
        Z --> AA[/DeepPlanning 基准 v1.0/]
    end

name: DeepResearchEval
intro: DeepResearchEval是一个针对深度研究系统的自动化评测框架。它采用角色驱动的流程来生成复杂的真实研究任务，这些任务需要整合多个来源的证据并进行外部检索。该框架包含一个自适应的单点质量评估机制，能根据每个任务动态调整评估维度、标准和权重，同时还具备主动事实核查功能，即使在没有引文的情况下也能通过网络搜索自主验证报告内容的准确性。
metric: Factual Accuracy, Comprehensiveness, Redundancy, Compliance, Privacy, Objectivity
eval_feature: 过程导向, 领域特定
intro_en: DeepResearchEval is an automated evaluation framework for deep research systems. It uses a role-driven process to generate complex, real-world research tasks that require integrating evidence from multiple sources and performing external retrieval. The framework includes an adaptive single-point quality assessment mechanism that dynamically adjusts evaluation dimensions, criteria, and weights for each task. It also features proactive fact-checking, enabling autonomous verification of report accuracy via web search even without citations.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(External Knowledge Sources) --> B[Information Retrieval]
    B --> C[Evidence Integration]
    C --> D{{LLM for Task Generation}}
    D --> E[Role-Driven Prompting]
    E --> F[Task Scenario Generation]
    F --> G{Task Complexity Validation}
    G -- Valid --> H[Automated Quality Check]
    G -- Invalid --> I[Task Refinement]
    I --> E
    H --> J(DeepResearchEval Task Set)
    J --> K[Model Performance Measurement]
    K --> L[Adaptive Single-Point Evaluation]
metric_en: Factual Accuracy, Comprehensiveness, Redundancy, Compliance, Privacy, Objectivity
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Phase1["Data Sourcing and Initial Collection"]
        A([Start]) --> B[/Identify Research Domains/]
        B --> C{Select Relevant Datasets?}
        C -- Yes --> D[/Collect Raw Data from Sources/]
        C -- No --> E[Re-evaluate Sourcing Strategy]
        E --> B
        D --> F([End Phase1])
    end

    subgraph Phase2["Data Preprocessing and Filtering"]
        F --> G[/Clean and Normalize Data/]
        G --> H{Apply Filtering Criteria?}
        H -- Yes --> I[Remove Irrelevant Data]
        H -- No --> J[Proceed with Raw Data]
        I --> K{Check Data Integrity?}
        J --> K
        K -- Pass --> L([End Phase2])
        K -- Fail --> G
    end

    subgraph Phase3["Data Annotation and Labeling"]
        L --> M[/Define Annotation Guidelines/]
        M --> N[Human Annotation Task Creation]
        N --> O[Distribute to Annotators]
        O --> P[/Receive Annotated Data/]
        P --> Q([End Phase3])
    end

    subgraph Phase4["Quality Control and Validation"]
        Q --> R{Perform Inter-Annotator Agreement Check?}
        R -- Yes --> S[Calculate IAA Score]
        R -- No --> T[Manual Review of Samples]
        S --> U{IAA Score > Threshold?}
        T --> U
        U -- Yes --> V[Validate Annotated Samples]
        U -- No --> N
        V --> W([End Phase4])
    end

    subgraph Phase5["Benchmark Construction and Evaluation Protocol"]
        W --> X[/Assemble Final Benchmark Dataset/]
        X --> Y[Define Evaluation Metrics]
        Y --> Z[Establish Evaluation Protocol]
        Z --> AA([End Phase5])
    end

    subgraph Phase6["Benchmark Application and Release"]
        AA --> BB[Integrate with DeepResearchEval Framework]
        BB --> CC[Run Automated Evaluation]
        CC --> DD[/Generate Performance Reports/]
        DD --> EE([End])
    end

name: DeepSearchQA
intro: DeepSearchQA是一个包含900个提示的基准测试，旨在评估智能体在17个不同领域中执行复杂多步信息检索任务的能力。该基准通过精心设计的手工任务，测试智能体系统性整理碎片信息、去重与实体解析以及在开放式搜索空间中判断停止标准的能力。所有任务均基于开放网络，并具有客观可验证的答案集。对现有智能体架构的评估显示，即使是最先进的模型也难以在高召回率和精确度之间取得平衡，揭示了当前智能体设计在深度研究能力方面的显著局限性。
intro_en: DeepSearchQA is a benchmark consisting of 900 prompts designed to evaluate agents' ability to perform complex multi-step information retrieval tasks across 17 different domains. The benchmark uses carefully crafted manual tasks to test agents' capabilities in systematically organizing fragmented information, deduplication and entity resolution, as well as determining stopping criteria in an open search space. All tasks are based on the open web and have objectively verifiable answer sets. Evaluations of existing agent architectures show that even state-of-the-art models struggle to balance high recall and precision, revealing significant limitations in current agent designs for deep research capabilities.

flowchart LR
    A(Diverse Information Sources) --> B[Expert Information Gathering]
    B --> C[Information Structuring]
    C --> D[Complex Task Design]
    D --> E[Prompt Generation]
    E --> F{{Manual Crafting & Review}}
    F --> G{Prompt Quality Check}
    G -- Pass --> H(DeepSearchQA Dataset)
    G -- Fail --> E
    H --> I(900 Prompts, 17 Domains)
    I --> J[Agent Evaluation]
    J --> K[Performance Measurement]

name: DefenderBench
intro: DefenderBench是一个实用开源工具包，用于评估语言智能体在攻防和网络安全知识任务中的表现。涵盖网络入侵、恶意内容检测、代码漏洞分析及安全知识评估，支持多种主流大型语言模型的公平严格测试。
metric: Winning Rate, Attack Success Rate, Detection Rate, DefenderBench Score, Macro-F1, CodeBLEU
task_type: Agent任务, 分类, 代码生成, 开放式问答
eval_feature: 领域特定, 多轮, 过程导向
task_type_en: Agent Task, Classification, Code Generation, Open-ended QA
intro_en: DefenderBench is a practical open-source toolkit for evaluating language agents' performance in offensive and defensive tasks and cybersecurity knowledge. It covers network intrusion, malicious content detection, code vulnerability analysis, and security knowledge assessment, supporting fair and rigorous testing of various mainstream large language models.

name: DeR2
intro: DeR2是一个受控的深度研究沙盒，旨在隔离文档基础推理，同时保留深度搜索的核心难点，如多步合成、去噪和基于证据的结论制定。它通过四种机制将证据访问与推理解耦，从而量化检索损失和推理损失，并实现细粒度的错误归因。为防止参数泄露，DeR2采用两阶段验证，确保在没有证据的情况下参数失败，同时保证预言概念的可解性。每个实例都提供了一个冻结的文档库，其中包含专家标注的概念和验证的理由。
org: M-A-P, ByteDance Seed, Taizhou University, Hangzhou Normal University, Johns Hopkins University, Sou
build_method: 专家构建, 人工标注
intro_en: DeR2 is a controlled deep research sandbox designed to isolate document-based reasoning while retaining core challenges of deep search, such as multi-step synthesis, denoising, and evidence-based conclusion formulation. It decouples evidence access from reasoning through four mechanisms, enabling quantification of retrieval loss and reasoning loss, as well as fine-grained error attribution. To prevent parameter leakage, DeR2 employs two-stage validation to ensure parameter failure without evidence while guaranteeing the solvability of predicted concepts. Each instance provides a frozen document corpus containing expert-annotated concepts and verified rationales.

name: Design2Code
intro: Design2Code是一个用于评估多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）将视觉设计（如网页截图）转换为前端代码能力的基准测试。它手动收集了484个真实世界的网页作为测试用例，并开发了一套自动评估指标来衡量模型生成代码的质量。该基准还通过全面的人工评估来验证性能排名，并测试了多种多模态提示方法，以揭示模型在召回视觉元素和生成正确布局设计方面的不足。
org: Stanford University, Georgia Institute of Technology
build_method: 人工标注, 自动生成
modality: 图像, 文本, 代码
intro_en: Design2Code is a benchmark for evaluating the ability of multimodal large language models (MLLMs) to convert visual designs (such as webpage screenshots) into front-end code. It manually collects 484 real-world webpages as test cases and develops a set of automatic evaluation metrics to measure the quality of the generated code. The benchmark also validates performance rankings through comprehensive human evaluation and tests various multimodal prompting methods to reveal models' shortcomings in recalling visual elements and generating correct layout designs.

    J --> K(Final Design2Code Dataset\n(484 Test Cases))
    K --> L[MLLM Evaluation]
    L --> M{{Automatic Evaluation Metrics}}

name: DiagnosisArena
intro: DiagnosisArena是一个全面且具有挑战性的基准，用于严格评估专业级诊断能力。它包含1113对分段患者病例及对应诊断，涵盖28个医学专科，数据来源于10个顶级医学期刊的临床病例报告。该基准旨在推动AI诊断推理能力的提升，以应对真实临床诊断挑战。
org: Shanghai Jiao Tong University, SPIRAL Lab, Generative AI Research Lab (GAIR), Shanghai Chest Hospita
build_method: 专家构建, 人工标注
task_type: 开放式问答, 生成, 选择题
intro_en: DiagnosisArena is a comprehensive and challenging benchmark designed to rigorously evaluate professional-level diagnostic capabilities. It includes 1,113 paired segmented patient cases and corresponding diagnoses across 28 medical specialties, sourced from clinical case reports in 10 top medical journals. This benchmark aims to advance AI diagnostic reasoning to address real-world clinical diagnostic challenges.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Clinical Case Reports) --> B[Sourcing from Medical Journals]
    B --> C(Raw Patient Cases)
    C --> D{{Expert Construction}}
    D --> E[Segmentation & Pairing]
    E --> F{{Manual Annotation}}
    F --> G[Expert Review]
    G --> H{Quality Check}
    H -->|Pass| I(Curated Dataset\n1,113 Cases, 28 Specialties)
    H -->|Fail| C
    I --> J[Model Evaluation]
    J --> K(Diagnostic Capabilities)

    style Output fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([Start]) --> DataSourcing(Identify 10 Top-Tier Medical Journals) 

    subgraph Data Acquisition["Data Sourcing & Collection"]
        DataSourcing --> DataCollection(Collect 4,175 Real-World Case Reports) 
        DataCollection --> RawData[/Raw Case Reports/]
    end

    subgraph Data Preparation["Data Structuring & Initial Filtering"]
        RawData --> DataSegmenting(Segment Raw Case Reports) 
        DataSegmenting --> StandardizedFormat[/Standardized Segmented Cases/]
        StandardizedFormat --> CaseInfo(Extract Case Information) 
        CaseInfo --> PhysicalExam(Extract Physical Examination Findings) 
        PhysicalExam --> DiagnosticTests(Extract Diagnostic Test Results) 
        DiagnosticTests --> GroundTruth(Identify Final Diagnosis as Ground Truth) 
        GroundTruth --> AIExpertAnalysis{AI Expert Analysis for Sufficiency?} 
        AIExpertAnalysis -- Yes --> AIReview(AI-based Review for Unambiguous Info) 
        AIExpertAnalysis -- No --> ExcludeInsufficient(Exclude Insufficient Cases) 
        AIReview --> KnowledgeFilter{Exclude Cases Solvable by Memorized Knowledge?} 
        KnowledgeFilter -- Yes --> ExcludeMemorized(Exclude Memorized Cases) 
        KnowledgeFilter -- No --> PreVerifiedCases[/Pre-Verified Cases/]
    end

    subgraph Quality Assurance["Expert-AI Collaborative Verification"]
        PreVerifiedCases --> LLMConsensus(Check Consensus from 8 Frontier LLMs) 
        LLMConsensus -- No Consensus --> ExcludeLLM(Exclude Cases Lacking LLM Consensus) 
        LLMConsensus -- Consensus --> PhysicianApproval(Board-Certified Physician Approval) 
        PhysicianApproval -- No Approval --> ExcludePhysician(Exclude Cases Lacking Physician Approval) 
        PhysicianApproval -- Approved --> FinalCases[/1,113 Structured Clinical Cases/]
    end

    subgraph Benchmark Finalization["Benchmark Output"]
        FinalCases --> BenchmarkOutput(DiagnosisArena Benchmark) 
        BenchmarkOutput --> End([End])
    end

name: Disco-X
intro: DiscoX是一个针对专家领域语篇级翻译的新基准，旨在解决现有评估方法主要关注片段级准确性和流畅性的不足。它包含200篇来自7个领域的专业文本，平均长度超过1700个词元。同时，该基准还开发了Metric-S，一个无参考的自动评估系统，用于评估翻译的准确性、流畅性和适当性。实验表明，即使是最先进的LLM在这些任务上的表现仍落后于人类专家，验证了DiscoX的难度。
eval_feature: 无参考评估, 细粒度评估
intro_en: DiscoX is a new benchmark for expert-domain discourse-level translation, designed to address the limitations of existing evaluations that mainly focus on segment-level accuracy and fluency. It includes 200 professional texts from 7 domains, with an average length of over 1700 tokens. The benchmark also introduces Metric-S, a reference-free automatic evaluation system for assessing translation accuracy, fluency, and appropriateness. Experiments show that even state-of-the-art LLMs still lag behind human experts on these tasks, confirming the difficulty of DiscoX.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Professional Text Sources) --> B[Expert Curation & Selection]
    B --> C{Domain & Quality Filtering}
    C -- Pass --> D[Text Preprocessing]
    C -- Fail --> L[Discard]
    D --> E{{Discourse Unit Identification}}
    E --> F[Translation Pair Alignment]
    F --> G{Expert Validation of Alignment}
    G -- Valid --> H(Disco-X Dataset\n200 Texts, 7 Domains)
    G -- Invalid --> B
    H --> I[Discourse-level Translation Evaluation]
    I --> J(Evaluation Metrics & Scores)
    J --> K[Model Performance Analysis]

name: DLC-Bench
intro: DLC-Bench是一个用于评估详细局部描述（DLC）模型的基准，旨在为图像和视频中的特定区域生成详细、准确的描述。该基准与“描述一切模型”（DAM）一同发布，其评估过程不依赖于人工标注的参考描述，而是采用创新的评估方法。DLC-Bench的数据集构建始于现有的分割数据集，并利用半监督学习（SSL）技术扩展到大量的网络图像。
build_method: 半监督学习, 迁移学习
modality: 文本, 图像, 视频, 多模态
scale: 包含7个不同粒度的子任务
intro_en: DLC-Bench is a benchmark for evaluating Detailed Local Captioning (DLC) models, designed to generate detailed and accurate descriptions of specific regions in images and videos. Released alongside the Description Anything Model (DAM), its evaluation process does not rely on manually annotated reference captions but uses an innovative assessment method. The DLC-Bench dataset is built upon existing segmentation datasets and expanded to a large number of web images using semi-supervised learning (SSL) techniques.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A[Large-scale Image/Video Data] --> B{{Description Anything Model (DAM)}}
    B --> C[Transfer Learning]
    C --> D[Semi-supervised Learning]
    D --> E[Generate Pseudo-Labels]
    E --> F{Quality Filtering}
    F -->|High Confidence| G(DLC-Bench Dataset)
    F -->|Low Confidence| H[Discard]
    G --> I[DLC Model Evaluation]
    I --> J[Automated Metrics]
    J --> K[Benchmark Results]
scale_en: Contains 7 items of sub-tasks with different granularity
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    style Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style Processing fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style Annotation fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style Evaluation fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style Output fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([Start]) --> Input[/Input: Benchmark Name, Paper URL, Description/]
    Input --> Parse(Parse Input String)
    Parse --> CheckURL{Is Paper URL arXiv?}

    CheckURL -- Yes --> DownloadPDF(Download PDF from arXiv) :::DataCollection
    DownloadPDF --> ExtractText(Extract Text from PDF) :::Processing
    ExtractText --> ReadPaper(Read Abstract & Methodology) :::DeepResearch
    ReadPaper --> IdentifyDLC_SDP(Identify DLC-SDP & DLC-Bench Details)

    subgraph DLC-SDP: Semi-supervised Data Pipeline
        direction LR
        DLC_SDP_Start([DLC-SDP Start]) --> Stage1(Stage 1: Leverage Existing Annotations) :::DataCollection
        Stage1 --> HumanAnnotated[/Human-Annotated Segmentation Datasets/] :::DataCollection
        HumanAnnotated --> VLMQuery(Query VLM for Keyword Expansion) :::Annotation
        VLMQuery --> DetailedCaptions[/Detailed Captions for Masked Regions/] :::Annotation
        DetailedCaptions --> ModelTraining(Train DAM with High-Quality Captions) :::Processing

        ModelTraining --> Stage2(Stage 2: SSL with Unlabeled Data) :::DataCollection
        Stage2 --> UnlabeledImages[/Unlabeled Web Images/] :::DataCollection
        UnlabeledImages --> MaskGen(Mask Generation using Open-Vocabulary Segmentation Models) :::Processing
        MaskGen --> GeneratedMasks[/Generated Object Masks/] :::Processing
        GeneratedMasks --> DescGen(DAM Generates Localized Descriptions) :::Annotation
        DescGen --> CLIPFilter(CLIP-based Confidence Filtering) :::QualityControl
        CLIPFilter -- High Quality --> DataExpansion(Add to Training Dataset) :::DataCollection
        CLIPFilter -- Low Quality --> Discard(Discard) :::QualityControl
        DataExpansion --> LLMSummary(LLM Summarizes Descriptions for Multi-Granularity) :::Processing
        LLMSummary --> FinalDLC_Data[/Final High-Quality DLC Data/] :::Output
    end

    IdentifyDLC_SDP --> DLC_SDP_Start
    FinalDLC_Data --> DLC_Bench_Intro(Introduce DLC-Bench for Evaluation)

    subgraph DLC-Bench: Evaluation
        direction LR
        DLC_Bench_Intro --> GenerateDesc(Model Generates Description for Masked Region) :::Evaluation
        GenerateDesc --> LLMJudge(LLM Judge Assesses Description) :::Evaluation
        LLMJudge --> QuestionSet[/Manually Curated Positive/Negative Questions/] :::Evaluation
        QuestionSet --> EvaluatePos(Evaluate Positive Attributes) :::Evaluation
        EvaluatePos -- Accurate --> AwardPoint(Award Point) :::Evaluation
        EvaluatePos -- Omission --> NoPoint(No Point) :::Evaluation
        EvaluatePos -- Factual Error --> Penalty(Incur Penalty) :::Evaluation
        EvaluatePos --> EvaluateNeg(Evaluate Negative Attributes) :::Evaluation
        EvaluateNeg -- Correctly Omitted --> AwardPoint
        EvaluateNeg -- Included --> Penalty
        AwardPoint --> Score(Aggregate Scores) :::Evaluation
        NoPoint --> Score
        Penalty --> Score
        Score --> FinalBenchmark(Final DLC-Bench Score) :::Output
    end

    CheckURL -- No --> NoPaper(No arXiv Paper Found) :::QualityControl
    NoPaper --> End([End])
    FinalBenchmark --> End
    Discard --> End

    classDef Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff;
    classDef End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff;
    classDef DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff;
    classDef Processing fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff;
    classDef Annotation fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff;
    classDef QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff;
    classDef Evaluation fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff;
    classDef Output fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff;
flowchart_zh:
flowchart TD
    style Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
