    Start([开始]) --> Input[/输入: 基准名称, 论文URL, 描述/]
    Input --> Parse(解析输入字符串)
    Parse --> CheckURL{论文URL是arXiv吗?}

    CheckURL -- 是 --> DownloadPDF(从arXiv下载PDF) :::DataCollection
    DownloadPDF --> ExtractText(从PDF提取文本) :::Processing
    ExtractText --> ReadPaper(阅读摘要和方法) :::DeepResearch
    ReadPaper --> IdentifyDLC_SDP(识别DLC-SDP和DLC-Bench细节)

    subgraph DLC-SDP: 半监督学习数据管道
        direction LR
        DLC_SDP_Start([DLC-SDP 开始]) --> Stage1(阶段1: 利用现有标注) :::DataCollection
        Stage1 --> HumanAnnotated[/人工标注的分割数据集/] :::DataCollection
        HumanAnnotated --> VLMQuery(查询VLM进行关键词扩展) :::Annotation
        VLMQuery --> DetailedCaptions[/蒙版区域的详细描述/] :::Annotation
        DetailedCaptions --> ModelTraining(使用高质量描述训练DAM) :::Processing

        ModelTraining --> Stage2(阶段2: 使用未标注数据进行SSL) :::DataCollection
        Stage2 --> UnlabeledImages[/未标注的网络图像/] :::DataCollection
        UnlabeledImages --> MaskGen(使用开放词汇分割模型生成蒙版) :::Processing
        MaskGen --> GeneratedMasks[/生成的对象蒙版/] :::Processing
        GeneratedMasks --> DescGen(DAM生成局部描述) :::Annotation
        DescGen --> CLIPFilter(基于CLIP的置信度过滤) :::QualityControl
        CLIPFilter -- 高质量 --> DataExpansion(添加到训练数据集) :::DataCollection
        CLIPFilter -- 低质量 --> Discard(丢弃) :::QualityControl
        DataExpansion --> LLMSummary(LLM总结多粒度描述) :::Processing
        LLMSummary --> FinalDLC_Data[/最终高质量DLC数据/] :::Output
    end

    IdentifyDLC_SDP --> DLC_SDP_Start
    FinalDLC_Data --> DLC_Bench_Intro(引入DLC-Bench进行评估)

    subgraph DLC-Bench: 评估
        direction LR
        DLC_Bench_Intro --> GenerateDesc(模型生成蒙版区域描述) :::Evaluation
        GenerateDesc --> LLMJudge(LLM评判员评估描述) :::Evaluation
        LLMJudge --> QuestionSet[/人工整理的正面/负面问题集/] :::Evaluation
        QuestionSet --> EvaluatePos(评估正面属性) :::Evaluation
        EvaluatePos -- 准确 --> AwardPoint(奖励分数) :::Evaluation
        EvaluatePos -- 遗漏 --> NoPoint(无分数) :::Evaluation
        EvaluatePos -- 事实错误 --> Penalty(扣分) :::Evaluation
        EvaluatePos --> EvaluateNeg(评估负面属性) :::Evaluation
        EvaluateNeg -- 正确省略 --> AwardPoint
        EvaluateNeg -- 包含 --> Penalty
        AwardPoint --> Score(汇总分数) :::Evaluation
        NoPoint --> Score
        Penalty --> Score
        Score --> FinalBenchmark(最终DLC-Bench分数) :::Output
    end

    CheckURL -- 否 --> NoPaper(未找到arXiv论文) :::QualityControl
    NoPaper --> End([结束])
    FinalBenchmark --> End
    Discard --> End

    classDef Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff;
    classDef End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff;
    classDef DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff;
    classDef Processing fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff;
    classDef Annotation fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff;
    classDef QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff;
    classDef Evaluation fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff;
    classDef Output fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff;

name: DUDE
intro: DUDE（Document Understanding Dataset and Evaluation）旨在重新评估文档AI领域当前的方法论，并推动创建更具实践导向的基准。该基准通过引入基于多行业、多领域、多页视觉丰富文档（VRDs）的新型问题、答案和文档布局，以解决视觉丰富文档理解研究进展停滞的问题。DUDE致力于通过多任务和多领域评估设置来更准确地模拟现实世界情况，以促进在低资源设置下的强大泛化和适应能力。
org: KU Leuven, Contract.fit, Instabase, Computer Vision Center, Universitat Autònoma de Barcelona, Snowf
eval_feature: 多行业, 多领域, 多页, 多任务, 泛化能力, 适应性
eval_feature_en: Multi-industry, multi-domain, multi-page, multi-task, generalization ability, adaptability
intro_en: DUDE (Document Understanding Dataset and Evaluation) aims to reassess current methodologies in the document AI field and promote the creation of more practical benchmarks. It addresses the stagnation in visual-rich document (VRD) understanding research by introducing new questions, answers, and document layouts based on multi-industry, multi-domain, multi-page VRDs. DUDE strives to more accurately simulate real-world scenarios through multi-task and multi-domain evaluation settings, fostering robust generalization and adaptability in low-resource environments.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Existing VRD Datasets) --> C[Data Collection]
    B(Real-world Document Sources) --> C
    C --> D[Document Parsing & Structuring]
    D --> E{{Hybrid Processing Methods}}
    E --> F[Question & Answer Generation]
    F --> G[Document Content Augmentation]
    G --> H{Quality Assurance Check}
    H -->|Pass| I(DUDE Dataset
New Q&A, VRD)
    H -->|Fail| J[Data Refinement]
    J --> E
    I --> K[Model Training & Evaluation]
    K --> L[Performance Analysis]
    L --> M(Benchmark Results)
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Data Sourcing & Collection
        A([Start]) --> B[/Initial Task Input: Benchmark Name, Paper URL, Description/]
        B --> C(Identify Publicly Accessible Paper URL)
        C --> D{Is Paper URL arXiv?}
        D -- Yes --> E(Navigate to arXiv Paper)
        D -- No --> F(Search for Publicly Accessible Paper)
        F --> G{Public Paper Found?}
        G -- Yes --> H(Navigate to Public Paper)
        G -- No --> I([End: Paper Not Accessible])
        E --> J(Download PDF from arXiv)
        H --> J
        J --> K(Extract Abstract & Methodology from PDF)
        K --> L(Manual Document Collection)
        L --> M[/Aggregated Websites: archive.org, wikimedia.org, documentcloud.org/]
        M --> N(Formulate Keyword Queries)
        N --> O{Public Domain / Permissive License?}
        O -- Yes --> P(Filter Documents by License)
        P --> Q(Select Visually Distinctive Documents)
        Q --> R(Remove Controversial/Privacy-Sensitive Content)
        R --> S[/Raw Document Corpus/]
    end

    subgraph Question & Answer Generation
        S --> T(VQA Paradigm for VRDs)
        T --> U(Generate Diverse Questions)
        U --> U1(Layout-Navigating Questions)
        U --> U2(Compositional Questions)
        U --> U3(Arithmetic/Comparison Questions)
        U --> U4(Multi-hop Questions)
        U --> V(Define Answer Types: Extractive, Abstractive, List, None, Numerical, Dates, Yes/No)
        V --> W[/Question-Answer Specifications/]
    end

    subgraph Annotation & Quality Control
        W --> X[Phase 1: All MTurkers Provide QA Pairs]
        X --> Y{Manual Review of QA Pairs?}
        Y -- Yes --> Z(Promote High-Quality QA Pairs to Final Dataset)
        Y -- No --> AA[Phase 2: Remaining QA Pairs to All MTurkers]
        AA --> BB(MTurkers Answer Questions from Previous Round)
        BB --> CC[/Question, Answer Variant 1, Answer Variant 2 Triples/]
        CC --> DD(Evaluate Inter-Answer ANLS > 0.8?)
        DD -- Yes --> Z
        DD -- No --> EE[Phase 3: Outlier QA Triples to Best MTurkers]
        EE --> FF(Best MTurkers Decide Correctness)
        FF --> GG{Overrule Previous Variants?}
        GG -- Yes --> HH(Corrected QA Pair)
        GG -- No --> HH
        HH --> II(Check for Outliers in Decisions)
        II --> JJ[/Final Annotated QA Pairs/]
    end

    subgraph Evaluation & Final Benchmark
        JJ --> KK(Split Data: Training, Validation, Test Sets)
        KK --> LL(Define Evaluation Metrics)
        LL --> LL1(ANLS: Normalized Levenshtein Similarity)
        LL --> LL2(ECE: Expected Calibration Error)
        LL --> LL3(AURC: Area-Under-Risk-Coverage-Curve)
        LL --> MM(Establish Human Baseline: 74.76 ANLS)
        MM --> NN(Evaluate Reference Models: BERT, Longformer, T5, GPT-3, Hi-VT5)
        NN --> OO(Analyze Model Performance Factors: Textual, Layout, Visual Elements, Fine-tuning, Size, Input Length)
        OO --> PP[/Performance Analysis & Insights/]
        PP --> QQ([End: DUDE Benchmark])
    end

        A([开始]) --> B[/初始任务输入：基准名称、论文URL、描述/]
        B --> C(识别可公开访问的论文URL)
        C --> D{论文URL是arXiv链接吗？}
        D -- 是 --> E(导航到arXiv论文)
        D -- 否 --> F(搜索可公开访问的论文)
        F --> G{找到公开论文了吗？}
        G -- 是 --> H(导航到公开论文)
        G -- 否 --> I([结束：论文不可访问])
        E --> J(从arXiv下载PDF)
        H --> J
        J --> K(从PDF中提取摘要和方法论)
        K --> L(手动文档收集)
        L --> M[/聚合网站：archive.org, wikimedia.org, documentcloud.org/]
        M --> N(制定关键词查询)
        N --> O{公共领域/许可协议？}
        O -- 是 --> P(按许可协议筛选文档)
        P --> Q(选择视觉上独特的文档)
        Q --> R(移除有争议/隐私敏感内容)
        R --> S[/原始文档语料库/]
    end

    subgraph 问题与答案生成
        S --> T(VRD的VQA范式)
        T --> U(生成多样化问题)
        U --> U1(布局导航问题)
        U --> U2(组合问题)
        U --> U3(算术/比较问题)
        U --> U4(多跳问题)
        U --> V(定义答案类型：抽取式、抽象式、列表、无、数值、日期、是/否)
        V --> W[/问题-答案规范/]
    end

    subgraph 标注与质量控制
        W --> X[阶段1：所有MTurkers提供问答对]
        X --> Y{人工审查问答对？}
        Y -- 是 --> Z(将高质量问答对提升至最终数据集)
        Y -- 否 --> AA[阶段2：剩余问答对交给所有MTurkers]
        AA --> BB(MTurkers回答上一轮的问题)
        BB --> CC[/问题、答案变体1、答案变体2三元组/]
        CC --> DD(评估答案间ANLS > 0.8?)
        DD -- 是 --> Z
        DD -- 否 --> EE[阶段3：异常问答三元组交给最佳MTurkers]
        EE --> FF(最佳MTurkers决定正确性)
        FF --> GG{推翻之前的变体？}
        GG -- 是 --> HH(修正后的问答对)
        GG -- 否 --> HH
        HH --> II(检查决策中的异常值)
        II --> JJ[/最终标注问答对/]
    end

    subgraph 评估与最终基准
        JJ --> KK(拆分数据：训练集、验证集、测试集)
        KK --> LL(定义评估指标)
        LL --> LL1(ANLS：归一化莱文斯坦相似度)
        LL --> LL2(ECE：预期校准误差)
        LL --> LL3(AURC：风险覆盖曲线下面积)
        LL --> MM(建立人类基线：74.76 ANLS)
        MM --> NN(评估参考模型：BERT, Longformer, T5, GPT-3, Hi-VT5)
        NN --> OO(分析模型性能因素：文本、布局、视觉元素、微调、大小、输入长度)
        OO --> PP[/性能分析与洞察/]
        PP --> QQ([结束：DUDE基准])
    end

    style A fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style I fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style QQ fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    style L fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

name: DynaMath
intro: DynaMath是一个动态视觉数学基准，旨在深入评估视觉语言模型（VLMs）的数学推理鲁棒性。该基准包含501个高质量、多主题的种子问题，每个问题都以Python程序表示，通过精心设计和标注，可以自动生成大量具体的变体问题。DynaMath通过评估VLM在不同输入条件下的表现，来衡量其泛化能力，揭示了当前VLM在数学推理方面的局限性。
eval_feature: 动态更新, 过程评估
intro_en: DynaMath is a dynamic visual math benchmark designed to thoroughly evaluate the mathematical reasoning robustness of visual language models (VLMs). It contains 501 high-quality, multi-topic seed problems, each represented as a Python program. Through careful design and annotation, a large number of specific variant problems can be automatically generated. DynaMath measures VLMs' generalization ability by assessing their performance under different input conditions, revealing current limitations in VLMs' mathematical reasoning.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Seed Problems\n501 Python Programs) --> B[Careful Design & Annotation]
    B --> C{{Automatic Generation Engine}}
    C --> D[Generate Diverse Problem Instances]
    D --> E[Generate Visual Representations]
    E --> F{Automated Quality Checks}
    F --|Pass| --> G[Human Review & Validation]
    F --|Fail| --> K[Discard Low-Quality]
    G --> H(DynaMath Benchmark Dataset)
    H --> I[VLM Mathematical Reasoning Evaluation]
    I --> J[Robustness Assessment]
scale_en: 501 items seed questions, 5010 items specific questions

name: EgoTempo
intro: EgoTempo是一个专门为评估多模态大模型在自我中心视频中时间理解能力而设计的数据集。它通过整合整个视频的信息来强调任务，确保模型需要依赖时间模式而非静态线索或预先存在的知识。实验表明，当前的多模态大模型在自我中心视频的时间推理方面仍有不足。
modality: 视频, 文本, 多模态
intro_en: EgoTempo is a dataset specifically designed to evaluate the temporal understanding ability of multimodal large models in egocentric videos. It emphasizes the task by integrating information from the entire video, ensuring models rely on temporal patterns rather than static cues or prior knowledge. Experiments show that current multimodal large models still have limitations in temporal reasoning for egocentric videos.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Raw Egocentric Videos) --> B[Video Data Collection]
    B --> C[Temporal Event Annotation]
    C --> D[Multimodal Feature Extraction]
    D --> E{Temporal Pattern Verification}
    E -->|Pass| F[Data Integration & Alignment]
    E -->|Fail| G[Re-annotation/Filtering]
    G --> C
    F --> H(EgoTempo Dataset)
    H --> I[Model Training/Fine-tuning]
    I --> J[Temporal Understanding Evaluation]
    J --> K[Performance Analysis]

name: EHRSQL
intro: EHRSQL是针对电子健康记录的实用文本到SQL基准，通过收集222名医院工作人员的提问并关联MIMIC-III和eICU数据库，评估QA模型在结构化EHR数据上的SQL生成和时间表达式理解能力。
build_method: 人工标注，医院工作人员提问
eval_feature: 生成反映医院广泛需求的SQL查询，理解时间表达式，区分问题可回答性
scale: 基于222名医院工作人员提问
build_method_en: Manual annotation, questions from hospital staff
eval_feature_en: Generate SQL queries reflecting the broad needs of hospitals, understand time expressions, distinguish question answerability
intro_en: EHRSQL is a practical text-to-SQL benchmark for electronic health records. It collects questions from 222 hospital staff and links them to the MIMIC-III and eICU databases to evaluate QA models' SQL generation and temporal expression understanding on structured EHR data.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Raw Electronic Health Records) --> B[EHR Data Extraction]
    B --> C[Data Structuring & Schema Mapping]
    C --> D[Natural Language Question Generation]
    D --> E[Corresponding SQL Query Generation]
    E --> F{{Expert Construction Method}}
    F --> G{Expert Review & Validation}
    G -- Validated --> H(High-Quality NL-SQL Pairs)
    G -- Rejected --> I[Refinement/Discard]
    H --> J(EHRSQL Benchmark Dataset)
    J --> K[Model Evaluation]
    K -- using --> L{{NL-to-SQL Models}}
scale_en: Based on questions from 222 hospital staff

name: EmbSpatial-Bench
intro: 大型视觉语言模型（LVLMs）的快速发展预示了其在具身智能方面的潜力，然而，具身环境中空间理解的关键能力尚未得到充分评估。EmbSpatial-Bench旨在评估LVLMs的具身空间理解能力。该基准测试从具身场景中自动生成，涵盖了六种以自我为中心的空间关系，揭示了当前LVLMs（包括GPT-4V）在空间理解方面的不足。同时，该研究还提出了EmbSpatial-SFT，一个旨在提升LVLMs具身空间理解能力的指令微调数据集。
eval_feature: 具身空间理解, 自我中心视角, 指令微调数据集
eval_feature_en: Embodied Spatial Understanding, Egocentric Perspective, Instruction Fine-tuning Dataset
intro_en: The rapid development of large vision-language models (LVLMs) highlights their potential in embodied intelligence; however, key spatial understanding abilities in embodied environments remain under-evaluated. EmbSpatial-Bench is designed to assess the embodied spatial understanding capabilities of LVLMs. This benchmark is automatically generated from embodied scenarios and covers six types of egocentric spatial relationships, revealing current shortcomings in spatial understanding among LVLMs, including GPT-4V. Additionally, the study introduces EmbSpatial-SFT, an instruction fine-tuning dataset aimed at enhancing LVLMs' embodied spatial understanding.

name: EMMA
intro: EMMA（Enhanced MultiModal reAsoning）是一个旨在评估多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）有机多模态推理能力的基准测试。它涵盖数学、物理、化学和编程等领域，任务需要跨模态推理，不能仅通过单一模态独立解决。对现有MLLMs的评估显示，在处理复杂多模态和多步推理任务方面存在显著局限性，突显了改进多模态架构和训练范式的必要性。
org: University of Electronic Science and Technology of China, Sun Yat-sen University, University of Wash
intro_en: EMMA (Enhanced MultiModal reAsoning) is a benchmark designed to evaluate the organic multimodal reasoning abilities of multimodal large language models (MLLMs). It covers fields such as mathematics, physics, chemistry, and programming, requiring cross-modal reasoning that cannot be solved by a single modality alone. Evaluations of existing MLLMs reveal significant limitations in handling complex multimodal and multi-step reasoning tasks, highlighting the need to improve multimodal architectures and training paradigms.

name: Encyclo-K
intro: Encyclo-K是一个基于语句的基准测试，它重新思考了基准测试的构建方式。其核心思想是知识语句而非问题可以作为策展单位，并可以从中构建问题。该基准测试通过从权威教科书中提取独立的知识语句，并在测试时通过随机抽样动态组合成评估问题，以解决数据污染、单知识点评估和昂贵领域专家标注的局限性。
org: Institute of Automation of the Chinese Academy Sciences, University of Manchester, Peking University
build_method: 自动生成, 网络爬取
eval_feature: 动态更新, 防污染, 思维链
intro_en: Encyclo-K is a statement-based benchmark that rethinks benchmark construction. Its core idea is that knowledge statements, rather than questions, serve as the curation units from which questions can be generated. This benchmark extracts independent knowledge statements from authoritative textbooks and dynamically assembles evaluation questions through random sampling during testing, addressing limitations such as data contamination, single-knowledge-point evaluation, and costly domain expert annotation.

    E --> F[Expert Validation]
    F --> G{Quality Check Passed?}
    G -->|Yes| H(Encyclo-K Dataset)
    G -->|No| E
    H --> I[Model Evaluation]

        filter_criteria -->|筛选出不相关/低质量| apply_filter
        filtered_statements[/筛选后的陈述/]
        style filtered_statements fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
        apply_filter -->|高质量陈述| filtered_statements
    end

    subgraph 陈述标注
        annotation_guidelines(制定标注指南)
        style annotation_guidelines fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
        human_annotation(人工标注陈述)
        style human_annotation fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
        annotation_review(审查标注数据)
        style annotation_review fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
        filtered_statements -->|创建详细说明| annotation_guidelines
        annotation_guidelines -->|标注特定属性| human_annotation
        human_annotation -->|确保一致性和准确性| annotation_review
        annotated_data[/标注后的陈述/]
        style annotated_data fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
        annotation_review -->|验证后的标注| annotated_data
    end

    subgraph 质量控制与验证
        qc_metrics(定义质量控制指标)
        style qc_metrics fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
        automated_qc(自动化质量检查)
        style automated_qc fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
        expert_review(专家复审边缘案例)
        style expert_review fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
        qc_decision{通过质量阈值？}
        style qc_decision fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
        annotated_data -->|建立阈值| qc_metrics
        qc_metrics -->|运行自动化脚本| automated_qc
        automated_qc -->|复审复杂案例| expert_review
        expert_review -->|对照标准检查| qc_decision
        qc_decision -->|否| human_annotation
    end

    subgraph 基准最终确定
        benchmark_assembly(组装最终基准)
        style benchmark_assembly fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
        documentation(生成文档)
        style documentation fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
        final_benchmark[/Encyclo-K 基准/]
        style final_benchmark fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
        qc_decision -->|是| benchmark_assembly
        benchmark_assembly -->|描述用法和功能| documentation
        documentation -->|准备发布| final_benchmark
    end

    final_benchmark --> end_node(["结束构建"])
    style end_node fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

name: EvalPlus
intro: EvalPlus是一个代码合成评估框架，旨在严格评估大型语言模型（LLMs）生成的代码的功能正确性。它通过LLM和基于变异的策略自动生成大量新的测试用例来增强现有评估数据集。EvalPlus能够发现LLM合成代码中大量未被检测到的错误，并揭示了测试不足可能导致的错误排名。
org: University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign, Nanjing University
intro_en: EvalPlus is a code synthesis evaluation framework designed to rigorously assess the functional correctness of code generated by large language models (LLMs). It enhances existing evaluation datasets by automatically generating numerous new test cases through LLMs and mutation-based strategies. EvalPlus can detect many previously undetected errors in LLM-synthesized code and reveals error rankings that may result from insufficient testing.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Existing Code Eval Datasets) --> B{{LLM-based Test Case Generation}}
    A --> C{{Mutation-based Test Case Generation}}
    B --> D(Generated Test Cases)
    C --> D
    D --> E[Integrate & Expand]
    E --> F{Test Case Validity Check}
    F -- Invalid --> G[Refine Test Cases]
    G --> D
    F -- Valid --> H(Enhanced EvalPlus Dataset)
    H --> I[Assess LLM Code Functionality]
    I --> J(Functional Correctness Report)
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Data Sourcing
        B[/Original Evaluation Dataset (e.g., HumanEval)/]:::output
    end

    subgraph Test Input Generation
        C(LLM-based Seed Input Generation):::data_collection
        D{Use ChatGPT for difficult/corner-case inputs?}:::decision
        E[/High-Quality Seed Inputs/]:::output
        F(Type-aware Mutation):::processing
        G[/Large Amount of Additional Test Inputs/]:::output
    end

    subgraph Validation & Quality Control
        H(Differential Testing):::quality_control
        I[/LLM-Generated Code/]:::data_collection
        J[/Ground-Truth Implementation/]:::data_collection
        K{f(x) = gt(x)?}:::decision
        L(Identify Wrong Code):::quality_control
    end

    subgraph Test-Suite Reduction (Optional)
        M(Greedy Set Cover Algorithm):::processing
        N(Mutation Analysis):::processing
        O(Empirical LLM Sample Killings):::processing
        P{Select valuable test-cases?}:::decision
        Q[/Reduced Test-Suite (HumanEval+-Mini)/]:::output
    end

    subgraph Output Datasets
        R[/EvalPlus Dataset/]:::output

    end

    subgraph Evaluation
        T(Assess Functional Correctness):::evaluation

        X(Improved LLM Evaluation):::output
    end

    A([Start: EvalPlus Benchmark Construction]):::start_end
    Y([End: Improved LLM Evaluation]):::start_end

name: EVMbench
intro: EVMbench是一个用于评估AI智能体在智能合约安全领域能力的基准。它通过检测、修补和利用智能合约漏洞来衡量智能体的能力。该基准包含来自40个代码库的120个精选漏洞，并在真实的以太坊执行环境中进行程序化评分。
build_method: 专家构建, 人工标注
task_type: Agent任务, 代码生成, 分类, 抽取
eval_feature: 领域特定, 结果导向
task_type_en: Agent Task, Code Generation, Classification, Extraction
intro_en: EVMbench is a benchmark for evaluating AI agents' capabilities in the field of smart contract security. It measures agents' abilities by detecting, patching, and exploiting smart contract vulnerabilities. The benchmark includes 120 selected vulnerabilities from 40 codebases and performs programmatic scoring in a real Ethereum execution environment.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A[Source: 40 Codebases] --> B[Collection: Programmatic Vulnerability Identification]
    B --> C{Selection: 120 Vulnerabilities}
    C --> D[Processing: Expert Construction]
    D --> E[Processing: Manual Annotation]
    E --> F{Quality Control: Expert Review}
    F --> G(Final Dataset: EVMbench
120 Vulnerabilities)
    G --> H[Evaluation: AI Agent Assessment]
    H --> I[Evaluation Metrics: Detection, Patching, Exploitation]
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD

    style OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    Start([Start]) -- Initializing Benchmark Creation --> DataSourcing[/Data Sourcing/]
    subgraph Phase1[Data Acquisition]
        DataSourcing[/Data Sourcing/] -- Identify Sources --> CollectRawData(Collect Raw Smart Contract Data)
        CollectRawData -- From Blockchain & Repositories --> InitialFiltering{Initial Filtering Criteria?}
        InitialFiltering -- Yes --> PreprocessData(Preprocess & Normalize Data)
        InitialFiltering -- No --> ReviewSources(Review Data Sources)
        PreprocessData -- Clean & Format --> StoreCleanData[/Cleaned Data Storage/]
        style DataSourcing DataCollection
        style CollectRawData DataCollection
        style InitialFiltering ProcessingFiltering
        style PreprocessData ProcessingFiltering
        style ReviewSources ProcessingFiltering
        style StoreCleanData OutputResult
    end
    StoreCleanData -- Input for Analysis --> VulnerabilityAnalysis(Vulnerability Analysis)
    subgraph Phase2[Vulnerability Identification & Test Case Generation]
        VulnerabilityAnalysis -- Static & Dynamic Analysis --> IdentifyVulnerabilities(Identify Potential Vulnerabilities)
        IdentifyVulnerabilities -- Manual Review & Expert Annotation --> AnnotateVulnerabilities(Annotate Vulnerabilities)
        AnnotateVulnerabilities -- Labeled Vulnerabilities --> GenerateTestCases(Generate Test Cases)
        GenerateTestCases -- Create Exploits/Scenarios --> TestCases[/Benchmark Test Cases/]
        style VulnerabilityAnalysis AnnotationLabeling
        style IdentifyVulnerabilities AnnotationLabeling
        style AnnotateVulnerabilities AnnotationLabeling
        style GenerateTestCases ProcessingFiltering
        style TestCases OutputResult
    end
    TestCases -- Input for Agent Evaluation --> AgentEvaluation(AI Agent Evaluation)
    subgraph Phase3[Agent Evaluation & Metric Calculation]
        AgentEvaluation -- Run Agents on Test Cases --> CollectAgentResults(Collect Agent Results)
        CollectAgentResults -- Raw Agent Outputs --> CalculateMetrics(Calculate Evaluation Metrics)
        CalculateMetrics -- Metrics: Precision, Recall, F1 --> MetricsReport[/Evaluation Metrics Report/]
        style AgentEvaluation EvaluationMetric
        style CollectAgentResults EvaluationMetric
        style CalculateMetrics EvaluationMetric
        style MetricsReport OutputResult
    end
    MetricsReport -- Review Performance --> QualityCheck{Meet Quality Thresholds?}
    subgraph Phase4[Quality Control & Finalization]
        QualityCheck -- No --> RefineBenchmark(Refine Benchmark & Re-evaluate)
        QualityCheck -- Yes --> DocumentBenchmark(Document Benchmark)
        RefineBenchmark -- Iterative Improvement --> AgentEvaluation
        DocumentBenchmark -- Prepare Documentation --> FinalBenchmark([Final EVMbench Benchmark])
        style QualityCheck QualityControl
        style RefineBenchmark QualityControl
        style DocumentBenchmark OutputResult
        style FinalBenchmark End
    end
    FinalBenchmark -- Ready for Use --> End([End])

    end
    MetricsReport -- Review Performance --> QualityCheck{达到质量阈值？}

name: FACTS Grounding
intro: FACTS Grounding 是一个在线排行榜和基准测试，旨在评估语言模型根据用户提示中提供的上下文生成事实准确文本的能力。该基准测试中的每个提示都包含一个用户请求和一个最长为 32k 词符的完整文档，要求模型生成完全基于所提供上下文的长篇回答。模型使用自动化的评判模型分两个阶段进行评估，确保回答既满足用户请求又完全基于所提供的文档。
org: Google DeepMind, Google Research, Google Cloud, Kaggle
eval_feature: 长文本, 防污染, 结果导向
intro_en: FACTS Grounding is an online leaderboard and benchmark designed to evaluate language models' ability to generate factually accurate text based on context provided in user prompts. Each prompt in this benchmark includes a user request and a full document up to 32k tokens, requiring the model to produce a long-form response entirely grounded in the given context. Models are evaluated in two stages using an automated judging model to ensure responses both satisfy the user request and are fully based on the provided document.

    E -->|Fail| G[Discard Invalid Data]
    F --> H{Quality Check}
    H -->|Pass| I(FACTS Grounding Dataset)
    H -->|Fail| F
    I --> J[Language Model Inference]
    J --> K[Factual Accuracy Evaluation]
    K --> L(Evaluation Results)
    L --> M[Online Leaderboard Update]

name: FEA-Bench
intro: FEA-Bench是一个用于评估大型语言模型（LLM）在代码仓库中执行增量开发能力的基准。它通过收集来自83个GitHub仓库的拉取请求，并利用基于规则和意图的过滤来构建专注于新功能开发任务实例。每个任务实例都配有相关的单元测试文件，以确保解决方案可以被验证，从而对LLM的自动化软件工程能力提供更全面的评估。
build_method: 网络爬取, 基于规则和意图的过滤
task_type: 代码生成, 代码编辑, 代码补全
task_type_en: Code Generation, Code Editing, Code Completion
build_method_en: Web Crawling, Rule- and Intent-based Filtering
intro_en: FEA-Bench is a benchmark designed to evaluate large language models' (LLM) ability to perform incremental development in code repositories. It collects pull requests from 83 GitHub repositories and uses rule-based and intent-based filtering to create task instances focused on new feature development. Each task instance includes associated unit test files to ensure solutions can be validated, providing a more comprehensive assessment of LLMs' automated software engineering capabilities.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A[GitHub Repositories] --> B{{Web Crawling}}
    B --> C(Raw Pull Requests)
    C --> D{Rule-based Filtering}
    D -->|Pass Rules| E(Rule-Filtered Pull Requests)
    D -->|Fail Rules| K[Discard Irrelevant PRs]
    E --> F{Intent-based Filtering}
    F -->|Focus on New Features| G(New Feature Development Instances)
    F -->|Not New Feature| L[Discard Other PRs]
    G --> H(FEA-Bench Dataset
LLM Incremental Development Tasks)
    H --> I[LLM Evaluation]
    I --> J[Benchmark Results]
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    style Start_Node fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style End_Node fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style Data_Collection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style Processing_Filtering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style Annotation_Labeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style Quality_Control fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style Evaluation_Metric fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style Output_Result fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([Start]) --> Initial_Scope(Identify Python packages from Top PyPI) 
    subgraph Repository Collection
        Initial_Scope --> Filter_License_PR{Has license & >1000 PRs?} 
        Filter_License_PR -- Yes --> Approx_600_Repos[/Approx. 600 Repositories/]
        Filter_License_PR -- No --> Discard_Repo(Discard Repository)
        Approx_600_Repos --> Exclude_SWE_Bench(Exclude SWE-bench repos)
        Exclude_SWE_Bench --> Default_Setup(Set up default pip install & pytest)
        Default_Setup --> Extract_20_PRs(Extract first 20 PRs with test file changes)
        Extract_20_PRs --> Check_UnitTest_Status{At least one unit test passed?}
        Check_UnitTest_Status -- Yes --> Identified_101_Packages[/101 Python Packages Identified/]
        Check_UnitTest_Status -- No --> Discard_Repo_2(Discard Repository)
    end

    Identified_101_Packages --> Crawl_PRs(Crawl all Pull Requests) 
    subgraph Task Collection and Filtering
        Crawl_PRs --> Initial_Task_Instances[/PRs with test file changes/]
        Initial_Task_Instances --> Extract_New_Components(Parse gold patch & extract new components)
        Extract_New_Components --> Filter_New_Components{Has new components & >25% edited lines?} 
        Filter_New_Components -- Yes --> GPT4o_Classify(Classify PR intent with GPT-4o)
        Filter_New_Components -- No --> Discard_Task_1(Discard Task Instance)
        GPT4o_Classify --> Filter_New_Feature{Classified as "new feature"?} 
        Filter_New_Feature -- Yes --> Setup_Env(Set up environment & testbed)
        Filter_New_Feature -- No --> Discard_Task_2(Discard Task Instance)
        Setup_Env --> Apply_Test_Patch(Apply test patch & run unit tests)
        Apply_Test_Patch --> Apply_Gold_Patch(Apply gold patch)
        Apply_Gold_Patch --> Rerun_Unit_Tests(Rerun unit tests)
        Rerun_Unit_Tests --> Check_Final_Status{All unit tests pass?}
        Check_Final_Status -- Yes --> Record_Test_Status(Record test status before/after gold patch)
        Check_Final_Status -- No --> Discard_Task_3(Discard Task Instance)
    end

    Record_Test_Status --> Final_Benchmark_Data[/1401 Task Instances from 83 Repositories/]
    subgraph Evaluation
        Final_Benchmark_Data --> Evaluate_LLMs(Evaluate LLMs on FEA-Bench)
        Evaluate_LLMs --> Execution_Metrics[/Execution-based Metrics/]
        Execution_Metrics --> LLM_Performance(Measure LLM performance)
        LLM_Performance --> DeepSeek_R1_Result[/DeepSeek-R1: ~10% Resolution/]
    end
    DeepSeek_R1_Result --> End([End])

    class Start_Node Start
    classDef End_Node fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    classDef Quality_Control fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff

    Record_Test_Status --> Final_Benchmark_Data[/来自83个仓库的1401个任务实例/]
    subgraph Evaluation["评估"]

name: FinSearchComp
intro: FinSearchComp是一个完全开源的Agent基准测试，旨在对金融搜索和推理能力进行真实、专家级别的评估。该基准包含三个任务：时间敏感数据获取、简单历史查询和复杂历史调查，以复现真实的金融分析师工作流程。通过70位专业金融专家的标注和严格的质量保证流程，确保了其难度和可靠性。它包含635个问题，涵盖全球和大中华区市场，并评估了21个模型。
build_method: 专家构建, 人工标注
eval_feature: 思维链, 过程评估, 动态更新
intro_en: FinSearchComp is a fully open-source Agent benchmark designed for realistic, expert-level evaluation of financial search and reasoning capabilities. It includes three tasks: time-sensitive data retrieval, simple historical queries, and complex historical investigations, replicating real financial analyst workflows. With annotations from 70 professional financial experts and a rigorous quality assurance process, it ensures both difficulty and reliability. The benchmark contains 635 questions covering global and Greater China markets and evaluates 21 models.

name: FLAMES
intro: FLAMES是一个针对大语言模型价值观对齐的中文评测基准，由上海人工智能实验室和复旦大学共同开发。它包含一套精心设计的对抗性提示，旨在揭示模型在安全性、公平性等方面的深层价值观对齐缺陷。该基准不仅涵盖了通用的无害原则，还融入了和谐等具有中国文化特色的价值观维度。
org: Shanghai Artificial Intelligence Laboratory, Fudan University
build_method: 专家构建, 人工标注
task_type: 安全测试, 开放式问答
intro_en: FLAMES is a Chinese evaluation benchmark for value alignment in large language models, jointly developed by Shanghai AI Laboratory and Fudan University. It includes a set of carefully designed adversarial prompts aimed at revealing deep value alignment flaws in areas such as safety and fairness. The benchmark covers general harmlessness principles and incorporates culturally specific values like harmony.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(LLM Safety & Alignment Principles) --> B[Expert Knowledge Elicitation]
    B --> C[Adversarial Prompt Design]
    C --> D{{Prompt Generation Tools}}
    D --> E[Initial Prompt Set]
    E --> F{Prompt Effectiveness Check}
    F -- Fail --> G[Refine Prompts]
    G --> C
    F -- Pass --> H[Manual Annotation & Review]
    H --> I[Categorize Prompts (Safety/Fairness)]
    I --> J(FLAMES Adversarial Prompt Dataset)
    J --> K[LLM Evaluation]
    K --> L[Value Alignment Analysis]

    end

    subgraph Prompt Generation["Prompt Generation"]
        E --> F(Design Adversarial Prompts)

    end

name: FOFO
intro: 本文提出了FOFO，一个开创性的基准，用于评估大型语言模型（LLM）遵循复杂、特定领域格式的能力，这是它们作为AI代理应用的一个关键但未被充分研究的能力。FOFO通过AI与人类协作的方法，开发了多样的真实世界格式和指令，填补了现有基准在评估格式遵循能力方面的空白。
org: Salesforce Research, University of Illinois at Chicago, Pennsylvania State University
task_type: 格式化文本生成, 指令遵循
intro_en: This paper introduces FOFO, a pioneering benchmark designed to evaluate large language models' (LLM) ability to follow complex, domain-specific formats—a critical yet underexplored capability for their use as AI agents. FOFO employs AI-human collaboration to develop diverse real-world formats and instructions, addressing the gap in existing benchmarks for assessing format adherence.

name: FRAMES
intro: FRAMES（Factuality, Retrieval, And reasoning MEasurement Set）是一个高质量数据集，旨在测试大型语言模型（LLMs）在生成最终答案时的事实性、检索能力和推理能力。该基准测试提供了一个统一的框架，用于评估LLMs在端到端检索增强生成（RAG）场景中的性能。FRAMES包含具有挑战性的多跳问题，需要整合来自多个来源的信息。
org: Harvard University, Google DeepMind, New York University, Duke University, University of Massachuset
intro_en: FRAMES (Factuality, Retrieval, And reasoning MEasurement Set) is a high-quality dataset designed to test large language models (LLMs) on factuality, retrieval ability, and reasoning when generating final answers. This benchmark provides a unified framework to evaluate LLMs' performance in end-to-end retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) scenarios. FRAMES includes challenging multi-hop questions that require integrating information from multiple sources.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A[Initial Data Sources] --> B[Task Definition & Design]
    B --> C{{Hybrid Data Generation}}
    C --> D[Manual Annotation]
    D --> E{Annotation Review & Conflict Resolution}
    E -->|Pass| F[Data Cleaning & Formatting]
    E -->|Fail| D
    F --> G[Expert Validation]
    G --> H(FRAMES Dataset\nFactuality, Retrieval, Reasoning)
    H --> I[LLM Answer Generation]
    I --> J[Automated & Manual Evaluation]
    J --> K[Performance Analysis]

    start_node([Start])

    subgraph Data Sourcing & Initial Collection["Data Sourcing & Initial Collection"]
        DS1[/Wikipedia Articles/]:::data_collection
        DC1(Prompt LLM for Multi-Article Questions):::data_collection
        DC2{LLM Questions Coherent & Factual?}:::processing
        DC3(High Hallucination Rate (>30%)):::quality_control
        DC4(LLM Struggles with >4 Articles):::quality_control
        DC5(Manual Cleaning of Synthetic Data):::processing
        DC6(Evaluate LLM on Cleaned Data):::evaluation
        DC7[/Challenging Questions (Accuracy ~32%)/]:::output
    end

    subgraph Human Annotation & Refinement["Human Annotation & Refinement"]
        HA1(Define Core Instruction for Human Annotators):::annotation
        HA2(Annotators Create 824 Questions):::annotation
        HA3(Annotators Provide Correct Responses):::annotation
        HA4(Annotators List Required Wikipedia Articles):::annotation
        HA5(Annotators Label 5 Reasoning Types):::annotation
        HA6[/Questions with Reasoning Types/]:::data_collection
        HA7(Engage Vetted Expert Annotators):::annotation
    end

    subgraph Quality Control & Validation["Quality Control & Validation"]
        QC1(Re-annotation for Correctness & Groundedness):::quality_control
        QC2{Is Answer Correct & Grounded to Wikipedia?}:::quality_control
        QC3(Filter Out 5.5% Invalid Samples):::quality_control
        QC4(Analyze Dataset Statistics):::evaluation
        QC5[/2-15 Wikipedia Articles per Question/]:::output
        QC6[/Distribution of Article Count/]:::output
        QC7[/Distribution of Reasoning Types/]:::output
        QC8(Ensure Diverse Topics & Reasoning Types):::quality_control
    end

    subgraph Final Benchmark["Final Benchmark"]
        FB1[/FRAMES Dataset (824 Test Samples)/]:::output
        FB2(Open-Source Dataset for Public Use):::output
    end

    end_node([End])
    style end_node fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

name: FrontierScience
intro: FrontierScience是一个评估前沿语言模型专家级科学推理能力的基准测试。它通过奥林匹克竞赛问题和博士级别的开放式研究问题两个互补的轨道来解决现有科学基准的不足。该基准包含数百个问题，涵盖物理、化学和生物学等子领域，并由国际奥林匹克奖牌获得者和博士科学家构建。它引入了基于细致评分标准的评估框架，以评估模型在解决研究任务过程中的能力。
org: OpenAI, Harvard University, University of Washington
build_method: 专家构建, 人工标注
metric: 基于细致评分标准的评估
task_type: 开放式问答, 选择题
scale: 数百个问题（包括160个开源黄金集）
intro_en: FrontierScience is a benchmark that evaluates cutting-edge language models' expert-level scientific reasoning abilities. It addresses the limitations of existing science benchmarks through two complementary tracks: Olympiad competition problems and open-ended doctoral-level research questions. The benchmark includes hundreds of questions across subfields such as physics, chemistry, and biology, developed by international Olympiad medalists and PhD scientists. It introduces a fine-grained scoring-based evaluation framework to assess models' capabilities in tackling research tasks.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Olympiad Competition Problems) --> C[Expert Problem Formulation]
    B(Doctoral-level Research Questions) --> C
    C --> D[Content Refinement & Structuring]
    D --> E{{Manual Annotation}}
    E --> F{Expert Review & Validation}
    F -- Pass --> H(FrontierScience Benchmark Dataset)
    F -- Fail --> G[Iterative Revision]
    G --> D
    H --> I[Model Evaluation]
    I --> J[Scientific Reasoning Assessment]
scale_en: Hundreds of questions (including 160 items open-source gold set)
metric_en: Evaluation based on detailed scoring criteria
flowchart_en:

    style start_node fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    subgraph Data Sourcing
        A[Identify Core Scientific Disciplines] --> B[Select Reputable Scientific Databases/Journals]
        style A fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
        style B fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    end

    subgraph Data Collection
        B --> C[Automated Scraping of Scientific Articles & Abstracts]
        C --> D[Extraction of Key Scientific Concepts & Relations]
        style C fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
        style D fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    end

    subgraph Data Processing
        D --> E[Text Normalization & Tokenization]
        E --> F[Entity Recognition for Scientific Terms]
        style E fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
        style F fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    end

    subgraph Filtering
        F --> G{Remove Irrelevant Articles (e.g., editorials, news)?}
        G -- Yes --> H[Filter for Language Quality & Readability]
        G -- No --> D
        style G fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
        style H fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    end

    H --> I{Data Volume > 100K Documents?}
    I -- Yes --> J[Develop Annotation Guidelines for Scientific Tasks]
    I -- No --> C
    style I fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff

    subgraph Annotation Phase
        J --> K[Human Annotation: Expert Scientists Generate Task Instances & Solutions]
        K --> L[Human Annotation: Cross-Annotation for Inter-Annotator Agreement (IAA)]
        style J fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff

        style L fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    end

    subgraph Quality Control Phase
        L --> M[Automated Consistency Checks on Annotations]

        style M fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff

    N --> O{Annotation Quality Score > 0.8?}
    O -- Yes --> P[Split Data into Train/Validation/Test Sets (80/10/10)]

    style O fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff

    subgraph Benchmark Assembly

        style R fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    R --> end_node([FrontierScience Benchmark Available])
    style end_node fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

name: GAIA
intro: GAIA是一个为通用AI助手设计的基准测试，旨在评估AI在现实世界问题中的表现。这些问题需要推理、多模态处理、网页浏览和工具使用等基本能力。GAIA的问题对人类来说概念简单，但对最先进的AI来说极具挑战性，旨在推动通用人工智能（AGI）的发展。
eval_feature: 思维链, 零样本, 多模态, 工具使用, 网页浏览
eval_feature_en: Chain-of-Thought, Zero-shot, Multimodal, Tool Use, Web Browsing
intro_en: GAIA is a benchmark designed for general AI assistants to evaluate their performance on real-world problems. These problems require core abilities such as reasoning, multimodal processing, web browsing, and tool use. GAIA’s tasks are conceptually simple for humans but highly challenging for state-of-the-art AI, aiming to advance the development of artificial general intelligence (AGI).
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Real-world Scenarios) --> B[Problem Identification]
    B --> C[Task Formulation]
    C --> D{{Manual Annotation}}
    D --> E[Multimodal Data Integration]
    E --> F[Tool Use Scenario Design]
    F --> G{Task Complexity Check}
    G -->|Pass| H[Human Expert Review]
    G -->|Fail| C
    H --> I(GAIA Benchmark Dataset)
    I --> J[AI Assistant Evaluation]
    J --> K[Performance Analysis]
    K --> L(Evaluation Results)
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    subgraph "1. Benchmark Definition"
        A([Start]) --> B(Define LLM Agent Capabilities)
        B --> C(Define Real-world Environment)
        C --> D(Define Asynchronous Interaction Model)
        D --> E(Establish Evaluation Goals)
    end

    subgraph "2. Task & Scenario Design"
        E --> F(Design Diverse Agent Tasks)
        F --> G{Are Tasks Sufficiently Complex?}
        G -- Yes --> H(Generate Specific Scenarios)
        G -- No --> F
        H --> I(Integrate External Tools/APIs)
    end

    subgraph "3. Data Sourcing & Collection"
        I --> J[/Identify Real-world Data Sources/]
        J --> K(Collect Raw Interaction Logs)
        K --> L(Collect Environmental States)
        L --> M{Is Data Volume Sufficient?}
        M -- Yes --> N(Store Raw Data)
        M -- No --> J
    end

    subgraph "4. Data Preprocessing & Cleaning"
        N --> O(Clean Raw Data)
        O --> P(Structure Data for Agent I/O)
        P --> Q(Handle Asynchronous Event Sequencing)
        Q --> R{Are Data Formats Consistent?}
        R -- Yes --> S[/Preprocessed Data/]
        R -- No --> O
    end

    subgraph "5. Ground Truth & Annotation"
        S --> T(Define Task Success Criteria)
        T --> U(Human Annotation of Ground Truth)
        U --> V(Automated Ground Truth Generation)
        V --> W{Are Annotations Consistent?}
        W -- Yes --> X[/Annotated Ground Truth/]
        W -- No --> U
    end

    subgraph "6. Quality Control & Validation"
        X --> Y(Review Annotated Data by Experts)
        Y --> Z(Perform Inter-Annotator Agreement Check)
        Z --> AA{Annotation Quality High?}
        AA -- Yes --> BB(Validate Scenario Realism)
        AA -- No --> U
        BB --> CC[/Validated Benchmark Data/]
    end

    subgraph "7. Agent Execution & Evaluation"
        CC --> DD(Execute LLM Agents in Environment)
        DD --> EE(Record Agent Behaviors & Outcomes)
        EE --> FF(Define Evaluation Metrics)
        FF --> GG(Compute Performance Scores)
        GG --> HH{Are Results Statistically Significant?}
        HH -- Yes --> II[/Evaluation Results/]
        HH -- No --> DD
    end

    subgraph "8. Benchmark Finalization"
        II --> JJ(Analyze Agent Performance)
        JJ --> KK(Document Benchmark Methodology)
        KK --> LL(Release Benchmark Dataset)
        LL --> MM([End])
    end

    style A fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style M fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style N fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style J fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style K fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

name: Gaia2
intro: Gaia2是一个用于在真实、异步环境中评估大型语言模型代理的基准。它引入了独立于代理行为而演变的环境场景，要求代理在时间限制下操作、适应动态和嘈杂的事件、解决模糊性并与其他代理协作。每个场景都配有写操作验证器，支持细粒度的动作级评估。该基准建立在开源的ARE平台之上，旨在推动下一代实用代理系统的发展。
modality: 文本, 图像, 多模态
task_type: Agent评估, 复杂推理, 动态适应, 多步推理
eval_feature: 异步, 动态环境, 多Agent协作, 时间限制, 噪声鲁棒性, 模糊性解决
task_type_en: Agent Evaluation, Complex Reasoning, Dynamic Adaptation, Multi-step Reasoning
eval_feature_en: Asynchronous, dynamic environment, multi-agent collaboration, time constraints, noise robustness, ambiguity resolution
intro_en: Gaia2 is a benchmark for evaluating large language model agents in real, asynchronous environments. It introduces environment scenarios that evolve independently of agent behavior, requiring agents to operate within time constraints, adapt to dynamic and noisy events, resolve ambiguities, and collaborate with other agents. Each scenario includes a write operation validator, enabling fine-grained, action-level evaluation. Built on the open-source ARE platform, this benchmark aims to advance the development of next-generation practical agent systems.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart TD
    A[定义基准目标] --> B[构建Agents Research Environments (ARE) 平台]
    B --> C[设计移动环境 (Smartphone-like)]
    C --> D[选择并集成应用和工具 (e.g., Email, Messaging, Calendar)]
    D --> E(合成数据生成)
    E --> F(PersonaHub人物采样)
    F --> G[跨应用数据一致性传播]
    G --> H[Human Annotation场景 (1120个)]
    H --> I[创建Gaia2场景集 (800个独特可Validation场景)]
    I --> J[创建Gaia2-mini子集 (160个场景)]
    J --> K[场景增强 (额外320个场景)]
    K --> L[开发写操作Validation器 (ARE Verifier)]
    L --> M[定义Evaluation指标 (细粒度、动作级别Evaluation)]
    M --> N[基于ReAct循环的代理编排]
    N --> O[执行代理Evaluation]
    O --> P[Validation器检查状态变化动作] 
    P --> Q{Validation结果}
    Q -- 通过 --> R[Final Dataset组成]
    Q -- 失败 --> H
    R --> S[发布Gaia2基准]
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    subgraph "1. Benchmark Definition"
        A([Start]) --> B(Define LLM Agent Capabilities)
        B --> C(Define Real-world Environment)
        C --> D(Define Asynchronous Interaction Model)
        D --> E(Establish Evaluation Goals)
    end

    subgraph "2. Task & Scenario Design"
        E --> F(Design Diverse Agent Tasks)
        F --> G{Are Tasks Sufficiently Complex?}
        G -- Yes --> H(Generate Specific Scenarios)
        G -- No --> F
        H --> I(Integrate External Tools/APIs)
    end

    subgraph "3. Data Sourcing & Collection"
        I --> J[/Identify Real-world Data Sources/]
        J --> K(Collect Raw Interaction Logs)
        K --> L(Collect Environmental States)
        L --> M{Is Data Volume Sufficient?}
        M -- Yes --> N(Store Raw Data)
        M -- No --> J
    end

    subgraph "4. Data Preprocessing & Cleaning"
        N --> O(Clean Raw Data)
        O --> P(Structure Data for Agent I/O)
        P --> Q(Handle Asynchronous Event Sequencing)
        Q --> R{Are Data Formats Consistent?}
        R -- Yes --> S[/Preprocessed Data/]
        R -- No --> O
    end

    subgraph "5. Ground Truth & Annotation"
        S --> T(Define Task Success Criteria)
        T --> U(Human Annotation of Ground Truth)
        U --> V(Automated Ground Truth Generation)
        V --> W{Are Annotations Consistent?}
        W -- Yes --> X[/Annotated Ground Truth/]
        W -- No --> U
    end

    subgraph "6. Quality Control & Validation"
        X --> Y(Review Annotated Data by Experts)
        Y --> Z(Perform Inter-Annotator Agreement Check)
        Z --> AA{Annotation Quality High?}
        AA -- Yes --> BB(Validate Scenario Realism)
        AA -- No --> U
        BB --> CC[/Validated Benchmark Data/]
    end

    subgraph "7. Agent Execution & Evaluation"
        CC --> DD(Execute LLM Agents in Environment)
        DD --> EE(Record Agent Behaviors & Outcomes)
        EE --> FF(Define Evaluation Metrics)
        FF --> GG(Compute Performance Scores)
        GG --> HH{Are Results Statistically Significant?}
        HH -- Yes --> II[/Evaluation Results/]
        HH -- No --> DD
    end

    subgraph "8. Benchmark Finalization"
        II --> JJ(Analyze Agent Performance)
        JJ --> KK(Document Benchmark Methodology)
        KK --> LL(Release Benchmark Dataset)
        LL --> MM([End])
    end

name: GaokaoBench
intro: GaokaoBench是一个直观的评测基准，它使用2010至2022年的中国高考题目来评估大型语言模型。该基准包含1781道客观题和1030道主观题，覆盖了理科和文科的9个科目。它采用零样本设置和人工评分相结合的方式，全面考察模型的语言理解、逻辑推理和生成能力。
org: 复旦大学、华东师范大学
build_method: 基于2010-2022年中国高考试题，通过自动化脚本和人工标注结合的方式构建
metric: Scoring Rate, Converted Total Score, Spearman Correlation, Kendall-Tau Correlation
task_type: 选择题, 开放式问答, 填空题, 改错题
task_type_en: Multiple Choice, Open-ended QA, Fill-in-the-blank, Error correction
build_method_en: Constructed based on 2010-2022 China Gaokao exam questions, combining automated scripts and manual annotation
intro_en: GaokaoBench is an intuitive evaluation benchmark that uses Chinese Gaokao exam questions from 2010 to 2022 to assess large language models. The benchmark includes 1,781 objective questions and 1,030 subjective questions, covering nine science and humanities subjects. It employs a zero-shot setting combined with human scoring to comprehensively evaluate models' language understanding, logical reasoning, and generation abilities.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart TD
    A[GAOKAO考试问题 (2010-2022)] --> B{Data Collection与预处理}
    B --> C[PDF格式原始问题]
    C --> D[自动化脚本处理] --> E[JSON格式问题]
    C --> F[Human Annotation] --> E
    E --> G[数学公式转换为LaTeX]
    G --> H{问题分类}
    H -- 客观题 --> I[1781道客观题]
    H -- 主观题 --> J[1030道主观题]
    I --> K[客观题Evaluation (正则匹配)]
    J --> L[主观题人工评分 (两位教师平均分)]
    J --> M[主观题LLM评分 (GPT-4-turbo)]
    L --> N{评分一致性检查}
    M --> N
    N -- 通过 --> O[Final Dataset构成]
    N -- 失败 --> J
    O --> P[零样本设置下的LLMEvaluation]
    P --> Q[LLM性能分析]
    Q --> R[GAOKAO-BenchEvaluation结果]
metric_en: Scoring Rate, Converted Total Score, Spearman Correlation, Kendall-Tau Correlation

name: GaRAGe
intro: GaRAGe是一个大型RAG基准，包含人工整理的长篇答案及每个依据段落的注释，支持细粒度评估LLM在生成RAG答案时识别相关依据的能力。该基准涵盖2366个多样化问题，包含3.5万多条来自私有文档和网络的注释段落，反映真实RAG应用场景。
build_method: 人工标注, 专家构建
metric: Relevance-Aware Factuality Score, F1 in attribution, true positive rate in deflections
task_type: 开放式问答, 事实验证
intro_en: GaRAGe is a large-scale RAG benchmark featuring manually curated long-form answers and annotated supporting paragraphs for each, enabling fine-grained evaluation of LLMs' ability to identify relevant evidence when generating RAG answers. The benchmark includes 2,366 diverse questions and over 35,000 annotated paragraphs from private documents and the web, reflecting real-world RAG application scenarios.
metric_en: Relevance-Aware Factuality Score, F1 in attribution, true positive rate in deflections

name: GDPVal
intro: GDPval是一个评估AI模型在真实世界中具有经济价值任务表现的基准测试。它涵盖了美国劳工统计局针对44个职业的大部分工作活动，这些职业来自对美国GDP贡献最大的9个行业。任务是根据平均拥有14年经验的行业专业人士的代表性工作构建的。该基准测试还提供了一个包含220个任务的黄金子集和公共自动化评分服务。
build_method: 专家构建, 人工标注
eval_feature: 思维链, 零样本, 过程评估
intro_en: GDPVal is a benchmark for evaluating AI models' performance on economically valuable tasks in the real world. It covers most work activities for 44 occupations from the 9 industries contributing most to the US GDP, based on data from the US Bureau of Labor Statistics. Tasks are constructed to represent work performed by industry professionals with an average of 14 years of experience. The benchmark also provides a gold subset of 220 tasks and a public automated scoring service.
scale_en: 44 items professions, 220 items golden subset tasks

name: GEdit-Bench
intro: GEdit-Bench是一个基于真实世界用户指令的图像编辑评测基准。它旨在提供一个更真实、更全面的评估框架，以衡量图像编辑模型的性能。该基准的发布旨在缩小开源模型与领先的闭源模型（如GPT-4o和Gemini）在图像编辑能力上的差距。
modality: 文本, 图像, 多模态
intro_en: GEdit-Bench is an image editing evaluation benchmark based on real-world user instructions. It aims to provide a more realistic and comprehensive framework for assessing the performance of image editing models. The benchmark is released to narrow the gap in image editing capabilities between open-source models and leading closed-source models such as GPT-4o and Gemini.

name: GMAI-MMBench
intro: GMAI-MMBench是一个全面的通用医疗AI基准，旨在评估大型视觉语言模型（LVLMs）在各种医疗应用中的有效性。该基准解决了现有基准在临床相关性、评估不完整性和交互式LVLMs指导不足等方面的局限性。它由284个数据集构建，涵盖38种医学图像模态、18项临床相关任务、18个科室和4种感知粒度，采用视觉问答（VQA）形式。
org: Shanghai AI Laboratory, University of Washington, Monash University, East China Normal University, U
eval_feature: 动态更新, 防污染, 过程评估
intro_en: GMAI-MMBench is a comprehensive general medical AI benchmark designed to evaluate the effectiveness of large vision-language models (LVLMs) across various medical applications. It addresses limitations of existing benchmarks in clinical relevance, incomplete evaluation, and insufficient guidance for interactive LVLMs. The benchmark is built from 284 datasets, covering 38 medical image modalities, 18 clinically relevant tasks, 18 departments, and 4 levels of perceptual granularity, using a visual question answering (VQA) format.

name: GPQA
intro: GPQA包含448个由领域专家编写的生物、物理和化学多项选择题，设计为“谷歌难题”，难以被熟练非专家和先进AI系统解答。旨在促进可扩展监督研究，帮助专家从超越人类能力的AI系统中获得真实信息。
org: New York University, Google DeepMind, Cohere
intro_en: GPQA contains 448 multiple-choice questions in biology, physics, and chemistry, written by domain experts and designed as "Google-proof" problems that are difficult for skilled non-experts and advanced AI systems to solve. It aims to promote scalable supervision research and help experts obtain reliable information from AI systems that surpass human capabilities.

intro_en: GPQA Diamond is a curated subset of the GPQA (Graduate-Level Google-Proof Q&A) benchmark, containing 198 highest-quality questions verified by domain experts. These questions cover graduate-level scientific fields such as physics, chemistry, and biology, and are widely used to evaluate the advanced scientific reasoning abilities of large language models.

name: GraphWalks
intro: GraphWalks是一个多跳推理长上下文基准测试。在该基准测试中，模型会获得一个以边列表表示的图，并被要求执行特定操作。模型需要根据图的结构和操作描述，返回操作结果中的节点集合，例如执行广度优先搜索（BFS）或查找节点的父节点。
intro_en: GraphWalks is a multi-hop reasoning long-context benchmark. In this benchmark, the model is given a graph represented by an edge list and is required to perform specific operations. The model must return the set of nodes resulting from the operation based on the graph structure and operation description, such as performing breadth-first search (BFS) or finding a node's parent.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart TD
    A[GraphWalks Benchmark Construction] --> B[Data Generation Process]
    B --> C[Graph Structure Generation]
    C --> D[Operation Type Selection]
    D{Operation Type} -- BFS --> E[BFS Operation Definition]
    D -- Parents --> F[Parents Operation Definition]
    E --> G[Ground Truth Answer Generation (BFS)]
    F --> H[Ground Truth Answer Generation (Parents)]
    G --> I(Raw Data Samples)
    H --> I
    I --> J[Prompt Formulation]
    J --> K(Prompt Data)
    K --> L(Answer Data)
    L --> M(Metadata)
    M --> N[Initial Dataset Compilation]
    N --> O{Quality Control & Validation}
    O -- Bug Identified --> P[Ground Truth Correction]
    P --> N
    O -- No Bug --> Q[Final Dataset Composition]
    Q --> R[Evaluation Methodology Definition]
    R --> S[Answer Extraction Logic]
    S --> T[Metric Calculation (Recall, Precision, F1)]
    T --> U(Evaluation Results)
    Q --> V(GraphWalks Dataset)

name: GSM8K
intro: GSM8K是一个包含8500个高质量、语言多样的小学数学应用题的数据集，旨在诊断和评估大型语言模型在多步数学推理方面的能力。尽管这些问题在概念上相对简单，但即便是最大规模的Transformer模型也难以在该基准上取得高分。
intro_en: GSM8K is a dataset of 8,500 high-quality, linguistically diverse elementary math word problems designed to diagnose and evaluate large language models' abilities in multi-step mathematical reasoning. Although these problems are conceptually simple, even the largest Transformer models struggle to achieve high scores on this benchmark.

    subgraph DataSourcing["Data Sourcing & Problem Creation"]

name: HalluEval
intro: HalluEval是一个用于评估大型语言模型幻觉的大规模基准。它包含约35000个带有人工标注的生成样本，涵盖了通用、问答、对话和摘要等多种场景。该基准旨在全面评估和分析大型语言模型在识别和避免生成不真实或与来源不符内容方面的能力。
org: Renmin University of China, Université de Montréal, Hugging Face
intro_en: HalluEval is a large-scale benchmark for evaluating hallucinations in large language models. It contains approximately 35,000 human-annotated generated samples across various scenarios, including general tasks, QA, dialogue, and summarization. The benchmark aims to comprehensively assess and analyze large language models' ability to identify and avoid generating false or source-inconsistent content.

name: HalluLens
intro: HalluLens是一个用于评估大型语言模型幻觉的综合基准。它提出了一个清晰的幻觉分类法，区分了与训练数据不一致的外源性幻觉和与输入上下文不一致的内源性幻觉。该基准包含动态生成的测试集以防止数据泄漏，并引入了新的外源性幻觉任务，旨在为模型的事实性和一致性评估提供标准化框架。
org: FAIR at Meta, GenAI at Meta, HKUST
metric: Hallucinated when not refused, Correct answer rate, False acceptance rate
task_type: 开放式问答, 生成, 事实验证, 分类, 安全测试
eval_feature: 动态, 实时, 防污染, 对抗性
task_type_en: Open-ended QA, Generation, Fact Verification, Classification, Safety Testing
intro_en: HalluLens is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating hallucinations in large language models. It proposes a clear hallucination taxonomy, distinguishing exogenous hallucinations inconsistent with training data from endogenous hallucinations inconsistent with input context. The benchmark includes dynamically generated test sets to prevent data leakage and introduces new exogenous hallucination tasks, aiming to provide a standardized framework for assessing model factuality and consistency.
metric_en: Hallucinated when not refused, Correct answer rate, False acceptance rate

name: HalluQA
intro: HalluQA是针对中文大型语言模型幻觉现象的问答基准。它包含450个跨越多个领域的精心设计的对抗性问题，特别考虑了中国的历史文化、风俗习惯和社会现象。该基准在构建过程中区分了模仿性虚假和事实性错误两种幻觉类型，并基于GLM-130B和ChatGPT构建了对抗性样本。
build_method: 人工标注, 专家构建, LLM生成
task_type: 开放式问答, 事实验证, 选择题
task_type_en: Open-ended QA, Fact Verification, Multiple Choice
build_method_en: Human Annotation, Expert Curation, LLM Generation
intro_en: HalluQA is a question-answering benchmark targeting hallucination phenomena in Chinese large language models. It contains 450 carefully designed adversarial questions across multiple domains, with special consideration of Chinese history, culture, customs, and social phenomena. The benchmark distinguishes between imitative falsehoods and factual errors as two types of hallucinations and constructs adversarial samples based on GLM-130B and ChatGPT.
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Sourcing & Initial Generation["Data Sourcing & Initial Generation"]
        A([Start]) --> B[/Input: TruthfulQA Patterns & Chinese Culture/]
        B --> C(Craft Misleading Questions)

        E --> F{Imitative Falsehoods >= 3/5?}

        F -- No --> H(Discard Question)
        C --> I[/Input: Popular Chinese Internet Questions/]
        I --> J(Generate Adversarial Questions with ChatGPT 3.5)

        M --> N(Annotate 4 Correct & 4 Incorrect Answers)

        R[/Input: Knowledge-Based Questions/]

    subgraph Quality Assurance["Quality Assurance"]

    end

    subgraph Final Benchmark["Final Benchmark"]
        AG --> AL[HalluQA Benchmark (450 Questions)]
        AJ --> AL
        AL --> AM[/Output: 30 Domains Covered/]
        AM --> AN(Evaluate Hallucination Phenomena)

        AO --> AP([End])
    end

name: HallusionBench
intro: HallusionBench是一个全面的基准测试，旨在评估图像-上下文推理能力。该基准测试通过强调对视觉数据的细致理解和解释，对先进的大型视觉语言模型（LVLMs）提出了重大挑战。它包含346张图像和1129个问题，全部由人类专家精心制作。通过新颖的视觉问题结构和对照组设计，HallusionBench能够对模型的响应趋势、逻辑一致性和各种故障模式进行定量分析，并深入理解语言幻觉和视觉错觉等问题。
org: University of Maryland, University of California, Beijing Institute of Technology, Tencent AI Lab, C
build_method: 专家构建, 人工标注
metric: Accuracy, Question Pair Acc, Figure Acc, Easy Question Acc, Hard Question Acc
modality: 图像, 文本, 多模态
eval_feature: 防污染, 对抗性, 过程评估
intro_en: HallusionBench is a comprehensive benchmark designed to evaluate image-context reasoning abilities. By emphasizing detailed understanding and interpretation of visual data, it poses significant challenges to advanced large vision-language models (LVLMs). It includes 346 images and 1,129 questions, all carefully crafted by human experts. Through novel visual question structures and control group designs, HallusionBench enables quantitative analysis of model response patterns, logical consistency, and various failure modes, providing deep insights into issues such as language hallucination and visual illusions.
metric_en: Accuracy, Question Pair Acc, Figure Acc, Easy Question Acc, Hard Question Acc

name: HarmBench
intro: HarmBench是一个用于自动化红队测试的标准化评估框架，旨在解决以往评估大型语言模型（LLM）恶意使用风险时缺乏统一标准的问题。该框架在设计时考虑了多个关键属性，并通过对18种红队方法和33个目标模型及防御策略的大规模比较，得出了新的见解。HarmBench的开源促进了攻击和防御方法的协同发展。
org: Center for AI Safety, University of California Berkeley, Stanford University, University of Illinois
build_method: 混合, LLM生成, 人工标注
metric: Attack Success Rate (ASR), GCG, GCG-M, GCG-T, PEZ, GBDA, UAT, AP, SFS, ZS, PAIR, TAP, TAP-T, AutoDAN
intro_en: HarmBench is a standardized evaluation framework for automated red teaming, designed to address the lack of unified standards in assessing the malicious use risks of large language models (LLMs). The framework incorporates multiple key attributes and provides new insights through a large-scale comparison of 18 red teaming methods and 33 target models and defense strategies. The open-source nature of HarmBench promotes the collaborative development of attack and defense methods.
metric_en: Attack Success Rate (ASR), GCG, GCG-M, GCG-T, PEZ, GBDA, UAT, AP, SFS, ZS, PAIR, TAP, TAP-T, AutoDAN
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    style Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style ProcessingFiltering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style AnnotationLabeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style EvaluationMetrics fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([Start]) --> InputData[/Input: Benchmark Name, Paper URL, Description/]

    subgraph Phase1["Data Sourcing & Initial Behavior Curation"]
        InputData --> ParseInput(Parse Input String)
        ParseInput --> AccessPaper(Access ArXiv Paper PDF)
        AccessPaper --> ReadPaper(Read Abstract & Methodology Sections)
        ReadPaper --> IdentifyHarmfulBehaviors(Identify Harmful Behaviors from Paper)
        IdentifyHarmfulBehaviors --> CollectUsePolicies(Collect Acceptable Use Policies: OpenAI, Anthropic, Meta, Inflection AI)
        CollectUsePolicies --> SummarizePolicies(Summarize Policies using GPT-4)
        SummarizePolicies --> ManualDesign(Manually Design Behaviors violating laws/norms)
    end

    subgraph Phase2["Behavior Categorization & Refinement"]
        ManualDesign --> CategorizeBehaviors(Categorize Behaviors: Semantic & Functional)
        CategorizeBehaviors --> SemanticCategories{7 Semantic Categories?}
        SemanticCategories -- Yes --> Cybercrime(Cybercrime & Unauthorized Intrusion)
        SemanticCategories -- Yes --> ChemBio(Chemical & Biological Weapons/Drugs)
        SemanticCategories -- Yes --> Copyright(Copyright Violations)
        SemanticCategories -- Yes --> Misinfo(Misinformation & Disinformation)
        SemanticCategories -- Yes --> Harassment(Harassment & Bullying)
        SemanticCategories -- Yes --> IllegalAct(Illegal Activities)
        SemanticCategories -- Yes --> GeneralHarm(General Harm)
        CategorizeBehaviors --> FunctionalCategories{4 Functional Categories?}
        FunctionalCategories -- Yes --> StandardBehaviors(Standard Behaviors: 200, from AdvBench, TDC 2023)
        FunctionalCategories -- Yes --> CopyrightBehaviors(Copyright Behaviors: 100, Hashing-based Classifier)
        FunctionalCategories -- Yes --> ContextualBehaviors(Contextual Behaviors: 100, Context String)
        FunctionalCategories -- Yes --> MultimodalBehaviors(Multimodal Behaviors: 110, Image Input)
        MultimodalBehaviors --> CheckDifferentialHarm{Check for Differential Harm / Online Searchability?}
        CheckDifferentialHarm -- Yes --> FilterDualIntent(Filter Dual-Intent Behaviors)
        FilterDualIntent --> HarmfulBehaviors(510 Unique Harmful Behaviors: 400 Textual, 110 Multimodal)
    end

    subgraph Phase3["Evaluation Pipeline Construction"]
        HarmfulBehaviors --> SplitDataset(Split Behaviors: 100 Validation, 410 Test)
        SplitDataset --> GenerateTestCases(Generate Test Cases (x) using Red Teaming Method (g))
        GenerateTestCases --> GenerateCompletions(Generate Completions (x') using Target LLM (f) with Greedy Decoding (T tokens))
        GenerateCompletions --> EvaluateCompletions(Evaluate Completions using Classifier (c))
        EvaluateCompletions --> ComputeASR(Compute Attack Success Rate (ASR))
        ComputeASR --> NonCopyrightClassifier(Non-Copyright Classifier: Fine-tuned Llama 2 13B Chat)
        ComputeASR --> CopyrightClassifier(Copyright Classifier: Hashing-based)
        NonCopyrightClassifier --> ValidateClassifier(Validate Classifier on Manually-Labeled Set)
        CopyrightClassifier --> ValidateClassifier
        ValidateClassifier --> CriteriaSuccess(Define Criteria for Successful Test Cases)
    end

    subgraph Phase4["Adversarial Training & Robustness Enhancement"]
        GenerateTestCases --> R2D2(Robust Refusal Dynamic Defense (R2D2))
        R2D2 --> InitializePool(Initialize Test Case Pool P)
        InitializePool --> InitializeModel(Initialize Model Parameters θ)
        InitializeModel --> IterationLoop(For M Iterations)
        IterationLoop --> SampleTestCases(Sample n Test Cases from P)
        SampleTestCases --> UpdateTestCases(Update x using GCG to Minimize L_GCG for m Steps)
        UpdateTestCases --> ComputeLosses(Compute L_away, L_toward, L_SFT)
        ComputeLosses --> UpdateModelParams(Update θ by Minimizing Combined Loss L_total)
        UpdateModelParams --> ResetTestCases{Iteration mod L == 0?}
        ResetTestCases -- Yes --> ResetKPercent(Reset K% of Test Cases in P)
        ResetTestCases -- No --> IterationLoop
        ResetKPercent --> IterationLoop
        IterationLoop --> ReturnTheta(Return Updated Model Parameters θ)
    end

    ReturnTheta --> FinalBenchmark([HarmBench Finalized])
    FinalBenchmark --> End([End])
flowchart_zh:
flowchart TD
    style Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style ProcessingFiltering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style AnnotationLabeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style EvaluationMetrics fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([开始]) --> InputData[/输入：基准名称、论文URL、描述/]

    subgraph Phase1["数据来源与初始行为筛选阶段"]
        InputData --> ParseInput(解析输入字符串)
        ParseInput --> AccessPaper(访问ArXiv论文PDF)
        AccessPaper --> ReadPaper(阅读摘要与方法论部分)
        ReadPaper --> IdentifyHarmfulBehaviors(从论文中识别有害行为)
        IdentifyHarmfulBehaviors --> CollectUsePolicies(收集可接受使用政策：OpenAI、Anthropic、Meta、Inflection AI)
        CollectUsePolicies --> SummarizePolicies(使用GPT-4总结政策)
        SummarizePolicies --> ManualDesign(手动设计违反法律/规范的行为)
    end

    subgraph Phase2["行为分类与细化阶段"]
        ManualDesign --> CategorizeBehaviors(分类行为：语义与功能)
        CategorizeBehaviors --> SemanticCategories{7种语义类别？}
        SemanticCategories -- 是 --> Cybercrime(网络犯罪与未经授权入侵)
        SemanticCategories -- 是 --> ChemBio(化学与生物武器/药物)
        SemanticCategories -- 是 --> Copyright(版权侵犯)
        SemanticCategories -- 是 --> Misinfo(错误信息与虚假信息)
        SemanticCategories -- 是 --> Harassment(骚扰与欺凌)
        SemanticCategories -- 是 --> IllegalAct(非法活动)
        SemanticCategories -- 是 --> GeneralHarm(一般危害)
        CategorizeBehaviors --> FunctionalCategories{4种功能类别？}
        FunctionalCategories -- 是 --> StandardBehaviors(标准行为：200个，来自AdvBench, TDC 2023)
        FunctionalCategories -- 是 --> CopyrightBehaviors(版权行为：100个，基于哈希的分类器)
        FunctionalCategories -- 是 --> ContextualBehaviors(上下文行为：100个，上下文字符串)
        FunctionalCategories -- 是 --> MultimodalBehaviors(多模态行为：110个，图像输入)
        MultimodalBehaviors --> CheckDifferentialHarm{检查差异性危害/在线可搜索性？}
        CheckDifferentialHarm -- 是 --> FilterDualIntent(过滤双重意图行为)
        FilterDualIntent --> HarmfulBehaviors(510个独特有害行为：400个文本，110个多模态)
    end

    subgraph Phase3["评估流程构建阶段"]
        HarmfulBehaviors --> SplitDataset(拆分数据集：100个验证集，410个测试集)
        SplitDataset --> GenerateTestCases(使用红队方法(g)生成测试用例(x))
        GenerateTestCases --> GenerateCompletions(使用目标LLM(f)和贪婪解码(T个token)生成补全(x'))
        GenerateCompletions --> EvaluateCompletions(使用分类器(c)评估补全)
        EvaluateCompletions --> ComputeASR(计算攻击成功率(ASR))
        ComputeASR --> NonCopyrightClassifier(非版权分类器：微调Llama 2 13B Chat)
        ComputeASR --> CopyrightClassifier(版权分类器：基于哈希)
        NonCopyrightClassifier --> ValidateClassifier(在手动标注集上验证分类器)
        CopyrightClassifier --> ValidateClassifier
        ValidateClassifier --> CriteriaSuccess(定义成功测试用例的标准)
    end

    subgraph Phase4["对抗训练与鲁棒性增强阶段"]
        GenerateTestCases --> R2D2(鲁棒拒绝动态防御 (R2D2))
        R2D2 --> InitializePool(初始化测试用例池P)
        InitializePool --> InitializeModel(初始化模型参数θ)
        InitializeModel --> IterationLoop(进行M次迭代)
        IterationLoop --> SampleTestCases(从P中采样n个测试用例)
        SampleTestCases --> UpdateTestCases(使用GCG更新x以最小化L_GCG，进行m步)
        UpdateTestCases --> ComputeLosses(计算L_away, L_toward, L_SFT)
        ComputeLosses --> UpdateModelParams(通过最小化总损失L_total更新θ)
        UpdateModelParams --> ResetTestCases{迭代次数 mod L == 0?}
        ResetTestCases -- 是 --> ResetKPercent(重置P中K%的测试用例)
        ResetTestCases -- 否 --> IterationLoop
        ResetKPercent --> IterationLoop
        IterationLoop --> ReturnTheta(返回更新后的模型参数θ)
    end

    ReturnTheta --> FinalBenchmark([HarmBench最终确定])
    FinalBenchmark --> End([结束])

name: HEAD-QA
intro: HEAD-QA是基于西班牙医疗系统专业资格考试的多项选择问答基准，涵盖医学、护理学、心理学、化学、药学和生物学六个领域，对人类专家也极具挑战性。
build_method: 人工收集，来自西班牙医疗专业资格考试
eval_feature: 复杂推理，跨语言能力，专业资格考试难度
build_method_en: Manually collected, from Spanish medical professional qualification exams
eval_feature_en: Complex reasoning, cross-lingual ability, professional qualification exam difficulty
intro_en: HEAD-QA is a multiple-choice question benchmark based on the Spanish healthcare system professional qualification exam, covering six fields: medicine, nursing, psychology, chemistry, pharmacy, and biology. It is highly challenging even for human experts.

    G --> I(HEAD-QA Dataset)
    I --> J[Model Evaluation]
    J --> K(Medical Knowledge Assessment)

        S6 --> F1(排除放射物理学考试):::ProcessingFiltering
        F1 --> F2(移除无效题目):::ProcessingFiltering
        F2 --> F3[/筛选后的西班牙语题目/]::DataCollection
    end

    subgraph 翻译与标注
        F3 --> T1(使用Google API翻译成英文):::AnnotationLabeling
        T1 --> T2[/HEAD-QA-EN (英文版)/]::DataCollection
        T2 --> T3(评估翻译充分性):::QualityControl
        T3 -- 充分性 >= 4.35/5 --> T4(评估翻译流畅性):::QualityControl
        T3 -- 充分性 < 4.35/5 --> T1(必要时重新翻译)
        T4 -- 流畅性 >= 4/5 --> T5[/高质量英文题目/]::DataCollection
        T4 -- 流畅性 < 4/5 --> T1
    end

    subgraph 数据集划分
        T5 --> D1(定义官方划分):::ProcessingFiltering
        D1 --> D2(2013-2014年考试作为训练集):::ProcessingFiltering
        D1 --> D3(2015年考试作为开发集):::ProcessingFiltering
        D1 --> D4(剩余考试作为测试集):::ProcessingFiltering
        D2 & D3 & D4 --> D5{避免随机划分?}:::ProcessingFiltering
        D5 -- 是 --> D6(保留与人类结果的比较):::ProcessingFiltering
        D6 --> D7[/最终HEAD-QA数据集/]::OutputResult
    end

    subgraph 评估
        D7 --> E1(计算准确率):::EvaluationMetric
        D7 --> E2(计算POINTS指标):::EvaluationMetric
        E2 --> E3(答对得3分):::EvaluationMetric
        E2 --> E4(答错扣1分):::EvaluationMetric
        E1 & E3 & E4 --> End([结束]):::End

    end

    classDef Start,End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    classDef DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    classDef ProcessingFiltering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    classDef AnnotationLabeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    classDef QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    classDef EvaluationMetric fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    classDef OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

name: HealthBench
intro: HealthBench是一个开源基准，用于衡量大型语言模型在医疗领域的性能和安全性。它包含5000个多轮对话，由262名医生基于48562条独特评分标准进行评估，涵盖多种健康场景和行为维度。该基准支持模型在紧急情况、临床数据转换及全球健康等场景下的开放式真实评测。
build_method: 专家构建, LLM生成, 混合
intro_en: HealthBench is an open-source benchmark designed to evaluate the performance and safety of large language models in the medical field. It includes 5,000 multi-turn dialogues assessed by 262 doctors based on 48,562 unique scoring criteria, covering various health scenarios and behavioral dimensions. The benchmark supports open-ended real-world evaluation of models in emergency situations, clinical data transformation, and global health contexts.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Raw Healthcare Conversation Data) --> B[Data Collection: Multi-turn Dialogues]
    B --> C{Dialogue Selection & Filtering}
    C -->|Selected Dialogues| D[Initial Dialogue Set]
    D --> E{{Manual Annotation by 262 Doctors}}
    E --> F[Annotation Review & Refinement]
    F --> G{Quality Assurance Check}
    G -->|Pass| H(HealthBench Dataset: 5000 Multi-turn Conversations)
    G -->|Fail| I[Re-annotation/Discard]
    H --> J[LLM Performance & Safety Evaluation]
    J --> K(Evaluation Results)

name: HealthSearchQA
intro: HealthSearchQA是Google在MultiMedQA论文中新引入的自由回答医学问题数据集，包含3173个来自健康搜索引擎的消费者医疗问题，旨在评估大型语言模型在真实用户健康查询场景中的表现。
build_method: 自动生成，来自健康搜索引擎查询
eval_feature: 评估LLM在消费者健康查询场景中的知识和推理能力
build_method_en: Automatically generated, from health search engine queries
eval_feature_en: Evaluates LLM knowledge and reasoning ability in consumer health query scenarios
intro_en: HealthSearchQA is a free-response medical question dataset newly introduced by Google in the MultiMedQA paper. It contains 3,173 consumer health questions from health search engines and aims to evaluate large language models' performance in real-world user health query scenarios.

    G --"Rejected"--> I[Discard Low Quality]
    H --> J(HealthSearchQA Dataset)
    J --> K[Model Evaluation]
    K --> L[Information Retrieval & QA Capabilities]
