        R --> S[收集智能体转录本]:::Data_Collection
        S --> T[评估监控器区分良性/恶意转录本的能力]:::Evaluation_Metric
        T --> U[/监控器性能数据 (AUC)/]
    end

    subgraph Quality_Control_and_Analysis["质量控制与分析"]
        U --> V{分析破坏成功率与监控难度？}
        V -- 是 --> W[识别模型优势与劣势]:::Quality_Control
        W --> X[/最终基准洞察/]
        V -- 否 --> F
    end

    X --> End([结束]):::End_Node

name: SimpleQA
intro: SimpleQA是一个由OpenAI开发的事实性问答基准，旨在评估大型语言模型回答简短、事实明确问题的能力。该基准通过对抗性方式收集，针对GPT-4的回答进行设计，确保问题具有单一且无争议的答案，从而简化评分。SimpleQA旨在测试模型是否能识别其知识边界，避免对不确定的问题做出猜测性回答。
build_method: LLM生成, 人工标注, 混合
task_type: 开放式问答, 事实验证
intro_en: SimpleQA is a factual question-answering benchmark developed by OpenAI to evaluate large language models' ability to answer short, fact-based questions. Collected adversarially and designed against GPT-4's responses, it ensures questions have a single, unambiguous answer to simplify scoring. SimpleQA aims to test whether models can recognize their knowledge boundaries and avoid guessing on uncertain questions.

name: SimpleVQA
intro: SimpleVQA是首个全面的多模态基准测试，旨在评估多模态大型语言模型（MLLMs）回答自然语言简短问题的真实性能力。它涵盖多任务和多场景，确保高质量和具有挑战性的查询，并维护静态和永恒的参考答案。该基准将视觉问答项目分为9个围绕客观事件或常识的不同任务，并置于9个主题中。通过严格的质量控制和LLM作为评判的评分系统，SimpleVQA对MLLMs的图像理解和文本生成能力进行全面评估。
org: Tsinghua University, Beijing Institute of Technology, Tencent AI Lab, University of Chinese Academy
build_method: 人工标注, 自动生成
intro_en: SimpleVQA is the first comprehensive multimodal benchmark designed to evaluate the factual accuracy of multimodal large language models (MLLMs) in answering natural language short questions. It covers multiple tasks and scenarios, ensuring high-quality and challenging queries while maintaining static and permanent reference answers. The benchmark divides visual question answering into 9 tasks focused on objective events or common sense, organized into 9 themes. Through strict quality control and an LLM-based scoring system, SimpleVQA provides a thorough assessment of MLLMs' image understanding and text generation capabilities.

name: SoMe
intro: SoMe是一个开创性的基准测试，旨在评估大型语言模型（LLM）在社交媒体场景中的能力。该基准包含8个社交媒体代理任务，超过900万条帖子和17,869条精细标注的任务查询，提供了一个多样化且真实的平台。SoMe旨在全面评估社交媒体代理在理解媒体内容、用户行为和做出复杂决策方面的能力。研究表明，无论是闭源还是开源的LLM在处理这些任务时都存在局限性，因此SoMe为未来的社交媒体代理提供了一个富有挑战性的测试平台。
org: University of Chinese Academy of Sciences, Institute of Automation, Chinese Academy of Sciences
build_method: 网络爬取, 人工标注, 混合
eval_feature: 动态, 实时, 多轮, 领域特定
intro_en: SoMe is a pioneering benchmark designed to evaluate large language models' (LLM) capabilities in social media scenarios. It includes 8 social media agent tasks, over 9 million posts, and 17,869 finely annotated task queries, offering a diverse and realistic platform. SoMe aims to comprehensively assess social media agents' abilities in understanding media content, user behavior, and making complex decisions. Studies show that both closed-source and open-source LLMs have limitations in handling these tasks, making SoMe a challenging testbed for future social media agents.

name: Spider 2.0
intro: Spider 2.0 是一个包含632个真实企业级数据库文本转SQL工作流问题的评测框架。数据库来自实际应用，常含1000多列，存储于本地或云端如BigQuery和Snowflake。挑战要求模型处理复杂SQL环境、长上下文、多条SQL语句及复杂推理，远超传统文本转SQL任务。
org: xlang.ai, The University of Hong Kong, The Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shanghai AI Laboratory,
eval_feature: 长文本, 多轮, 领域特定
intro_en: Spider 2.0 is a benchmark consisting of 632 real-world enterprise-level database text-to-SQL workflow problems. The databases originate from practical applications, often containing over 1,000 columns, stored locally or in the cloud such as BigQuery and Snowflake. The challenge requires models to handle complex SQL environments, long contexts, multiple SQL statements, and intricate reasoning, far exceeding traditional text-to-SQL tasks.
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Data Sourcing & Initial Setup
        A([Start]) --> B[/Real-world Enterprise Use Cases/]
        B --> C(Identify Database Systems)
        C --> D{Cloud or Local DB?}
        D -- Cloud --> D1[/BigQuery, Snowflake Data/]
        D -- Local --> D2[/SQLite, DuckDB, Postgres, Clickhouse Data/]
        D1 & D2 --> E(Collect Raw Data from Applications)
        E --> F[/Large-scale Schemas (avg. 812 columns), Terabyte-scale Data Volumes/]
    end

    subgraph Task Creation & Annotation
        F --> G(Define 632 Text-to-SQL Workflow Problems)
        G --> H{Is it a DBT Project?}
        H -- Yes --> I(Run DBT Project & Generate DAG)
        I --> J(Analyze DBT Project YML/MD Files for Data Models)
        J --> K(Remove SQL Files for Specific Data Flows)
        K --> L(Write Task Instructions for Incomplete Project)
        H -- No --> M(Rewrite Original SQL to Gold SQL)
        M --> N(Incorporate External Knowledge & Documentation)
        L & N --> O[/Initial Task & Gold SQL/]
    end

    subgraph Quality Control & Validation
        O --> P(Perform Multi-step Execution & Reasoning)
        P --> Q{Requires Debugging?}
        Q -- Yes --> R(Iteratively Refine SQL Queries)
        R --> Q
        Q -- No --> S(Evaluate using Execution Accuracy (EX) or Success Rate (SR))
        S --> T{Meets Performance Thresholds?}
        T -- No --> U(Identify Error Categories: Wrong Table/Column, Incorrect Dialect, Data Calculation, Planning, Schema Linking, External Knowledge Misinterpretation, Syntax Error)
        U --> P
        T -- Yes --> V([End])
    end

    style A fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

name: SpreadsheetBench
intro: SpreadsheetBench是一个具有挑战性的电子表格操作基准，完全来源于真实世界的场景，旨在让当前的大型语言模型（LLMs）沉浸在电子表格用户的实际工作流程中。与依赖合成查询和简化电子表格文件的现有基准不同，SpreadsheetBench由从在线Excel论坛收集的912个真实问题构建而成，这些问题反映了用户错综复杂的需求。相关的电子表格包含多种表格数据，如多表、非标准关系表和丰富的非文本元素。此外，我们提出了一种更可靠的评估指标，类似于在线评测平台，为每个指令创建多个电子表格文件作为测试用例，确保评估能够处理不同值的电子表格的鲁棒解决方案。我们对各种LLMs在单轮和多轮推理设置下的综合评估揭示了最先进（SOTA）模型与人类表现之间存在巨大差距，突显了基准的难度。
org: Renmin University of China, Tsinghua University
eval_feature: 真实世界场景, 鲁棒性评估
intro_en: SpreadsheetBench is a challenging spreadsheet operation benchmark entirely derived from real-world scenarios, designed to immerse current large language models (LLMs) in the actual workflows of spreadsheet users. Unlike existing benchmarks that rely on synthetic queries and simplified spreadsheet files, SpreadsheetBench is built from 912 real questions collected from online Excel forums, reflecting users' complex needs. The associated spreadsheets contain diverse tabular data, including multi-sheet, non-standard relational tables, and rich non-text elements. Additionally, we propose a more reliable evaluation metric, similar to online testing platforms, by creating multiple spreadsheet files as test cases for each instruction to ensure the assessment can handle robust solutions across spreadsheets with varying values. Our comprehensive evaluation of various LLMs under single-turn and multi-turn reasoning settings reveals a significant gap between state-of-the-art (SOTA) models and human performance, highlighting the benchmark’s difficulty.

    subgraph Phase["Phase 2: Data Filtering"]

        style E fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff

        style F fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff

        style G fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
        H(Filter out irrelevant posts)
        style H fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
        B --> D

        E -- Yes --> F
        F -- Yes --> G
        G -- Yes --> H
        D -- No --> I([Discard])
        style I fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

        F -- No --> I
        G -- No --> I
    end

    subgraph Phase["Phase 3: Data Formatting"]
        J(Generate self-contained instruction from post)
        style J fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
        K(Annotators verify and refine instruction)
        style K fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
        L(Annotate answer position)
        style L fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
        M{Manipulation Level?}
        style M fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff

        style N fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
        O(Specify entire spreadsheet range, e.g., A2:D6)
        style O fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
        H --> J --> K --> L --> M
        M -- Cell-Level --> N
        M -- Sheet-Level --> O
    end

    subgraph Phase["Phase 4: Test Case Construction"]
        P(Create multiple spreadsheets for each instruction)
        style P fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff

        style R fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
        S[/Final Benchmark: 912 instructions, 2729 test cases/]
        style S fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
        T([End])
        style T fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
        O --> P
        N --> P
        P --> Q --> R --> S --> T
    end
flowchart_zh:
flowchart TD

        A([开始])

        N --> P
        P --> Q --> R --> S --> T
    end

name: StableToolBench
intro: StableToolBench是一个旨在解决工具学习基准不稳定问题的项目。它通过引入虚拟API服务器和稳定的评估系统，在ToolBench的基础上进行了改进。虚拟API服务器结合了缓存系统和API模拟器，以减轻API状态变化带来的影响；而稳定的评估系统则利用GPT-4作为自动评估器，设计了可解的通过率和获胜率，以消除评估过程中的随机性。
eval_feature: 虚拟API服务器, 稳定评估系统
eval_feature_en: Virtual API server, Stability evaluation system
intro_en: StableToolBench is a project aimed at addressing the instability issues in tool learning benchmarks. It improves upon ToolBench by introducing a virtual API server and a stable evaluation system. The virtual API server combines a caching system and an API simulator to mitigate the impact of API state changes, while the stable evaluation system uses GPT-4 as an automatic evaluator and designs solvable pass rates and win rates to eliminate randomness in the evaluation process.
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    start_node([Start]) --> data_sourcing[/ToolBench Dataset/]

    subgraph Virtual API Server
        data_sourcing --> caching_system(Caching System)
        caching_system --> cache_hit{Cache Hit?}
        cache_hit -- Yes --> api_response_cache[/Cached API Response/]
        cache_hit -- No --> api_simulator(API Simulator)
        llm_simulation(LLM-based API Simulation)
        api_simulator --> llm_simulation
        llm_simulation --> api_response_sim[/Simulated API Response/]
        api_response_cache --> virtual_api_output[/Virtual API Output/]
        api_response_sim --> virtual_api_output
        virtual_api_output --> update_cache(Update Cache)
    end

    subgraph Stable Evaluation System
        virtual_api_output --> task_execution(Execute Task with Virtual API)
        task_execution --> gpt4_evaluation(GPT-4 Automatic Evaluation)
        gpt4_evaluation --> solvable_check{Task Solvable?}
        solvable_check -- Yes --> calculate_sopr(Calculate SoPR)
        solvable_check -- No --> calculate_sowr(Calculate SoWR)
        calculate_sopr --> evaluation_metrics[/SoPR Metric/]
        calculate_sowr --> evaluation_metrics
        evaluation_metrics --> quality_control{Meet Stability Criteria?}
        quality_control -- Yes --> final_benchmark(StableToolBench Benchmark)
        quality_control -- No --> refine_system(Refine System Parameters)
        refine_system --> virtual_api_output
    end

    final_benchmark --> end_node([End])

    style start_node fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style end_node fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style data_sourcing fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style caching_system fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style api_simulator fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style llm_simulation fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style api_response_cache fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    style api_response_sim fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    style virtual_api_output fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    style update_cache fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style task_execution fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style gpt4_evaluation fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style solvable_check fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style calculate_sopr fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style calculate_sowr fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style evaluation_metrics fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    style quality_control fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style final_benchmark fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    style refine_system fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style cache_hit fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
flowchart_zh:
flowchart TD
    start_node([开始]) --> data_sourcing[/ToolBench 数据集/]

    subgraph Virtual API Server["虚拟 API 服务器"]
        data_sourcing --> caching_system(缓存系统)
        caching_system --> cache_hit{缓存命中?}
        cache_hit -- 是 --> api_response_cache[/缓存的 API 响应/]
        cache_hit -- 否 --> api_simulator(API 模拟器)
        llm_simulation(基于 LLM 的 API 模拟)
        api_simulator --> llm_simulation
        llm_simulation --> api_response_sim[/模拟的 API 响应/]
        api_response_cache --> virtual_api_output[/虚拟 API 输出/]
        api_response_sim --> virtual_api_output
        virtual_api_output --> update_cache(更新缓存)
    end

    subgraph Stable Evaluation System["稳定评估系统"]
        virtual_api_output --> task_execution(使用虚拟 API 执行任务)
        task_execution --> gpt4_evaluation(GPT-4 自动评估)
        gpt4_evaluation --> solvable_check{任务可解决?}
        solvable_check -- 是 --> calculate_sopr(计算 SoPR)
        solvable_check -- 否 --> calculate_sowr(计算 SoWR)
        calculate_sopr --> evaluation_metrics[/SoPR 指标/]
        calculate_sowr --> evaluation_metrics
        evaluation_metrics --> quality_control{符合稳定性标准?}
        quality_control -- 是 --> final_benchmark(StableToolBench 基准)
        quality_control -- 否 --> refine_system(调整系统参数)
        refine_system --> virtual_api_output
    end

    final_benchmark --> end_node([结束])

name: Step-Audio-Edit
intro: Step-Audio-Edit-Benchmark 是一个用于可控语音合成的综合评测框架，专注于情感、说话风格和副语言等细粒度属性。该基准随 Step-Audio-EditX 模型一同发布，后者是一个基于大语言模型的音频编辑模型。此评测框架的提出旨在解决现有评测方法在全面性上的不足。
org: Shanghai Jieyue Xingchen Intelligent Technology Co., Ltd
intro_en: Step-Audio-Edit-Benchmark is a comprehensive evaluation framework for controllable speech synthesis, focusing on fine-grained attributes such as emotion, speaking style, and paralinguistics. The benchmark is released alongside the Step-Audio-EditX model, an audio editing model based on large language models. This evaluation framework aims to address the limitations of existing methods in terms of comprehensiveness.

name: StrongReject
intro: StrongREJECT是一个用于评估大型语言模型（LLM）越狱攻击的基准。它旨在解决现有越狱研究中普遍存在的夸大攻击效果的问题，提供了一个包含特定、有害信息请求的标准化提示数据集。其核心是一个自动评估器，能够衡量模型响应中提供有害信息的程度，并与人类判断达到很高的吻合度，从而提供更准确、更稳健的越狱有效性评估。
org: Center for Human-Compatible AI, UC Berkeley
intro_en: StrongREJECT is a benchmark for evaluating jailbreak attacks on large language models (LLMs). It aims to address the common issue of exaggerated attack effectiveness in existing jailbreak research by providing a standardized prompt dataset containing specific harmful information requests. At its core is an automatic evaluator that measures the extent of harmful information in model responses and achieves high alignment with human judgments, offering a more accurate and robust assessment of jailbreak effectiveness.
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    style start_end fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style data_collection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style processing_filtering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style annotation_labeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style quality_control fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style evaluation_metric fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style output_result fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    subgraph Overall Benchmark Construction
        start([Start]):::start_end
        subgraph Data Sourcing & Collection
            A[Identify shortcomings of existing benchmarks]:::data_collection
            B[/Existing Forbidden Prompt Datasets/]:::data_collection
            C(Analyze existing datasets for issues like repetitiveness, ill-posed prompts, unanswerability):::processing_filtering
            D{Are existing prompts robustly forbidden?}:::quality_control
            D -- No --> E(Filter out non-robustly forbidden prompts):::processing_filtering
            D -- Yes --> F(Proceed with robust prompts) 
            G[/StrongREJECT Forbidden Prompt Dataset/]:::data_collection
            E --> G
            F --> G
        end
        subgraph Prompt Generation & Refinement
            H(Generate new forbidden prompts with specific, harmful information):::data_collection
            I(Ensure prompts have factually verifiable answers for objective evaluation):::quality_control
            J(Categorize prompts into harmful behavior categories):::annotation_labeling
            K{Are prompts specific and answerable?}:::quality_control
            K -- No --> H
            K -- Yes --> L[/Refined StrongREJECT Prompt Dataset/]:::data_collection
        end
        subgraph Automated Evaluator Development
            M(Develop automated evaluator to score responses):::processing_filtering
            N(Measure usefulness of response for achieving harmful end):::evaluation_metric
            O(Evaluate both willingness and capabilities of model):::evaluation_metric
            P(Require specific information for full score):::evaluation_metric
            Q{Does evaluator agree with human judgments?}:::quality_control
            Q -- No --> M
            Q -- Yes --> R[/StrongREJECT Automated Evaluator/]:::output_result
        end
        subgraph Benchmark Validation & Finalization
            S(Compare StrongREJECT evaluator to human labelers):::evaluation_metric
            T(Identify overstatement of jailbreak effectiveness by existing methods):::evaluation_metric
            U(Underscore need for high-quality benchmark) 
            V([StrongREJECT Benchmark]):::output_result
            S --> T
            T --> U
            U --> V
        end
        start --> A
        A --> B
        B --> C
        C --> D
        G --> H
        L --> M
        R --> S
        V --> end([End]):::start_end
    end

            B[/现有禁用提示数据集/]:::data_collection

            D{现有提示是否被严格禁止？}:::quality_control
            D -- 否 --> E(过滤掉非严格禁止的提示):::processing_filtering
            D -- 是 --> F(使用严格禁止的提示) 
            G[/StrongREJECT禁用提示数据集/]:::data_collection
            E --> G
            F --> G
        end
        subgraph 提示生成与优化
            H(生成包含特定有害信息的新禁用提示):::data_collection
            I(确保提示具有可客观评估的事实可验证答案):::quality_control
            J(将提示分类为有害行为类别):::annotation_labeling
            K{提示是否具体且可回答？}:::quality_control
            K -- 否 --> H
            K -- 是 --> L[/精炼的StrongREJECT提示数据集/]:::data_collection
        end
        subgraph 自动化评估器开发
            M(开发自动化评估器以对响应进行评分):::processing_filtering
            N(衡量响应对实现有害目的的有用性):::evaluation_metric
            O(评估模型的意愿和能力):::evaluation_metric

            Q{评估器是否与人类判断一致？}:::quality_control
            Q -- 否 --> M
            Q -- 是 --> R[/StrongREJECT自动化评估器/]:::output_result
        end
        subgraph 基准验证与最终确定
            S(将StrongREJECT评估器与人工标注者进行比较):::evaluation_metric
            T(识别现有方法对越狱有效性的夸大):::evaluation_metric
            U(强调对高质量基准的需求) 
            V([StrongREJECT基准]):::output_result
            S --> T
            T --> U
            U --> V
        end
        start --> A
        A --> B
        B --> C
        C --> D
        G --> H
        L --> M
        R --> S
        V --> end([结束]):::start_end
    end

name: SUPERChem
intro: SUPERChem是一个具有挑战性的、由专家策划的多模态基准测试，旨在严格评估大型语言模型（LLMs）和多模态大型语言模型（MLLMs）的化学推理能力。它包含500个专家策划的推理密集型化学问题，涵盖不同子领域，并以多模态和纯文本格式提供。该基准测试引入了推理路径保真度（RPF）评分，以评估模型推理的质量，超越了最终答案的准确性。
eval_feature: 多模态, 过程评估, 防污染
intro_en: SUPERChem is a challenging, expert-curated multimodal benchmark designed to rigorously evaluate the chemical reasoning abilities of large language models (LLMs) and multimodal large language models (MLLMs). It contains 500 expert-curated, reasoning-intensive chemistry questions covering various subfields, presented in both multimodal and pure text formats. The benchmark introduces a Reasoning Path Fidelity (RPF) score to assess the quality of model reasoning beyond final answer accuracy.

name: SuperGPQA
intro: SuperGPQA 是一个全面的评测基准，旨在评估大型语言模型（LLM）在285个研究生级别学科中的知识和推理能力。该基准通过人机协同过滤机制，消除了简单或模棱两可的问题，从而揭示了当前最先进的LLM在不同知识领域中的性能仍有显著提升空间。SuperGPQA的构建过程涉及80多位专家标注员和一个人机协作系统，为未来大规模研究项目提供了方法论指导。
build_method: 专家构建, LLM生成, 混合
task_type: 选择题, 开放式问答
intro_en: SuperGPQA is a comprehensive benchmark designed to evaluate large language models' (LLM) knowledge and reasoning abilities across 285 graduate-level disciplines. Using a human-AI collaborative filtering mechanism, it eliminates simple or ambiguous questions, revealing significant room for improvement in state-of-the-art LLMs across various knowledge domains. The construction of SuperGPQA involved over 80 expert annotators and a human-AI collaboration system, providing methodological guidance for future large-scale research projects.

        style F fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
        G[将非英文问题翻译成学术英文]
        style G fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
        H[将非选择题转换为选择题格式]

        J[生成补充混淆选项]
        style J fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff

        L{通过抄袭检查和过滤（SOTA LLM）？}
        style L fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    end

    subgraph 质量检查
        M[基于规则的质量检查（格式问题）]

        N{格式是否正确？}
        style N fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff

        style O fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
        P[LLM检查：有效性、负面/极端查询、多模态排除、领域相关性、完整性、区分度标记]

    end

    T[/最终SuperGPQA基准问题/]
    style T fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    end_node([结束基准构建])

name: SWE-bench
intro: SWE-bench是一个用于评估大型语言模型解决真实世界GitHub问题能力的基准测试。它从12个流行的Python代码库中收集了2294个软件工程问题，每个问题都配有对应的拉取请求和测试补丁。该基准旨在衡量模型在理解代码库、定位问题和生成正确修复方面的能力。
intro_en: SWE-bench is a benchmark for evaluating large language models' ability to solve real-world GitHub issues. It collects 2,294 software engineering problems from 12 popular Python repositories, each paired with corresponding pull requests and test patches. The benchmark aims to assess models' capabilities in understanding codebases, identifying issues, and generating correct fixes.
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    style StartEnd fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style ProcessingFiltering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style AnnotationLabeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style EvaluationMetric fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([Start]):::StartEnd

    subgraph Data Sourcing & Collection["Data Sourcing & Collection"]
        A[Identify 12 Popular Python Repositories]:::DataCollection
        B[/GitHub Pull Requests (PRs)/]:::DataCollection
        C(Scrape ~90,000 PRs from GitHub):::DataCollection
        D{Focus on Popular Repositories?}:::Decision
        C --> D
        D -- Yes --> E[Associate Each PR with Base Commit Codebase]:::DataCollection
        D -- No --> C
    end

    subgraph Filtering["Filtering"]
        F{Is PR Merged?}:::Decision
        E --> F
        F -- Yes --> G{Does PR Resolve a GitHub Issue?}:::Decision
        F -- No --> DiscardPR(Discard PR):::QualityControl
        G -- Yes --> H{Does PR Modify Test Files?}:::Decision
        G -- No --> DiscardPR
        H -- Yes --> I[/Candidate Task Instances/]:::ProcessingFiltering
        H -- No --> DiscardPR

        J(Apply PR's Test Content):::ProcessingFiltering
        I --> J
        K(Log Test Results Before & After PR Content):::ProcessingFiltering
        J --> K
        L{At Least One Fail-to-Pass Test?}:::Decision
        K --> L
        L -- Yes --> M{No Installation/Runtime Errors?}:::Decision
        L -- No --> DiscardTask(Discard Task):::QualityControl
        M -- Yes --> N[/2,294 Final Task Instances/]:::OutputResult
        M -- No --> DiscardTask
    end

    subgraph Task Formulation & Evaluation["Task Formulation & Evaluation"]
        O[/Issue Description & Codebase/]:::AnnotationLabeling
        N --> O
        P(Model Generates Codebase Edit (Patch File)):::AnnotationLabeling
        O --> P
        Q(Apply Generated Patch via Unix 'patch' Program):::EvaluationMetric
        P --> Q
        R(Execute Unit & System Tests):::EvaluationMetric
        Q --> R
        S{Patch Applied Successfully & All Tests Pass?}:::Decision
        R --> S
        S -- Yes --> T[Task Resolved]:::EvaluationMetric
        S -- No --> U[Task Unresolved]:::EvaluationMetric
        V(Calculate Percentage of Resolved Tasks):::EvaluationMetric
        T --> V
        U --> V
        W([End]):::StartEnd
        V --> W
    end

    Start --> A
    N --> O

    style DiscardPR fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style DiscardTask fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style Decision fill:#ffc107,stroke:#ff9800,color:#000
flowchart_zh:
flowchart TD
    style StartEnd fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style ProcessingFiltering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style AnnotationLabeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style EvaluationMetric fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([开始]):::StartEnd

    subgraph Data Sourcing & Collection["数据来源与收集"]
        A[识别 12 个流行的 Python 仓库]:::DataCollection
        B[/GitHub 拉取请求 (PRs)/]:::DataCollection
        C(从 GitHub 抓取约 90,000 个 PR):::DataCollection
        D{是否关注流行仓库？}:::Decision
        C --> D
        D -- 是 --> E[将每个 PR 与基础提交代码库关联]:::DataCollection
        D -- 否 --> C
    end

    subgraph Filtering["过滤"]
        F{PR 是否已合并？}:::Decision
        E --> F
        F -- 是 --> G{PR 是否解决了 GitHub 问题？}:::Decision
        F -- 否 --> DiscardPR(丢弃 PR):::QualityControl
        G -- 是 --> H{PR 是否修改了测试文件？}:::Decision
        G -- 否 --> DiscardPR
        H -- 是 --> I[/候选任务实例/]:::ProcessingFiltering
        H -- 否 --> DiscardPR

        J(应用 PR 的测试内容):::ProcessingFiltering
        I --> J
        K(记录应用 PR 内容前后的测试结果):::ProcessingFiltering
        J --> K
        L{至少有一个从失败到通过的测试？}:::Decision
        K --> L
        L -- 是 --> M{没有安装/运行时错误？}:::Decision
        L -- 否 --> DiscardTask(丢弃任务):::QualityControl
        M -- 是 --> N[/2,294 个最终任务实例/]:::OutputResult
        M -- 否 --> DiscardTask
    end

    subgraph Task Formulation & Evaluation["任务制定与评估"]
        O[/问题描述与代码库/]:::AnnotationLabeling
        N --> O
        P(模型生成代码库编辑 (补丁文件)):::AnnotationLabeling
        O --> P
        Q(通过 Unix 'patch' 程序应用生成的补丁):::EvaluationMetric
        P --> Q
        R(执行单元和系统测试):::EvaluationMetric
        Q --> R
        S{补丁应用成功且所有测试通过？}:::Decision
        R --> S
        S -- 是 --> T[任务已解决]:::EvaluationMetric
        S -- 否 --> U[任务未解决]:::EvaluationMetric
        V(计算已解决任务的百分比):::EvaluationMetric
        T --> V
        U --> V
        W([结束]):::StartEnd
        V --> W
    end

    Start --> A
    N --> O

    style DiscardPR fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style DiscardTask fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style Decision fill:#ffc107,stroke:#ff9800,color:#000

name: SWE-Bench Pro
intro: SWE-Bench Pro是一个极具挑战性的基准测试，旨在评估人工智能代理在解决长周期、复杂软件工程任务方面的能力。它包含来自41个活跃代码库的1865个问题，真实反映了企业级的开发难题。该基准测试经过精心设计，可有效防止数据污染，从而真实地还原了软件开发的复杂性。
intro_en: SWE-Bench Pro is a highly challenging benchmark designed to evaluate AI agents' ability to solve long-term, complex software engineering tasks. It includes 1,865 problems from 41 active codebases, accurately reflecting enterprise-level development challenges. The benchmark is carefully designed to prevent data contamination, thereby authentically capturing the complexity of software development.
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    subgraph "Benchmark Construction Process: SWE-Bench Pro"
        start([Start])
        style start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

        subgraph "Phase 1: Problem Sourcing & Collection"
            P1_S1[/Input: 41 Active Repositories/]
            style P1_S1 fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
            P1_S2(Identify 1,865 Problems)
            style P1_S2 fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
            P1_S3{Problem Complexity Check}
            style P1_S3 fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
            P1_S4(Select Long-Horizon Tasks)
            style P1_S4 fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
        end

        subgraph "Phase 2: Task Definition & Augmentation"
            P2_S1(Define Task Scope: Multi-file Patches & Code Mods)
            style P2_S1 fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
            P2_S2(Augment with Sufficient Context)
            style P2_S2 fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
            P2_S3{Context Sufficiency Check}
            style P2_S3 fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
            P2_S4(Ensure Resolvability)
            style P2_S4 fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
        end

        subgraph "Phase 3: Human Verification & Quality Control"
            P3_S1(Human Verification of All Tasks)
            style P3_S1 fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
            P3_S2{Verification Passed?}
            style P3_S2 fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
            P3_S3(Refine Task Description)
            style P3_S3 fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
        end

        subgraph "Phase 4: Benchmark Partitioning"
            P4_S1(Partition Repositories)
            style P4_S1 fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
            P4_S2[/Output: Public Set (11 Repositories)/]
            style P4_S2 fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
            P4_S3[/Output: Held-out Set (12 Repositories)/]
            style P4_S3 fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
            P4_S4[/Output: Commercial Set (18 Proprietary Repositories)/]
            style P4_S4 fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
        end

        subgraph "Phase 5: Evaluation & Analysis"
            P5_S1(Collect Agent Trajectories)
            style P5_S1 fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
            P5_S2(Cluster Failure Modes)
            style P5_S2 fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
            P5_S3(Characterize Error Patterns)
            style P5_S3 fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
        end

        finish([End])
        style finish fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

        start --> P1_S1
        P1_S1 --> P1_S2
        P1_S2 --> P1_S3
        P1_S3 -- Yes --> P1_S4
        P1_S3 -- No --> P1_S1
        P1_S4 --> P2_S1
        P2_S1 --> P2_S2
        P2_S2 --> P2_S3
        P2_S3 -- Yes --> P2_S4
        P2_S3 -- No --> P2_S2
        P2_S4 --> P3_S1
        P3_S1 --> P3_S2
        P3_S2 -- Yes --> P4_S1
        P3_S2 -- No --> P3_S3
        P3_S3 --> P2_S2
        P4_S1 --> P4_S2
        P4_S1 --> P4_S3
        P4_S1 --> P4_S4
        P4_S2 --> P5_S1
        P4_S3 --> P5_S1
        P4_S4 --> P5_S1
        P5_S1 --> P5_S2
        P5_S2 --> P5_S3
        P5_S3 --> finish
    end
flowchart_zh:
flowchart TD
    subgraph "基准测试构建过程：SWE-Bench Pro"
        start([开始])
        style start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

        subgraph "阶段 1: 问题来源与收集"

        start --> P1_S1
        P1_S1 --> P1_S2
        P1_S2 --> P1_S3

name: SWE-bench Verified
intro: SWE-bench Verified 是SWE-bench的一个人工筛选子集，包含500个实例，由OpenAI合作创建。人工标注者确保问题描述清晰、测试补丁正确且任务可解，提升了原版SWE-bench的可靠性。
build_method: 人工标注, 专家构建, 网络爬取
eval_feature: 过程导向, 领域特定
build_method_en: Human Annotation, Expert Curation, Web Crawling
intro_en: SWE-bench Verified is a manually curated subset of SWE-bench containing 500 instances, created in collaboration with OpenAI. Human annotators ensure clear problem descriptions, correct test patches, and solvable tasks, enhancing the reliability of the original SWE-bench.
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    style Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style ProcessingFiltering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([Start]) --> DataCollection(Scrape Pull Requests)

    subgraph Data Sourcing & Collection
        DataCollection --> RepoSelection(Select 12 Popular Python Repositories)
        RepoSelection --> ScrapePRs(Scrape ~90,000 PRs from GitHub)
        ScrapePRs --> CodebaseSnapshot[/Codebase Snapshot/]
        CodebaseSnapshot --> PRData[/Pull Request Data/]
    end

    subgraph Attribute-based Filtering
        PRData --> CheckMerged(Check if PR is Merged)
        CheckMerged -- Yes --> ResolveIssue{Resolves GitHub Issue?}
        ResolveIssue -- Yes --> ChangeTestFiles{Changes Test Files?}
        ChangeTestFiles -- Yes --> CandidateTasks[/Candidate Tasks/]
        ResolveIssue -- No --> DiscardPR1(Discard PR)
        ChangeTestFiles -- No --> DiscardPR2(Discard PR)
    end

    subgraph Execution-based Filtering
        CandidateTasks --> ApplyTestContent(Apply PR\'s Test Content)
        ApplyTestContent --> LogTestResults(Log Test Results Before & After PR Content)
        LogTestResults --> FailToPassTest{At least one Fail-to-Pass Test?}
        FailToPassTest -- Yes --> NoErrors{No Installation/Runtime Errors?}
        NoErrors -- Yes --> FinalBenchmark[/2,294 SWE-bench Instances/]
        FailToPassTest -- No --> DiscardTask1(Discard Task)
        NoErrors -- No --> DiscardTask2(Discard Task)
    end

    FinalBenchmark --> Output(SWE-bench Benchmark)
    Output --> End([End])

    style DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style RepoSelection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style ScrapePRs fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style CodebaseSnapshot fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style PRData fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    style CheckMerged fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style ResolveIssue fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style ChangeTestFiles fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style CandidateTasks fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style DiscardPR1 fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style DiscardPR2 fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff

    style ApplyTestContent fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style LogTestResults fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style FailToPassTest fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style NoErrors fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style FinalBenchmark fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    style DiscardTask1 fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style DiscardTask2 fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style Output fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
flowchart_zh:
flowchart TD
    style Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style ProcessingFiltering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([开始]) --> DataCollection(抓取拉取请求)

    subgraph 数据来源与收集
        DataCollection --> RepoSelection(选择12个流行的Python仓库)
        RepoSelection --> ScrapePRs(从GitHub抓取约90,000个拉取请求)
        ScrapePRs --> CodebaseSnapshot[/代码库快照/]
        CodebaseSnapshot --> PRData[/拉取请求数据/]
    end

    subgraph 基于属性的过滤
        PRData --> CheckMerged(检查拉取请求是否已合并)
        CheckMerged -- 是 --> ResolveIssue{解决GitHub问题?}
        ResolveIssue -- 是 --> ChangeTestFiles{更改测试文件?}
        ChangeTestFiles -- 是 --> CandidateTasks[/候选任务/]
        ResolveIssue -- 否 --> DiscardPR1(丢弃拉取请求)
        ChangeTestFiles -- 否 --> DiscardPR2(丢弃拉取请求)
    end

    subgraph 基于执行的过滤
        CandidateTasks --> ApplyTestContent(应用拉取请求的测试内容)
        ApplyTestContent --> LogTestResults(记录拉取请求内容应用前后的测试结果)
        LogTestResults --> FailToPassTest{至少一个从失败到通过的测试?}
        FailToPassTest -- 是 --> NoErrors{无安装/运行时错误?}
        NoErrors -- 是 --> FinalBenchmark[/2,294个SWE-bench实例/]
        FailToPassTest -- 否 --> DiscardTask1(丢弃任务)
        NoErrors -- 否 --> DiscardTask2(丢弃任务)
    end

    FinalBenchmark --> Output(SWE-bench基准测试)
    Output --> End([结束])

name: SWE-bench-Live
intro: SWE-bench-Live是一个实时更新的基准测试，旨在解决现有软件工程基准（如SWE-bench）的静态和手动构建问题。它通过自动化流程从2024年以来的真实GitHub问题中提取任务，并为每个任务提供专用的Docker镜像以确保可复现性。该基准的发布旨在提供一个更动态、多样化和抗污染的评估环境，以促进大型语言模型和智能体在真实软件开发场景中的发展。
build_method: 自动生成, 网络爬取
eval_feature: 动态, 实时, 防污染
intro_en: SWE-bench-Live is a continuously updated benchmark designed to address the static and manual construction issues of existing software engineering benchmarks like SWE-bench. It automatically extracts tasks from real GitHub issues since 2024 and provides dedicated Docker images for each task to ensure reproducibility. The benchmark aims to offer a more dynamic, diverse, and contamination-resistant evaluation environment to advance large language models and agents in real-world software development scenarios.

name: SWE-Evo
intro: 现有针对AI编码代理的基准测试侧重于孤立的、单一问题任务，例如修复错误或添加小功能。然而，现实世界的软件工程本质上是一项长期任务：开发人员必须理解高级需求，协调跨多个文件的更改，并通过多次迭代演进代码库，同时保留现有功能。我们引入了SWE-EVO，这是一个评估代理在此类长期软件演进挑战中的基准。它由七个成熟的开源Python项目的发布说明和版本历史构建，包含48个演进任务，需要代理实现跨平均21个文件的多步骤修改，并通过每个实例平均874个测试的综合测试套件进行验证。实验表明，即使是GPT-5在SWE-EVO上的解决率也仅为21%，而SWE-Bench Verified上的解决率为65%，这表明当前代理在持续的、多文件推理方面存在显著的能力差距。我们还提出了Fix Rate，一个细粒度指标，用于捕获解决这些复杂、长期任务的部分进展。
org: FPT Software AI Center, University of Melbourne, Marvis AI
build_method: 自动生成, 网络爬取
task_type: 代码生成, Agent任务, 开放式问答
scale: 48个演进任务, 7个开源Python项目, 平均21个文件, 每个实例874个测试
task_type_en: Code Generation, Agent Task, Open-ended QA
intro_en: Existing benchmarks for AI coding agents focus on isolated, single-task problems such as bug fixing or adding small features. However, real-world software engineering is inherently a long-term process: developers must understand high-level requirements, coordinate changes across multiple files, and evolve the codebase through multiple iterations while preserving existing functionality. We introduce SWE-EVO, a benchmark that evaluates agents on such long-term software evolution challenges. It is built from release notes and version histories of seven mature open-source Python projects, comprising 48 evolution tasks that require agents to perform multi-step modifications across an average of 21 files, validated by comprehensive test suites averaging 874 tests per instance. Experiments show that even GPT-5 achieves only a 21% pass rate on SWE-EVO, compared to 65% on SWE-Bench Verified, highlighting a significant capability gap in continuous, multi-file reasoning. We also propose Fix Rate, a fine-grained metric to capture partial progress in solving these complex, long-term tasks.
scale_en: 48 items evolutionary tasks, 7 items open-source Python projects, average 21 items files, each items instance 874 items tests

name: SWE-fficiency
intro: SWE-fficiency是一个用于评估语言模型在真实工作负载下优化存储库级别性能的基准。该基准包含498个任务，涵盖了九个广泛使用的数据科学、机器学习和HPC存储库。在给定完整代码库和缓慢工作负载的情况下，代理必须调查代码语义、定位瓶颈和相关测试，并生成一个能够匹配或超过专家加速效果同时通过相同单元测试的补丁。
org: Harvard University, Google DeepMind, Princeton University, Xi'an Jiaotong University
build_method: 从GitHub PR中自动挖掘和过滤
task_type: 代码优化, 性能工程
eval_feature: 真实世界代码库, 性能优化
build_method_en: Automatically mined and filtered from GitHub PR
intro_en: SWE-fficiency is a benchmark for evaluating language models' ability to optimize repository-level performance under real-world workloads. It includes 498 tasks across nine widely used data science, machine learning, and HPC repositories. Given the full codebase and a slow workload, the agent must analyze code semantics, identify bottlenecks and relevant tests, and generate a patch that matches or surpasses expert-level acceleration while passing the same unit tests.

name: SWE-Lancer
intro: SWE-Lancer收录了超过1400个来自Upwork的自由职业软件工程任务，实际支付总额达100万美元。该基准测试涵盖了从50美元的错误修复到32000美元的功能实现等独立的工程任务，以及模型需要选择技术实施方案的管理任务。通过端到端测试和与人类管理者决策的对比来评估模型的表现，旨在促进对人工智能模型经济影响的研究。
build_method: 网络爬取, 专家构建
task_type: 代码生成, 补全, Agent任务
eval_feature: 领域特定, 结果导向
intro_en: SWE-Lancer includes over 1,400 freelance software engineering tasks from Upwork, with a total payment exceeding $1 million. The benchmark covers independent engineering tasks ranging from $50 bug fixes to $32,000 feature implementations, as well as management tasks where models must choose technical implementation plans. Model performance is evaluated through end-to-end testing and comparison with human manager decisions, aiming to advance research on the economic impact of AI models.

name: SWE-PolyBench
intro: SWE-PolyBench是一个多语言基准，用于评估大型语言模型在多种编程语言和真实场景下的软件工程任务表现。它包含来自21个代码仓库的2110个实例，涵盖Java、JavaScript、TypeScript和Python，任务包括修复漏洞、添加功能和代码重构。
build_method: 专家构建, 网络爬取, 混合
metric: Resolve Rate, Pass Rate, Syntax Tree Analysis
eval_feature: 领域特定, 结果导向
intro_en: SWE-PolyBench is a multilingual benchmark designed to evaluate large language models on software engineering tasks across various programming languages and real-world scenarios. It includes 2,110 instances from 21 code repositories, covering Java, JavaScript, TypeScript, and Python, with tasks such as bug fixing, feature addition, and code refactoring.
metric_en: Resolve Rate, Pass Rate, Syntax Tree Analysis

name: SWE-Sharp-Bench
intro: SWE-Sharp-Bench是一个针对C#语言的可复现软件工程基准测试。它包含了从17个真实世界的C#代码仓库中精心挑选的150个软件工程任务实例，填补了现有基准测试中对C#语言支持的空白。该基准的评估结果揭示了当前AI编码代理在处理C#任务时，其性能显著低于处理Python任务，显示出明显的性能差距。
build_method: 专家构建, 网络爬取, 混合
eval_feature: 结果导向, 领域特定
intro_en: SWE-Sharp-Bench is a reproducible software engineering benchmark for the C# language. It includes 150 software engineering task instances carefully selected from 17 real-world C# code repositories, filling the gap in existing benchmarks for C# support. Evaluation results reveal that current AI coding agents perform significantly worse on C# tasks compared to Python tasks, showing a clear performance gap.

name: SysBench
intro: SysBench是一个用于系统性分析大型语言模型（LLM）遵循系统消息能力的基准。它主要通过三个方面来评估现有LLM的局限性：约束违规、指令误判和多轮不稳定性。该基准包含500个基于六种常见约束类型手工构建的系统消息和多轮用户对话，覆盖了多样的交互关系。
build_method: 人工标注, 专家构建
metric: Constraint Violation Rate, Instruction Misjudgment Rate, Multi-turn Instability Rate
intro_en: SysBench is a benchmark for systematically analyzing large language models' (LLM) ability to follow system messages. It evaluates existing LLMs' limitations through three aspects: constraint violations, instruction misinterpretation, and multi-turn instability. The benchmark includes 500 system messages and multi-turn user dialogues manually constructed based on six common constraint types, covering diverse interaction scenarios.
metric_en: Constraint Violation Rate, Instruction Misjudgment Rate, Multi-turn Instability Rate
flowchart_en:

        A([Start]) --> B[/Real-world Scenarios/]
        B --> C(Define Data Design Principles)
        C --> C1{Question 1: Understand Constraints?}
        C --> C2{Question 2: Determine Alignment?}
        C --> C3{Question 3: Multi-turn Following?}
        C1 & C2 & C3 --> D(Identify 6 Prevalent Constraint Types)
        D --> E(Develop Expert Guidelines for Annotation)
        E --> F[/500 System Messages & Multi-turn Conversations/]

        style B fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
        style C fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

name: T-Eval
intro: T-Eval系统地分解大型语言模型的工具使用能力，涵盖指令遵循、规划、推理、检索、理解和复审等子过程。基于此，提出了逐步评估工具利用能力的T-Eval基准，填补了对工具使用细节分析的空白。
org: University of Science and Technology of China, Shanghai AI Laboratory, Tsinghua University, Jilin Un
build_method: 人工标注, 专家构建
metric: Planning, Reasoning, Retrieval, Understanding, Instruction Following, Review
intro_en: T-Eval systematically breaks down the tool-using capabilities of large language models, covering subprocesses such as instruction following, planning, reasoning, retrieval, comprehension, and review. Based on this, the T-Eval benchmark was proposed for step-by-step evaluation of tool utilization abilities, filling the gap in detailed analysis of tool usage.
metric_en: Planning, Reasoning, Retrieval, Understanding, Instruction Following, Review
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    style Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style ProcessingFiltering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style AnnotationLabeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style EvaluationMetrics fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([Start]) --> Phase1

    subgraph Phase1[Data Sourcing & Tool Collection]
        Phase1_1[/Tool Requirements/] --> A1(Define Tool Domains: Research, Travel, Entertainment, Web, Life, Financials)
        A1 --> A2(Select 1-2 Tools per Domain)
        A2 --> A3(Total 15 Basic Tools)
        A3 --> A4(Manually Generate High-Quality Tool Documentation)
        A4 --> A5[/Detailed Tool Documentation/]
        A5 --> Phase2
    end

    subgraph Phase2[Instruction Generation]
        Phase2_1[/Tool Documentation/] --> B1(Uniformly Sample 2-3 Tools)
        B1 --> B2(Prompt GPT-3.5 for Initial Queries)
        B2 --> B3[/Initial Queries (N instructions)/]
        B3 --> B4(Refine Instructions with GPT-4)
        B4 --> B5[/Refined Queries/]
        B5 --> Phase3
    end

    subgraph Phase3[Golden Solution Annotation]
        Phase3_1[/Refined Queries/]
        Phase3_1 --> C1(Multi-Agent Paradigm for Annotation)
        C1 --> C2(GPT-3.5 as Machine Annotator)
        C2 --> C3{Agent Role: Planner?}
        C3 -- Yes --> C4(Planner: Decide Next Step)
        C3 -- No --> C5{Agent Role: Executor?}
        C5 -- Yes --> C6(Executor: Generate Tool Name & Params)
        C6 --> C7(Executor: Execute Tool & Get Response)
        C5 -- No --> C8{Agent Role: Reviewer?}
        C8 -- Yes --> C9(Reviewer: Revise Response & Judge Task Completion)
        C9 --> C10[/Solution Paths & Tool Responses/]
        C10 --> C11(Human Annotators Review Solution Chain)
        C11 --> C12(Manually Filter Invalid Instruction-Solution Pairs)
        C12 --> C13[/1,500 Initial Instruction-Solution Pairs/]
        C13 --> C14(Two-Round Human Verification)
        C14 --> C15[/553 Verified Instruction-Solution Pairs/]
        C15 --> Phase4
    end

    subgraph Phase4[Evaluation Protocol & Dataset Construction]
        Phase4_1[/Verified Pairs/] --> D1(Design Instruction Task Prompt)
        D1 --> D2(Define Evaluation Granularity)
        D2 --> D3{Difficulty Level: Easy?}
        D3 -- Yes --> D4(Easy Level: Simple String Format, Semantic Quality Focus)
        D3 -- No --> D5(Difficult Level: JSON Format, Strict Match on Tool Name/Params)
        D5 --> D6(Extract Information for Subsets)
        D6 --> D7(Construct INSTRUCT, RETRIEVE, PLAN, REASON, UNDERSTAND, REVIEW Subsets)
        D7 --> D8[/23,305 Total Test Cases/]
        D8 --> End([End])
    end

name: TACO
intro: TACO是一个开源的大规模代码生成数据集，专注于算法的光学，旨在为代码生成模型领域提供更具挑战性的训练数据集和评估基准。TACO包含具有挑战性的竞赛级编程问题，以增强或评估在实际编程场景中的问题理解和推理能力。每个TACO问题都包含任务主题、算法、编程技能和难度级别等细粒度标签，为代码生成模型的训练和评估提供了更精确的参考。
org: ByteDance, Shanghai AI Lab, The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology, Tsinghua University,
scale: 25433个训练问题，1000个测试问题，多达155万个解决方案
intro_en: TACO is an open-source large-scale code generation dataset focused on algorithm optics, designed to provide more challenging training data and evaluation benchmarks for code generation models. TACO includes challenging competition-level programming problems to enhance or assess problem understanding and reasoning abilities in real-world programming scenarios. Each TACO problem contains fine-grained labels such as task topic, algorithm, programming skills, and difficulty level, offering more precise references for training and evaluating code generation models.
scale_en: 25433 items training problems, 1000 items test problems, up to 1,550,000 items solutions
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Phase["Phase 1: Data Sourcing"]
        A([Start])
        B[/Publicly Available Datasets/]
        C[/Manually Verified Open-Source Problems/]
    end

    subgraph Phase["Phase 2: Data Collection"]
        D(Collect Python 3 Solutions)
        E{Focus on Python 3}
    end

    subgraph Phase["Phase 3: Data Processing & Filtering"]
        F(Initial Dataset of 26,443 Problems)
        G{Filter for High-Quality Problems}
        H(Create Training and Test Sets)
    end

    subgraph Phase["Phase 4: Annotation & Labeling"]
        I(Meticulously Revise and Categorize Raw Tags)
        J{Categorize into 36 Primary Algorithm Topics}
        K(Supplement with Foundational Programming Skills)
        L(Annotate Time and Space Complexity)
    end

    subgraph Phase["Phase 5: Quality Control"]
        M(Manual Review of Problems and Solutions)
        N{Check for Correctness and Clarity}
        O(Ensure High-Quality Test Cases)
    end

    subgraph Phase["Phase 6: Final Benchmark Generation"]
        P(Generate Training Set with 25,433 Problems)
        Q(Generate Test Set with 1,000 Problems)
        R(Total of 1.55 Million Diverse Solutions)
        S([End])
    end

    A --> B
    A --> C
    B --> D

name: TAU-Bench
intro: TAU-Bench是一个用于评估语言代理在真实世界领域中与工具、代理和用户交互的基准。它通过模拟用户（由语言模型扮演）和配备了特定领域API工具及策略指南的语言代理之间的动态对话来进行。该基准采用一种高效且可靠的评估方法，通过比较对话结束时的数据库状态与预设的注释目标状态来评分，并引入了pass@k指标来衡量代理在多次试验中的行为可靠性。
intro_en: TAU-Bench is a benchmark for evaluating language agents interacting with tools, agents, and users in real-world domains. It simulates dynamic dialogues between users (played by language models) and language agents equipped with domain-specific API tools and policy guidelines. The benchmark uses an efficient and reliable evaluation method by scoring based on the database state at the end of the dialogue compared to predefined annotated target states, and introduces the pass@k metric to measure agent behavior reliability across multiple trials.

        T --> U[/Benchmark Evaluation Results/]
    end

    U --> End([End])

name: TAU2Bench
intro: τ²-Bench是一个用于评估在双控环境中对话式AI代理的基准。与现有基准不同，它模拟了AI代理和用户都能使用工具与共享动态环境交互的真实场景。该基准包含一个新颖的电信双控域，一个可程序化创建多样化任务的组合任务生成器，以及一个与环境紧密耦合的可靠用户模拟器。实验表明，从单用户控制到双用户控制的转变会导致代理性能显著下降，这凸显了指导用户所面临的挑战。
org: Sierra, University of Southern California, University of Toronto, UCLA, Princeton University
task_type: 对话式任务, 工具调用
eval_feature: 双控环境, 人机协作, 动态环境
scale: 4个领域、12个工具集、75个任务模板
eval_feature_en: Dual-control Environment, Human-machine Collaboration, Dynamic Environment
intro_en: τ²-Bench is a benchmark for evaluating conversational AI agents in dual-control environments. Unlike existing benchmarks, it simulates realistic scenarios where both AI agents and users can interact with a shared dynamic environment using tools. The benchmark features a novel telecom dual-control domain, a compositional task generator for programmable diverse tasks, and a reliable user simulator tightly coupled with the environment. Experiments show that shifting from single-user control to dual-user control significantly degrades agent performance, highlighting the challenges in guiding users.
scale_en: 4 items domains, 12 items toolsets, 75 tasks templates

name: TempCompass
intro: TempCompass是一个旨在全面评估视频大语言模型（Video LLMs）时间感知能力的基准测试。现有基准未能区分不同的时间维度，且任务形式单一。TempCompass通过引入多样化的时间维度和任务形式，并采用构建冲突视频和LLM生成指令的策略来收集高质量数据。该基准揭示了当前Video LLMs在时间感知能力上的不足。
org: Tsinghua University, Peking University, Tencent AI Lab, The Hong Kong University of Science and Tech
task_type: 开放式问答, 选择题
intro_en: TempCompass is a benchmark designed to comprehensively evaluate the temporal awareness of Video LLMs. Existing benchmarks fail to differentiate between various temporal dimensions and have limited task formats. TempCompass addresses this by introducing diverse temporal dimensions and task types, and by using strategies such as constructing conflicting videos and generating instructions with LLMs to collect high-quality data. This benchmark reveals the current shortcomings of Video LLMs in temporal understanding.

name: Terminal Bench 2
intro: terminal-bench-2-verified是Terminal-Bench 2.0的改进版，评估AI智能体在命令行环境下执行任务的能力。该版本修正了环境不一致和指令模糊等问题，提供更可靠的终端场景能力评测。它由89个在计算机终端环境中、受真实工作流启发的难题组成。
org: Zhipu AI, Anthropic, Laude Institute, University of Washington, Google
eval_feature: 过程导向, 真实世界任务
intro_en: terminal-bench-2-verified is an improved version of Terminal-Bench 2.0 that evaluates AI agents' ability to perform tasks in command-line environments. This version addresses issues like environment inconsistency and ambiguous instructions, providing a more reliable assessment of terminal scenario capabilities. It consists of 89 challenges inspired by real workflows in computer terminal environments.

    data_source --> data_collection(Data Collection)
    data_collection --> data_preprocessing(Data Preprocessing)
    data_preprocessing --> data_filtering{Filter Irrelevant Data?}
    data_filtering -- Yes --> filtered_data[/Filtered Data/]
    data_filtering -- No --> data_preprocessing
    filtered_data --> task_definition(Define Evaluation Tasks)
    task_definition --> agent_selection(Select AI Agents)
    agent_selection --> test_case_generation(Generate Test Cases)
    test_case_generation --> human_annotation(Human Annotation for Ground Truth)
    human_annotation --> quality_control{Annotation Quality Check?}
    quality_control -- Pass --> annotated_data[/Annotated Ground Truth Data/]

name: TestExplora
intro: TestExplora是一个评估大型语言模型作为主动测试者能力的基准，包含2389个任务，覆盖482个代码仓库。模型需通过对比代码实现与文档意图发现漏洞，填补了当前评测中缺乏主动漏洞发现能力的空白。
build_method: 基于真实世界数据, 自动生成
build_method_en: Based on real-world data, Automated Generation
intro_en: TestExplora is a benchmark for evaluating large language models as proactive testers, comprising 2,389 tasks across 482 code repositories. Models are required to identify vulnerabilities by comparing code implementations with documentation intent, addressing the current gap in active vulnerability detection capabilities.

name: TiC-LM
intro: TiC-LM 是一个用于时间连续语言模型预训练的网络规模基准。它包含一个源自114个Common Crawl（CC）数据转储的大规模时间分层数据集，比以前的基准大几个数量级。该基准还设计了跨通用领域（CC数据）和特定领域（维基百科、StackExchange和代码文档）的时间分层评估，以评估持续学习方法在适应新数据的同时保留过去知识的能力。
metric: held-out loss, Perplexity, Forgetting, Forward Transfer
eval_feature: 长文本, 动态, 实时
intro_en: TiC-LM is a network scale benchmark for pretraining time-continuous language models. It includes a large-scale time-layered dataset derived from 114 Common Crawl (CC) data dumps, several orders of magnitude larger than previous benchmarks. The benchmark also features time-layered evaluations across general domains (CC data) and specific domains (Wikipedia, StackExchange, and code documentation) to assess continual learning methods' ability to retain past knowledge while adapting to new data.
metric_en: held-out loss, Perplexity, Forgetting, Forward Transfer

name: ToM-Bench
intro: ToM-Bench是一个系统的、自动化的、原创的双语心智理论（ToM）基准，旨在评估大型语言模型（LLM）的ToM能力。它包含一个涵盖8个任务和31个社会认知能力的评估框架，采用多项选择题格式以支持自动化和无偏见的评估，并从头开始构建双语清单以避免数据泄漏。
eval_feature: 多轮, 长文本, 结果导向, 领域特定
intro_en: ToM-Bench is a systematic, automated, and original bilingual Theory of Mind (ToM) benchmark designed to evaluate the ToM capabilities of large language models (LLMs). It features an evaluation framework covering 8 tasks and 31 social cognitive abilities, uses multiple-choice questions to enable automated and unbiased assessment, and is built from scratch with a bilingual checklist to prevent data leakage.
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    subgraph "Benchmark Construction Process"
        start([Start]) --> data_sourcing[/Psychological Literature/]
        data_sourcing --> framework_design(Systematic Framework Design)

        subgraph "Framework Design"
            framework_design --> identify_tasks(Identify 8 ToM Tasks)
            identify_tasks --> ground_atoms(Ground in ATOMS Framework)
            ground_atoms --> define_31_abilities(Define 31 ToM Abilities)
            define_31_abilities --> check_coverage{8 Tasks Cover 19/31 Abilities?}
            check_coverage -- Yes --> data_collection_start
            check_coverage -- No --> create_extra_samples(Create 12 Extra Ability Samples)
            create_extra_samples --> data_collection_start
        end

        subgraph "Data Collection & Annotation"
            data_collection_start([Data Collection Start])
            data_collection_start --> build_inventory(Build From Scratch Inventory)
            build_inventory --> manual_creation(Manually Create 2,860 Chinese Samples)
            manual_creation --> sample_format(Story + MCQ + Options)
            sample_format --> annotation_qc(Annotation & Quality Control)

            subgraph "Quality Control"
                annotation_qc --> round1_validation(Round 1 Validation: Worker A vs. B)
                round1_validation --> consensus1{Consensus Reached?}
                consensus1 -- No --> round2_validation(Round 2 Validation: Worker C + A + B)
                consensus1 -- Yes --> final_agreement(Final Agreement: 99.4%)
                round2_validation --> final_agreement
            end
        end

        subgraph "Translation & Finalization"
            final_agreement --> translate_to_en(Translate to English via GPT-4-0613)
            translate_to_en --> manual_check(Manually Check Translated Samples)
            manual_check --> bilingual_inventory[/Bilingual Inventory (2,860 Samples)/]
            bilingual_inventory --> benchmark_composition(Benchmark Composition)
            benchmark_composition --> output_task_view(Task View: 8 Tasks)
            benchmark_composition --> output_ability_view(Ability View: 31 Abilities)
        end

        output_ability_view --> end_node([End])

    style start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

name: TOMATO
intro: 我们引入了TOMATO，一个时间推理多模态评估基准，旨在严格评估多模态基础模型（MFMs）在视频理解中的时间推理能力。TOMATO包含1,484个精心策划、人工标注的问题，涵盖六项任务，应用于1,417个视频，包括805个自录和自生成视频。我们的综合评估揭示了最佳模型与人类之间57.3%的性能差距，并揭示了当前MFMs在解释连续视频序列方面的基本局限性。我们相信TOMATO将成为评估下一代MFMs的关键测试平台，并呼吁社区开发能够通过视频模态理解人类世界动态的AI系统。
eval_feature: 动态更新, 防污染, 思维链
intro_en: We introduce TOMATO, a temporal reasoning multimodal benchmark designed to rigorously evaluate the temporal reasoning capabilities of multimodal foundation models (MFMs) in video understanding. TOMATO contains 1,484 carefully curated and manually annotated questions across six tasks, applied to 1,417 videos, including 805 self-recorded and self-generated videos. Our comprehensive evaluation reveals a 57.3% performance gap between the best models and humans, highlighting fundamental limitations of current MFMs in interpreting continuous video sequences. We believe TOMATO will become a key testing platform for evaluating the next generation of MFMs and call on the community to develop AI systems capable of understanding human world dynamics through video modalities.

name: Toolathlon
intro: Toolathlon是一个用于评估语言代理在多样化、真实和长视域任务中性能的基准测试。它涵盖了32个软件应用和604个工具，从日常平台（如Google日历和Notion）到专业平台（如WooCommerce、Kubernetes和BigQuery）。该基准测试包含108个手动来源或制作的任务，平均需要约20轮交互才能完成。
org: The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology, All Hands AI, Carnegie Mellon University, Duke U
task_type: 多步工作流, 软件应用交互
eval_feature: 长视域任务, 真实环境
intro_en: Toolathlon is a benchmark for evaluating language agents' performance on diverse, realistic, and long-horizon tasks. It covers 32 software applications and 604 tools, ranging from everyday platforms like Google Calendar and Notion to professional platforms such as WooCommerce, Kubernetes, and BigQuery. The benchmark includes 108 manually sourced or created tasks, each requiring an average of about 20 interaction rounds to complete.

name: ToolSandbox
intro: ToolSandbox是一个用于评估大型语言模型（LLM）在有状态、对话式和交互式场景中工具使用能力的基准。它包含状态化的工具执行、工具间的隐式状态依赖，以及一个支持策略性对话评估的内置用户模拟器。该基准旨在揭示开源与专有模型在处理状态依赖、规范化及信息不足等复杂任务时的性能差距。
build_method: 专家构建, LLM生成, 混合
eval_feature: 多轮, 过程导向, State-based
intro_en: ToolSandbox is a benchmark for evaluating large language models' (LLM) ability to use tools in stateful, conversational, and interactive scenarios. It includes stateful tool execution, implicit state dependencies between tools, and a built-in user simulator that supports strategic dialogue evaluation. The benchmark aims to reveal performance gaps between open-source and proprietary models in handling complex tasks involving state dependencies, normalization, and information insufficiency.

name: TreeBench
intro: TreeBench是一个诊断性基准测试，旨在评估多模态AI模型在视觉基础推理方面的能力。它基于三个原则构建：复杂场景中细微目标的视觉感知、通过边界框评估的可追溯证据以及超越简单目标定位的二阶推理。该基准包含405个具有挑战性的视觉问答对，即使是最先进的模型也难以达到60%的准确率。
org: Institute of Automation, Chinese Academy of Sciences, University of Chinese Academy of Sciences, Byt
build_method: 人工标注, 专家构建, 混合
intro_en: TreeBench is a diagnostic benchmark designed to evaluate multimodal AI models' capabilities in visual fundamental reasoning. It is built on three principles: visual perception of subtle objects in complex scenes, traceable evidence assessed through bounding boxes, and second-order reasoning beyond simple object localization. The benchmark includes 405 challenging visual question-answer pairs, with even state-of-the-art models struggling to achieve 60% accuracy.

name: TrialBench
intro: TrialBench是一个综合性多模态AI就绪临床试验数据集套件，包含23个精心策划的数据集，涵盖多模态输入特征和8个关键预测挑战，旨在推动临床试验设计中先进AI方法的发展。
build_method: 人工策展，整合临床试验数据
eval_feature: 涵盖多模态输入特征和8个关键预测挑战
build_method_en: Manual curation, integration of clinical trial data
eval_feature_en: Covers multimodal input features and 8 key prediction challenges
intro_en: TrialBench is a comprehensive multimodal AI-ready clinical trial dataset suite, comprising 23 carefully curated datasets that cover multimodal input features and 8 key prediction challenges, aimed at advancing state-of-the-art AI methods in clinical trial design.
mermaid_flowchart:
flowchart LR
    A(Raw Clinical Trial Data) --> B[Data Collection]
    B --> C[Data Preprocessing & Anonymization]
    C --> D[Task Definition & Formulation]
    D --> E{{Expert Construction & Annotation}}
    E --> F{Data Validation}
    F --|Pass| --> G(TrialBench Dataset)
    F --|Fail| --> C
    G --> H[AI Model Evaluation]
    H --> I[Performance Analysis]
    I --> J[Benchmark Release]

name: TroubleshootingBench
intro: TroubleshootingBench是一个用于评估模型识别和纠正生物协议中实际错误能力的基准测试。它包含一个由专家编写的湿实验室程序故障排除数据集，侧重于隐含知识和未在线发布的未受污染程序。该数据集包括52个协议，每个协议都配有三个专家编写的故障排除问题，旨在测试模型在非公开、基于经验的协议和依赖于隐性程序知识的错误上的表现。
build_method: 专家构建, 人工标注
intro_en: TroubleshootingBench is a benchmark designed to evaluate models' ability to identify and correct real errors in biological protocols. It includes a dataset of wet lab protocol troubleshooting cases written by experts, focusing on implicit knowledge and unpublished, uncontaminated protocols. The dataset contains 52 protocols, each paired with three expert-crafted troubleshooting questions, aimed at testing model performance on non-public, experience-based protocols and errors relying on implicit procedural knowledge.

name: TrustLLM
intro: TrustLLM是一项关于LLM可信度的综合研究，提出涵盖八个维度的可信原则，并基于此建立包含真诚性、安全性、公平性、鲁棒性、隐私及机器伦理等六个维度的评测基准。研究评估了16个主流LLM，涉及30多个数据集。
org: Linköping University, University of Copenhagen, Alexandra Institute, Norwegian University of Science
build_method: 混合, 人工标注, 专家构建, 网络爬取
metric: Accuracy, F1, BLEU, ROUGE, Perplexity, LLM-as-Judge
task_type: 选择题, 开放式问答, 分类, 生成
eval_feature: 多轮, 对抗性, 领域特定
task_type_en: Multiple Choice, Open-ended QA, Classification, Generation
build_method_en: Hybrid, Human Annotation, Expert Curation, Web Crawling
intro_en: TrustLLM is a comprehensive study on LLM trustworthiness, proposing trust principles across eight dimensions and establishing an evaluation benchmark covering six dimensions: sincerity, safety, fairness, robustness, privacy, and machine ethics. The study assesses 16 mainstream LLMs using over 30 datasets.
metric_en: Accuracy, F1, BLEU, ROUGE, Perplexity, LLM-as-Judge
flowchart_en:

        style P1_A fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    end

    subgraph Phase2["Phase 2: Benchmark Establishment"]

        style P2_A fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff

        style P2_B fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

        P2_D(Define Experimental Settings)
        style P2_D fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    end

    subgraph Phase3["Phase 3: Trustworthiness Assessment"]
        subgraph Truthfulness["Truthfulness Assessment"]
            T_A(Misinformation Generation)
            style T_A fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff

            S_D(Misuse Scenario Evaluation)

        end

            F_A(Stereotype Recognition)
            style F_A fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff

            style F_B fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
            F_C(Preference Bias Evaluation)
            style F_C fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
        end

        subgraph Robustness["Robustness Assessment"]
            R_A(Natural Noise Input Testing)
            style R_A fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
            R_B(OOD Task Resilience Assessment)
            style R_B fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
        end

        subgraph Privacy["Privacy Assessment"]
            Pr_A(Privacy Awareness Evaluation)
            style Pr_A fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
            Pr_B(Privacy Leakage Detection)
            style Pr_B fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
        end

        subgraph MachineEthics["Machine Ethics Assessment"]
            ME_A(Implicit Ethics Evaluation)
            style ME_A fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
            ME_B(Explicit Ethics Evaluation)
            style ME_B fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
            ME_C(Ethical Awareness Assessment)
            style ME_C fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
        end
    end

    subgraph Phase4["Phase 4: Evaluation and Analysis"]
        E_A(Evaluate 16 LLMs with 30+ Datasets)
        style E_A fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
        E_B{Analyze Findings & Insights}
        style E_B fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
        E_C(Identify Trustworthiness-Utility Relationship)
        style E_C fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
        E_D(Compare Proprietary vs. Open-Source LLMs)
        style E_D fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
        E_E(Detect Over-calibration Issues)
        style E_E fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    end

    subgraph Phase5["Phase 5: Dissemination"]
        D_A[/Release Dataset, Code, Toolkit/]
        style D_A fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
        D_B[/Publish Leaderboard on Website/]
        style D_B fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff
    end

    finish([End TrustLLM Benchmark Construction])
    style finish fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

    start --> P1_A
    P1_A --> P2_A
    P2_A --> P2_B
    P2_B --> P2_C
    P2_C --> P2_D
    P2_D --> T_A

    T_A --> T_B
    T_B --> T_C

    S_A --> S_B

    S_C --> S_D
    S_D --> F_A

    F_A --> F_B

    F_C --> R_A

    R_A --> R_B
    R_B --> Pr_A

    Pr_A --> Pr_B
    Pr_B --> ME_A

    ME_A --> ME_B
    ME_B --> ME_C
    ME_C --> E_A

    E_C --> E_D
    E_D --> E_E
    E_E --> D_A

    D_A --> D_B
    D_B --> finish

name: TruthfulQA
intro: TruthfulQA基准用于衡量语言模型回答问题时的真实性。它包含817个问题，涵盖38个类别，旨在诱导模型产生基于常见人类误解的错误答案，从而促使模型避免重复网络文本中的虚假信息。
metric: Accuracy, BLEURT, ROUGE, BLEU, GPT-judge, GPT-info
task_type: 开放式问答, 选择题
intro_en: The TruthfulQA benchmark measures the truthfulness of language models' answers. It contains 817 questions across 38 categories, designed to induce models to produce errors based on common human misconceptions, thereby encouraging them to avoid repeating false information found in web text.
metric_en: Accuracy, BLEURT, ROUGE, BLEU, GPT-judge, GPT-info
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    style Start fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style End fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style DataCollection fill:#0d3b66,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff
    style ProcessingFiltering fill:#1b4332,stroke:#52b788,color:#fff
    style AnnotationLabeling fill:#3d1a78,stroke:#9b59b6,color:#fff
    style QualityControl fill:#7d2a2a,stroke:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style EvaluationMetric fill:#2d4a22,stroke:#27ae60,color:#fff
    style OutputResult fill:#1a3a4a,stroke:#3498db,color:#fff

    Start([Start]) --> A(Define Truthfulness Objective)
    A --> B(Identify Imitative Falsehoods)

    subgraph Question Generation
        direction LR
        C(Write Initial Questions) --> D{Questions elicit false answers?}
        D -- Yes --> E(Test on GPT-3-175B)
        E --> F{GPT-3 answers correctly?}
        F -- No --> G[/437 Filtered Questions/]
        D -- No --> H(Write 380 Unfiltered Questions)
        G & H --> I(Combine Filtered & Unfiltered Questions)
        I --> J[/817 Total Questions/]
    end

    J --> K(Craft True/False Reference Answers)
    K --> L(Identify Supporting Sources for Answers)

    subgraph Benchmark Validation
        direction LR
        L --> M(Recruit External Researchers)
        M --> N(Validator Task: 100 Random Questions)
        N --> O{Validator Disagreement > 0%?}
        O -- Yes (7%) --> P(Note Disagreement)
        M --> Q(Participant Task: 250 Random Questions)
        Q --> R(Participant Answers as Human Baseline)
        R --> S{6% of Participant Answers False?}
        S -- Yes --> T(Confirm Human Baseline)
    end

    T --> U(Define Evaluation Metrics)

    subgraph Model Evaluation
        direction LR
        U --> V(Main Task: Natural Language Generation)
        V --> W(Human Evaluation: Truthfulness & Informativeness)
        U --> X(Additional Task: Multiple-Choice)
        X --> Y(Compute Likelihood of Reference Answers)
        W & Y --> Z(Develop Automated Metric: GPT-judge)
        Z --> AA(Finetune GPT-judge on 6.9k Author Examples)
        AA --> BB(Finetune GPT-judge on 15.5k Model-Human Examples)
        BB --> CC{GPT-judge Accuracy 90-96%?}
        CC -- Yes --> DD(Validate GPT-judge)
    end

    DD --> End([End])

        BB --> CC{GPT-judge准确率90-96%?}
        CC -- 是 --> DD(验证GPT-judge)
    end

    DD --> End([结束])

name: TVBench
intro: TVBench是一个新颖的开源视频多项选择问答基准，旨在评估视频语言模型中的时间理解能力。它通过设计具有时间挑战性的候选答案、减少文本偏见以及排除需要先验知识的问题来解决现有视频语言基准的局限性。TVBench要求模型高度依赖时间理解，而非静态信息或世界知识。
org: University of Santiago de Compostela, University of Amsterdam, University of Technology Nuremberg
eval_feature: 动态更新, 防污染, 思维链
intro_en: TVBench is a novel open-source video multiple-choice question answering benchmark designed to evaluate temporal understanding in video language models. It addresses limitations of existing video language benchmarks by creating temporally challenging candidate answers, reducing textual biases, and excluding questions requiring prior knowledge. TVBench demands models rely heavily on temporal understanding rather than static information or world knowledge.
flowchart_en:
flowchart TD
    subgraph "Phase 1: Data Sourcing"
        A0([Start])
        A1[/Perception Test, CLEVRER, STAR, MoVQA, Charades-STA, NTU RGB+D, FunQA, CSV/]
        A0 --> A1
    end

    subgraph "Phase 2: Task & QA Generation"
        B1("Filter videos based on 10 task categories")
        B2("Generate QA pairs using task-specific templates")
        B3{"Is the task 'Action Antonym'?"}
        B4("Select opposite action as negative candidate")
        B5{"Is the task 'Action/Object Count'?"}
        B6("Use balanced 4-option candidate set")
        B7{"Is the task 'Action Sequence'?"}
        B8("Use the other action as the negative candidate")
        B9{"Is the task 'Egocentric Sequence'?"}
        B10("Generate 3 negative candidates by swapping actions")
        A1 --> B1 --> B2
        B2 --> B3
        B3 -->|Yes| B4
        B3 -->|No| B5
        B5 -->|Yes| B6
        B5 -->|No| B7
        B7 -->|Yes| B8
        B7 -->|No| B9
        B9 -->|Yes| B10
        B4 --> C1
        B6 --> C1
        B8 --> C1
        B10 --> C1
        B9 -->|No| C1
    end

    subgraph "Phase 3: Quality Control"
        C1("Initial QA Pair Set")
        C2{"Does the QA pair require temporal reasoning?"}
        C3("Discard QA pair")
        C4{"Is the question overly informative (textual bias)?"}
        C5("Rewrite question using minimal template")
        C6{"Can the question be answered by world knowledge?"}
        C7("Discard QA pair")
        C1 --> C2
        C2 -->|No| C3
        C2 -->|Yes| C4
        C4 -->|Yes| C5
        C4 -->|No| C6
        C5 --> C6
        C6 -->|Yes| C7
        C6 -->|No| D1
    end

    subgraph "Phase 4: Human Validation"
        D1[/2,654 Generated QA Pairs/]
        D2("Select a 400-video subset for human annotation")
        D3("14 annotators label the subset")
        D4{"Is human accuracy high?"}
        D5("Finalize benchmark")
        D1 --> D2 --> D3 --> D4
        D4 -->|Yes, 94.8%| D5
    end

    subgraph "Phase 5: Final Benchmark"
        E1([/TVBench/])
        D5 --> E1
    end

    style A0 fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#4a90d9,color:#fff

name: Vending Bench
intro: Vending-Bench是一个模拟环境，旨在测试基于LLM的Agent在长期运行的业务场景中保持连贯性的能力。该基准要求Agent管理自动售货机，包括平衡库存、下订单、定价和处理日常费用。实验表明，LLM在长期任务中的表现差异很大，一些模型能够盈利，但所有模型都可能因误解交付计划或遗忘订单而失败。该基准有助于评估AI系统在复杂、长期决策中的持续连贯性。
metric: 盈利能力, 任务完成率
intro_en: Vending-Bench is a simulated environment designed to test the ability of LLM-based Agents to maintain coherence in long-running business scenarios. The benchmark requires Agents to manage vending machines, including balancing inventory, placing orders, pricing, and handling daily expenses. Experiments show significant performance variation among LLMs in long-term tasks; some models can turn a profit, but all may fail due to misunderstanding delivery schedules or forgetting orders. This benchmark helps evaluate AI systems' sustained coherence in complex, long-term decision-making.

name: VIBE
intro: VIBE是MiniMax推出的新基准，旨在衡量模型从零开始构建完整、可运行应用的全栈能力。它采用“Agent-as-a-Verifier” (AaaV) 范式，在真实的执行环境中自动评估应用的交互逻辑和视觉呈现，覆盖了Web、移动端（Android/iOS）、后端和科学模拟等多个领域。
org: Institute of Automation, Chinese Academy of Sciences, University of Chinese Academy of Sciences
metric: 执行、交互和视觉/美学层
modality: 文本, 代码, 多模态
eval_feature: 过程导向, 结果导向, 领域特定
intro_en: VIBE is a new benchmark introduced by MiniMax designed to measure a model's full-stack ability to build complete, functional applications from scratch. It uses the "Agent-as-a-Verifier" (AaaV) paradigm to automatically evaluate the interaction logic and visual presentation of applications in real execution environments, covering multiple domains including Web, mobile (Android/iOS), backend, and scientific simulation.
